## 大( )同( )区(區)
---
## Datong District, Taipei
### Dàtóng Qū
---
- **traditional:** 大同區
- **definition:** Datong District, Taipei
- **pinyin:** Dàtóng Qū
- **components:**
  - 大同
    - dàtóng
    - Great Unity; Datong
  - 区(區)
    - qū
    - district; area; region; zone
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 大同 (dàtóng, "Great Unity") is a Confucian utopian ideal from the Book of Rites (禮記/礼记), and 區 (qū, "district") refers to an administrative division.
  - **interpretation:** The term 大同區 (大同區) (dàtóng qū, "Datong District") is named after a philosophical concept emphasizing harmony and unity. It symbolizes the aspiration for a harmonious community within the administrative area.
- **examples:**
  - 我住在大( )同( )区(區)。
    - Wǒ zhù zài Dàtóng Qū.
    - I live in Datong District.
  - 大( )同( )区(區)历(歷)史( )悠( )久( )，街( )道( )繁( )华(華)。
    - Dàtóng Qū lìshǐ yōujiǔ, jiēdào fánhuá.
    - Datong District has a long history, and its streets are bustling.
  - 大( )同( )区(區)保( )留( )传(傳)统(統)，吸( )引( )游(遊)客( )。
    - Dàtóng Qū bǎoliú chuántǒng, xīyǐn yóukè.
    - Datong District preserves traditions and attracts tourists.
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 大同
- **definition:** Great Unity; Datong
- **pinyin:** dàtóng
- **components:**
  - 大
    - dà
    - great; big; large
  - 同
    - tóng
    - same; unity; together; common
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 大 (dà, "great") + 同 (tóng, "same; unity") =
    - Great Unity, a concept of harmony where all people are united as one.
  - **interpretation:** 大同 (dàtóng, "Great Unity") is a Confucian utopian ideal described in the Book of Rites (禮記/礼记) where society achieves a state of perfect harmony, justice, and equality. It is often referenced in both historical and philosophical contexts as an aspiration for social order.
- **examples:**
  - 全世界人民向往大同。
    - Quán shìjiè rénmín xiàngwǎng dàtóng.
    - People around the world yearn for Great Unity.
  - 先賢夢想大同，後人追求和諧。
    - Xiānxián mèngxiǎng dàtóng, hòurén zhuīqiú héxié.
    - The wise of the past dream of Great Unity, while later generations strive for harmony.
  - 柏拉图論大同，啟發現代理想社會。
    - Bǎilātú lùn dàtóng, qǐfā xiàndài lǐxiǎng shèhuì.
    - Plato discussed Great Unity, inspiring the modern ideal society.
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 大同 → 大
- **definition:** great; big; large
- **pinyin:** dà
- **components:**
  - 夫
    - fū
    - man
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a person facing forward ->
    - Borrowed phonetically for 'big'; original form of 夫 (fū, 'man')
  - **interpretation:** The character 大(dà) depicts a person facing forward, representing 'big' by suggesting the idea of someone standing prominently. Although often thought of as a person with outstretched arms, early scripts do not support this interpretation. It was originally the form of 夫 (fū, 'man') and was later phonetically borrowed for the meaning 'big.'
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)房( )子( )很( )大( )。
    - Zhè ge fángzi hěn dà.
    - This house is very big.
  - 大( )海( )波( )涛(濤)汹(洶)涌(湧)，展( )现(現)出( )大( )自( )然( )的( )力( )量( )。
    - Dàhǎi bōtāo xiōngyǒng, zhǎnxiàn chū dàzìrán de lìliàng.
    - The vast ocean waves surge, showcasing the power of nature.
  - 大( )人( )物( )登( )场(場)，留( )下( )传(傳)奇( )。
    - Dà rénwù dēngchǎng, liúxià chuánqí.
    - Great figures make an appearance, leaving behind legends.
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 大同 → 大 → 夫
- **definition:** man
- **pinyin:** fū
- **components:**
  - 大
    - dà
    - big
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a variant form of 大 (dà, "big") representing an adult man with a hairpin ->
    - became associated with 夫 (fū, "man").
  - **interpretation:** 夫 (fū, "man") was visually similar to 大 (dà, "big") and was originally interchangeable. The addition of the horizontal stroke was interpreted as a hairpin, symbolizing adulthood for men.
- **examples:**
  - 此( )夫( )心( )怀(懷)大( )志( )。
    - Cǐ fū xīn huái dà zhì.
    - This man has lofty aspirations.
  - 他( )勤( )劳(勞)努( )力( )，堪( )称(稱)良( )夫( )。
    - Tā qín láo nǔ lì, kān chēng liáng fū.
    - He works diligently and is regarded as an exemplary man.
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 大同 → 同
- **definition:** same; unity; together; common
- **pinyin:** tóng
- **components:**
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - "mouth"
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Originally depicted as a pipe 筒 (tǒng, "pipe") ->
    - 口 added as decoration ->
    - interpreted as mouth 口 (kǒu, "mouth") giving orders ->
    - evolved to mean togetherness and unity
  - **interpretation:** The character 同 (tóng, "same; unity; together; common") originally depicted a pipe but later took on the symbolic meaning of unity, possibly influenced by the shared cooperation in activities like music or rigid group tasks. The addition of 口 (kǒu, "mouth") emphasizes communication and collaboration.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )们(們)同( )心( )协(協)力( )。
    - Wǒmen tóngxīn xiélì.
    - We work together with one heart and mind.
  - 他( )们(們)穿( )着(著)同( )款( )衣( )服( )，看( )起( )来(來)十( )分( )整( )齐(齊)。
    - Tāmen chuānzhe tóng kuǎn yīfú, kàn qǐlái shífēn zhěngqí.
    - They are wearing the same style of clothes and look very orderly.
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 大同 → 同 → 口
- **definition:** "mouth"
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 区(區)
- **traditional:** 區
- **definition:** district; area; region; zone
- **pinyin:** qū
- **components:**
  - 匸
    - xì
    - enclosure
  - 品
    - pǐn
    - goods/people
  - 乂
    - yì
    - to manage
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 匸 (enclosed space) + 品 (many people) =
    - many people within an enclosure ->
    - area, district
  - **interpretation:** The character 區 (qū, 'district') visually represents an area where many people reside, symbolized by 品 (pǐn, 'goods/people') enclosed within 匸 (xì, 'enclosure'). This layout effectively conveys the idea of a defined space containing a community or district.
  - **simplification:** 區 was simplified to 区(區) by reducing 品 to 乂 (yì, 'to manage'), keeping the enclosure meaning while reducing strokes.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )住( )在( )繁( )华(華)的( )区(區)。
    - Wǒ zhù zài fán huá de qū.
    - I live in a bustling district.
  - 古( )代( )文( )人( )常( )在( )这(這)区(區)，寻(尋)觅(覓)灵(靈)感( )。
    - Gǔ dài wén rén cháng zài zhè qū, xún mì líng gǎn.
    - Ancient literati often sought inspiration in this region.
  - 他( )参(參)观(觀)了( )多( )个(個)区(區)，体(體)验(驗)了( )不( )同( )的( )文( )化( )。
    - Tā cān guān le duō gè qū, tǐ yàn le bù tóng de wén huà.
    - He visited several districts and experienced diverse cultures.
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 区(區) → 匸
- **definition:** enclosure
- **pinyin:** xì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 𠃊 (symbolic graphic) ->
    - depicts an enclosure hiding something or someone ->
    - evolved to represent a concealed space or item
  - **interpretation:** The character 匸(xì, 'box') is a pictogram representing a concealed or enclosed space. Its graphical form suggests something contained or hidden, which aligns with its meaning as a 'box' or an enclosure. Unlike other related pictograms that depict physical containers, 匸 emphasizes the aspect of concealment or secrecy.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )把( )匸( )搬( )到( )了( )房( )间(間)角( )落( )。
    - Tā bǎ xì bān dào le fángjiān jiǎoluò.
    - He moved the box to the corner of the room.
  - 我( )们(們)在( )仓(倉)库(庫)找( )到( )了( )匸( )，里(裡)面( )装(裝)有( )秘( )密( )文( )件( )。
    - Wǒmen zài cāngkù zhǎodào le xì, lǐmiàn zhuāng yǒu mìmì wénjiàn.
    - We found the box in the warehouse, and it contained secret documents.
  - 工( )厂(廠)工( )人( )把( )旧(舊)匸( )清( )理( )了( )，然( )后(後)再( )利( )用( )。
    - Gōngchǎng gōngrén bǎ jiù xì qīnglǐ le, ránhòu zài lìyòng.
    - Factory workers cleaned the old box and then reused it.
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 区(區) → 品
- **definition:** goods/people
- **pinyin:** pǐn
- **components:**
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - Three 口 (kǒu, 'mouth') arranged together =
    - multiple mouths speaking or consuming ->
    - representing many or various items
  - **interpretation:** The use of three mouths suggests plurality or variety. It's similar in structure to 晶 (jīng, 'sparkle'), which uses three 日 (rì, 'sun') to suggest brightness or vibrancy with multiple light sources.
- **examples:**
  - 比( )赛(賽)吸( )引( )了( )数(數)以( )千( )计(計)的( )品( )，个(個)个(個)英( )姿( )飒(颯)爽( )。
    - Bǐsài xīyǐnle shù yǐ qiān jì de pǐn, gège yīngzī sàshuǎng.
    - The competition attracted thousands of people, and everyone looked heroic.
  - 节(節)日( )庆(慶)典( )上( )品( )众(眾)多( )，场(場)面( )热(熱)烈( )非( )凡( )。
    - Jiérì qìngdiǎn shàng pǐn zhòngduō, chǎngmiàn rèliè fēifán.
    - The festive celebration saw a large crowd, and the scene was extraordinarily lively.
  - 广(廣)场(場)上( )聚( )集( )了( )众(眾)多( )品( )，大( )家( )热(熱)情( )互( )动(動)。
    - Guǎngchǎng shàng jùjíle zhòngduō pǐn, dàjiā rèqíng hùdòng.
    - The square gathered a multitude of people, and everyone interacted enthusiastically.
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 区(區) → 品 → 口
- **definition:** mouth
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.
### 大( )同( )区(區) → 区(區) → 乂
- **definition:** to manage
- **pinyin:** yì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a tool (similar to a sickle or a pair of scissors) for mowing or weeding ->
    - used for governing ->
    - final form 乂
  - **interpretation:** The character 乂 originally depicted a tool used for cutting, and by extension, it was associated with governing due to the notion of cutting or managing weeds as a metaphor for ruling effectively. This sense of order is crucial in governance.
- **examples:**
  - 这些年，他致力于地方的治理(這些年，他致力於地方的治理)。
    - Zhèxiē nián, tā zhìlì yú dìfāng de zhìlǐ.
    - Over the years, he has dedicated himself to local governance.
  - 为民者，先自齐以治家(為民者，先自齊以治家)。
    - Wèi mín zhě, xiān zì qí yǐ zhì jiā.
    - Those who govern for the people, first manage their own households.
  - 长(長)者( )都( )希( )望( )后(後)代( )能( )好( )好( )治( )理( )天( )下( )。
    - Zhǎng zhě dōu xīwàng hòudài néng hǎohǎo zhìlǐ tiānxià.
    - The elders hope that future generations can govern the world well.

## 三弦
---
## three-stringed Chinese lute used in Pingtan
### sānxián
---
- **definition:** three-stringed Chinese lute used in Pingtan
- **pinyin:** sānxián
- **components:**
  - 三
    - sān
    - three
  - 弦
    - xián
    - string
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 三 (sān, "three") + 弦 (xián, "string")
  - **interpretation:** The word 三弦 (sānxián, "three-stringed lute") refers to a traditional Chinese musical instrument with three strings. It is often used in the storytelling art form known as Pingtan, popular in the Jiangsu and Zhejiang provinces.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )听(聽)三( )弦( )的( )旋( )律( )陶( )醉( )。
    - Tā tīng sānxián de xuànlǜ táozuì.
    - He becomes enchanted by the melody of the three-stringed lute.
  - 在( )评(評)弹(彈)表( )演( )中( )，艺(藝)人( )弹(彈)奏( )三( )弦( )伴( )奏( )。
    - Zài píngtán biǎoyǎn zhōng, yìrén dànzòu sānxián bànzòu.
    - During the Pingtan performance, the artist plays the three-stringed lute to accompany the show.
  - 古( )时(時)的( )文( )人( )常( )以( )三( )弦( )为(為)乐(樂)器( )。
    - Gǔshí de wénrén cháng yǐ sānxián wéi yuèqì.
    - In ancient times, scholars often used the three-stringed lute as a musical instrument.
### 三弦 → 三
- **definition:** three
- **pinyin:** sān
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Ideogram
  - **description:**
    - 一 (yī, "one") triplicated ->
    - 三 (sān, "three")
  - **interpretation:** The character 三 (sān, "three") is an ideogram depicting the concept of the number three through the use of three parallel horizontal strokes. This straightforward representation makes it consistent with related numeric characters like 一 (yī, "one") and 二 (èr, "two").
- **examples:**
  - 今( )天( )我( )买(買)了( )三( )斤( )苹(蘋)果( )。
    - Jīntiān wǒ mǎi le sān jīn píngguǒ.
    - Today I bought three jin of apples.
  - 他( )有( )三( )支( )笔(筆)，我只想要其中一支。
    - Tā yǒu sān zhī bǐ, wǒ zhǐ xiǎng yào qízhōng yī zhī.
    - He has three pens, but I only want one of them.
  - 他( )们(們)吃( )了( )三( )碗( )面(麵)，觉(覺)得( )非( )常( )满(滿)意( )。
    - Tāmen chī le sān wǎn miàn, juéde fēicháng mǎnyì.
    - They ate three bowls of noodles and felt very satisfied.
### 三弦 → 弦
- **definition:** string
- **pinyin:** xián
- **components:**
  - 糸
    - mì
    - silk thread
  - 玄
    - xuán
    - dark/mysterious
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 糸 (mì, "silk thread") + 玄 (xuán) =
    - thread or string-themed meaning ->
    - string of a bow or musical instrument
  - **interpretation:** The character 弦(xián, "string") combines 糸 (mì, "silk thread") as the semantic part to indicate its association with threads or strings, and 玄 (xuán) provides the phonetic element. Together, it conveys the idea of threads used as strings in bows or musical instruments.
- **examples:**
  - 吉他有六根弦。
    - Jítā yǒu liù gēn xián.
    - The guitar has six strings.
  - 我( )拨(撥)动(動)古( )琴( )的( )弦( )，奏( )出( )优(優)美( )的( )旋( )律( )。
    - Wǒ bōdòng gǔqín de xián, zòuchū yōuměi de xuànlǜ.
    - I pluck the strings of the guqin and play a beautiful melody.
### 三弦 → 弦 → 糸
- **definition:** silk thread
- **pinyin:** mì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a skein of silk
  - **interpretation:** The character 糸(mì, "silk; thread") represents a pictorial representation of a skein of silk, visually capturing the concept of thread or fiber.
- **examples:**
  - 她( )手( )工( )制(製)作( )了( )一( )条(條)漂( )亮( )的( )丝(絲)巾( )。
    - Tā shǒugōng zhìzuòle yī tiáo piàoliang de sī jīn.
    - She handcrafted a beautiful silk scarf.
  - 这(這)个(個)设(設)计(計)图(圖)必( )须(須)用( )细(細)糸( )画(畫)出( )来(來)。
    - Zhège shèjìtú bìxū yòng xì mì huà chūlái.
    - This design drawing must be sketched with fine thread.
  - 我( )们(們)参(參)观(觀)了( )古( )代( )的( )织(織)布( )技( )术(術)，对(對)丝(絲)绸(綢)制(製)作( )产(產)生( )了( )兴(興)趣( )。
    - Wǒmen cānguānle gǔdài de zhībù jìshù, duì sīchóu zhìzuò chǎnshēngle xìngqù.
    - We visited ancient weaving techniques and developed an interest in silk production.
### 三弦 → 弦 → 玄
- **definition:** dark/mysterious
- **pinyin:** xuán
- **components:**
  - 幺
    - yāo
    - tiny threads
  - 亠
    - tóu
    - top mark
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:**
    - 幺 (yāo, "tiny threads") + 亠 (tóu, "top mark") =
    - two interwoven threads with a top mark representing a rope ->
    - dark/mysterious
  - **interpretation:** The character combines tiny threads 幺 with a distinguishing top mark 亠, symbolizing complexity or intricacy akin to a rope. This imagery connects to its meaning of something dark or mysterious, possibly alluding to complexity not easily understood.
- **examples:**
  - 夜( )幕( )降( )临(臨)时(時)，大( )地( )笼(籠)罩( )着(著)玄( )秘( )的( )色( )彩( )。
    - Yèmù jiànglín shí, dàdì lǒngzhào zhe xuánmì de sècǎi.
    - When night falls, the earth is enveloped by mysterious, dark hues.
  - 这(這)幅( )山( )水( )画(畫)充( )满(滿)玄( )妙( )意( )境( )。
    - Zhè fú shānshuǐhuà chōngmǎn xuánmiào yìjìng.
    - This landscape painting is full of mystical allure.
  - 他的故事令( )人( )感( )到( )玄( )虚(虛)莫( )测(測)。
    - Tā de gùshì lìngrén gǎndào xuánxū mòcè.
    - His story gives an impression of enigmatic inscrutability.
### 三弦 → 弦 → 玄 → 幺
- **definition:** tiny threads
- **pinyin:** yāo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a thin silk thread
  - **interpretation:** The character 幺 (yāo, "tiny threads") illustrates a thin silk thread, reflecting its meaning directly through its shape. It resembles a representation of something small and delicate, like the strands of silk used in weaving.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)微( )小( )的( )幺( )在( )织(織)物( )中( )闪(閃)耀( )。
    - Zhège wéixiǎo de yāo zài zhīwù zhōng shǎnyào.
    - The tiny thread sparkles within the fabric.
  - 织(織)布( )工( )巧( )妙( )地( )利( )用( )幺( )，将(將)其( )编(編)织(織)进(進)精( )美( )的( )锦(錦)缎(緞)中( )。
    - Zhībùgōng qiǎomiào de lìyòng yāo, jiāng qí biānzhī jìn jīngměi de jǐnduàn zhōng.
    - The weaver cleverly employs the tiny thread, integrating it into exquisite brocade.
### 三弦 → 弦 → 玄 → 亠
- **definition:** top mark
- **pinyin:** tóu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Couvercle with a handle ->
    - false friend: roof of a tower in 高 (gāo, "high") ->
    - head and open arms in 立 (lì, "stand")/交 (jiāo, "exchange") ->
    - roof of a hut in 六 (liù, "six") ->
    - top of traditional cloth in 衣 (yī, "clothing") ->
    - mark over silk threads in 玄 (xuán, "mysterious") ->
    - top of knife/chisel to mark prisoners in 辛 (xīn, "bitter") ->
    - grass in 离 (lí, "leave")
  - **interpretation:** 亠 is primarily a pictogram that initially represented a cover with a handle. However, its usage varies in different characters, symbolizing roofs, heads, tops of clothing, and marks, showing its flexibility and evolution in form and meaning across contexts.
- **examples:**
  - 在( )汉(漢)字( )结(結)构(構)中( )，亠( )作( )为(為)顶(頂)标(標)发(發)挥(揮)作( )用( )。
    - Zài hànzì jiégòu zhōng, tóu zuòwéi dǐngbiāo fāhuī zuòyòng.
    - In the structure of Chinese characters, 亠 acts as a top mark to exert its function.
  - 古( )代( )书(書)法( )作( )品( )中( )，亠( )显(顯)示( )出( )独(獨)特( )的( )审(審)美( )价(價)值( )。
    - Gǔdài shūfǎ zuòpǐn zhōng, tóu xiǎnshì chū dútè de shěnměi jiàzhí.
    - In ancient calligraphy, 亠 demonstrates a unique aesthetic value.

## 庆(慶)
---
## celebrate
### qìng
---
- **traditional:** 慶
- **definition:** celebrate
- **pinyin:** qìng
- **components:**
  - 广
    - guǎng
    - shelter
  - 大
    - dà
    - big
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 广 (guǎng, 'shelter') + phonetic: 大 (dà, 'big') =
    - grand festivities under shelter ->
    - celebrate
  - **interpretation:** The character 庆(慶) (qìng, 'celebrate') combines a sense of grandness and a place of gathering, suggesting a communal celebration, often under a roof.
  - **simplification:** 广 (guǎng, 'shelter') was retained, while a simplified component 大 (dà, 'big') maintained the phonetic element, focusing on the sense of a grand event.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )们(們)庆(慶)祝( )朋( )友( )的( )生( )日( )。
    - Wǒmen qìngzhù péngyǒu de shēngrì.
    - We celebrate our friend's birthday.
  - 每( )逢( )佳( )节(節)时(時)，家( )家( )户(戶)户(戶)共( )庆(慶)团(團)圆(圓)。
    - Měi féng jiājié shí, jiājiā hùhù gòng qìng tuányuán.
    - Every festive season, every household celebrates reunion.
  - 春( )风(風)拂( )面( )时(時)，大( )地( )复(復)苏(蘇)促( )人( )庆(慶)新( )生( )。
    - Chūnfēng fú miàn shí, dàdì fùsū cù rén qìng xīnshēng.
    - When the spring breeze caresses the face, the revitalized earth prompts people to celebrate new life.
### 庆(慶) → 广(廣)
- **traditional:** 廣
- **definition:** shelter
- **pinyin:** guǎng
- **components:**
  - 宀
    - mián
    - roof
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a house with a roof, similar to 宀 (mián, "roof") ->
    - a building with three walls ->
    - shelter
  - **interpretation:** As a pictogram, 广(廣) (guǎng, "shelter") visually represents a three-walled structure under a roof, conveying the concept of shelter or covering. It is closely related to the character 宀 (mián, "roof"), which includes four walls, indicating the structural nuance captured by ancient scribes.
  - **simplification:** 广(廣) was simplified by using the pictographic element alone, omitting the phonetic component 黃.
- **examples:**
  - 暴( )风(風)骤(驟)雨( )时(時)，山( )洞( )广(廣)可( )供( )人( )躲( )避( )。
    - Bào fēng zhòu yǔ shí, shān dòng guǎng kě gòng rén duǒ bì.
    - When the storm rages, the cave provides shelter.
  - 旅( )途( )中( )疲( )惫(憊)时(時)，老( )树(樹)下( )广(廣)给(給)予( )行( )人( )庇( )护(護)。
    - Lǚ tú zhōng pí bèi shí, lǎo shù xià guǎng jǐ yǔ xíng rén bì hù.
    - During a tiring journey, the shelter under the old tree gives travelers protection.
### 庆(慶) → 广(廣) → 宀
- **definition:** roof
- **pinyin:** mián
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a house with four walls ->
    - roof
  - **interpretation:** 宀(mián, "roof") is a pictogram representing a house with four walls. It emphasizes an enclosed space, distinguishing it from similar structures like 广(guǎng, "broad") which depicts a building with three walls. This makes 宀 a logical choice for representing a roof in various characters.
- **examples:**
  - 含( )有( )宀( )的( )汉(漢)字( )代( )表( )家( )的( )温(溫)暖( )。
    - Hán yǒu mián de hànzì dàibiǎo jiā de wēnnuǎn.
    - Chinese characters that include the radical '宀' represent the warmth of home.
  - 屋( )顶(頂)遮( )风(風)挡(擋)雨( )，家( )园(園)散( )发(發)温(溫)馨( )。
    - Wūdǐng zhē fēng dǎng yǔ, jiāyuán sànfā wēnxīn.
    - The roof shields from wind and rain, and the home radiates warmth.
### 庆(慶) → 大
- **definition:** big
- **pinyin:** dà
- **components:**
  - 夫
    - fū
    - man
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a person facing forward ->
    - Borrowed phonetically for 'big'; original form of 夫 (fū, 'man')
  - **interpretation:** The character 大(dà) depicts a person facing forward, representing 'big' by suggesting the idea of someone standing prominently. Although often thought of as a person with outstretched arms, early scripts do not support this interpretation. It was originally the form of 夫 (fū, 'man') and was later phonetically borrowed for the meaning 'big.'
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)房( )子( )很( )大( )。
    - Zhè ge fángzi hěn dà.
    - This house is very big.
  - 大( )海( )波( )涛(濤)汹(洶)涌(湧)，展( )现(現)出( )大( )自( )然( )的( )力( )量( )。
    - Dàhǎi bōtāo xiōngyǒng, zhǎnxiàn chū dàzìrán de lìliàng.
    - The vast ocean waves surge, showcasing the power of nature.
  - 大( )人( )物( )登( )场(場)，留( )下( )传(傳)奇( )。
    - Dà rénwù dēngchǎng, liúxià chuánqí.
    - Great figures make an appearance, leaving behind legends.
### 庆(慶) → 大 → 夫
- **definition:** man
- **pinyin:** fū
- **components:**
  - 大
    - dà
    - big
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a variant form of 大 (dà, "big") representing an adult man with a hairpin ->
    - became associated with 夫 (fū, "man").
  - **interpretation:** 夫 (fū, "man") was visually similar to 大 (dà, "big") and was originally interchangeable. The addition of the horizontal stroke was interpreted as a hairpin, symbolizing adulthood for men.
- **examples:**
  - 此( )夫( )心( )怀(懷)大( )志( )。
    - Cǐ fū xīn huái dà zhì.
    - This man has lofty aspirations.
  - 他( )勤( )劳(勞)努( )力( )，堪( )称(稱)良( )夫( )。
    - Tā qín láo nǔ lì, kān chēng liáng fū.
    - He works diligently and is regarded as an exemplary man.

## 凉(涼)茶( )
---
## herbal tea
### liángchá
---
- **traditional:** 涼茶
- **definition:** herbal tea
- **pinyin:** liángchá
- **components:**
  - 凉(涼)
    - liáng
    - cool
  - 茶
    - chá
    - tea
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 涼(凉) (liáng, "cool") + 茶 (chá, "tea") =
    - herbal tea
  - **interpretation:** 涼茶 (liángchá, "herbal tea") refers to a type of tea believed to have cooling properties, which helps reduce internal heat according to traditional Chinese medicinal beliefs. The combination makes sense as 涼(凉) (liáng, "cool") implies the cooling effect the tea is supposed to provide.
- **examples:**
  - 夏( )天( )很( )热(熱)，喝( )一( )杯( )凉(涼)茶( )。
    - Xiàtiān hěn rè, hē yī bēi liángchá.
    - It's very hot in summer, so drink a cup of herbal tea.
  - 凉(涼)茶( )解( )渴( )。
    - Liángchá jiě kě.
    - Herbal tea quenches thirst.
  - 忙( )碌( )一( )天( )后(後)，喝( )点(點)凉(涼)茶( )。
    - Mánglù yī tiān hòu, hē diǎn liángchá.
    - After a busy day, have some herbal tea.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 凉(涼)
- **traditional:** 涼
- **definition:** cool
- **pinyin:** liáng
- **components:**
  - 氵(水)
    - shuǐ
    - water
  - 京
    - jīng
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 京 (jīng) =
    - coolness related to water ->
    - 涼
  - **interpretation:** The character 涼 (liáng, "cool") combines the water radical 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") with the phonetic 京 (jīng) to convey the idea of coolness or freshness typically associated with water.
  - **simplification:** 涼 was simplified to 凉(涼) by removing the water radical 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") and retaining the phonetic 京, thus reducing strokes while maintaining phonetic sound.
- **examples:**
  - 夏( )日( )午( )后(後)很( )凉(涼)。
    - Xià rì wǔhòu hěn liáng.
    - The summer afternoon is cool.
  - 微( )风(風)吹( )过(過)，带(帶)来(來)丝(絲)丝(絲)凉(涼)意( )。
    - Wēifēng chuīguò, dài lái sī sī liáng yì.
    - The breeze blows by, bringing a hint of coolness.
  - 夏( )夜( )乘( )凉(涼)，享( )受( )微( )风(風)。
    - Xià yè chéng liáng, xiǎngshòu wēifēng.
    - Taking in the coolness of a summer night while enjoying the gentle breeze.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 凉(涼) → 氵
- **definition:** water
- **pinyin:** shuǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 氵is the radical form of 水 (shuǐ, "water") ->
    - depicts flowing water, compressed and stylized next to characters.
  - **interpretation:** The character 氵is a simplified variant used as a radical to represent water-related meanings in other characters. It retains the flowing water depiction of 水 (shuǐ, "water") but is stylized to fit alongside other components.
- **examples:**
  - 水是生命之源。
    - Shuǐ shì shēngmìng zhī yuán.
    - Water is the source of life.
  - 汉(漢)字( )中( )偏( )旁( )氵( )，代表水。
    - Hànzì zhōng piān páng shuǐ, dàibiǎo shuǐ.
    - In Chinese characters, the radical '氵' signifies water.
  - 河水清澈透明，鱼(魚)儿(兒)欢(歡)快( )戏(戲)水( )。
    - Hé shuǐ qīngchè tòumíng, yú er huānkuaì xì shuǐ.
    - The river water is clear and transparent, and the fish happily play in it.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 凉(涼) → 京
- **pinyin:** jīng
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a tall structure resembling a tower
  - **interpretation:** The character 京(jīng, "tower") visually captures the essence of a tall, tower-like building. It is a straightforward representation using a simple, vertical form to suggest height.
- **examples:**
  - 山( )顶(頂)上( )矗( )立( )着(著)京( )，诉(訴)说(說)着(著)古( )老( )传(傳)说(說)。
    - Shāndǐng shàng chùlì zhe jīng, sùshuō zhe gǔlǎo chuánshuō.
    - The tower stands on the mountaintop, telling ancient legends.
  - 夜( )幕( )降( )临(臨)时(時)京( )闪(閃)烁(爍)着(著)光( )芒( )。
    - Yèmù jiànglín shí jīng shǎnshuò zhe guāngmáng.
    - At nightfall, the tower flickers with light.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶
- **definition:** tea
- **pinyin:** chá
- **components:**
  - 艹
    - cǎo
    - grass
  - 余
    - yú
    - phonetic component
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 艹 (cǎo, "grass") + 余 (yú) =
    - plant component and phonetic ->
    - tea
  - **interpretation:** The character 茶(chá) combines 艹 (cǎo, "grass") implying a plant, with 余 (yú) for its phonetic aspect. Originally derived from a modification of 荼, which meant "bitter plant" in classical texts, 茶(chá) specifically came to signify "tea." This combines the concept of a plant with a sound similar to the word for tea.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )喜( )欢(歡)喝( )茶( )。
    - Wǒ xǐhuān hē chá.
    - I like to drink tea.
  - 早上喝茶，心( )情( )变(變)好( )。
    - Zǎoshang hē chá, xīnqíng biàn hǎo.
    - Drinking tea in the morning improves the mood.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 艹
- **definition:** grass
- **pinyin:** cǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts blades of grass
  - **interpretation:** 艹 is a pictogram representing grass or plants, resembling blades of grass. It is commonly used as a radical in other characters related to grass or vegetation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )写(寫)字( )时(時)用( )艹( )作( )草( )字( )的( )偏( )旁( )。
    - Tā xiězì shí yòng cǎo zuò cǎozì de piānpáng.
    - He uses '艹' as the radical for grass in his characters.
  - 春( )天( )到( )来(來)时(時)，艹( )生( )长(長)得( )很( )快( )。
    - Chūntiān dàolái shí, cǎo shēngzhǎng de hěn kuài.
    - When spring arrives, grass grows rapidly.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 余(餘)
- **traditional:** 餘
- **definition:** phonetic component
- **pinyin:** yú
- **components:**
  - 食
    - shí
    - "eat"
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - phonetic: 余 (yú) + semantic: 食 (shí, "eat") =
    - eating that is remaining ->
    - surplus, remainder
  - **interpretation:** The character 餘 (yú) combines 余 (yú), a phonetic element meaning "I," with 食 (shí, "eat") to convey the concept of a surplus or remainder after eating, which fittingly extends to the idea of something left over.
  - **simplification:** 余 retains the phonetic component without the semantic 食, focusing on simplicity by removing the eating element while keeping the core pronunciation.
- **examples:**
  - 余( )喜( )欢(歡)读(讀)书(書)。
    - Yú xǐhuān dúshū.
    - I enjoy reading.
  - 余( )游(遊)历(歷)名( )山( )，探索古今。
    - Yú yóulì míngshān, tànsuǒ gǔjīn.
    - I travel through famous mountains and explore the ancient and the modern.
  - 余( )静(靜)坐( )思( )过(過)。
    - Yú jìngzuò sīguò.
    - I sit quietly and reflect on my mistakes.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 余(餘) → 食
- **definition:** "eat"
- **pinyin:** shí
- **components:**
  - 亼
    - jí
    - assemble
  - 𠊊
    - jí
    - bowl with stand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Pictogram
  - **description:** 亼 (jí, "assemble") over a pictographic bowl of rice on a stand, symbolized by 𠊊 (jí, "bowl with stand")
  - **interpretation:** Initially, the character 食 (shí, "eat") visually represented a mouth over a bowl of rice, conveying the act of eating or a meal intuitively through this imagery.
- **examples:**
  - 他食得很快。
    - Tā shí dé hěn kuài.
    - He eats very quickly.
  - 我( )准(準)备(備)午( )食( )，与(與)朋( )友( )共( )享( )佳( )肴(餚)。
    - Wǒ zhǔnbèi wǔ shí, yǔ péngyǒu gòngxiǎng jiāyáo.
    - I am preparing lunch to share a delicious meal with my friends.
  - 民( )以( )食( )为(為)天( )。
    - Mín yǐ shí wéi tiān.
    - Food is regarded as the most important element for the people.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 余(餘) → 食 → 亼
- **definition:** assemble
- **pinyin:** jí
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 入 (rù, "enter") + 一 (yī, "one") =
    - gather from three sides
  - **interpretation:** 9.61
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)幅( )画(畫)描( )绘(繪)了( )亼( )的( )独(獨)特( )形( )状(狀)。
    - Zhè fú huà miáohuì le jī de dú tè xíngzhuàng.
    - This painting depicts the unique shape of 亼, resembling an open mouth flipped upside down.
  - 古( )籍( )记(記)载(載)亼( )符( )号(號)的( )古( )怪( )寓( )意( )，令( )后(後)人( )赞(讚)叹(嘆)不( )已( )。
    - Gǔ jí jì zǎi jī fú hào de gǔ guài yù yì, lìng hòu rén zàn tàn bù yǐ.
    - Ancient texts record the bizarre symbolism of 亼, which inspires endless admiration from later generations.
### 凉(涼)茶( ) → 茶 → 余(餘) → 食 → 𠊊
- **definition:** bowl with stand
- **pinyin:** jí
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 亼 (mouth) + 皀 (bowl of rice on a stand) =
    - bowl with stand
  - **interpretation:** 19.07
- **examples:**
  - 我用𠊊来(來)盛( )汤(湯)。
    - Wǒ yòng jí lái chéng tāng.
    - I use the bowl with stand to serve soup.
  - 他买了一个𠊊，来(來)装(裝)热(熱)汤(湯)。
    - Tā mǎi le yīgè jí, lái zhuāng rè tāng.
    - He bought a bowl with stand to hold hot soup.
  - 古人常以𠊊为(為)器( )，供祭祀之用。
    - Gǔrén cháng yǐ jí wéi qì, gòng jìsì zhī yòng.
    - Ancients often used the bowl with stand as an instrument for sacrificial ceremonies.

## 卫(衛)生( )
---
## hygiene
### wèishēng
---
- **traditional:** 衛生
- **definition:** hygiene
- **pinyin:** wèishēng
- **components:**
  - 卫(衛)
    - wèi
    - guard; protect; defend
  - 生
    - shēng
    - life; health; live; raw
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 卫(衛) (wèi, "guard") + 生 (shēng, "life") =
    - guarding life ->
    - hygiene
  - **interpretation:** Hygiene involves practices that guard or protect one's health and life by promoting cleanliness and preventing disease. In this context, 卫(衛) (wèi, "guard") implies protection, while 生 (shēng, "life") refers to health and life itself.
  - **simplification:** 卫生 is the simplified form of 衛生.
- **examples:**
  - 保( )持( )卫(衛)生( )很( )重( )要( )。
    - Bǎochí wèishēng hěn zhòngyào.
    - Maintaining hygiene is important.
  - 在( )餐( )厅(廳)用( )餐( )前( )，请(請)检(檢)查( )卫(衛)生( )。
    - Zài cāntīng yòngcān qián, qǐng jiǎnchá wèishēng.
    - Before dining in the restaurant, please check the hygiene.
  - 环(環)境( )卫(衛)生( )关(關)系(係)到( )健( )康( )。
    - Huánjìng wèishēng guānxì dào jiànkāng.
    - Environmental hygiene is related to health.
### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛)
- **traditional:** 衛
- **definition:** guard; protect; defend
- **pinyin:** wèi
- **components:**
  - 行
    - xíng
    - go
  - 韋
    - wéi
    - tanned leather
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 行 (xíng, 'go') + 韋 (wéi, 'tanned leather') =
    - to guard or defend as if walking to protect ->
    - replacement by visually similar katakana ヱ
  - **interpretation:** The simplified form 卫 replaces the traditional character 衛 by using a visual analogy with the Japanese katakana ヱ, streamlining its components while preserving the original semantic layer of 'guarding' or 'protecting' implied by the notion of walking as protection.
  - **simplification:** 卫 simplifies 衛 through visual analogy with ヱ and cursive simplification, capturing key phonetic elements while omitting or altering strokes for brevity.
- **examples:**
  - 卫(衛)兵( )巡( )逻(邏)边(邊)境( )。
    - Wèi bīng xúnluó biānjìng.
    - The guards patrol the border.
  - 他( )肩( )负(負)卫(衛)国(國)责(責)任( )，誓( )言( )守( )护(護)家( )园(園)。
    - Tā jiānfù wèi guó zérèn, shìyán shǒuhù jiāyuán.
    - He carries the responsibility to defend the nation and vows to protect his homeland.
  - 警( )卫(衛)密( )切( )监(監)察( )入( )口( )。
    - Jǐngwèi mìqiè jiānchá rùkǒu.
    - The guards closely monitor the entrance.
### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛) → 行
- **definition:** go
- **pinyin:** xíng
- **components:**
  - 彳
    - chì
    - step
  - 亍
    - chù
    - step
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Originally a symmetric depiction of a street intersection; simplified asymmetrically ->
    - 彳 (chì, "step") + 亍 (chù, "step")
  - **interpretation:** The character 行(xíng) visually represented a street intersection, with two symmetrical parts symbolizing the act of going or walking along paths. Despite its current breakdown into separate elements, it was originally a unified pictogram rather than a compound.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )行( )往( )学(學)校( )。
    - Wǒ xíng wǎng xuéxiào.
    - I go to school.
  - 他( )行( )于(於)青( )山( )之( )间(間)，徒步旅行至古城。
    - Tā xíng yú qīngshān zhī jiān, túbù lǚxíng zhì gǔchéng.
    - He walks among the green mountains and hikes to the ancient city.
  - 他( )们(們)行( )在( )林( )间(間)小( )路( )上( )。
    - Tāmen xíng zài línjiān xiǎolù shàng.
    - They walk on the forest trail.
### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛) → 行 → 彳
- **definition:** step
- **pinyin:** chì
- **components:**
  - 行
    - xíng
    - go
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts 街 intersection viewed from above, simplified from 行 (xíng, "go")
  - **interpretation:** The character 彳(chì, "walking") represents the image of a street intersection, which symbolically indicates movement or walking. It is used to denote the concept of going or moving along a path.
- **examples:**
  - 他彳亍而行。
    - Tā chìchù ér xíng.
    - He ambled along slowly.
  - 老人彳步，穿( )过(過)小( )巷( )。
    - Lǎorén chì bù, chuānguò xiǎoxiàng.
    - The old man walked along, crossing the narrow alley.
  - 孩( )子( )们(們)看( )到( )蝴( )蝶( )，便( )兴(興)奋(奮)地( )彳( )走( )前( )去( )。
    - Háizimen kàn dào húdié, biàn xīngfèn de chì zǒu qiánqù.
    - Upon seeing the butterflies, the children excitedly walked forward.
### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛) → 行 → 亍
- **definition:** step
- **pinyin:** chù
- **components:**
  - 丿
    - piě
    - slash
  - 一
    - yī
    - one
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 丿 (piě, "slash") + 一 (yī, "one") =
    - forming a path ->
    - step
  - **interpretation:** The character 亍 represents a form of walking or stepping through its simplistic depiction of a path or trail. Consisting of an introductory stroke 丿 (piě, "slash") followed by 一 (yī, "one"), it suggests the concept of a basic step or movement.
- **examples:**
  - 每走一亍，都( )是( )前( )进(進)的( )一( )步( )。
    - Měi zǒu yī chù, dōu shì qiánjìn de yībù.
    - Every step is a step forward.
  - 他( )细(細)细(細)品( )味( )着(著)生( )活( )的( )每( )一( )亍( )，步步向前。
    - Tā xìxì pǐnwèi zhe shēnghuó de měi yī chù, bù bù xiàng qián.
    - He savors every step of life and steadily moves forward.
### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛) → 行 → 亍 → 丿
- **definition:** slash
- **pinyin:** piě
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a diagonal slash
  - **interpretation:** The character 丿 (piě, "slash") is a simple pictogram representing a diagonal line or stroke. It is used as a basic component in many more complex Chinese characters.
- **examples:**
  - 书(書)法( )中( )的( )丿( )极(極)富( )动(動)感( )。
    - Shūfǎ zhōng de piě jí fù dònggǎn.
    - The slash in calligraphy is full of dynamic expression.
  - 他( )写(寫)字( )时(時)用( )丿( )勾( )勒( )出( )灵(靈)动(動)的( )笔(筆)触(觸)，令( )作( )品( )显(顯)得( )生( )动(動)丰(豐)富( )。
    - Tā xiězì shí yòng piě gōulè chū língdòng de bǐchù, lìng zuòpǐn xiǎn de shēngdòng fēngfù.
    - When he writes, he uses the slash stroke to outline dynamic brushwork, making the work appear vivid and rich.
### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛) → 行 → 亍 → 一
- **definition:** one
- **pinyin:** yī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:** A horizontal stroke representing the concept of 'one'.
  - **interpretation:** The character 一(yī, "one") is a simple horizontal line signifying the singular and unified concept of 'one'. It directly represents being singular or the number one.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )有( )一( )个(個)苹(蘋)果( )。
    - Wǒ yǒu yī gè píngguǒ.
    - I have one apple.
  - 他( )只( )看( )到( )一( )只(隻)鸟(鳥)，飞(飛)进(進)了( )花( )园(園)。
    - Tā zhǐ kàn dào yī zhī niǎo, fēi jìn le huā yuán.
    - He only saw one bird, which flew into the garden.
  - 一( )条(條)直( )路( )通( )向( )远(遠)方( )。
    - Yī tiáo zhí lù tōng xiàng yuǎn fāng.
    - One straight road leads to the distance.
### 卫(衛)生( ) → 卫(衛) → 韋
- **definition:** tanned leather
- **pinyin:** wéi
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts leather being twisted or tanned
  - **interpretation:** The character 韋 (wéi, 'soft leather') visually represents a process in leatherworking, such as twisting or tanning, which softens the tough material.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)种(種)韋( )皮( )手( )感( )细(細)腻(膩)，更( )适(適)合( )制(製)作( )高( )档(檔)家( )具( )。
    - Zhè zhǒng wěi pí shǒu gǎn xì nì, gèng shì hé zhì zuò gāo dàng jiā jù.
    - This kind of soft leather has a delicate texture and is more suitable for making high-end furniture.
  - 博( )物( )馆(館)展( )出( )了( )许(許)多( )用( )韋( )制( )成( )的( )文( )物( )，吸( )引( )了( )众(眾)多( )游(遊)客( )。
    - Bówùguǎn zhǎnchūle xǔduō yòng wěi zhìchéng de wénwù, xīyǐnle zhòngduō yóukè.
    - The museum exhibited numerous artifacts made of soft leather, attracting many visitors.
### 卫(衛)生( ) → 生
- **definition:** life; health; live; raw
- **pinyin:** shēng
- **components:**
  - 屮
    - chè
    - sprout
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 屮 (chè, "sprout") on top of a horizontal line ->
    - signifies a plant sprouting from the ground ->
    - symbolizes life
  - **interpretation:** The character 生 (shēng, "life") represents the concept of life force or vitality, symbolized by a sprout emerging from the earth, embodying growth and renewal.
- **examples:**
  - 生( )命( )是( )一( )场(場)美( )丽(麗)的( )旅( )程( )。
    - Shēngmìng shì yī chǎng měilì de lǚchéng.
    - Life is a beautiful journey.
  - 他( )在( )战(戰)场(場)上( )奋(奮)力( )生( )存( )。
    - Tā zài zhànchǎng shàng fènlì shēngcún.
    - He fights to live on the battlefield.
  - 这(這)家( )餐( )厅(廳)的( )生( )鱼(魚)片( )非( )常( )新( )鲜(鮮)。
    - Zhè jiā cāntīng de shēngyúpiàn fēicháng xīnxiān.
    - The raw fish slices at this restaurant are very fresh.
### 卫(衛)生( ) → 生 → 屮
- **definition:** sprout
- **pinyin:** chè
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a sprout emerging from the ground
  - **interpretation:** 屮 (chè, 'sprout') is a pictogram that directly represents a young plant or sprout emerging from the ground. Its visual form captures the essence of new growth, and it historically served as an early representation of plant life or new vegetation.
- **examples:**
  - 春( )雨( )滋( )润(潤)后(後)，小( )屮( )冲(沖)破( )泥( )土( )而( )出( )。
    - chūn yǔ zī rùn hòu, xiǎo chè chōng pò ní tǔ ér chū.
    - After the spring rain nurtured the earth, the little sprout broke through the soil.
  - 草屮初生露尖端。
    - cǎo chè chū shēng lù jiān duān.
    - The grass sprout's tip has just emerged.

## 江( )山( )如( )画(畫)
---
## picturesque scenery
### jiāngshān rúhuà
---
- **traditional:** 江山如畫
- **definition:** picturesque scenery
- **pinyin:** jiāngshān rúhuà
- **components:**
  - 江
    - jiāng
    - river
  - 山
    - shān
    - mountain
  - 如
    - rú
    - like; as if
  - 画(畫)
    - huà
    - painting; drawing
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 江 (jiāng, "river") and 山 (shān, "mountain") refer to natural features, while 如 (rú, "like; as if") and 画(畫) (huà, "painting") compare them to a painting. Together, they create the phrase 江( )山( )如( )畫(画) (jiāngshān rúhuà, "picturesque scenery"), evoking an image of nature so beautifully composed it resembles a work of art.
  - **interpretation:** This expression describes landscapes or scenery that are so magnificent and visually stunning that they seem like a painting. It invokes an appreciation for nature's beauty.
- **examples:**
  - 中( )国(國)江( )山( )如( )画(畫)，游(遊)客( )流( )连(連)忘( )返( )。
    - Zhōngguó jiāngshān rúhuà, yóukè liúlián wàngfǎn.
    - China's picturesque scenery captivates tourists.
  - 祖( )国(國)江( )山( )如( )画(畫)，诗(詩)人( )笔(筆)下( )常( )吟( )咏(詠)。
    - Zǔguó jiāngshān rúhuà, shīrén bǐxià cháng yínyǒng.
    - The motherland's picturesque scenery is often celebrated by poets.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 江
- **definition:** river
- **pinyin:** jiāng
- **components:**
  - 氵
    - shuǐ
    - water
  - 工
    - gōng
    - work
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 氵 (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic: 工 (gōng, from its Old Chinese pronunciation) =
    - water-related meaning reinforced by phonetic component ->
    - river
  - **interpretation:** The character 江 (jiāng, "river") combines the water radical 氵 (shuǐ, "water") with the phonetic component 工 (gōng). This combination signifies a large body of water, fitting the meaning "river."
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)条(條)江( )水( )流( )湍( )急( )。
    - Zhè tiáo jiāng shuǐ liú tuān jí.
    - This river flows rapidly.
  - 江( )面( )上( )漂(飄)浮( )着(著)渔(漁)船( )。
    - Jiāng miàn shàng piāo fú zhe yú chuán.
    - Fishing boats float on the river surface.
  - 夜晚月光映在江上。
    - Yè wǎn yuè guāng yìng zài jiāng shàng.
    - At night, the moonlight reflects on the river.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 江 → 氵
- **definition:** water
- **pinyin:** shuǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 氵is the radical form of 水 (shuǐ, "water") ->
    - depicts flowing water, compressed and stylized next to characters.
  - **interpretation:** The character 氵is a simplified variant used as a radical to represent water-related meanings in other characters. It retains the flowing water depiction of 水 (shuǐ, "water") but is stylized to fit alongside other components.
- **examples:**
  - 水是生命之源。
    - Shuǐ shì shēngmìng zhī yuán.
    - Water is the source of life.
  - 汉(漢)字( )中( )偏( )旁( )氵( )，代表水。
    - Hànzì zhōng piān páng shuǐ, dàibiǎo shuǐ.
    - In Chinese characters, the radical '氵' signifies water.
  - 河水清澈透明，鱼(魚)儿(兒)欢(歡)快( )戏(戲)水( )。
    - Hé shuǐ qīngchè tòumíng, yú er huānkuaì xì shuǐ.
    - The river water is clear and transparent, and the fish happily play in it.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 江 → 工
- **definition:** work
- **pinyin:** gōng
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a bladed tool to plow the ground and to mark a border, or a carpenter's square
  - **interpretation:** 工 (gōng, "work") represents either a tool used in agriculture for marking or a carpenter's square, reflecting its connection to traditional manual labor and craftsmanship.
- **examples:**
  - 他每天下午去做工。
    - Tā měitiān xiàwǔ qù zuò gōng.
    - He goes to work every afternoon.
  - 工( )厂(廠)里(裡)的( )机(機)器( )不( )停( )地( )运(運)转(轉)。
    - Gōng chǎng lǐ de jīqì bù tíng de yùnzhuǎn.
    - The machines in the factory run nonstop.
  - 这(這)位( )工( )人( )技( )艺(藝)精( )湛( )。
    - Zhè wèi gōngrén jìyì jīngzhàn.
    - This worker is highly skilled.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 山
- **definition:** mountain
- **pinyin:** shān
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts three mountain peaks
  - **interpretation:** The character 山(shān, "mountain") visually represents the concept of a mountain using three vertical lines that suggest peaks. This depiction is consistent with early forms found in Oracle Bone Script, emphasizing the notion of natural elevation associated with mountains.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )们(們)去( )爬( )山( )。
    - Wǒmen qù pá shān.
    - We go climb the mountain.
  - 那( )座( )大( )山( )让(讓)人( )惊(驚)叹(嘆)。
    - Nà zuò dà shān ràng rén jīngtàn.
    - That mountain astonishes people.
  - 远(遠)处(處)的( )山( )隐(隱)约(約)在( )云(雲)中( )，令( )人( )心( )旷(曠)神( )怡( )。
    - Yuǎnchù de shān yǐnyuē zài yún zhōng, lìng rén xīnkuàng shényí.
    - The distant mountain fades into the clouds, leaving one in a state of peace.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 如
- **definition:** like; as if
- **pinyin:** rú
- **components:**
  - 女
    - nǚ
    - woman
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 女 (nǚ, "woman") + 口 (kǒu, "mouth") =
    - semantic element (woman talking) + phonetic element (sound similar to "rú") ->
    - like; as if
  - **interpretation:** The character 如 (rú, "like; as if") combines 女 (nǚ, "woman") with 口 (kǒu, "mouth"). It suggests the idea of something resembling or being like another, possibly through speech or assertion.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )跑( )得( )如( )风(風)一( )般( )快( )。
    - Tā pǎo de rú fēng yībān kuài.
    - He runs as fast as the wind.
  - 她( )说(說)话(話)如( )诗(詩)般( )动(動)听(聽)，举(舉)止( )如( )画(畫)般( )优(優)雅( )。
    - Tā shuōhuà rú shī bān dòngtīng, jǔzhǐ rú huà bān yōuyǎ.
    - She speaks as beautifully as poetry, and her demeanor is as elegant as a painting.
  - 生( )活( )如( )旅( )程( )般( )充( )满(滿)未( )知( )，梦(夢)想( )如( )星( )空( )般( )璀( )璨( )。
    - Shēnghuó rú lǚchéng bān chōngmǎn wèizhī, mèngxiǎng rú xīngkōng bān cuǐcàn.
    - Life is as full of uncertainties as a journey, and dreams shine as brilliantly as the starry sky.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 如 → 女
- **definition:** woman
- **pinyin:** nǚ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a woman kneeling or standing ->
    - enclosed area as remnant of right breast
  - **interpretation:** The character 女(nǚ, "woman") is a pictogram originally depicting a woman with highlighted bodily features, such as the breasts, symbolizing femininity. Its orientation changed from the Oracle Bone script to face right, simplifying over time while still retaining the essence of its original depiction.
- **examples:**
  - 那( )位( )才( )华(華)横(橫)溢( )的( )女( )，赢(贏)得( )了( )全( )场(場)注( )目( )。
    - Nà wèi cáihuá héngyì de nǚ, yíng dé le quánchǎng zhùmù.
    - That talented woman won everyone's attention.
  - 古时候‘女’常被用来象征温柔(古時候‘女’常被用來象徵溫柔)，而( )如( )今( )它( )代( )表( )独(獨)立( )自( )信( )。
    - Gǔshíhòu ‘nǚ’ cháng bèi yòng lái xiàngzhēng wēnróu, ér rújīn tā dàibiǎo dúlì zìxìn.
    - In ancient times, the character '女' was often used to symbolize gentleness, whereas today it represents independence and confidence.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 如 → 口
- **definition:** mouth
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 画(畫)
- **traditional:** 畫
- **definition:** painting; drawing
- **pinyin:** huà
- **components:**
  - 聿
    - yù
    - brush
  - 規
    - guī
    - compass
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:** Phono-semantic compound: semantic 聿 (yù, "brush") + phonetic 規 (guī, "compass") added later, originally 畵 without 𦘒
  - **interpretation:** The character 畫 originally depicted the idea of making lines or designs using a brush, reflecting the semantic 聿 (yù, "brush") aspect. The additional component 規 (guī, "compass") serves as a phonetic element and reinforces the notion of creating bounded or regulated art forms.
  - **simplification:** The simplified character 画 omits 𦘒 from the original 畵, retaining the central semantic of brushwork while streamlining the complexity.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )正( )在( )画(畫)风(風)景( )。
    - Tā zhèngzài huà fēngjǐng。
    - He is painting a landscape.
  - 小( )明( )喜( )欢(歡)画(畫)动(動)物( )，他( )的( )画(畫)充( )满(滿)生( )命( )力( )。
    - Xiǎomíng xǐhuān huà dòngwù, tā de huà chōngmǎn shēngmìnglì。
    - Xiaoming enjoys painting animals, and his paintings are full of vitality.
  - 博( )物( )馆(館)里(裡)有( )几(幾)幅( )名( )家( )画(畫)。
    - Bówùguǎn lǐ yǒu jǐ fú míngjiā huà。
    - There are several famous paintings in the museum.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 画(畫) → 聿
- **definition:** brush
- **pinyin:** yù
- **components:**
  - 手
    - shǒu
    - hand
  - 𰀁
    - yù
    - brush
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 手 (shǒu, 'hand') stylized as 又 (yòu, 'hand') + 𰀁 (yù, 'brush') =
    - hand holding a brush
  - **interpretation:** The character 聿(yù) originally depicted a hand holding a brush, symbolizing the act of writing or drawing. It was initially used for the writing brush itself and later borrowed for other meanings.
- **examples:**
  - 古( )代( )文( )人( )以( )聿( )书(書)写(寫)心( )志( )。
    - Gǔdài wénrén yǐ yù shūxiě xīnzhì.
    - Ancient literati used the brush to write their aspirations.
  - 书(書)法( )之( )美( )在( )于(於)用( )聿( )，笔(筆)随(隨)心( )动(動)。
    - Shūfǎ zhī měi zàiyú yòng yù, bǐ suí xīndòng.
    - The beauty of calligraphy lies in using a brush, as the brush follows the movements of the heart.
  - 他( )用( )聿( )写(寫)出( )了( )一( )篇( )优(優)美( )的( )诗(詩)。
    - Tā yòng yù xiě chūle yī piān yōuměi de shī.
    - He used the brush to compose a beautiful poem.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 画(畫) → 聿 → 手
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a hand with fingers; top stroke is a bent over middle finger, horizontal strokes represent fingers
  - **interpretation:** The character 手 (shǒu, "hand") is a direct pictorial representation of a human hand with all five fingers, which distinguishes it from similar characters representing claws or simplified fingers. This visually conveys its meaning as a hand clearly.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )手( )写(寫)字( )。
    - Tā yòng shǒu xiě zì.
    - He writes with his hand.
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)牵(牽)着(著)我( )的( )手( )走( )路( )。
    - Māmā qiān zhe wǒ de shǒu zǒu lù.
    - Mom holds my hand while walking.
  - 他( )的( )手( )很( )温(溫)暖( )，带(帶)给(給)我( )力( )量( )。
    - Tā de shǒu hěn wēn nuǎn, dài gěi wǒ lìliàng.
    - His hand is very warm, giving me strength.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 画(畫) → 聿 → 𰀁(聿)
- **traditional:** 聿
- **definition:** brush
- **pinyin:** yù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a hand holding a brush or writing implement
  - **interpretation:** The character 聿 (yù, "brush") visually represents a hand grasping a writing tool, which is suggestive of its association with writing or drawing actions.
- **examples:**
  - 书(書)法( )家( )拿( )起( )𰀁( )，挥(揮)洒(灑)时(時)代( )风(風)采( )。
    - Shūfǎjiā ná qǐ yù, huīsǎ shídài fēngcǎi.
    - The calligrapher picked up the brush and displayed the spirit of the times.
  - 他用𰀁蘸上墨汁，练(練)习(習)工( )整( )字( )迹(跡)。
    - Tā yòng yù zhàn shàng mòzhī, liànxí gōngzhěng zìjì.
    - He dipped the brush into ink and practiced writing with neat strokes.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 画(畫) → 規
- **definition:** compass
- **pinyin:** guī
- **components:**
  - 見
    - jiàn
    - see
  - 瞿
    - qú
    - phonetic
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 見 (jiàn, "see") + phonetic: 瞿 (qú) =
    - tool for seeing precise lines ->
    - compass
  - **interpretation:** The character 規 (guī, "compass") combines the idea of seeing with precision, represented by 見 (jiàn, "see"), with the phonetic component 瞿 (qú) to form a character representing a tool for creating precise lines, such as a compass.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)規( )指( )引( )着(著)航( )海( )者( )找( )到( )方( )向( )。
    - Zhège guī zhǐyǐn zhe hánghǎi zhě zhǎodào fāngxiàng.
    - This compass guides the seafarers to find their direction.
  - 迷路的水手依靠規，穿( )越( )惊(驚)涛(濤)骇(駭)浪( )。
    - Mílù de shuǐshǒu yīkào guī, chuānyuè jīngtāo hàilàng.
    - The lost sailor relies on the compass, braving the tumultuous waves.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 画(畫) → 規 → 見
- **definition:** see
- **pinyin:** jiàn
- **components:**
  - 目
    - mù
    - eye
  - 卩
    - jié
    - kneeling person
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 目 (mù, "eye") + 卩 (jié, "kneeling person") =
    - a kneeling figure with large eye ->
    - see
  - **interpretation:** The combination of a human figure and an eye visually represents the act of seeing. The eye symbolizes vision, while the person emphasizes the human context of this action, making the meaning intuitive.
  - **simplification:** 见(見) is derived from using a cursive variant of the character in place of the traditional form.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )昨( )天( )见(見)到( )她( )。
    - Wǒ zuótiān jiàn dào tā.
    - I saw her yesterday.
  - 太( )阳(陽)升( )起( )时(時)，我( )看( )见(見)了( )大( )海( )。
    - Tàiyáng shēng qǐ shí, wǒ kàn jiàn le dàhǎi.
    - When the sun rose, I saw the sea.
  - 在( )公( )园(園)里(裡)，我( )见(見)到( )了( )老( )朋( )友( )。
    - Zài gōngyuán lǐ, wǒ jiàn dào le lǎo péngyǒu.
    - In the park, I met an old friend.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 画(畫) → 規 → 見 → 目
- **definition:** eye
- **pinyin:** mù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a human eye, now represented and rotated 90 degrees ->
    - eye
  - **interpretation:** The character 目(mù, 'eye') is a pictogram representing a human eye. Its original orientation has changed over time, resembling an eye seen from a sideways view.
- **examples:**
  - 他的目光炯炯有神。
    - Tā de mùguāng jiǒngjiǒng yǒu shén.
    - His gaze is bright and full of spirit.
  - 她( )目( )不( )转(轉)睛( )，注( )视(視)前( )方( )。
    - Tā mù bù zhuǎn jīng, zhùshì qiánfāng.
    - She stared intently, gazing ahead.
  - 雄( )鹰(鷹)展( )翅( )，双(雙)目( )炯( )炯( )看( )天( )际(際)。
    - Xióng yīng zhǎn chì, shuāng mù jiǒngjiǒng kàn tiānjì.
    - The eagle spreads its wings, its eyes piercingly gaze at the horizon.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 画(畫) → 規 → 見 → 卩
- **definition:** kneeling person
- **pinyin:** jié
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a kneeling person from sideview
  - **interpretation:** The character 卩 (jié, "kneeling person") is a simple pictogram showing the silhouette of a person kneeling. It is unrelated to other kneeling or similar figures in different contexts such as 欠 (qiàn, "yawn") where the mouth is open, or 女 (nǚ, "woman") where the figure represents a female. Its form is solely based on the posture of kneeling.
- **examples:**
  - 那( )位( )卩( )正( )虔( )诚(誠)祈( )祷(禱)。
    - Nà wèi jié zhèng qiánchéng qídǎo.
    - That kneeling person is praying devoutly.
  - 礼(禮)仪(儀)师(師)指( )出( )，卩( )代( )表( )谦(謙)卑( )与(與)敬( )意( )。
    - Lǐyí shī zhǐchū, jié dàibiǎo qiānbēi yǔ jìngyì.
    - The ritual master pointed out that the kneeling person represents humility and respect.
  - 在( )庄(莊)严(嚴)仪(儀)式( )中( )，卩( )象( )征(徵)着(著)内(內)心( )的( )虔( )敬( )。
    - Zài zhuāngyán yíshì zhōng, jié xiàngzhēngzhe nèixīn de qiánjìng.
    - In the solemn ceremony, the kneeling figure symbolizes inner devotion.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 画(畫) → 規 → 瞿
- **definition:** phonetic
- **pinyin:** qú
- **components:**
  - 目
    - mù
    - eye
  - 取
    - qǔ
    - take
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Semantic: 目 (mù, "eye") + Phonetic: 取 (qǔ, "take") =
    - related to vision or seeing ->
    - final sound and meaning
  - **interpretation:** 瞿 (qú) combines 目 (mù, "eye") for the semantic element related to vision, with 取 (qǔ) to hint at the pronunciation. Despite its eye-related component, the specific applications may vary.
- **examples:**
  - 瞿( )是( )一( )个(個)不( )常( )见(見)的( )姓( )。
    - Qú shì yī gè bù chángjiàn de xìng。
    - Qú is an uncommon surname.
  - 很( )多( )古( )籍( )论(論)及( )家( )族( )姓( )氏( )，其中'瞿'因其发音独特而受到关注(其中『瞿』因其發音獨特而受到關注)。
    - Hěn duō gǔjí lùn jí jiāzú xìngshì，qízhōng 'qú' yīn qí fāyīn dútè ér shòudào guānzhù。
    - Many ancient texts discuss family surnames, among which 'Qú' has attracted attention for its unique pronunciation.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 画(畫) → 規 → 瞿 → 取
- **definition:** take
- **pinyin:** qǔ
- **components:**
  - 耳
    - ěr
    - ear
  - 又
    - yòu
    - hand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 耳 (ěr, "ear") + 又 (yòu, "hand") =
    - taking the ear of prey (hunter's rite) ->
    - "take"
  - **interpretation:** 取 (qǔ, "take") combines 耳 (ěr, "ear") and 又 (yòu, "hand") to depict the act of taking the ear of a fallen prey, a ritual from the Rites of Zhou. This reflects the character's association with taking or acquiring something.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )从(從)箱( )子( )里(裏)取( )出( )文( )件( )。
    - Tā cóng xiāngzi lǐ qǔchū wénjiàn.
    - He took out documents from the box.
  - 请(請)取( )钱(錢)包( )，并(並)支( )付( )费(費)用( )。
    - Qǐng qǔ qiánbāo, bìng zhīfù fèiyòng.
    - Please take the wallet and pay the fee.
  - 他( )决(決)定( )取( )胜(勝)于(於)对(對)手( )。
    - Tā juédìng qǔshèng yú duìshǒu.
    - He decided to prevail over his opponent.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 画(畫) → 規 → 瞿 → 取 → 耳
- **definition:** ear
- **pinyin:** ěr
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an ear
  - **interpretation:** The character 耳 (ěr, "ear") is a pictogram that visually represents an ear with its distinctive shape. This straightforward representation made it intuitive for ancient speakers to associate the character directly with the concept of an ear.
- **examples:**
  - 音( )乐(樂)直( )入( )耳( )。
    - Yīn yuè zhí rù ěr.
    - The music goes straight to the ear.
  - 他( )亲(親)耳( )听(聽)到( )谣(謠)言( )，心( )中( )充( )满(滿)疑( )虑(慮)。
    - Tā qīn ěr tīng dào yáo yán, xīn zhōng chōng mǎn yí lǜ.
    - He heard the rumor with his own ears, and his heart was filled with doubt.
### 江( )山( )如( )画(畫) → 画(畫) → 規 → 瞿 → 取 → 又
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** yòu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a right hand
  - **interpretation:** The character 又 (yòu, "hand") is a pictogram representing a right hand, showing the fingers and palm, which highlights its original meaning related to manual actions.
- **examples:**
  - 「又」这个字最早来源于手(「又」這個字最早來源於手)。
    - 「Yòu」 zhè gè zì zuì zǎo láiyuán yú shǒu。
    - The character '又' originally comes from the hand.
  - 很( )多( )汉(漢)字( )含( )有( )又( )部( )首( )，表( )示( )与(與)手( )有( )关(關)的( )动(動)作( )。
    - Hěn duō hànzì hányǒu Yòu bùshǒu, biǎoshì yǔ shǒu yǒuguān de dòngzuò。
    - Many Chinese characters include the '又' radical, indicating actions related to the hand.
  - 古( )人( )用( )又( )描( )绘(繪)手( )的( )形( )态(態)，体(體)现(現)了( )古( )汉(漢)字( )的( )独(獨)特( )魅( )力( )。
    - Gǔrén yòng Yòu miáohuì shǒu de xíngtài, tǐxiàn le gǔ hànzì de dútè mèilì。
    - Ancient people used '又' to depict the shape of the hand, showcasing the unique charm of ancient Chinese characters.
