## 运(運)动(動)
---
## exercise; sports
### yùndòng
---
- **traditional:** 運動
- **definition:** exercise; sports
- **pinyin:** yùndòng
- **components:**
  - 运(運)
    - yùn
    - transport; move; fortune; luck
  - 动(動)
    - dòng
    - move; act; stir; change
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 運 (yùn, "transport; move") and 動 (dòng, "move; act") together convey the concept of movement or activity. The combination suggests engaging in physical activity or movement, which can be interpreted as exercise or sports.
  - **interpretation:** The word 运(運)动(動) (yùndòng) refers to engaging in physical activities or sports, emphasizing the idea of movement and action.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )每( )天( )运(運)动(動)。
    - Wǒ měitiān yùndòng.
    - I exercise every day.
  - 运(運)动(動)使( )身( )体(體)更( )强(強)壮(壯)，也( )带(帶)来(來)快( )乐(樂)。
    - Yùndòng shǐ shēntǐ gèng qiángzhuàng, yě dàilái kuàilè.
    - Exercise makes the body stronger and brings joy.
  - 参(參)加( )运(運)动(動)会(會)可( )以( )结(結)识(識)朋( )友( )，体(體)验(驗)团(團)队(隊)精( )神( )。
    - Cānjiā yùndònghuì kěyǐ jiéshí péngyǒu, tǐyàn tuánduì jīngshén.
    - Participating in a sports meet can help you make friends and experience team spirit.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運)
- **traditional:** 運
- **definition:** transport; move; fortune; luck
- **pinyin:** yùn
- **components:**
  - 辵(辶)
    - chuò
    - walk
  - 云(雲)
    - yún
    - cloud
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 辵(辶) (chuò, "walk") + phonetic: 云(雲) (yún, "cloud") =
    - movement (walk) + phonetic hint (cloud) ->
    - transport and movement
  - **interpretation:** The character uses 辵(辶) (chuò, "walk") to signify movement and 云(雲) (yún, "cloud") as a phonetic element. This combination suggests the idea of moving or transporting, as well as conveying abstract ideas like fortune or luck.
  - **simplification:** 运(運) replaces 雲 with 云 (both pronounced as yún) to create a more cursive representation of the phonetic component. This change simplifies writing while maintaining phonetic value.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )的( )运(運)气(氣)很( )好( )。
    - Tā de yùnqì hěn hǎo.
    - His fortune is very good.
  - 这(這)辆(輛)卡( )车(車)运(運)送( )货(貨)物( )，经(經)过(過)长(長)长(長)的( )公( )路( )。
    - Zhè liàng kǎchē yùnsòng huòwù, jīngguò chángcháng de gōnglù.
    - This truck transports goods, traveling along a long highway.
  - 成功需要努力，也( )少( )不( )了( )运(運)气(氣)。
    - Chénggōng xūyào nǔlì, yě shǎo bùliǎo yùnqì.
    - Success requires hard work, but luck is also indispensable.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵
- **definition:** walk
- **pinyin:** chuò
- **components:**
  - 彳
    - chì
    - walk
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - foot
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 彳 (chì, "walk") + 止 (zhǐ, "foot" or "to stop") ->
    - 𣥆 ->
    - stylized as 龰 ->
    - 辵
  - **interpretation:** The character 辵 combines 彳 (chì, "walk"), which represents movement, with 止 (zhǐ, "foot" or "to stop"), suggesting the concept of stepping or traveling forward. This character represents the idea of movement, particularly the action of walking or traveling, as originally depicted by the more conservative form 𣥆. Over time, the bottom component has been stylized as 龰, forming the current representation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )辵( )在( )乡(鄉)间(間)的( )小( )路( )上( )。
    - Tā chuò zài xiāngjiān de xiǎolù shàng.
    - He walks on the rural path.
  - 晨( )光( )洒(灑)满(滿)大( )地( )，他( )辵( )出( )门(門)开(開)始( )旅( )程( )。
    - Chén guāng sǎ mǎn dàdì, tā chuò chūmén kāishǐ lǚchéng.
    - As the morning light fills the earth, he sets out to begin his journey.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 彳
- **definition:** walk
- **pinyin:** chì
- **components:**
  - 行
    - xíng
    - go
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts 街 intersection viewed from above, simplified from 行 (xíng, "go")
  - **interpretation:** The character 彳(chì, "walking") represents the image of a street intersection, which symbolically indicates movement or walking. It is used to denote the concept of going or moving along a path.
- **examples:**
  - 他彳亍而行。
    - Tā chìchù ér xíng.
    - He ambled along slowly.
  - 老人彳步，穿( )过(過)小( )巷( )。
    - Lǎorén chì bù, chuānguò xiǎoxiàng.
    - The old man walked along, crossing the narrow alley.
  - 孩( )子( )们(們)看( )到( )蝴( )蝶( )，便( )兴(興)奋(奮)地( )彳( )走( )前( )去( )。
    - Háizimen kàn dào húdié, biàn xīngfèn de chì zǒu qiánqù.
    - Upon seeing the butterflies, the children excitedly walked forward.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 彳 → 行
- **definition:** go
- **pinyin:** xíng
- **components:**
  - 彳
    - chì
    - step
  - 亍
    - chù
    - step
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Originally a symmetric depiction of a street intersection; simplified asymmetrically ->
    - 彳 (chì, "step") + 亍 (chù, "step")
  - **interpretation:** The character 行(xíng) visually represented a street intersection, with two symmetrical parts symbolizing the act of going or walking along paths. Despite its current breakdown into separate elements, it was originally a unified pictogram rather than a compound.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )行( )往( )学(學)校( )。
    - Wǒ xíng wǎng xuéxiào.
    - I go to school.
  - 他( )行( )于(於)青( )山( )之( )间(間)，徒步旅行至古城。
    - Tā xíng yú qīngshān zhī jiān, túbù lǚxíng zhì gǔchéng.
    - He walks among the green mountains and hikes to the ancient city.
  - 他( )们(們)行( )在( )林( )间(間)小( )路( )上( )。
    - Tāmen xíng zài línjiān xiǎolù shàng.
    - They walk on the forest trail.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 彳 → 行 → 亍
- **definition:** step
- **pinyin:** chù
- **components:**
  - 丿
    - piě
    - slash
  - 一
    - yī
    - one
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 丿 (piě, "slash") + 一 (yī, "one") =
    - forming a path ->
    - step
  - **interpretation:** The character 亍 represents a form of walking or stepping through its simplistic depiction of a path or trail. Consisting of an introductory stroke 丿 (piě, "slash") followed by 一 (yī, "one"), it suggests the concept of a basic step or movement.
- **examples:**
  - 每走一亍，都( )是( )前( )进(進)的( )一( )步( )。
    - Měi zǒu yī chù, dōu shì qiánjìn de yībù.
    - Every step is a step forward.
  - 他( )细(細)细(細)品( )味( )着(著)生( )活( )的( )每( )一( )亍( )，步步向前。
    - Tā xìxì pǐnwèi zhe shēnghuó de měi yī chù, bù bù xiàng qián.
    - He savors every step of life and steadily moves forward.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 止
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zhǐ
- **components:**
  - 趾
    - zhǐ
    - foot
  - 龰
    - yù
    - stylized feet
  - 手
    - shǒu
    - hand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Original: Foot pointing up with 3 toes and a sole ->
    - Stylized as 龰 ->
    - Derivative 趾 refers to original 'foot' meaning
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicting a footprint with toes and a sole, 止 (zhǐ, "foot") represents the foot. The character's form resembles a foot with three toes, analogous to how the hand is represented with fingers in other characters.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)的( )止( )需( )要( )静(靜)养(養)。
    - Tā shòu shāng de zhǐ xū yào jìng yǎng.
    - His injured foot needs adequate rest.
  - 他( )走( )路( )时(時)小( )心( )谨(謹)慎( )，因( )为(爲)止( )若( )受( )伤(傷)便( )难(難)前( )行( )。
    - Tā zǒu lù shí xiǎo xīn jǐn shèn, yīn wèi zhǐ ruò shòu shāng biàn nán qián xíng.
    - He walks carefully because if his foot gets injured, it becomes difficult to move forward.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 止 → 趾
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zhǐ
- **components:**
  - 足
    - zú
    - foot
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - stop
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 足 (zú, "foot") + 止 (zhǐ, "stop") =
    - semantic: foot + phonetic: stop ->
    - toe
  - **interpretation:** The character combines 足 (zú, "foot") as the semantic indicator and 止 (zhǐ, "stop") as the phonetic component to represent the idea of a 'toe,' which is a part of the foot.
- **examples:**
  - 他的趾因寒冷而僵硬了。
    - tā de zhǐ yīn hánlěng ér jiāngyìng le.
    - His toe became stiff due to the cold.
  - 我( )走( )路( )时(時)，不小心扭到了趾。
    - wǒ zǒulù shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ dào le zhǐ.
    - While I was walking, I accidentally twisted my toe.
  - 古( )代( )剑(劍)客( )战(戰)斗(鬥)时(時)，常( )因( )疏( )忽( )伤(傷)及( )趾( )。
    - gǔdài jiànkè zhàndòu shí, cháng yīn shūhū shāng jí zhǐ.
    - Ancient swordsmen often injured their toes due to carelessness during battles.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 止 → 趾 → 足
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zú
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a leg ->
    - originally depicted as ⽦ ->
    - represents 'foot'
  - **interpretation:** The character 足(zú, 'foot') is a pictographic representation of a human leg, which directly portrays the concept of a 'foot'. The pictographic origin emphasizes its fundamental meaning in the language.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)后(後)，他( )的( )足( )不( )能( )动(動)。
    - tā shòushāng hòu, tā de zú bù néng dòng。
    - After he was injured, his foot couldn't move.
  - 小( )明( )打( )球( )时(時)，不( )小( )心( )扭( )伤(傷)了( )足( )。
    - xiǎo míng dǎ qiú shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ shāng le zú。
    - When Xiaoming was playing ball, he accidentally twisted his foot.
  - 我( )每( )天( )锻(鍛)炼(鍊)足( )部( )。
    - wǒ měitiān duànliàn zúbù。
    - I exercise my foot muscles every day.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 止 → 龰
- **definition:** stylized feet
- **pinyin:** yù
- **components:**
  - 趾
    - zhǐ
    - toe
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a footprint pointing up, originally representing 'foot' ->
    - stylized as 龰 ->
    - derived to 趾 (zhǐ, "toe")
  - **interpretation:** The character 龰 was originally a pictogram representing a footprint pointing upwards, illustrating the base concept of 'foot'. This visualization evolved into the specific derivative 趾 (zhǐ, "toe"), while 龰 retained the idea of stylized feet.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 辵 → 止 → 手
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a hand with fingers; top stroke is a bent over middle finger, horizontal strokes represent fingers
  - **interpretation:** The character 手 (shǒu, "hand") is a direct pictorial representation of a human hand with all five fingers, which distinguishes it from similar characters representing claws or simplified fingers. This visually conveys its meaning as a hand clearly.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )手( )写(寫)字( )。
    - Tā yòng shǒu xiě zì.
    - He writes with his hand.
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)牵(牽)着(著)我( )的( )手( )走( )路( )。
    - Māmā qiān zhe wǒ de shǒu zǒu lù.
    - Mom holds my hand while walking.
  - 他( )的( )手( )很( )温(溫)暖( )，带(帶)给(給)我( )力( )量( )。
    - Tā de shǒu hěn wēn nuǎn, dài gěi wǒ lìliàng.
    - His hand is very warm, giving me strength.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 云(雲)
- **traditional:** 雲
- **definition:** cloud
- **pinyin:** yún
- **components:**
  - 二
    - èr
    - two
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 云 (yún, "say") + phonetic: 二 (èr) =
    - cloud association; originally depicted the form of clouds
  - **interpretation:** The character 云 (yún, "cloud") combines 云 (yún, 'say') as a semantic element, originally meaning 'to say' or 'to speak,' but is used phonetically to represent a similar-sounding word for 'cloud.' The character was simplified from 雲 to 云, retaining its phonetic and semantic components.
  - **simplification:** The traditional 雲 was simplified by removing the radical 厶 (sī, "private") and merging 冖 (mì, "cover") with 二 (èr, "two") for a simpler form 云.
- **examples:**
  - 天( )空( )中( )飘(飄)着(著)洁(潔)白( )的( )云(雲)。
    - Tiānkōng zhōng piāo zhe jiébái de yún.
    - Beautiful white clouds are floating in the sky.
  - 乌(烏)云(雲)密( )布(佈)时(時)，可( )能( )马(馬)上( )下( )雨( )。
    - Wūyún mìbù shí, kěnéng mǎshàng xiàyǔ.
    - When dark clouds gather, it might rain soon.
### 运(運)动(動) → 运(運) → 云(雲) → 二
- **definition:** two
- **pinyin:** èr
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts two parallel strokes ->
    - represents the number two
  - **interpretation:** The character 二 (èr, "two") consists of two horizontal lines that visually represent the concept of 'two'. This method of using parallel strokes to denote numbers is a straightforward representation of quantity, similar to how 一 (yī, "one") represents 'one' and 三 (sān, "three") represents 'three'.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )有( )二( )支( )笔(筆)。
    - Wǒ yǒu èr zhī bǐ.
    - I have two pens.
  - 他( )买(買)了( )二( )个(個)苹(蘋)果( )，我( )们(們)一( )起( )分( )享( )。
    - Tā mǎi le èr gè píngguǒ, wǒmen yīqǐ fēnxiǎng.
    - He bought two apples, and we shared them together.
### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動)
- **traditional:** 動
- **definition:** move; act; stir; change
- **pinyin:** dòng
- **components:**
  - 力
    - lì
    - strength
  - 重
    - zhòng
    - heavy
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 力 (lì, "strength") + 重 (zhòng, "heavy") =
    - effort applied to shift weight ->
    - move
  - **interpretation:** 力 (lì, "strength") indicates action or exertion of force, while 重 (zhòng, "heavy") serves as a phonetic component capturing the character's pronunciation. Together, they convey the idea of using strength to move or change something.
  - **simplification:** 动(動): Removed 艹 from 重, leaving just another simpler form to represent the sound and meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )动(動)了( )动(動)手( )指( )，同( )意( )了( )建( )议(議)。
    - Tā dòngle dòng shǒuzhǐ, tóngyìle jiànyì.
    - He moved his finger and agreed to the suggestion.
  - 心( )动(動)不( )已( )。
    - Xīn dòng bù yǐ.
    - His heart is constantly stirred.
  - 局( )势(勢)频(頻)繁( )动(動)荡(蕩)，民( )众(眾)渴( )望( )安( )定( )。
    - Jú shì pínfán dòng dàng, mínzhòng kěwàng āndìng.
    - The situation is frequently changing, and the people long for stability.
### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動) → 力
- **definition:** strength
- **pinyin:** lì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts either an arm bending out from the side of the body, or a wooden plough
  - **interpretation:** The character 力(lì, 'strength') visually represents physical power through the image of an arm or tool, emphasizing the fundamental concept of muscular force.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )尽(盡)全( )力( )，赢(贏)得( )比( )赛(賽)。
    - Tā yòng jìn quánlì, yíng dé bǐsài.
    - He used all his strength to win the competition.
  - 他( )把( )力( )气(氣)用( )在( )学(學)习(習)上( )。
    - Tā bǎ lìqì yòng zài xuéxí shàng.
    - He applies his strength to his studies.
  - 面( )对(對)困( )难(難)，他( )毫( )不( )退( )缩(縮)，全力以赴。
    - Miànduì kùnnán, tā háo bù tuìsuō, quánlì yǐ fù.
    - Facing difficulties, he neither retreats nor gives up, but goes all out.
### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動) → 重
- **definition:** heavy
- **pinyin:** zhòng
- **components:**
  - 人
    - rén
    - man
  - 東
    - dōng
    - bag
  - 土
    - tǔ
    - earth
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Phono-semantic compound ( 形聲 / 形声 , OC *doŋ, *doŋʔ, *doŋs ) and ideogrammic compound ( 會意 / 会意 )
  - **description:**
    - 人 (rén, "man") + 東 (dōng, "bag") =
    - a man carrying a bag ->
    - 土 (tǔ, "earth") added to show the bag-carrying man is on the ground ->
    - current form shows heaviness
  - **interpretation:** This character combines the meaning of a person carrying a burden or bag, emphasizing the weight (heavy) of the load, with the semantic reinforcement of standing on the ground. This visually conveys the idea of heaviness.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)箱( )子( )很( )重( )。
    - Zhège xiāngzi hěn zhòng.
    - This box is very heavy.
  - 我( )挑( )水( )井( )水( )时(時)，感( )觉(覺)瓶( )子( )异(異)常( )重( )。
    - Wǒ tiāo shuǐjǐng shuǐ shí, gǎnjué píngzi yìcháng zhòng.
    - While I was drawing water from the well, I felt that the bottle was unusually heavy.
### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動) → 重 → 人
- **definition:** man
- **pinyin:** rén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts side view of a standing man, highlighting an arm and a leg ->
    - visual representation of a person
  - **interpretation:** The character 人 (rén, 'person') effectively uses simple lines to depict the essential form of a human, making it immediately recognizable and intuitive as meaning 'person.'
- **examples:**
  - 他( )是( )个(個)好( )人( )。
    - Tā shì gè hǎo rén.
    - He is a good person.
  - 每( )个(個)人( )都( )有( )责(責)任( )，为(為)社( )会(會)作( )出( )贡(貢)献(獻)。
    - Měi gè rén dōu yǒu zérèn, wèi shèhuì zuòchū gòngxiàn.
    - Every person has a responsibility to contribute to society.
  - 人在旅途中，心( )灵(靈)获(獲)得( )了( )成( )长(長)。
    - Rén zài lǚtú zhōng, xīnlíng huòdé le chéngzhǎng.
    - People gain growth along their journey.
### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動) → 重 → 東
- **definition:** bag
- **pinyin:** dōng
- **components:**
  - 束
    - shù
    - bundle
  - 橐
    - tuó
    - bag
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Jiajie and ideogram
  - **description:**
    - Originally borrowed from 束 (shù, 'bundle') for sound similarity with 橐 (tuó, 'bag') ->
    - a horizontal stroke added to differentiate 東 (dōng, 'east') ->
    - final form
  - **interpretation:** 東 (dōng) was adapted from existing characters to convey its phonetic sound. It was visually modified to create a unique form, aiding differentiation from related or sound-alike characters.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )买(買)了( )个(個)新( )東( )。
    - Wǒ mǎi le gè xīn dōng.
    - I bought a new bag.
  - 他把東放在桌上，准(準)备(備)去( )旅( )行( )。
    - Tā bǎ dōng fàng zài zhuō shàng, zhǔnbèi qù lǚxíng.
    - He put the bag on the table, preparing to travel.
### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動) → 重 → 東 → 束
- **definition:** bundle
- **pinyin:** shù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a bundle of sticks tied together
  - **interpretation:** The character 束 (shù, 'bundle') visually represents a group of items, such as sticks or reeds, that have been gathered and tied tightly together. This depiction is direct and intuitive for conveying the idea of a bundle.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )拎( )着(著)一( )束( )鲜(鮮)花( )。
    - Tā līn zhe yī shù xiānhuā.
    - He is carrying a bundle of fresh flowers.
  - 园(園)丁( )修( )剪( )完( )花( )朵( )后(後)，将(將)它( )们(們)整( )理( )成( )一( )束( )。
    - Yuándīng xiūjiǎn wán huāduǒ hòu, jiāng tāmen zhěnglǐ chéng yī shù.
    - After trimming the flowers, the gardener arranged them into a bundle.
  - 小( )贩(販)把( )蔬( )菜( )捆( )成( )一( )束( )，然( )后(後)去( )市( )场(場)出( )售( )。
    - Xiǎofàn bǎ shūcài kǔn chéng yī shù, ránhòu qù shìchǎng chūshòu.
    - The vendor bundled the vegetables and then went to the market to sell them.
### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動) → 重 → 東 → 橐
- **definition:** bag
- **pinyin:** tuó
- **components:**
  - 𣒚
    - n/a
    - bag with a bottom
  - 石
    - shí
    - stone
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 𣒚 (depicts a bag with a bottom) + 石 (shí, 'stone') as phonetic =
    - bag-like object ->
    - concept of 'bag'
  - **interpretation:** The character 橐 combines the semantic element 𣒚, which originally depicted a bag with a distinct bottom, with 石 (shí, 'stone') to convey the sound. The character represents the concept of a 'bag' by visually incorporating the shape of a bag and using 石 to aid pronunciation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )把( )珍( )贵(貴)的( )卷( )轴(軸)放( )进(進)橐( )里(裡)。
    - tā bǎ zhēnguì de juǎnzhóu fàng jìn tuó lǐ.
    - He put the precious scroll into the bag.
  - 晋(晉)朝( )文( )人( )描( )绘(繪)官( )员(員)携(攜)橐( )归(歸)来(來)。
    - jìn cháo wénrén miáohuì guānyuán xié tuó guī lái.
    - Literati from the Jin Dynasty depicted officials returning with the bag.
  - 他( )的( )小( )橐( )装(裝)满(滿)了( )长(長)江( )水( )，激起了浪花。
    - tā de xiǎo tuó zhuāng mǎn le chángjiāng shuǐ, jī qǐ le lànghuā.
    - His small bag was filled with water from the Yangtze, splashing into waves.
### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動) → 重 → 東 → 橐 → 𣒚
- **definition:** bag with a bottom
- **pinyin:** n/a
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a bag with a closed bottom, a stylized representation of a container or sack
  - **interpretation:** The character 𣒚 visually represents a bag design, emphasizing the concept of a closed base preventing contents from falling through. It is an ancient Chinese pictogram reflecting practical aspects of storage or carrying.
### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動) → 重 → 東 → 橐 → 石
- **definition:** stone
- **pinyin:** shí
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a stone beneath a cliff, where the upper part originally looked like a cliff (厂) but was distorted into 丆 over time.
  - **interpretation:** The character 石(shí, 'stone') visually represents a stone in a natural setting, highlighting the element of a cliff or mountain. Its visual design aims to reflect the appearance of rocks lodged or embedded in a mountainous or cliff-like setting.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )在( )河( )边(邊)发(發)现(現)一( )块(塊)石( )。
    - Tā zài hé biān fāxiàn yī kuài shí.
    - He discovered a piece of stone by the riverside.
  - 孩( )子( )们(們)喜( )欢(歡)堆( )石( )，玩( )得( )开(開)心( )。
    - Háizimen xǐhuān duī shí, wán de kāixīn.
    - Children like to stack stones, playing happily.
### 运(運)动(動) → 动(動) → 重 → 土
- **definition:** earth
- **pinyin:** tǔ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a mound of earth
  - **interpretation:** The character 土(tǔ, "earth") visually represents a mound or piece of land, with a horizontal line indicating the surface and a vertical line the height of the mound. It's a straightforward depiction of earth or soil.
- **examples:**
  - 农(農)民( )耕( )土( )，种(種)出( )丰(豐)收( )。
    - Nóngmín gēng tǔ, zhòng chū fēngshōu.
    - Farmers cultivate the earth and harvest abundantly.
  - 院( )子( )里(裡)的( )土( )，适(適)合( )种(種)花( )。
    - Yuànzi lǐ de tǔ, shìhé zhòng huā.
    - The soil in the yard is suitable for planting flowers.

## 喷(噴)
---
## spray
### pēn
---
- **traditional:** 噴
- **definition:** spray
- **pinyin:** pēn
- **components:**
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
  - 賁
    - bēn
    - related to "decorate"
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 口 (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 賁 (bēn, related to "decorate") =
    - sound coming from the mouth ->
    - spray
  - **interpretation:** The character 噴 (pēn, "spray") combines the semantic element 口 (kǒu, "mouth") with the phonetic element 賁 (bēn), suggesting the action of emitting something like spray or mist from the mouth.
  - **simplification:** 噴 -> 喷(噴): 壴 (zhù, "drum") replaced by 巾 (jīn, "towel") for a simpler phonetic match.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )喷(噴)壶(壺)，给(給)花( )浇(澆)水( )。
    - Tā yòng pēn hú, gěi huā jiāo shuǐ.
    - He uses a spray bottle to water the flowers.
  - 水( )龙(龍)头(頭)在( )下( )雨( )时(時)，不( )断(斷)喷(噴)出( )清( )水( )。
    - Shuǐ lóngtóu zài xià yǔ shí, bù duàn pēn chū qīng shuǐ.
    - The faucet continuously sprays clear water during the rain.
### 喷(噴) → 口
- **definition:** mouth
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.
### 喷(噴) → 賁
- **definition:** related to "decorate"
- **pinyin:** bēn
- **components:**
  - 卉
    - huì
    - plant
  - 貝
    - bèi
    - cowry
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phonetic: 卉 (huì, 'plant') + Semantic: 貝 (bèi, 'cowry') ->
    - loaned for 奔 (bēn, 'rush') ->
    - used for 'decorate'
  - **interpretation:** Originally, 賁(bēn) used 貝 ('cowry') symbolizing valuable decoration and paired with phonetic 卉 ('plant'). It was borrowed early on to represent 奔 ('rush'), diversifying its meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 他賁飾了院子，使( )之( )焕(煥)然( )一( )新( )。
    - Tā bēn shì le yuànzi, shǐ zhī huànrán yī xīn.
    - He decorated the courtyard, making it look completely renewed.
  - 节(節)日( )来(來)临(臨)时(時)，市( )集( )被( )彩( )灯(燈)賁( )耀( )。
    - Jiérì láilín shí, shìjí bèi cǎidēng bēn yào.
    - When the festival arrived, the market was brilliantly decorated with colorful lights.
### 喷(噴) → 賁 → 卉
- **definition:** plant
- **pinyin:** huì
- **components:**
  - 丗
    - shì
    - generation
  - 十
    - shí
    - ten
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 丗 (shì, "generation") + 十 (shí, "ten") =
    - plants growing in a field
  - **interpretation:** The character 卉(huì, "plant") is composed of 丗 (shì, "generation") and 十 (shí, "ten"), symbolizing a multitude of plants sprouting in a field, representing abundance and growth.
- **examples:**
  - 花( )卉( )争(爭)艳(艷)。
    - Huā huì zhēng yàn.
    - The plants compete in beauty.
  - 公( )园(園)里(裡)种(種)满(滿)各( )色( )卉( )，营(營)造( )了( )生( )机(機)勃( )勃( )的( )景( )象( )。
    - Gōngyuán lǐ zhòng mǎn gè sè huì, yíngzào le shēngjī bóbó de jǐngxiàng.
    - The park is filled with a variety of plants, creating a vibrant scene.
  - 古( )人( )赞(讚)美( )卉( )木( )，吟( )诗(詩)咏(詠)其( )秀( )丽(麗)。
    - Gǔrén zànměi huì mù, yín shī yǒng qí xiùlì.
    - Ancient scholars praised the flora and composed poems to admire its beauty.
### 喷(噴) → 賁 → 卉 → 丗
- **definition:** generation
- **pinyin:** shì
- **components:**
  - 卅
    - sà
    - thirty
  - 十
    - shí
    - 10
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** variant form
  - **description:**
    - 丗 is a variant form of 卅 (十, "10") repeated three times ->
    - represents thirty
  - **interpretation:** The character 丗 is a variant of 卅, symbolically representing the number thirty by repeating 十 "ten" three times. This highlights how ancient numeric characters often used repetition to denote multiplication.
### 喷(噴) → 賁 → 卉 → 丗 → 卅
- **definition:** thirty
- **pinyin:** sà
- **components:**
  - 十
    - shí
    - ten
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 十 (shí, "10") repeated three times ->
    - symbol for "thirty"
  - **interpretation:** The character 卅 (sà, "thirty") visually represents three instances of the character 十 (shí, "10"), symbolizing the number thirty.
### 喷(噴) → 賁 → 卉 → 丗 → 卅 → 十
- **definition:** ten
- **pinyin:** shí
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Original vertical stroke ->
    - later horizontal stroke added ->
    - forms a cross ->
    - represents 'ten'
  - **interpretation:** The character 十(shí, 'ten') represents the number ten with a cross shape that could be seen as tally marks. It evolved from a simple vertical stroke to a cross by adding a horizontal stroke, symbolizing a complete set of ten.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )有( )十( )本( )书(書)。
    - Wǒ yǒu shí běn shū.
    - I have ten books.
  - 花( )园(園)里(裡)有( )十( )棵( )树(樹)，景( )色( )十( )分( )优(優)美( )。
    - Huāyuán lǐ yǒu shí kē shù, jǐngsè shífēn yōuměi.
    - There are ten trees in the garden, and the scenery is very beautiful.
  - 十( )年( )磨( )一( )剑(劍)。
    - Shí nián mó yī jiàn.
    - It takes ten years of practice to hone a sword.
### 喷(噴) → 賁 → 貝
- **definition:** cowry
- **pinyin:** bèi
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a cowrie shell ->
    - used as currency in ancient China ->
    - stylized as 貝
  - **interpretation:** The character 貝(bèi, 'shell/money') visually represents a cowrie shell, which was used as a form of currency in ancient China. This explains why it is associated with money or valuables.
  - **simplification:** 贝 is a cursive script simplification of 貝, transforming the character into a more streamlined form while retaining its essence.
- **examples:**
  - 他把贝(貝)当作护(護)身( )符( )，放在口( )袋( )里(裡)。
    - Tā bǎ bèi dāng zuò hùshēn fú, fàng zài kǒudài lǐ.
    - He uses shells as a talisman, placing them in his pocket.
  - 古( )代( )人( )用( )贝(貝)作( )为(為)货(貨)币(幣)。
    - Gǔdài rén yòng bèi zuòwéi huòbì.
    - In ancient times, people used shells as currency.

## bouquet
### huāshù
---
- **definition:** bouquet
- **pinyin:** huāshù
- **components:**
  - 花
    - huā
    - flower
  - 束
    - shù
    - bundle; bunch; to bind
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 花 (huā, "flower") + 束 (shù, "bundle") =
    - bouquet
  - **interpretation:** A 花 (huā, "flower") 束 (shù, "bundle") is a collection of flowers arranged together as a bouquet. The term directly embodies the idea of a 'bundle of flowers', capturing the visual and physical arrangement typically associated with bouquets.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )给(給)母( )亲(親)买(買)了( )一( )束( )花( )束( )。
    - Wǒ gěi mǔqīn mǎile yī shù huāshù.
    - I bought a bouquet for my mother.
  - 他( )在( )纪(紀)念( )日( )晚( )宴( )上( )送( )她( )花( )束( )，表( )达(達)了( )深( )情( )。
    - Tā zài jìniànrì wǎnyàn shàng sòng tā huāshù, biǎodále shēnqíng.
    - He gave her a bouquet during the anniversary dinner, expressing his deep affection.
  - 花( )店( )为(為)婚( )礼(禮)准(準)备(備)了( )一( )束( )花( )束( )。
    - Huādiàn wèi hūnlǐ zhǔnbèile yī shù huāshù.
    - The florist prepared a bouquet for the wedding.
### 花束 → 花
- **definition:** flower
- **pinyin:** huā
- **components:**
  - 艹
    - cǎo
    - grass; plant
  - 化
    - huà
    - transform
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 艹 (cǎo, "grass; plant") + phonetic 化 (huà, "transform") =
    - plant + phonetic indicator ->
    - flower
  - **interpretation:** 花 (huā, "flower") combines 艹 (cǎo, "grass; plant") indicating its association with vegetation, and 化 (huà, "transform") for its phonetic value. This illustrates both the natural aspect and the phonetic pronunciation.
- **examples:**
  - 花香四溢。
    - huā xiāng sì yì。
    - The fragrance of flowers fills the air.
  - 阳(陽)光( )明( )媚( )，花( )儿(兒)争(爭)艳(艷)。
    - yáng guāng míng mèi，huā ér zhēng yàn。
    - The bright sunshine shines as the flowers compete in beauty.
  - 她( )用( )花( )装(裝)点(點)房( )间(間)。
    - tā yòng huā zhuāng diǎn fáng jiān。
    - She decorates the room with flowers.
### 花束 → 花 → 艹
- **definition:** grass; plant
- **pinyin:** cǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts blades of grass
  - **interpretation:** 艹 is a pictogram representing grass or plants, resembling blades of grass. It is commonly used as a radical in other characters related to grass or vegetation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )写(寫)字( )时(時)用( )艹( )作( )草( )字( )的( )偏( )旁( )。
    - Tā xiězì shí yòng cǎo zuò cǎozì de piānpáng.
    - He uses '艹' as the radical for grass in his characters.
  - 春( )天( )到( )来(來)时(時)，艹( )生( )长(長)得( )很( )快( )。
    - Chūntiān dàolái shí, cǎo shēngzhǎng de hěn kuài.
    - When spring arrives, grass grows rapidly.
### 花束 → 花 → 化
- **definition:** transform
- **pinyin:** huà
- **components:**
  - 亻
    - rén
    - person
  - 𠤎
    - rén
    - person
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 亻 (rén, "person") + 𠤎 (rén, "person") upside down =
    - two people, one upright, one reversed ->
    - transformation or change
  - **interpretation:** The character 化 (huà, "transform") visually represents change or transformation, signified by two 人 (rén, "person") characters in opposite orientations, suggesting reversal or alteration.
- **examples:**
  - 科( )学(學)进(進)步( )，推( )动(動)社( )会(會)化( )转(轉)型( )。
    - Kēxué jìnbù, tuīdòng shèhuì huà zhuǎnxíng.
    - Scientific progress transforms social structures.
  - 时(時)间(間)能( )让(讓)伤(傷)痛( )化( )作( )记(記)忆(憶)。
    - Shíjiān néng ràng shāngtòng huà zuò jìyì.
    - Time transforms pain into memories.
  - 困( )境( )中( )他( )不( )断(斷)努( )力( )，生( )命( )终(終)将(將)化( )为(為)辉(輝)煌( )。
    - Kùnjìng zhōng tā bùduàn nǔlì, shēngmìng zhōng jiāng huà wéi huīhuáng.
    - Amid difficulties he perseveres, and his life will eventually transform into brilliance.
### 花束 → 花 → 化 → 亻
- **definition:** person
- **pinyin:** rén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 亻 (rén, "person") is a stylization of 人 (rén, "person") ->
    - simplified for use as a component in other characters
  - **interpretation:** 亻 (rén, "person") is used as a component form of 人 (rén, "person") in compound characters, representing the human or person-related aspect.
- **examples:**
  - 亻( )是( )构(構)成( )许(許)多( )汉(漢)字( )的( )关(關)键(鍵)部( )首( )。
    - Rén shì gòuchéng xǔduō hànzì de guānjiàn bùshǒu.
    - The radical 亻 is a key component in many Chinese characters.
  - 学(學)习(習)汉(漢)字( )时(時)，了( )解( )亻( )的( )意( )义(義)很( )重( )要( )。
    - Xuéxí hànzì shí, liǎojiě rén de yìyì hěn zhòngyào.
    - When learning Chinese characters, understanding the meaning of 亻 is important.
  - 古( )人( )造( )字( )时(時)常( )取( )亻( )以( )表( )达(達)人( )物( )情( )感( )，因( )此( )亻( )贯(貫)穿( )于(於)文( )明( )史( )中( )。
    - Gǔrén zào zì shí cháng qǔ rén yǐ biǎodá rénwù qínggǎn, yīncǐ rén guànchuān yú wénmíng shǐ zhōng.
    - Ancient people often used the radical 亻 to express human emotions in character formation, and thus 亻 runs through the history of civilization.
### 花束 → 花 → 化 → 𠤎
- **definition:** person
- **pinyin:** rén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - Two 人 (rén, "person"), one upright (亻) and one upside down (𠤎) ->
    - reversal or change
  - **interpretation:** 924
- **examples:**
  - 他是勇敢的𠤎。
    - Tā shì yǒnggǎn de rén.
    - He is a brave person.
  - 这(這)个(個)𠤎( )勤( )奋(奮)好( )学(學)，为(為)家( )人( )争(爭)光( )。
    - Zhège rén qínfèn hàoxué, wèi jiārén zhēngguāng.
    - This person is diligent and studious, bringing honor to his family.
  - 老( )𠤎( )智( )慧( )丰(豐)富( )。
    - Lǎo rén zhìhuì fēngfù.
    - The elderly person is wise and knowledgeable.
### 花束 → 束
- **definition:** bundle; bunch; to bind
- **pinyin:** shù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a bundle of sticks tied together
  - **interpretation:** The character 束 (shù, 'bundle') visually represents a group of items, such as sticks or reeds, that have been gathered and tied tightly together. This depiction is direct and intuitive for conveying the idea of a bundle.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )拎( )着(著)一( )束( )鲜(鮮)花( )。
    - Tā līn zhe yī shù xiānhuā.
    - He is carrying a bundle of fresh flowers.
  - 园(園)丁( )修( )剪( )完( )花( )朵( )后(後)，将(將)它( )们(們)整( )理( )成( )一( )束( )。
    - Yuándīng xiūjiǎn wán huāduǒ hòu, jiāng tāmen zhěnglǐ chéng yī shù.
    - After trimming the flowers, the gardener arranged them into a bundle.
  - 小( )贩(販)把( )蔬( )菜( )捆( )成( )一( )束( )，然( )后(後)去( )市( )场(場)出( )售( )。
    - Xiǎofàn bǎ shūcài kǔn chéng yī shù, ránhòu qù shìchǎng chūshòu.
    - The vendor bundled the vegetables and then went to the market to sell them.

## 寺
---
## temple
### sì
---
- **definition:** temple
- **pinyin:** sì
- **components:**
  - 之
    - zhī
    - go/arrive
  - 寸
    - cùn
    - inch
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 之 (zhī, "go/arrive") + 寸 (cùn, "inch") =
    - a place measured and designated for a special purpose ->
    - temple
  - **interpretation:** The character 寺(sì, "temple") combines 之 (zhī, "go/arrive") indicating a place of arrival or visiting, with 寸 (cùn, "inch"), suggesting precision or formal measurement. Together, they conceptually define a space set apart for religious or ceremonial purposes.
- **examples:**
  - 古( )寺( )静(靜)卧(臥)山( )间(間)。
    - Gǔ sì jìng wò shān jiān.
    - The ancient temple lies quietly among the mountains.
  - 人( )们(們)来(來)到( )寺( )参(參)拜( )，祈( )求( )平( )安( )与(與)幸( )福( )。
    - Rén men lái dào sì cān bài, qí qiú píng ān yǔ xìng fú.
    - People come to the temple to offer their respects, praying for peace and happiness.
  - 城外有一座小寺，供( )奉( )着(著)千( )年( )佛( )像( )。
    - Chéng wài yǒu yī zuò xiǎo sì, gòng fèng zhe qiān nián fó xiàng.
    - Outside the city, there is a small temple enshrining a thousand-year-old Buddha statue.
### 寺 → 之
- **definition:** go/arrive
- **pinyin:** zhī
- **components:**
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - foot
  - 一
    - yī
    - the beginning place
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 止 (zhǐ, "foot") + 一 (yī, "the beginning place") =
    - to go ->
    - footprint ->
    - evolved to a point above ->
    - final form 之
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicting a foot at the start of movement, the character evolved into a possessive marker, possibly due to conceptual association with movement or direction.
- **examples:**
  - 知( )识(識)之( )光( )照( )亮( )心( )灵(靈)。
    - Zhīshì zhī guāng zhào liàng xīn líng.
    - The light of knowledge illuminates the heart.
  - 理( )想( )之( )翼( )助( )人( )飞(飛)翔( )，努( )力( )之( )心( )成( )就( )未( )来(來)。
    - Lǐxiǎng zhī yì zhù rén fēi xiáng, Nǔlì zhī xīn chéngjiù wèilái.
    - The wings of ideals help people to soar, and the heart of effort accomplishes the future.
### 寺 → 之 → 止
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zhǐ
- **components:**
  - 趾
    - zhǐ
    - foot
  - 龰
    - yù
    - stylized feet
  - 手
    - shǒu
    - hand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Original: Foot pointing up with 3 toes and a sole ->
    - Stylized as 龰 ->
    - Derivative 趾 refers to original 'foot' meaning
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicting a footprint with toes and a sole, 止 (zhǐ, "foot") represents the foot. The character's form resembles a foot with three toes, analogous to how the hand is represented with fingers in other characters.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)的( )止( )需( )要( )静(靜)养(養)。
    - Tā shòu shāng de zhǐ xū yào jìng yǎng.
    - His injured foot needs adequate rest.
  - 他( )走( )路( )时(時)小( )心( )谨(謹)慎( )，因( )为(爲)止( )若( )受( )伤(傷)便( )难(難)前( )行( )。
    - Tā zǒu lù shí xiǎo xīn jǐn shèn, yīn wèi zhǐ ruò shòu shāng biàn nán qián xíng.
    - He walks carefully because if his foot gets injured, it becomes difficult to move forward.
### 寺 → 之 → 止 → 趾
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zhǐ
- **components:**
  - 足
    - zú
    - foot
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - stop
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 足 (zú, "foot") + 止 (zhǐ, "stop") =
    - semantic: foot + phonetic: stop ->
    - toe
  - **interpretation:** The character combines 足 (zú, "foot") as the semantic indicator and 止 (zhǐ, "stop") as the phonetic component to represent the idea of a 'toe,' which is a part of the foot.
- **examples:**
  - 他的趾因寒冷而僵硬了。
    - tā de zhǐ yīn hánlěng ér jiāngyìng le.
    - His toe became stiff due to the cold.
  - 我( )走( )路( )时(時)，不小心扭到了趾。
    - wǒ zǒulù shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ dào le zhǐ.
    - While I was walking, I accidentally twisted my toe.
  - 古( )代( )剑(劍)客( )战(戰)斗(鬥)时(時)，常( )因( )疏( )忽( )伤(傷)及( )趾( )。
    - gǔdài jiànkè zhàndòu shí, cháng yīn shūhū shāng jí zhǐ.
    - Ancient swordsmen often injured their toes due to carelessness during battles.
### 寺 → 之 → 止 → 趾 → 足
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zú
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a leg ->
    - originally depicted as ⽦ ->
    - represents 'foot'
  - **interpretation:** The character 足(zú, 'foot') is a pictographic representation of a human leg, which directly portrays the concept of a 'foot'. The pictographic origin emphasizes its fundamental meaning in the language.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)后(後)，他( )的( )足( )不( )能( )动(動)。
    - tā shòushāng hòu, tā de zú bù néng dòng。
    - After he was injured, his foot couldn't move.
  - 小( )明( )打( )球( )时(時)，不( )小( )心( )扭( )伤(傷)了( )足( )。
    - xiǎo míng dǎ qiú shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ shāng le zú。
    - When Xiaoming was playing ball, he accidentally twisted his foot.
  - 我( )每( )天( )锻(鍛)炼(鍊)足( )部( )。
    - wǒ měitiān duànliàn zúbù。
    - I exercise my foot muscles every day.
### 寺 → 之 → 止 → 龰
- **definition:** stylized feet
- **pinyin:** yù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a stylized footprint ->
    - represents feet
  - **interpretation:** 龰 depicts a footprint oriented upward, representing feet. It serves as a stylized version of 足 (zú, "foot") and is related to the original pictogram depiction of a sole with three toes. This simplification focuses on the essential shape of the foot in written form.
### 寺 → 之 → 止 → 手
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a hand with fingers; top stroke is a bent over middle finger, horizontal strokes represent fingers
  - **interpretation:** The character 手 (shǒu, "hand") is a direct pictorial representation of a human hand with all five fingers, which distinguishes it from similar characters representing claws or simplified fingers. This visually conveys its meaning as a hand clearly.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )手( )写(寫)字( )。
    - Tā yòng shǒu xiě zì.
    - He writes with his hand.
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)牵(牽)着(著)我( )的( )手( )走( )路( )。
    - Māmā qiān zhe wǒ de shǒu zǒu lù.
    - Mom holds my hand while walking.
  - 他( )的( )手( )很( )温(溫)暖( )，带(帶)给(給)我( )力( )量( )。
    - Tā de shǒu hěn wēn nuǎn, dài gěi wǒ lìliàng.
    - His hand is very warm, giving me strength.
### 寺 → 之 → 一
- **definition:** the beginning place
- **pinyin:** yī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:** A horizontal stroke representing the concept of 'one'.
  - **interpretation:** The character 一(yī, "one") is a simple horizontal line signifying the singular and unified concept of 'one'. It directly represents being singular or the number one.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )有( )一( )个(個)苹(蘋)果( )。
    - Wǒ yǒu yī gè píngguǒ.
    - I have one apple.
  - 他( )只( )看( )到( )一( )只(隻)鸟(鳥)，飞(飛)进(進)了( )花( )园(園)。
    - Tā zhǐ kàn dào yī zhī niǎo, fēi jìn le huā yuán.
    - He only saw one bird, which flew into the garden.
  - 一( )条(條)直( )路( )通( )向( )远(遠)方( )。
    - Yī tiáo zhí lù tōng xiàng yuǎn fāng.
    - One straight road leads to the distance.
### 寺 → 寸
- **definition:** inch
- **pinyin:** cùn
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:**
    - Line on the hand (ideographic) emphasizing the elbow ->
    - represents measurement unit between wrist and specific forearm position ->
    - meaning 'inch'
  - **interpretation:** 寸 (cùn, "inch") originated as a depiction of a line on the hand, indicating the area near the elbow. This specific placement was considered significant for pulse palpation, originally suggesting a forearm position, which then came to denote the measurement 'inch'.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)块(塊)布( )每( )一( )寸( )都( )是( )精( )心( )制(製)作( )。
    - Zhè kuài bù měi yī cùn dōu shì jīngxīn zhìzuò.
    - Every inch of this cloth is crafted with care.
  - 请(請)用( )卷( )尺( )量( )一( )寸( )，每( )一( )寸( )数(數)据(據)都( )准(準)确(確)。
    - Qǐng yòng juǎnchǐ liàng yī cùn, měi yī cùn shùjù dōu zhǔnquè.
    - Please use a tape measure to measure an inch, every inch's data is accurate.

## 龙(龍)华(華)
---
## Longhua
### Lónghuá
---
- **traditional:** 龍華
- **definition:** Longhua
- **pinyin:** Lónghuá
- **components:**
  - 龙(龍)
    - lóng
    - dragon; imperial
  - 华(華)
    - huá
    - splendor; China
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 龍 (lóng, "dragon") and 華 (huá, "splendor; China") suggest a name evoking grandeur and cultural richness.
  - **interpretation:** 龍 (lóng, "dragon") is often associated with power, authority, and good fortune, while 華 (huá, "splendor; China") conveys beauty and cultural brilliance. Together, 龍華 (Lónghuá) evokes an image of a culturally rich and prosperous place, which is fitting for areas named 龙(龍)华(華) in China, such as districts known for their historical or cultural significance.
  - **simplification:** 龙 become simpler
- **examples:**
  - 龙(龍)华(華)闪(閃)耀( )着(著)古( )典( )魅( )力( )。
    - Lónghuá shǎnyào zhe gǔdiǎn mèilì.
    - Longhua shines with classical charm.
  - 在( )龙(龍)华(華)，历(歷)史( )与(與)现(現)代( )交( )织(織)。
    - Zài Lónghuá, lìshǐ yǔ xiàndài jiāozhī.
    - In Longhua, history and modern life intertwine.
### 龙(龍)华(華) → 龙(龍)
- **traditional:** 龍
- **definition:** dragon; imperial
- **pinyin:** lóng
- **components:**
  - 辰
    - chén
    - early form of dragon
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 辰 (chén, "early form of dragon") as phonetic + stylized depiction of a dragon body =
    - 龙 ->
    - dragon
  - **interpretation:** 龙(龍) (lóng, "dragon") combines a phonetic element from 辰 (chén, "early form of dragon") with a stylized representation of a dragon's body, capturing both sound and essence to represent the mythical creature.
  - **simplification:** 龙(龍) simplified by combining phonetic 辰 with a less detailed body form to represent the dragon.
- **examples:**
  - 传(傳)说(說)中( )的( )龙(龍)能( )呼( )风(風)唤(喚)雨( )。
    - Chuánshuō zhōng de lóng néng hū fēng huàn yǔ.
    - The legendary dragon can summon wind and rain.
  - 皇( )宫(宮)中( )悬(懸)挂(掛)着(著)龙(龍)袍( )，象( )征(徵)着(著)至( )高( )无(無)上( )的( )权(權)力( )。
    - Huánggōng zhōng xuánguà zhe lóng páo, xiàngzhēng zhe zhìgāo wúshàng de quánlì.
    - In the imperial palace, the dragon robe hangs, symbolizing supreme power.
### 龙(龍)华(華) → 龙(龍) → 辰
- **definition:** early form of dragon
- **pinyin:** chén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a heavenly body or a celestial presence, often associated with dragons in ancient mythology.
  - **interpretation:** 辰 (chén, "early form of dragon") originally depicted a celestial body that was believed to influence time and was associated with dragons due to mythological links between dragons and celestial phenomena.
- **examples:**
  - 古( )代( )传(傳)说(說)中( )的( )辰( )是( )神( )秘( )而( )强(強)大( )的( )生( )物( )。
    - Gǔdài chuánshuō zhōng de chén shì shénmì ér qiángdà de shēngwù.
    - In ancient legends, the chén is a mysterious and powerful creature.
  - 东(東)方( )初( )升( )的( )朝( )阳(陽)映( )照( )着(著)辰( )的( )鳞(鱗)光( )，唤(喚)起( )了( )古( )老( )的( )神( )话(話)。
    - Dōngfāng chūshēng de cháoyáng yìngzhào zhe chén de línguāng, huànqǐ le gǔlǎo de shénhuà.
    - The rising morning sun in the east illuminates the scales of the chén, evoking ancient myths.
  - 传(傳)承( )了( )古( )老( )智( )慧( )的( )文( )献(獻)记(記)载(載)了( )辰( )的( )奇( )迹(蹟)，使( )后(後)人( )对(對)它( )充( )满(滿)敬( )畏( )。
    - Chuánchéng le gǔlǎo zhìhuì de wénxiàn jìzǎi le chén de qíjī, shǐ hòurén duì tā chōngmǎn jìngwèi.
    - Ancient texts carrying old wisdom recorded the miracles of the chén, filling later generations with awe.
### 龙(龍)华(華) → 华(華)
- **traditional:** 華
- **definition:** splendor; China
- **pinyin:** huá
- **components:**
  - 化
    - huà
    - transform
  - 十
    - shí
    - stalk and leaves
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phonetic: 化 (huà, "transform") + Semantic: 十 ("stalk and leaves") ->
    - 華 ("magnificent") ->
    - Simplified to 华 with 化
  - **interpretation:** The traditional character 華 uses 十 to evoke a stalk with leaves, representing growth and magnificence. In the simplified form 华, 化 helps retain the original sound while removing visual complexity.
  - **simplification:** The character was simplified by using 化 to streamline its phonetic component and reduce stroke count, while 十 was omitted.
- **examples:**
  - 中( )国(國)古( )代( )文( )明( )辉(輝)煌( )，成( )就( )了( )华(華)夏( )的( )美( )名( )。
    - Zhōngguó gǔdài wénmíng huīhuáng, chéngjiùle huáxià de měimíng.
    - The brilliance of ancient Chinese civilization has earned China a magnificent reputation.
  - 他( )穿( )着(著)一( )件( )华(華)服( )，显(顯)得( )格( )外( )自( )信( )。
    - Tā chuānzhuó yī jiàn huáfú, xiǎnde géwài zìxìn.
    - He wears a magnificent garment, looking exceptionally confident.
### 龙(龍)华(華) → 华(華) → 化
- **definition:** transform
- **pinyin:** huà
- **components:**
  - 亻
    - rén
    - person
  - 𠤎
    - rén
    - person
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 亻 (rén, "person") + 𠤎 (rén, "person") upside down =
    - two people, one upright, one reversed ->
    - transformation or change
  - **interpretation:** The character 化 (huà, "transform") visually represents change or transformation, signified by two 人 (rén, "person") characters in opposite orientations, suggesting reversal or alteration.
- **examples:**
  - 科( )学(學)进(進)步( )，推( )动(動)社( )会(會)化( )转(轉)型( )。
    - Kēxué jìnbù, tuīdòng shèhuì huà zhuǎnxíng.
    - Scientific progress transforms social structures.
  - 时(時)间(間)能( )让(讓)伤(傷)痛( )化( )作( )记(記)忆(憶)。
    - Shíjiān néng ràng shāngtòng huà zuò jìyì.
    - Time transforms pain into memories.
  - 困( )境( )中( )他( )不( )断(斷)努( )力( )，生( )命( )终(終)将(將)化( )为(為)辉(輝)煌( )。
    - Kùnjìng zhōng tā bùduàn nǔlì, shēngmìng zhōng jiāng huà wéi huīhuáng.
    - Amid difficulties he perseveres, and his life will eventually transform into brilliance.
### 龙(龍)华(華) → 华(華) → 化 → 亻
- **definition:** person
- **pinyin:** rén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 亻 (rén, "person") is a stylization of 人 (rén, "person") ->
    - simplified for use as a component in other characters
  - **interpretation:** 亻 (rén, "person") is used as a component form of 人 (rén, "person") in compound characters, representing the human or person-related aspect.
- **examples:**
  - 亻( )是( )构(構)成( )许(許)多( )汉(漢)字( )的( )关(關)键(鍵)部( )首( )。
    - Rén shì gòuchéng xǔduō hànzì de guānjiàn bùshǒu.
    - The radical 亻 is a key component in many Chinese characters.
  - 学(學)习(習)汉(漢)字( )时(時)，了( )解( )亻( )的( )意( )义(義)很( )重( )要( )。
    - Xuéxí hànzì shí, liǎojiě rén de yìyì hěn zhòngyào.
    - When learning Chinese characters, understanding the meaning of 亻 is important.
  - 古( )人( )造( )字( )时(時)常( )取( )亻( )以( )表( )达(達)人( )物( )情( )感( )，因( )此( )亻( )贯(貫)穿( )于(於)文( )明( )史( )中( )。
    - Gǔrén zào zì shí cháng qǔ rén yǐ biǎodá rénwù qínggǎn, yīncǐ rén guànchuān yú wénmíng shǐ zhōng.
    - Ancient people often used the radical 亻 to express human emotions in character formation, and thus 亻 runs through the history of civilization.
### 龙(龍)华(華) → 华(華) → 化 → 𠤎
- **definition:** person
- **pinyin:** rén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - Two 人 (rén, "person"), one upright (亻) and one upside down (𠤎) ->
    - reversal or change
  - **interpretation:** 924
- **examples:**
  - 他是勇敢的𠤎。
    - Tā shì yǒnggǎn de rén.
    - He is a brave person.
  - 这(這)个(個)𠤎( )勤( )奋(奮)好( )学(學)，为(為)家( )人( )争(爭)光( )。
    - Zhège rén qínfèn hàoxué, wèi jiārén zhēngguāng.
    - This person is diligent and studious, bringing honor to his family.
  - 老( )𠤎( )智( )慧( )丰(豐)富( )。
    - Lǎo rén zhìhuì fēngfù.
    - The elderly person is wise and knowledgeable.
### 龙(龍)华(華) → 华(華) → 十
- **definition:** stalk and leaves
- **pinyin:** shí
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Original vertical stroke ->
    - later horizontal stroke added ->
    - forms a cross ->
    - represents 'ten'
  - **interpretation:** The character 十(shí, 'ten') represents the number ten with a cross shape that could be seen as tally marks. It evolved from a simple vertical stroke to a cross by adding a horizontal stroke, symbolizing a complete set of ten.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )有( )十( )本( )书(書)。
    - Wǒ yǒu shí běn shū.
    - I have ten books.
  - 花( )园(園)里(裡)有( )十( )棵( )树(樹)，景( )色( )十( )分( )优(優)美( )。
    - Huāyuán lǐ yǒu shí kē shù, jǐngsè shífēn yōuměi.
    - There are ten trees in the garden, and the scenery is very beautiful.
  - 十( )年( )磨( )一( )剑(劍)。
    - Shí nián mó yī jiàn.
    - It takes ten years of practice to hone a sword.

## 家( )乡(鄉)
---
## hometown
### jiāxiāng
---
- **traditional:** 家鄉
- **definition:** hometown
- **pinyin:** jiāxiāng
- **components:**
  - 家
    - jiā
    - home; family; house
  - 乡(鄉)
    - xiāng
    - village; countryside; hometown
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 家 (jiā, "home") + 乡(鄉) (xiāng, "village") refers to one's place of origin or hometown. Together they form 家( )乡(鄉) (jiāxiāng, "hometown"), emphasizing the idea of a home village or the place where one feels a deep connection due to personal roots or heritage.
  - **interpretation:** 家( )乡(鄉) (jiāxiāng, "hometown") signifies the place that is culturally and sentimentally significant to an individual, often where they were born and raised.
  - **simplification:** 家乡 is the simplified form of 家鄉.
- **examples:**
  - 我热爱家( )乡(鄉)。
    - Wǒ rè'ài jiāxiāng.
    - I love my hometown.
  - 每( )当(當)春( )节(節)到( )来(來)时(時)，我都会想念家( )乡(鄉)。
    - Měi dāng chūnjié dào lái shí, wǒ dōu huì xiǎngniàn jiāxiāng.
    - Whenever the Spring Festival arrives, I always miss my hometown.
  - 经(經)历(歷)长(長)途( )跋( )涉( )后(後)，我终于回到了家( )乡(鄉)。
    - Jīnglì chángtú báshè hòu, wǒ zhōngyú huí dào le jiāxiāng.
    - After a long journey, I finally returned to my hometown.
### 家( )乡(鄉) → 家
- **definition:** home; family; house
- **pinyin:** jiā
- **components:**
  - 宀
    - mián
    - roof
  - 豕
    - shǐ
    - pig
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 宀 (mián, 'roof') + 豕 (shǐ, 'pig') =
    - pig under a roof ->
    - home, house
  - **interpretation:** The character 家 (jiā, 'home; family; house') uses the pictorial elements of a 'roof' and a 'pig' to evoke an ancient image of a house, where pigs were commonly kept under the same roof as a family, indicating the concept of a home.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )爱(愛)我( )的( )家( )。
    - Wǒ ài wǒ de jiā.
    - I love my home.
  - 回( )家( )路( )上( )遇( )见(見)老( )朋( )友( )，聊起往事。
    - Huí jiā lùshàng yùjiàn lǎo péngyǒu, liáo qǐ wǎngshì.
    - On the way home, I met an old friend, and we reminisced about the past.
### 家( )乡(鄉) → 家 → 宀
- **definition:** roof
- **pinyin:** mián
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a house with four walls ->
    - roof
  - **interpretation:** 宀(mián, "roof") is a pictogram representing a house with four walls. It emphasizes an enclosed space, distinguishing it from similar structures like 广(guǎng, "broad") which depicts a building with three walls. This makes 宀 a logical choice for representing a roof in various characters.
- **examples:**
  - 含( )有( )宀( )的( )汉(漢)字( )代( )表( )家( )的( )温(溫)暖( )。
    - Hán yǒu mián de hànzì dàibiǎo jiā de wēnnuǎn.
    - Chinese characters that include the radical '宀' represent the warmth of home.
  - 屋( )顶(頂)遮( )风(風)挡(擋)雨( )，家( )园(園)散( )发(發)温(溫)馨( )。
    - Wūdǐng zhē fēng dǎng yǔ, jiāyuán sànfā wēnxīn.
    - The roof shields from wind and rain, and the home radiates warmth.
### 家( )乡(鄉) → 家 → 豕
- **definition:** pig
- **pinyin:** shǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a pig with distinct features like snout and legs.
  - **interpretation:** The character 豕 (shǐ, 'pig') is a pictogram that visually represents a pig's profile, emphasizing features such as its snout and legs, which help to convey the idea of a pig or boar.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)头(頭)豕( )很( )肥( )。
    - Zhè tóu shǐ hěn féi.
    - This pig is very fat.
  - 农(農)民( )饲(飼)养(養)豕( )，供( )应(應)市( )场(場)。
    - Nóngmín sìyǎng shǐ, gōngyìng shìchǎng.
    - Farmers raise pigs to supply the market.
  - 豕( )在( )泥( )潭( )中( )嬉( )戏(戲)。
    - Shǐ zài ní tán zhōng xīxì.
    - The pig frolics in the mud.
### 家( )乡(鄉) → 乡(鄉)
- **traditional:** 鄉
- **definition:** village; countryside; hometown
- **pinyin:** xiāng
- **components:**
  - 𠨍
    - two people facing each other
    - two people facing each other
  - 皀
    - round-mouthed food vessel
    - round-mouthed food vessel
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 𠨍 (𠨍) (two people facing each other) + 皀 (皀) (round-mouthed food vessel) ->
    - original meaning 'to feast' ->
    - phonetic borrowing or semantic shifting to mean 'village'
  - **interpretation:** The character initially represented two people facing a food vessel, symbolizing communal feasting, which was a central part of village life. Over time, it came to denote 'village' through borrowing or semantic shift, aligning with the social aspect it originally depicted.
  - **simplification:** 乡(鄉) simplifies by using a component resembling 良 (liáng, 'good'), unrelated to 郎 (láng, 'young man'), to streamline its form, based on 皀 during the clerical script development.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )来(來)自( )乡(鄉)。
    - Wǒ láizì xiāng.
    - I come from the countryside.
  - 我( )怀(懷)念( )乡(鄉)，那( )里(裡)有( )我( )的( )故( )事( )。
    - Wǒ huáiniàn xiāng, nàlǐ yǒu wǒ de gùshì.
    - I miss my hometown, where my stories lie.
  - 他( )常( )回( )乡(鄉)，寻(尋)找( )旧(舊)日( )情( )怀(懷)。
    - Tā cháng huí xiāng, xúnzhǎo jiù rì qínghuái.
    - He often returns to his hometown, seeking the old sentiments.
### 家( )乡(鄉) → 乡(鄉) → 𠨍
- **definition:** two people facing each other
- **pinyin:** two people facing each other
- **components:**
  - 卯
    - mǎo
    - fourth earthly branch
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** variant form
  - **description:** Variant form of 卯 (mǎo, "fourth earthly branch")
  - **interpretation:** 𠨍 is a variant form of 卯 (mǎo, "fourth earthly branch"), traditionally depicting two interlocking components associated with the fourth earthly branch in the Chinese zodiac cycle.
### 家( )乡(鄉) → 乡(鄉) → 𠨍 → 卯
- **definition:** fourth earthly branch
- **pinyin:** mǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Originally depicts a pair of knives used in a ritual sacrifice, showing an animal or human cut in half for Shang dynasty sacrifices.
  - **interpretation:** 卯(mǎo) was originally a term associated with sacrificial rituals involving cutting, reflecting cultural practices of the Shang dynasty. Over time, its meaning has shifted away from these origins.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)字( )的( )卯( )部( )分( )揭( )示( )了( )它( )的( )读(讀)音( )，构(構)成( )了( )汉(漢)字( )的( )声(聲)音( )系( )统(統)。
    - Zhège zì de mǎo bùfen jiēshì le tā de dúyīn, gòuchéng le hànzì de shēngyīn xìtǒng.
    - The phonetic component of this character reveals its pronunciation, forming the sound system of Chinese characters.
  - 研( )究( )卯( )的( )作( )用( )有( )助( )于(於)理( )解( )汉(漢)字( )结(結)构(構)。
    - Yánjiū mǎo de zuòyòng yǒuzhù yú lǐjiě hànzì jiégòu.
    - Studying the role of the phonetic component helps to understand the structure of Chinese characters.
### 家( )乡(鄉) → 乡(鄉) → 皀
- **definition:** round-mouthed food vessel
- **pinyin:** round-mouthed food vessel
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a bowl of rice on a stand
  - **interpretation:** The character 皀 is a pictogram representing a bowl of rice settled on a stand or support, capturing the idea of a round-mouthed food vessel used in traditional settings.

## 跑步
---
## run; jog
### pǎobù
---
- **definition:** run; jog
- **pinyin:** pǎobù
- **components:**
  - 跑
    - pǎo
    - run; escape
  - 步
    - bù
    - step; pace
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 跑 (pǎo, "run") + 步 (bù, "step") is a combination that describes the action of taking running steps, thus forming the concept of running or jogging.
  - **interpretation:** By combining the action of running with steps, 跑步(pǎobù) captures the essence of moving rapidly on foot and is commonly used to describe the activity of jogging or running.
- **examples:**
  - 我每天跑步。
    - Wǒ měitiān pǎobù.
    - I run every day.
  - 他( )爱(愛)运(運)动(動)，尤( )其( )喜( )欢(歡)跑( )步( )。
    - Tā ài yùndòng, yóuqí xǐhuān pǎobù.
    - He loves sports, especially running.
  - 春天到了，我( )去( )公( )园(園)跑( )步( )。
    - Chūntiān dàole, wǒ qù gōngyuán pǎobù.
    - Spring has arrived, and I go running in the park.
### 跑步 → 跑
- **definition:** run; escape
- **pinyin:** pǎo
- **components:**
  - 足
    - zú
    - foot
  - 包
    - bāo
    - wrap
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 足 (zú, "foot") + phonetic: 包 (bāo, "wrap") =
    - movement involving feet ->
    - run
  - **interpretation:** 跑 (pǎo, "run") combines the semantic element 足 (zú, "foot"), indicating movement, with the phonetic component 包 (bāo, "wrap"), which suggests pronunciation. This pairing highlights the action of using one's feet to move rapidly.
- **examples:**
  - 他跑得很快，想( )赢(贏)得( )比( )赛(賽)。
    - Tā pǎo dé hěn kuài, xiǎng yíngdé bǐsài.
    - He runs very fast, hoping to win the race.
  - 孩( )子( )们(們)在( )操( )场(場)上( )跑( )。
    - Háizimen zài cāochǎng shàng pǎo.
    - The children are running on the playground.
  - 危( )险(險)来(來)临(臨)时(時)，他迅速跑向出口。
    - Wēixiǎn láilín shí, tā xùnsù pǎo xiàng chūkǒu.
    - When danger approaches, he quickly runs toward the exit.
### 跑步 → 跑 → 足
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zú
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a leg ->
    - originally depicted as ⽦ ->
    - represents 'foot'
  - **interpretation:** The character 足(zú, 'foot') is a pictographic representation of a human leg, which directly portrays the concept of a 'foot'. The pictographic origin emphasizes its fundamental meaning in the language.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)后(後)，他( )的( )足( )不( )能( )动(動)。
    - tā shòushāng hòu, tā de zú bù néng dòng。
    - After he was injured, his foot couldn't move.
  - 小( )明( )打( )球( )时(時)，不( )小( )心( )扭( )伤(傷)了( )足( )。
    - xiǎo míng dǎ qiú shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ shāng le zú。
    - When Xiaoming was playing ball, he accidentally twisted his foot.
  - 我( )每( )天( )锻(鍛)炼(鍊)足( )部( )。
    - wǒ měitiān duànliàn zúbù。
    - I exercise my foot muscles every day.
### 跑步 → 跑 → 包
- **definition:** wrap
- **pinyin:** bāo
- **components:**
  - 巳
    - sì
    - fetus
  - 勹
    - bāo
    - wrap
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts fetus 巳 (sì, "fetus") in womb 勹 (bāo, "wrap") ->
    - swaddled baby ->
    - wrap
  - **interpretation:** The character 包(bāo) visually represents a fetus enclosed in the womb, akin to wrapping. This imagery conveys the concept of enveloping or wrapping something securely.
- **examples:**
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)正( )在( )包( )饺(餃)子( )。
    - māmā zhèngzài bāo jiǎozi.
    - Mom is wrapping dumplings.
  - 她( )学(學)习(習)如( )何( )包( )礼(禮)物( )，让(讓)它( )看( )起( )来(來)更( )漂( )亮( )。
    - tā xuéxí rúhé bāo lǐwù, ràng tā kàn qǐlái gèng piàoliang.
    - She learns how to wrap gifts to make them look more beautiful.
  - 厨(廚)师(師)熟( )练(練)地( )包( )春( )卷(捲)，确(確)保( )每( )一( )个(個)都( )紧(緊)紧(緊)地( )包( )着(著)。
    - chúshī shúliàn de bāo chūnjuǎn, quèbǎo měi yīgè dōu jǐn jǐn de bāozhe.
    - The chef skillfully wraps spring rolls, ensuring each one is tightly wrapped.
### 跑步 → 跑 → 包 → 巳
- **definition:** fetus
- **pinyin:** sì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a fetus or snake.
  - **interpretation:** The character 巳(sì, "fetus") likely represents the shape of a fetus curled in the womb or possibly the sinuous form of a snake. This ambiguity is due to its pictographic origin, imitating the curves and forms seen in nature.
- **examples:**
  - 那小小的巳在( )温(溫)暖( )的( )子( )宫(宮)中( )悄( )然( )成( )长(長)。
    - Nà xiǎoxiǎo de sì zài wēnnuǎn de zǐgōng zhōng qiǎorán chéngzhǎng.
    - The tiny fetus is growing quietly in the warm womb.
  - 医(醫)生( )发(發)现(現)巳( )异(異)常( )，提( )醒( )家( )长(長)早( )期( )注( )意( )。
    - Yīshēng fāxiàn sì yìcháng, tíxǐng jiāzhǎng zǎoqī zhùyì.
    - The doctor found an abnormality in the fetus and urged the parents to pay early attention.
  - 母( )亲(親)感( )受( )到( )巳( )微( )弱( )的( )心( )跳( )伴( )随(隨)着(著)温(溫)柔( )的( )希( )望( )。
    - Mǔqīn gǎnshòudào sì wēiruò de xīntiào bànsuízhe wēnróu de xīwàng.
    - The mother felt the fetus's faint heartbeat accompanied by a gentle hope.
### 跑步 → 跑 → 包 → 勹
- **definition:** wrap
- **pinyin:** bāo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a person bending over
  - **interpretation:** The character 勹(bāo, "wrap") illustrates a person bending over, signifying an action of encircling or enclosing, which aligns well with meanings related to wrapping or containing.
- **examples:**
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)用( )柔( )软(軟)的( )布( )包( )住( )易( )碎( )品( )。
    - Māmā yòng róuruǎn de bù bāo zhù yì suìpǐn.
    - Mother wraps fragile items with soft cloth.
  - 他( )熟( )练(練)地( )把( )礼(禮)物( )包( )成( )漂( )亮( )的( )形( )状(狀)，让(讓)人( )感( )受( )到( )温(溫)情( )。
    - Tā shúliàn de bǎ lǐwù bāo chéng piàoliang de xíngzhuàng, ràng rén gǎnshòu dào wēnqíng.
    - He skillfully wraps the gift into a beautiful shape, letting people feel warmth.
### 跑步 → 步
- **definition:** step; pace
- **pinyin:** bù
- **components:**
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - foot
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 止 (zhǐ, "foot") facing forwards + 止 (zhǐ, "foot") reversed =
    - one foot in front of the other ->
    - step
  - **interpretation:** The character 步 (bù, "step") visually represents two feet positioned for walking, symbolizing the act of taking a step. This ideogrammatic compound effectively conveys movement through the arrangement of its components.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )迈(邁)出( )坚(堅)实(實)的( )一( )步( )。
    - Tā mài chū jiānshí de yī bù.
    - He took a sturdy step.
  - 他( )缓(緩)缓(緩)走( )来(來)，每( )一( )步( )都( )充( )满(滿)期( )待( )。
    - Tā huǎn huǎn zǒu lái, měi yī bù dōu chōngmǎn qídài.
    - He came slowly, each step filled with anticipation.
  - 他( )走( )出( )一( )步( )一( )个(個)脚(腳)印( )。
    - Tā zǒu chū yī bù yī gè jiǎoyìn.
    - He progressed step by step.
### 跑步 → 步 → 止
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zhǐ
- **components:**
  - 趾
    - zhǐ
    - foot
  - 龰
    - yù
    - stylized feet
  - 手
    - shǒu
    - hand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Original: Foot pointing up with 3 toes and a sole ->
    - Stylized as 龰 ->
    - Derivative 趾 refers to original 'foot' meaning
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicting a footprint with toes and a sole, 止 (zhǐ, "foot") represents the foot. The character's form resembles a foot with three toes, analogous to how the hand is represented with fingers in other characters.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)的( )止( )需( )要( )静(靜)养(養)。
    - Tā shòu shāng de zhǐ xū yào jìng yǎng.
    - His injured foot needs adequate rest.
  - 他( )走( )路( )时(時)小( )心( )谨(謹)慎( )，因( )为(爲)止( )若( )受( )伤(傷)便( )难(難)前( )行( )。
    - Tā zǒu lù shí xiǎo xīn jǐn shèn, yīn wèi zhǐ ruò shòu shāng biàn nán qián xíng.
    - He walks carefully because if his foot gets injured, it becomes difficult to move forward.
### 跑步 → 步 → 止 → 趾
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zhǐ
- **components:**
  - 足
    - zú
    - foot
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - stop
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 足 (zú, "foot") + 止 (zhǐ, "stop") =
    - semantic: foot + phonetic: stop ->
    - toe
  - **interpretation:** The character combines 足 (zú, "foot") as the semantic indicator and 止 (zhǐ, "stop") as the phonetic component to represent the idea of a 'toe,' which is a part of the foot.
- **examples:**
  - 他的趾因寒冷而僵硬了。
    - tā de zhǐ yīn hánlěng ér jiāngyìng le.
    - His toe became stiff due to the cold.
  - 我( )走( )路( )时(時)，不小心扭到了趾。
    - wǒ zǒulù shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ dào le zhǐ.
    - While I was walking, I accidentally twisted my toe.
  - 古( )代( )剑(劍)客( )战(戰)斗(鬥)时(時)，常( )因( )疏( )忽( )伤(傷)及( )趾( )。
    - gǔdài jiànkè zhàndòu shí, cháng yīn shūhū shāng jí zhǐ.
    - Ancient swordsmen often injured their toes due to carelessness during battles.
### 跑步 → 步 → 止 → 趾 → 足
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zú
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a leg ->
    - originally depicted as ⽦ ->
    - represents 'foot'
  - **interpretation:** The character 足(zú, 'foot') is a pictographic representation of a human leg, which directly portrays the concept of a 'foot'. The pictographic origin emphasizes its fundamental meaning in the language.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)后(後)，他( )的( )足( )不( )能( )动(動)。
    - tā shòushāng hòu, tā de zú bù néng dòng。
    - After he was injured, his foot couldn't move.
  - 小( )明( )打( )球( )时(時)，不( )小( )心( )扭( )伤(傷)了( )足( )。
    - xiǎo míng dǎ qiú shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ shāng le zú。
    - When Xiaoming was playing ball, he accidentally twisted his foot.
  - 我( )每( )天( )锻(鍛)炼(鍊)足( )部( )。
    - wǒ měitiān duànliàn zúbù。
    - I exercise my foot muscles every day.
### 跑步 → 步 → 止 → 龰
- **definition:** stylized feet
- **pinyin:** yù
- **components:**
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - foot
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Stylized footprint pointing up ->
    - simplified from original foot depiction in 止
  - **interpretation:** 龰 represents a stylized version of a footprint pointing upwards, originating from the character 止 (zhǐ, "foot") which initially depicted a footprint. This visual form was later abstracted and simplified to 龰.
### 跑步 → 步 → 止 → 手
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a hand with fingers; top stroke is a bent over middle finger, horizontal strokes represent fingers
  - **interpretation:** The character 手 (shǒu, "hand") is a direct pictorial representation of a human hand with all five fingers, which distinguishes it from similar characters representing claws or simplified fingers. This visually conveys its meaning as a hand clearly.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )手( )写(寫)字( )。
    - Tā yòng shǒu xiě zì.
    - He writes with his hand.
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)牵(牽)着(著)我( )的( )手( )走( )路( )。
    - Māmā qiān zhe wǒ de shǒu zǒu lù.
    - Mom holds my hand while walking.
  - 他( )的( )手( )很( )温(溫)暖( )，带(帶)给(給)我( )力( )量( )。
    - Tā de shǒu hěn wēn nuǎn, dài gěi wǒ lìliàng.
    - His hand is very warm, giving me strength.
