## 医(醫)生( )
---
## doctor
### yīshēng
---
- **traditional:** 醫生
- **definition:** doctor
- **pinyin:** yīshēng
- **components:**
  - 医(醫)
    - yī
    - medical; heal; doctor
  - 生
    - shēng
    - life; living; person; student
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 医(醫) (yī, "medical") refers to healing or medicine, while 生 (shēng, "person") typically denotes a person or someone who is involved in a particular activity.
  - **interpretation:** An 医(醫)生( ) (yīshēng, "doctor") is a person involved in the practice of medicine and healing.
  - **simplification:** 医 is simplified from 醫.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )是( )一( )位( )医(醫)生( )。
    - Tā shì yī wèi yīshēng.
    - He is a doctor.
  - 病( )人( )来(來)到( )医(醫)院( )，医(醫)生( )耐( )心( )问(問)诊(診)。
    - Bìngrén lái dào yīyuàn, yīshēng nàixīn wènzhěn.
    - The patient came to the hospital, and the doctor patiently examined him.
  - 遇( )到( )紧(緊)急( )情( )况(況)时(時)，医(醫)生( )总(總)是( )第( )一( )时(時)间(間)出( )现(現)。
    - Yù dào jǐnjí qíngkuàng shí, yīshēng zǒng shì dì yī shíjiān chūxiàn.
    - In emergencies, the doctor always shows up first.
### 医(醫)生( ) → 医(醫)
- **traditional:** 醫
- **definition:** medical; heal; doctor
- **pinyin:** yī
- **components:**
  - 匸
    - xì
    - that which hides; that which stores
  - 矢
    - shǐ
    - arrow
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 匸 (xì, "that which hides; that which stores") + 矢 (shǐ, "arrow") =
    - hidden arrow ->
    - healing
  - **interpretation:** The character 医(醫) (yī, "medical") represents the concept of healing by depicting a hidden arrow. The idea is related to removing or addressing the harmful 'arrow' that might metaphorically cause illness, aligning with the role of a doctor to treat and heal.
  - **simplification:** The traditional form 醫 retains related components, while 医 (yī, "medical") simplifies these using 匸 to maintain the core semantic elements related to healing.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )生( )病( )时(時)，去( )看( )了( )医(醫)生( )。
    - Tā shēngbìng shí, qù kàn le yīshēng.
    - When he got sick, he went to see a doctor.
  - 她( )立( )志( )成( )为(為)名( )医(醫)，帮(幫)助( )病( )人( )康( )复(復)。
    - Tā lìzhì chéngwéi míngyī, bāngzhù bìngrén kāngfù.
    - She is determined to become a renowned doctor and help patients recover.
### 医(醫)生( ) → 医(醫) → 匸
- **definition:** that which hides; that which stores
- **pinyin:** xì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 𠃊 (symbolic graphic) ->
    - depicts an enclosure hiding something or someone ->
    - evolved to represent a concealed space or item
  - **interpretation:** The character 匸(xì, 'box') is a pictogram representing a concealed or enclosed space. Its graphical form suggests something contained or hidden, which aligns with its meaning as a 'box' or an enclosure. Unlike other related pictograms that depict physical containers, 匸 emphasizes the aspect of concealment or secrecy.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )把( )匸( )搬( )到( )了( )房( )间(間)角( )落( )。
    - Tā bǎ xì bān dào le fángjiān jiǎoluò.
    - He moved the box to the corner of the room.
  - 我( )们(們)在( )仓(倉)库(庫)找( )到( )了( )匸( )，里(裡)面( )装(裝)有( )秘( )密( )文( )件( )。
    - Wǒmen zài cāngkù zhǎodào le xì, lǐmiàn zhuāng yǒu mìmì wénjiàn.
    - We found the box in the warehouse, and it contained secret documents.
  - 工( )厂(廠)工( )人( )把( )旧(舊)匸( )清( )理( )了( )，然( )后(後)再( )利( )用( )。
    - Gōngchǎng gōngrén bǎ jiù xì qīnglǐ le, ránhòu zài lìyòng.
    - Factory workers cleaned the old box and then reused it.
### 医(醫)生( ) → 医(醫) → 矢
- **definition:** arrow
- **pinyin:** shǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an arrow
  - **interpretation:** The character 矢(shǐ, 'arrow') is a pictogram representing an arrow. Its resemblance to arrows was more evident in ancient script forms.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )拉( )开(開)弓( )，射出一支矢。
    - Tā lā kāi gōng, shè chū yī zhī shǐ.
    - He drew his bow and released an arrow.
  - 古( )代( )战(戰)士( )用( )矢( )，攻( )破( )敌(敵)阵(陣)。
    - Gǔdài zhànshì yòng shǐ, gōng pò dí zhèn.
    - Ancient warriors used arrows to breach enemy formations.
### 医(醫)生( ) → 生
- **definition:** life; living; person; student
- **pinyin:** shēng
- **components:**
  - 屮
    - chè
    - sprout
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 屮 (chè, "sprout") on top of a horizontal line ->
    - signifies a plant sprouting from the ground ->
    - symbolizes life
  - **interpretation:** The character 生 (shēng, "life") represents the concept of life force or vitality, symbolized by a sprout emerging from the earth, embodying growth and renewal.
- **examples:**
  - 生( )命( )是( )一( )场(場)美( )丽(麗)的( )旅( )程( )。
    - Shēngmìng shì yī chǎng měilì de lǚchéng.
    - Life is a beautiful journey.
  - 他( )在( )战(戰)场(場)上( )奋(奮)力( )生( )存( )。
    - Tā zài zhànchǎng shàng fènlì shēngcún.
    - He fights to live on the battlefield.
  - 这(這)家( )餐( )厅(廳)的( )生( )鱼(魚)片( )非( )常( )新( )鲜(鮮)。
    - Zhè jiā cāntīng de shēngyúpiàn fēicháng xīnxiān.
    - The raw fish slices at this restaurant are very fresh.
### 医(醫)生( ) → 生 → 屮
- **definition:** sprout
- **pinyin:** chè
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a sprout emerging from the ground
  - **interpretation:** 屮 (chè, 'sprout') is a pictogram that directly represents a young plant or sprout emerging from the ground. Its visual form captures the essence of new growth, and it historically served as an early representation of plant life or new vegetation.
- **examples:**
  - 春( )雨( )滋( )润(潤)后(後)，小( )屮( )冲(沖)破( )泥( )土( )而( )出( )。
    - chūn yǔ zī rùn hòu, xiǎo chè chōng pò ní tǔ ér chū.
    - After the spring rain nurtured the earth, the little sprout broke through the soil.
  - 草屮初生露尖端。
    - cǎo chè chū shēng lù jiān duān.
    - The grass sprout's tip has just emerged.

### yīshēng

## 贤(賢)
---
## virtuous; worthy
### xián
---
- **traditional:** 賢
- **definition:** virtuous; worthy
- **pinyin:** xián
- **components:**
  - 貝
    - bèi
    - cowry
  - 臤
    - xián
    - strong
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 貝 (bèi, "cowry") + phonetic: 臤 (xián, associated with "strong") =
    - wealth managed by a capable person ->
    - virtuous
  - **interpretation:** In ancient times, virtue or worthiness was often correlated with the ability to manage wealth or valuable resources effectively. The 貝 (bèi, "cowry") indicates value or resources, while 臤 (xián) provides the pronunciation and hints at strength or capability, reinforcing the idea of a worthy individual.
  - **simplification:** The simplified form 贤(賢) retains the same phonetic component 臤 and semantic component 貝, making it a direct simplification without altering the structural formula or meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )为(為)人( )贤(賢)良( )。
    - Tā wéi rén xiánliáng.
    - He is virtuous.
  - 长(長)者( )推( )崇( )贤(賢)人( )，晚( )辈(輩)尊( )敬( )贤(賢)者( )。
    - Zhǎngzhě tuīchóng xiánrén, wǎnbiè zūnjìng xiánzhě.
    - Elders esteem the virtuous, and the younger generations respect the wise.
  - 君( )子( )常( )怀(懷)贤(賢)心( )。
    - Jūnzǐ cháng huái xiánxīn.
    - A gentleman always harbors a virtuous heart.
### 贤(賢) → 貝
- **definition:** cowry
- **pinyin:** bèi
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a cowrie shell ->
    - used as currency in ancient China ->
    - stylized as 貝
  - **interpretation:** The character 貝(bèi, 'shell/money') visually represents a cowrie shell, which was used as a form of currency in ancient China. This explains why it is associated with money or valuables.
  - **simplification:** 贝 is a cursive script simplification of 貝, transforming the character into a more streamlined form while retaining its essence.
- **examples:**
  - 他把贝(貝)当作护(護)身( )符( )，放在口( )袋( )里(裡)。
    - Tā bǎ bèi dāng zuò hùshēn fú, fàng zài kǒudài lǐ.
    - He uses shells as a talisman, placing them in his pocket.
  - 古( )代( )人( )用( )贝(貝)作( )为(為)货(貨)币(幣)。
    - Gǔdài rén yòng bèi zuòwéi huòbì.
    - In ancient times, people used shells as currency.
### 贤(賢) → 臤
- **definition:** strong
- **pinyin:** xián
- **components:**
  - 臣
    - chén
    - subject; minister
  - 意
    - yì
    - meaning
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 臣 (chén, "subject; minister") + 意 (yì, "meaning") =
    - strong sense or principle ->
    - 臤
  - **interpretation:** The character 臤 (xián, "strong") combines elements related to being a subject or servant with meaning or intention, suggesting a sense of inner strength or principle.
### 贤(賢) → 臤 → 臣
- **definition:** subject; minister
- **pinyin:** chén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a vertical eye of a man looking downwards
  - **interpretation:** The character 臣(chén) originally depicted an eye oriented vertically, symbolizing a person looking downwards, conveying subordination or observance. Over time, it evolved to represent roles such as 'slave,' 'servant,' and 'minister,' emphasizing relationships defined by subservience.
- **examples:**
  - 臣( )愿(願)为(為)国(國)家( )尽(盡)忠( )。
    - Chén yuàn wèi guójiā jìnzhōng。
    - I, as a loyal subject, am willing to serve the nation faithfully.
  - 陛( )下( )召( )见(見)臣( )，臣必不辱使命。
    - Bìxià zhàojiàn chén, chén bì bù rǔ shǐmìng。
    - Your Majesty summons me, and I will not fail in my duty.
  - 昔( )日( )臣( )拜( )见(見)明( )君( )，如( )今( )仍( )怀(懷)赤( )诚(誠)之( )心( )。
    - Xīrì chén bàijiàn míngjūn, rújīn réng huái chìchéng zhī xīn。
    - In the past, I paid my respects to a wise ruler, and even now I remain sincerely devoted.
### 贤(賢) → 臤 → 意
- **definition:** meaning
- **pinyin:** yì
- **components:**
  - 音
    - yīn
    - sound
  - 心
    - xīn
    - heart
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 音 (yīn, 'sound') + 心 (xīn, 'heart') =
    - sound in the heart ->
    - concept of thought and intention
  - **interpretation:** The character 意(yì) represents abstract concepts of thought, intention, and meaning by combining elements symbolizing sound and heart, reflecting processed sensory input and emotional response.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )的( )意( )思( )很( )明( )确(確)。
    - Tā de yìsi hěn míngquè.
    - His meaning is clear.
  - 我( )有( )一( )个(個)好( )主( )意( )，想( )改( )变(變)现(現)状(狀)。
    - Wǒ yǒu yīgè hǎo zhǔyì, xiǎng gǎibiàn xiànzhuàng.
    - I have a good idea and want to change the current situation.
  - 他( )的( )用( )意( )并(並)非( )恶(惡)意( )。
    - Tā de yòngyì bìngfēi èyì.
    - His intention is not malicious.
### 贤(賢) → 臤 → 意 → 音
- **definition:** sound
- **pinyin:** yīn
- **components:**
  - 言
    - yán
    - word
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
  - 甘
    - gān
    - sweet
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Ideogram
  - **description:**
    - 言 (yán, "word") + something in 口 (kǒu, "mouth") written like 甘 =
    - sound coming from the mouth ->
    - vertical trait representing tongue disappeared ->
    - 甘 stylized as 曰 ->
    - final form
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicting words or sounds coming from the mouth with an implied symbolic depiction of the tongue, the character evolved by stylizing the components. The progression from 甘 to 曰 indicates a refinement in the depiction of sound production over time.
- **examples:**
  - 在( )合( )成( )汉(漢)字( )中( )，音( )决(決)定( )读(讀)音( )。
    - Zài héchéng hànzì zhōng, yīn juédìng dúyīn.
    - In compound characters, the phonetic component determines the pronunciation.
  - 这(這)个(個)字( )的( )音( )使( )我( )们(們)联(聯)想( )到( )原( )始( )发(發)音( )。
    - Zhège zì de yīn shǐ wǒmen liánxiǎng dào yuánshǐ fàyīn.
    - The phonetic component of this character reminds us of its original pronunciation.
### 贤(賢) → 臤 → 意 → 音 → 言
- **definition:** word
- **pinyin:** yán
- **components:**
  - 舌
    - shé
    - tongue
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Ideogram
  - **description:**
    - 舌 (shé, "tongue") with added horizontal stroke ->
    - indicates movement of tongue for speech ->
    - modern form adds another stroke on top
  - **interpretation:** The character 言(yán, "speech") visually emphasizes the movement of the tongue, illustrating how speech is produced through tongue actions. The additional stroke and evolution to a more abstract form highlight the facet of communication.
- **examples:**
  - 他的言令人信服。
    - Tā de yán lìng rén xìn fú.
    - His speech is convincing.
  - 他( )喜( )欢(歡)研( )究( )古( )人( )的( )言( )论(論)，并(並)整( )理( )成( )册(冊)。
    - Tā xǐhuān yánjiū gǔ rén de yánlùn, bìng zhěnglǐ chéng cè.
    - He likes to study the speeches of ancient people and compile them into volumes.
### 贤(賢) → 臤 → 意 → 音 → 言 → 舌
- **definition:** tongue
- **pinyin:** shé
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a forked tongue emerging upwards from a mouth
  - **interpretation:** The character 舌(shé, 'tongue') visually represents a tongue extending out from a mouth, which aligns with its meaning. This direct depiction makes it easy to remember its association with speech and taste in its semantic family, also relating to words like 言(yán, 'word').
- **examples:**
  - 他( )的( )舌( )非( )常( )灵(靈)敏( )。
    - Tā de shé fēicháng língmǐn.
    - His tongue is very sensitive.
  - 品( )尝(嚐)美( )食( )时(時)，他( )的( )舌( )细(細)品( )滋( )味( )。
    - Pǐncháng měishí shí, tā de shé xì pǐn zīwèi.
    - While tasting delicious food, his tongue savors every flavor.
  - 他( )长(長)舌( )无(無)忌( )，经(經)常( )泄( )露( )秘( )密( )。
    - Tā chángshé wújì, jīngcháng xièlòu mìmì.
    - He is talkative and often spills secrets.
### 贤(賢) → 臤 → 意 → 音 → 口
- **definition:** mouth
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.
### 贤(賢) → 臤 → 意 → 音 → 甘
- **definition:** sweet
- **pinyin:** gān
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a mouth with a sweet flavor
  - **interpretation:** The character 甘 (gān, 'sweet') visually represents the idea of sweetness being savored by the mouth. It's a straightforward depiction of tasting pleasure.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)果( )子( )很( )甘( )。
    - Zhège guǒzi hěn gān.
    - This fruit is sweet.
  - 这(這)种(種)酒( )甘( )而( )不( )腻(膩)，让(讓)人( )回( )味( )无(無)穷(窮)。
    - Zhè zhǒng jiǔ gān ér bù nì, ràng rén huí wèi wú qióng.
    - This wine is sweet without being cloying, leaving a lasting aftertaste.
### 贤(賢) → 臤 → 意 → 心
- **definition:** heart
- **pinyin:** xīn
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a stylized representation of a heart
  - **interpretation:** The character 心(xīn, 'heart') visually represents the shape of a heart, emphasizing its central and essential role in both a physical and metaphorical sense.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )的( )心( )温(溫)暖( )无(無)比( )。
    - Tā de xīn wēnnuǎn wúbǐ.
    - His heart is incredibly warm.
  - 她( )把( )心( )放( )在( )艺(藝)术(術)上( )，追求完美。
    - Tā bǎ xīn fàng zài yìshù shàng, zhuīqiú wánměi.
    - She dedicates her heart to art, pursuing perfection.
  - 音( )乐(樂)直( )击(擊)心( )灵(靈)。
    - Yīnyuè zhí jī xīnlíng.
    - Music strikes straight at the heart (soul).

## 一( )下( )儿(兒)
---
## a bit; for a moment
### yíxiàr
---
- **traditional:** 一下兒
- **definition:** a bit; for a moment
- **pinyin:** yíxiàr
- **components:**
  - 一
    - yī
    - one
  - 下
    - xià
    - down
  - 儿(兒)
    - ér
    - child; son; diminutive suffix
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 一(yī, "one") + 下(xià, "down") + 儿(兒) (ér, "child or diminutive suffix")
  - **interpretation:** 一( )下( )儿(兒) (yíxiàr) is commonly used in Mandarin to soften the tone of a request or action, indicating a short or quick action. The character 一(yī, "one") suggests singularity, and 下(xià, "down") often indicates a brief duration or a minor action, while 儿(兒) (ér, "child or diminutive suffix") adds an informal tone.
  - **simplification:** 儿 simplified to 儿
- **examples:**
  - 请(請)坐( )一( )下( )儿(兒)。
    - Qǐng zuò yíxiàr.
    - Please have a seat for a moment.
  - 我( )休( )息( )一( )下( )儿(兒)，再( )继(繼)续(續)工( )作( )。
    - Wǒ xiūxí yíxiàr, zài jìxù gōngzuò.
    - I'll rest for a moment and then continue working.
  - 你( )试(試)一( )下( )儿(兒)，看看效果如何。
    - Nǐ shì yíxiàr, kànkan xiàoguǒ rúhé.
    - Try it for a bit and see how it works.
### 一( )下( )儿(兒) → 一
- **definition:** one
- **pinyin:** yī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:** A horizontal stroke representing the concept of 'one'.
  - **interpretation:** The character 一(yī, "one") is a simple horizontal line signifying the singular and unified concept of 'one'. It directly represents being singular or the number one.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )有( )一( )个(個)苹(蘋)果( )。
    - Wǒ yǒu yī gè píngguǒ.
    - I have one apple.
  - 他( )只( )看( )到( )一( )只(隻)鸟(鳥)，飞(飛)进(進)了( )花( )园(園)。
    - Tā zhǐ kàn dào yī zhī niǎo, fēi jìn le huā yuán.
    - He only saw one bird, which flew into the garden.
  - 一( )条(條)直( )路( )通( )向( )远(遠)方( )。
    - Yī tiáo zhí lù tōng xiàng yuǎn fāng.
    - One straight road leads to the distance.
### 一( )下( )儿(兒) → 下
- **definition:** down
- **pinyin:** xià
- **components:**
  - 一
    - yī
    - one
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:**
    - 一 (yī, "one") below horizontal line =
    - below ->
    - down
  - **interpretation:** The character 下(xià, "down") visually represents something placed below another object. The simple representation of a line under a horizontal stroke symbolically conveys the concept of 'down' or 'below'.
- **examples:**
  - 请(請)你( )下( )来(來)。
    - Qǐng nǐ xiàlái.
    - Please come down.
  - 他( )走( )下( )楼(樓)梯( )，进(進)入( )房( )间(間)。
    - Tā zǒu xià lóutī, jìnrù fángjiān.
    - He walked down the stairs and entered the room.
  - 雨下得很大，街( )道( )上( )满(滿)是( )积(積)水( )。
    - Yǔ xià de hěn dà, jiēdào shàng mǎn shì jīshuǐ.
    - It rained heavily, and the streets were full of puddles.
### 一( )下( )儿(兒) → 下 → 一
- **definition:** one
- **pinyin:** yī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:** A horizontal stroke representing the concept of 'one'.
  - **interpretation:** The character 一(yī, "one") is a simple horizontal line signifying the singular and unified concept of 'one'. It directly represents being singular or the number one.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )有( )一( )个(個)苹(蘋)果( )。
    - Wǒ yǒu yī gè píngguǒ.
    - I have one apple.
  - 他( )只( )看( )到( )一( )只(隻)鸟(鳥)，飞(飛)进(進)了( )花( )园(園)。
    - Tā zhǐ kàn dào yī zhī niǎo, fēi jìn le huā yuán.
    - He only saw one bird, which flew into the garden.
  - 一( )条(條)直( )路( )通( )向( )远(遠)方( )。
    - Yī tiáo zhí lù tōng xiàng yuǎn fāng.
    - One straight road leads to the distance.
### 一( )下( )儿(兒) → 儿(兒)
- **traditional:** 兒
- **definition:** child; son; diminutive suffix
- **pinyin:** ér
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a person standing upright, showing legs
  - **interpretation:** The character 儿(兒) (ér) originally represents a stylized depiction of a person, particularly emphasizing the legs as if they are walking or standing. This provides a visual representation of the concept 'person'.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )是( )个(個)勇( )敢( )的( )儿(兒)。
    - Tā shì gè yǒnggǎn de ér.
    - He is a brave person.
  - 村( )里( )有( )一( )个(個)勤( )劳(勞)的( )儿(兒)，总(總)帮(幫)助( )邻(鄰)居( )解( )决(決)问(問)题(題)。
    - Cūn lǐ yǒu yī gè qínláo de ér, zǒng bāngzhù línjū jiějué wèntí.
    - There is a hardworking person in the village, who always helps neighbors solve problems.
  - 古( )城( )中( )流( )传(傳)着(著)一( )位( )智( )者( )儿(兒)，他( )的( )故( )事( )激( )励(勵)后(後)人( )。
    - Gǔ chéng zhōng liúchuán zhe yī wèi zhìzhě de ér, tā de gùshì jīlì hòurén.
    - In the ancient city, the story of a wise person is passed down, and his tale inspires future generations.

### yíxiàr

## 小妹妹
---
## little sister; young girl
### xiǎomèimei
---
- **definition:** little sister; young girl
- **pinyin:** xiǎomèimei
- **components:**
  - 小
    - xiǎo
    - small; little
  - 妹妹
    - mèimei
    - younger sister; young girl
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 小 (xiǎo, "small") denotes something small or little; 妹妹 (mèimei, "younger sister") refers to a younger sister or a young girl. Together, 小妹妹 (xiǎomèimei) can mean 'little sister' or 'young girl', indicating youth or affection.
  - **interpretation:** The combination of 小 (xiǎo, "small") and 妹妹 (mèimei, "younger sister") creates a term of endearment or familiarity, often used to refer to a young girl or to address a younger sister.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )的( )小( )妹( )妹( )很( )可( )爱(愛)。
    - Wǒ de xiǎomèimei hěn kě'ài.
    - My little sister is very cute.
  - 小( )妹( )妹( )正( )在( )学(學)习(習)，期( )待( )表( )现(現)出( )色( )。
    - Xiǎomèimei zhèngzài xuéxí, qīdài biǎoxiàn chūsè.
    - The little girl is studying and is eager to perform excellently.
  - 在( )游(遊)乐(樂)场(場)遇( )见(見)了( )小( )妹( )妹( )，大( )家( )都( )很( )开(開)心( )。
    - Zài yóulèchǎng yùjiànle xiǎomèimei, dàjiā dōu hěn kāixīn.
    - We met the little girl at the amusement park, and everyone was very happy.
### 小妹妹 → 小
- **definition:** small; little
- **pinyin:** xiǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts three small dots arranged symmetrically to represent something small
  - **interpretation:** The character 小(xiǎo, 'small') uses three small strokes to symbolize size or quantity, visually capturing the essence of 'smallness'.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)小( )猫(貓)很( )可( )爱(愛)。
    - Zhège xiǎo māo hěn kě'ài.
    - This small cat is very cute.
  - 这(這)座( )小( )桥(橋)横(橫)跨( )河( )流( )，连(連)接( )两(兩)岸( )。
    - Zhè zuò xiǎo qiáo héngkuà héliú, liánjiē liǎng àn.
    - This small bridge spans the river, connecting both banks.
  - 请(請)给(給)我( )一( )小( )杯( )水( )。
    - Qǐng gěi wǒ yī xiǎo bēi shuǐ.
    - Please give me a small cup of water.
### 小妹妹 → 妹妹
- **definition:** younger sister; young girl
- **pinyin:** mèimei
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 妹 (mèi, "younger sister") + 妹 (mèi, "younger sister") =
    - repetition for emphasis or endearment, referring collectively to younger sisters or a young girl
  - **interpretation:** 妹妹 (mèimei) combines 妹 (mèi, "younger sister") twice to emphasize the idea of a younger female sibling, often used affectionately. The repetition not only reinforces the familial relationship but can also imply endearment or a familial role, similar to saying 'little sister' or affectionately addressing a young girl.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )的( )妹( )妹( )很( )可( )爱(愛)。
    - Wǒ de mèimei hěn kě'ài.
    - My younger sister is very cute.
  - 我陪妹妹玩耍，她( )笑( )得( )很( )开(開)心( )。
    - Wǒ péi mèimei wánshuǎ, tā xiào de hěn kāixīn.
    - I play with my younger sister, and she laughs happily.
  - 妹( )妹( )在( )花( )园(園)里(裡)采(採)花( )。
    - Mèimei zài huāyuán lǐ cǎihuā.
    - My younger sister picks flowers in the garden.
### 小妹妹 → 妹妹 → 妹
- **definition:** younger sister; sister
- **pinyin:** mèi
- **components:**
  - 女
    - nǚ
    - female, woman
  - 未
    - mèi
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 女 (nǚ, "female, woman") + phonetic 未 (mèi) ->
    - female sibling ->
    - younger sister
  - **interpretation:** 妹 (mèi, "younger sister") combines 女 (nǚ, "female, woman") to indicate gender, with 未 (mèi) as a phonetic component to guide pronunciation. This reflects the traditional familial structure where siblings are often distinguished by relative age.
- **examples:**
  - 我有妹妹。
    - Wǒ yǒu mèimei.
    - I have a younger sister.
  - 昨( )天( )见(見)到( )妹( )妹( )，她( )笑( )得( )很( )开(開)心( )。
    - Zuótiān jiàndào mèimei, tā xiào dé hěn kāixīn.
    - I saw my younger sister yesterday, and she was smiling happily.
  - 我( )的( )妹( )妹( )喜( )欢(歡)画(畫)画(畫)，她( )梦(夢)想( )成( )为(為)艺(藝)术(術)家( )。
    - Wǒ de mèimei xǐhuān huàhuà, tā mèngxiǎng chéngwéi yìshùjiā.
    - My younger sister loves painting, and she dreams of becoming an artist.
### 小妹妹 → 妹妹 → 妹 → 女
- **definition:** female, woman
- **pinyin:** nǚ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a woman kneeling or standing ->
    - enclosed area as remnant of right breast
  - **interpretation:** The character 女(nǚ, "woman") is a pictogram originally depicting a woman with highlighted bodily features, such as the breasts, symbolizing femininity. Its orientation changed from the Oracle Bone script to face right, simplifying over time while still retaining the essence of its original depiction.
- **examples:**
  - 那( )位( )才( )华(華)横(橫)溢( )的( )女( )，赢(贏)得( )了( )全( )场(場)注( )目( )。
    - Nà wèi cáihuá héngyì de nǚ, yíng dé le quánchǎng zhùmù.
    - That talented woman won everyone's attention.
  - 古时候‘女’常被用来象征温柔(古時候‘女’常被用來象徵溫柔)，而( )如( )今( )它( )代( )表( )独(獨)立( )自( )信( )。
    - Gǔshíhòu ‘nǚ’ cháng bèi yòng lái xiàngzhēng wēnróu, ér rújīn tā dàibiǎo dúlì zìxìn.
    - In ancient times, the character '女' was often used to symbolize gentleness, whereas today it represents independence and confidence.
### 小妹妹 → 妹妹 → 妹 → 未
- **pinyin:** mèi
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a tree with vertical growth not yet reaching full maturity.
  - **interpretation:** The character 未(mèi) represents a tree that has not yet reached its full height, symbolizing the concept of 'not yet' or 'not yet fulfilled'. This pictorial representation highlights the state of incompleteness or being unfinished.
- **examples:**
  - 他未到家。
    - Tā mèi dào jiā.
    - He has not arrived home.
  - 我( )未( )看( )见(見)他( )，也( )未( )听(聽)到( )消( )息( )。
    - Wǒ mèi kànjiàn tā, yě mèi tīngdào xiāoxī.
    - I did not see him, nor did I hear any news.
  - 他( )未( )明( )白( )问(問)题(題)。
    - Tā mèi míngbái wèntí.
    - He did not understand the problem.

## 颜(顏)

## surname Yan
### Yán
---
- **traditional:** 顏
- **definition:** surname Yan
- **pinyin:** Yán
- **components:**
  - 頁
    - yè
    - leaf, page
  - 彥
    - yàn
    - accomplished person
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 頁 (yè, 'leaf, page') + phonetic: 彥 (yàn, 'accomplished person') ->
    - 顏(顏, 'face')
  - **interpretation:** The traditional character 顏 (yán, 'face, countenance') combines 頁 (yè, 'leaf, page') as the semantic component, representing the face, with 彥 (yàn, 'accomplished person') for its phonetic value, suggesting a distinguished appearance or expression. The elements relate to physical presence and facial expression.
  - **simplification:** The traditional components were simplified: 彥 (yàn, 'accomplished person') was simplified to 彦 and 頁 (yè, 'leaf, page') was simplified to 页, maintaining three of the original lines for resemblance.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )认(認)识(識)颜(顏)老( )师(師)。
    - Wǒ rènshi Yán lǎoshī.
    - I know Teacher Yan.
  - 颜(顏)先( )生( )家( )在( )北( )京( )，他( )的( )家( )族( )历(歷)史( )悠( )久( )。
    - Yán xiānsheng jiā zài Běijīng, tā de jiāzú lìshǐ yōujiǔ.
    - Mr. Yan's home is in Beijing, and his family history is long.
### 颜(顏) → 頁
- **definition:** leaf, page
- **pinyin:** yè
- **components:**
  - 首
    - shǒu
    - head
  - 卩
    - jié
    - kneeling person
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 首 (shǒu, "head") + 卩 (jié, "kneeling person") =
    - head of a kneeling person ->
    - emphasis on "head" ->
    - later used for "page"
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicting the head of a kneeling person, 頁(yè) was adapted to signify 'page' by association with leaves (葉). While its original sense of 'head' is retained in some radicals, its contemporary meaning as 'page' is more commonly recognized.
  - **simplification:** 頁 was simplified by adopting the cursive script version, resulting in 页. This process focused on reducing the complexity of the character while maintaining its phonetic resemblance.
- **examples:**
  - 书(書)的( )第( )一( )页(頁)上( )印( )有( )精( )美( )的( )插( )图(圖)。
    - Shū de dì yī yè shàng yìn yǒu jīng měi de chā tú.
    - The first page of the book is printed with exquisite illustrations.
  - 她( )认(認)真( )阅(閱)读(讀)每( )一( )頁( )，体(體)会(會)作( )者( )的( )用( )意( )。
    - Tā rèn zhēn yuè dú měi yī yè, tǐ huì zuò zhě de yòng yì.
    - She carefully reads every page to appreciate the author's intentions.
  - 翻( )开(開)这(這)几(幾)頁( )，你( )将(將)发(發)现(現)一( )个(個)新( )世( )界( )。
    - Fān kāi zhè jǐ yè, nǐ jiāng fā xiàn yī gè xīn shì jiè.
    - Turn over these pages, and you will discover a new world.
### 颜(顏) → 頁 → 首
- **definition:** head
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a head with the stylized representation of eyes and hair
  - **interpretation:** The character 首 (shǒu, 'head') depicts a head, with the top portion representing hair and a central line suggesting a facial feature, effectively conveying the concept of a head.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )昂( )首( )挺( )胸( )走( )上( )领(領)奖(獎)台( )。
    - Tā áng shǒu tǐng xiōng zǒu shàng lǐng jiǎng tái.
    - He walked onto the podium with his head held high.
  - 受( )伤(傷)后(後)，他( )低( )下( )首( )表( )示( )谦(謙)逊(遜)。
    - Shòu shāng hòu, tā dī xià shǒu biǎo shì qiān xùn.
    - After being injured, he lowered his head to show humility.
  - 他( )被( )选(選)为(為)公( )司( )的( )首( )席( )执(執)行( )官( )。
    - Tā bèi xuǎn wéi gōng sī de shǒu xí zhí xíng guān.
    - He was elected as the company's chief executive officer.
### 颜(顏) → 頁 → 卩
- **definition:** kneeling person
- **pinyin:** jié
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a kneeling person from sideview
  - **interpretation:** The character 卩 (jié, "kneeling person") is a simple pictogram showing the silhouette of a person kneeling. It is unrelated to other kneeling or similar figures in different contexts such as 欠 (qiàn, "yawn") where the mouth is open, or 女 (nǚ, "woman") where the figure represents a female. Its form is solely based on the posture of kneeling.
- **examples:**
  - 那( )位( )卩( )正( )虔( )诚(誠)祈( )祷(禱)。
    - Nà wèi jié zhèng qiánchéng qídǎo.
    - That kneeling person is praying devoutly.
  - 礼(禮)仪(儀)师(師)指( )出( )，卩( )代( )表( )谦(謙)卑( )与(與)敬( )意( )。
    - Lǐyí shī zhǐchū, jié dàibiǎo qiānbēi yǔ jìngyì.
    - The ritual master pointed out that the kneeling person represents humility and respect.
  - 在( )庄(莊)严(嚴)仪(儀)式( )中( )，卩( )象( )征(徵)着(著)内(內)心( )的( )虔( )敬( )。
    - Zài zhuāngyán yíshì zhōng, jié xiàngzhēngzhe nèixīn de qiánjìng.
    - In the solemn ceremony, the kneeling figure symbolizes inner devotion.
### 颜(顏) → 彥
- **definition:** accomplished person
- **pinyin:** yàn
- **components:**
  - 厂
    - hǎn
    - cliff
  - 彣
    - wén
    - variegated; literary talent
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - phonetic: 厂 (hǎn) + semantic: 彣 (wén, "variegated; literary talent") =
    - represents someone with literary or scholarly talent ->
    - talented
  - **interpretation:** The character combines the phonetic element 厂 (hǎn) with the semantic 彣 (wén, "literary talent"), highlighting the esteemed nature of the talent indicated. It suggests a person skilled or knowledgeable in literature.
- **examples:**
  - 他是一位才華橫溢的彥。
    - Tā shì yī wèi cái huá héng yì de yàn.
    - He is a person of exceptional talent.
  - 在科技界中有一位彥展露非凡天賦，他的創新理念令人耳目一新。
    - Zài kē jì jiè zhōng yǒu yī wèi yàn zhǎn lù fēi fán tiān fù, tā de chuàng xīn lǐ niàn lìng rén ěr mù yī xīn.
    - In the world of technology, there is a talented individual demonstrating extraordinary abilities, and his innovative ideas are truly refreshing.
### 颜(顏) → 彥 → 厂(廠)
- **traditional:** 廠
- **definition:** cliff
- **pinyin:** hǎn
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a cliff or overhang
  - **interpretation:** The character 厂(hǎn, 'cliff') originally depicts a cliff or overhang, representing the natural landscape feature. This simple depiction efficiently conveys its meaning as a pictograph, illustrating the sloping edge and steepness of a cliff.
  - **simplification:** 厂(hǎn, 'factory') is the simplified form of 廠, where the character was reduced to its more basic pictogram form for modern usage, representing both 'cliff' and 'factory'.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )站( )在( )高( )厂( )边(邊)，凝( )视(視)远(遠)处(處)海( )浪( )。
    - Tā zhàn zài gāo hǎn biān, níng shì yuǎn chù hǎi làng.
    - He stands at the edge of the high cliff, gazing at the distant waves.
  - 我( )们(們)攀( )登( )陡( )厂( )壁( )，体(體)验(驗)大( )自( )然( )壮(壯)观(觀)。
    - Wǒmen pān dēng dǒu hǎn bì, tǐ yàn dà zì rán zhuàng guān.
    - We climbed the steep cliff face, experiencing the grandeur of nature.
### 颜(顏) → 彥 → 彣
- **definition:** variegated; literary talent
- **pinyin:** wén
- **components:**
  - 文
    - wén
    - literary
  - 彡
    - shān
    - decorative lines
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phonetic: 文 (wén, "literary") + Semantic: 彡 ("decorative lines") =
    - variegated; literary talent
  - **interpretation:** The character 彣(wén) combines 文(wén, "literary"), suggesting its phonetic and literary aspect, with 彡("decorative lines"), highlighting its connection to ornamental detail. This signifies an association with variegation or literary decoration.
### 颜(顏) → 彥 → 彣 → 文
- **definition:** literary
- **pinyin:** wén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a crested animal pattern, representing patterns of writing.
  - **interpretation:** 文 (wén, 'language; culture') originally depicted a symbol or pattern on objects, which is closely related to text and writing as forms of patterned representation, embodying cultural expression.
- **examples:**
  - 他研究古文。
    - Tā yánjiū gǔwén.
    - He studies classical literature.
  - 我( )们(們)的( )文( )化( )深( )厚( )，文( )学(學)绵(綿)长(長)悠( )远(遠)。
    - Wǒmen de wénhuà shēnhòu, wénxué miáncháng yōuyuǎn.
    - Our culture is profound, and our literature is long and far-reaching.
  - 书(書)中( )蕴(蘊)含( )丰(豐)厚( )的( )文( )采( )。
    - Shū zhōng yùnhán fēnghòu de wéncǎi.
    - The book is imbued with rich literary elegance.
### 颜(顏) → 彥 → 彣 → 彡
- **definition:** decorative lines
- **pinyin:** shān
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts three strands of hair ->
    - used to indicate three light rays in certain contexts
  - **interpretation:** 彡(shān, "rays") originally depicted strands of hair but also came to represent light rays, reflecting a change in symbolic meaning over time. Its use as a representation of 'ornament' and in characters like 昜 and 參 highlights its versatility in indicating rays or decorative elements.
- **examples:**
  - 天( )空( )洒(灑)下( )金( )色( )的( )彡( )，大( )地( )迎( )来(來)温(溫)暖( )。
    - Tiānkōng sǎ xià jīnsè de shān, dàdì yíng lái wēnnuǎn.
    - The sky showers golden rays, and the earth welcomes warmth.
  - 晨( )曦( )中( )的( )彡( )闪(閃)耀( )着(著)希( )望( )。
    - Chénxī zhōng de shān shǎnyào zhe xīwàng.
    - The rays in the morning light shine with hope.

## 潇(瀟)
---
## deep and clear (literary); brisk
### xiāo
---
- **traditional:** 瀟
- **definition:** deep and clear (literary); brisk
- **pinyin:** xiāo
- **components:**
  - 水
    - shuǐ
    - water
  - 萧(蕭)
    - xiāo
    - rustling sound
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 水 (shuǐ, "water") + 蕭(萧) (xiāo, "rustling sound/ph) =
    - clear water characteristics ->
    - deep and clear water
  - **interpretation:** The character 潇 (瀟) combines 水 (shuǐ, "water") for its semantic component, indicating a relationship with water, with the phonetic 蕭(萧) (xiāo, "rustling sound/ph)" to convey the sound of flowing water. This reflects the ideas of clarity and briskness associated with moving water.
  - **simplification:** The character 潇 is the simplified form of 瀟, retaining the semantic 水 (shuǐ, "water") component while simplifying the complex 蕭(萧) (xiāo, "rustling sound/ph)" component by replacing it with the simpler 萧 (xiāo, "rustling sound/ph)", retaining its phonetic properties.
- **examples:**
  - 秋( )风(風)潇(瀟)洒(灑)。
    - qiū fēng xiāo sǎ
    - The autumn wind is brisk and elegant.
  - 他( )的( )步( )伐( )潇(瀟)洒(灑)，眼( )神( )深( )远(遠)。
    - tā de bùfá xiāo sǎ, yǎnshén shēnyuǎn
    - His stride is brisk and relaxed, and his gaze is profound.
  - 湖( )面( )波( )光( )潇(瀟)洒(灑)，映( )出( )天( )际(際)长(長)空( )。
    - húmiàn bōguāng xiāo sǎ, yìng chū tiānjì chángkōng
    - The shimmering waves on the lake reflect the vast, clear sky.
### 潇(瀟) → 水
- **definition:** water
- **pinyin:** shuǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts flowing water
  - **interpretation:** 水 (shuǐ, 'water') is a pictogram that directly represents the appearance of flowing water, making it intuitive and easy to recognize. Its simplicity and direct visual reference to its meaning have allowed it to remain unchanged over millennia.
- **examples:**
  - 水( )是( )一( )种(種)生( )命( )之( )源( )。
    - Shuǐ shì yī zhǒng shēngmìng zhī yuán.
    - Water is the source of life.
  - 夏( )天( )很( )热(熱)，喝水能解暑。
    - Xiàtiān hěn rè, hē shuǐ néng jiě shǔ.
    - It is very hot in summer, drinking water can relieve the heat.
  - 河水潺潺，沿( )岸( )绿(綠)树(樹)成( )荫(蔭)。
    - Hé shuǐ chánchán, yán'àn lǜshù chéng yīn.
    - The river flows babbling, and green trees along the bank provide shade.
### 潇(瀟) → 萧(蕭)
- **traditional:** 蕭
- **definition:** rustling sound
- **pinyin:** xiāo
- **components:**
  - 艹
    - cǎo
    - grass
  - 肖
    - xiāo
    - resemble
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 艹 (cǎo, "grass") + phonetic: 肖 (xiāo, "resemble") =
    - grass rustling sound ->
    - rustling sound
  - **interpretation:** 萧(蕭) (xiāo) combines the grass radical 艹 (cǎo, "grass") with the phonetic 肖 (xiāo, "resemble") to signify the sound of wind rustling through grass. This character effectively conveys the auditory imagery associated with wind passing through vegetation.
  - **simplification:** 蕭→萧(蕭): The component 肖 (xiāo, "resemble") directly replaced 簫(箫) (xiāo, "end-blown flute") for simplicity, focusing on the phonetic aspect.
- **examples:**
  - 夜( )风(風)吹( )过(過)竹( )林( )时(時)，传(傳)来(來)阵(陣)阵(陣)萧(蕭)声(聲)。
    - Yè fēng chuī guò zhúlín shí, chuán lái zhèn zhèn xiāo shēng.
    - When the night breeze blows through the bamboo grove, a rustling sound is heard.
  - 落( )叶(葉)纷(紛)飞(飛)时(時)，耳( )边(邊)回(迴)响(響)着(著)萧(蕭)的( )低( )语(語)。
    - Luò yè fēn fēi shí, ěr biān huí xiǎng zhe xiāo de dī yǔ.
    - As the leaves flutter down, a rustling whisper echoes by the ear.
### 潇(瀟) → 萧(蕭) → 艹
- **definition:** grass
- **pinyin:** cǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts blades of grass
  - **interpretation:** 艹 is a pictogram representing grass or plants, resembling blades of grass. It is commonly used as a radical in other characters related to grass or vegetation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )写(寫)字( )时(時)用( )艹( )作( )草( )字( )的( )偏( )旁( )。
    - Tā xiězì shí yòng cǎo zuò cǎozì de piānpáng.
    - He uses '艹' as the radical for grass in his characters.
  - 春( )天( )到( )来(來)时(時)，艹( )生( )长(長)得( )很( )快( )。
    - Chūntiān dàolái shí, cǎo shēngzhǎng de hěn kuài.
    - When spring arrives, grass grows rapidly.
### 潇(瀟) → 萧(蕭) → 肖
- **definition:** resemble
- **pinyin:** xiāo
- **components:**
  - 小
    - xiǎo
    - small
  - 月
    - yuè
    - moon/flesh
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Semantic: 小 (xiǎo, "small") + Phonetic: 月 (yuè, "moon/flesh") =
    - small-like or resemble ->
    - resemblance
  - **interpretation:** 肖 (xiào, "resemble") combines 小 (xiǎo, "small") for resembling something in a smaller form, with 月 (yuè, "moon/flesh") acting as the phonetic component. This suggests a likeness or resemblance, much like something that resembles but is smaller or of similar form.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )长(長)得( )很( )肖( )他( )的( )父( )亲(親)。
    - Tā zhǎng de hěn xiào tā de fùqīn.
    - He greatly resembles his father.
  - 那( )幅( )画(畫)中( )的( )人( )物( )肖( )古( )代( )仕( )女( )，散( )发(發)着(著)艺(藝)术(術)的( )韵(韻)味( )。
    - Nà fú huà zhōng de rénwù xiào gǔdài shìnǚ, sànfā zhe yìshù de yùnwèi.
    - The figure in that painting resembles an ancient court lady and exudes artistic charm.
  - 她( )的( )笑( )容( )总(總)是( )与(與)花( )开(開)的( )季( )节(節)肖( )似( )，让(讓)人( )心( )情( )愉( )快( )。
    - Tā de xiàoróng zǒng shì yǔ huākāi de jìjié xiào sì, ràng rén xīnqíng yúkuài.
    - Her smile always resembles the blossoming season, uplifting people's moods.
### 潇(瀟) → 萧(蕭) → 肖 → 小
- **definition:** small
- **pinyin:** xiǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts three small dots arranged symmetrically to represent something small
  - **interpretation:** The character 小(xiǎo, 'small') uses three small strokes to symbolize size or quantity, visually capturing the essence of 'smallness'.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)小( )猫(貓)很( )可( )爱(愛)。
    - Zhège xiǎo māo hěn kě'ài.
    - This small cat is very cute.
  - 这(這)座( )小( )桥(橋)横(橫)跨( )河( )流( )，连(連)接( )两(兩)岸( )。
    - Zhè zuò xiǎo qiáo héngkuà héliú, liánjiē liǎng àn.
    - This small bridge spans the river, connecting both banks.
  - 请(請)给(給)我( )一( )小( )杯( )水( )。
    - Qǐng gěi wǒ yī xiǎo bēi shuǐ.
    - Please give me a small cup of water.
### 潇(瀟) → 萧(蕭) → 肖 → 月
- **definition:** moon/flesh
- **pinyin:** yuè
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a crescent moon
  - **interpretation:** The character 月(yuè, "moon") is a pictographic representation of a crescent moon, straightforwardly capturing the curved shape often associated with the moon's appearance. Though interchangeable with 夕 (xī, "evening") in the past, it distinctly represents the moon in this usage.
- **examples:**
  - 夜( )空( )挂(掛)着(著)明( )月( )。
    - Yè kōng guà zhe míng yuè.
    - The bright moon hangs in the night sky.
  - 人( )们(們)赏(賞)月( )，寄( )托(託)相( )思( )。
    - Rén men shǎng yuè, jì tuō xiāng sī.
    - People enjoy the moon, expressing their longing.

## 水

## water
### shuǐ
---
- **definition:** water
- **pinyin:** shuǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts flowing water
  - **interpretation:** 水 (shuǐ, 'water') is a pictogram that directly represents the appearance of flowing water, making it intuitive and easy to recognize. Its simplicity and direct visual reference to its meaning have allowed it to remain unchanged over millennia.
- **examples:**
  - 水( )是( )一( )种(種)生( )命( )之( )源( )。
    - Shuǐ shì yī zhǒng shēngmìng zhī yuán.
    - Water is the source of life.
  - 夏( )天( )很( )热(熱)，喝水能解暑。
    - Xiàtiān hěn rè, hē shuǐ néng jiě shǔ.
    - It is very hot in summer, drinking water can relieve the heat.
  - 河水潺潺，沿( )岸( )绿(綠)树(樹)成( )荫(蔭)。
    - Hé shuǐ chánchán, yán'àn lǜshù chéng yīn.
    - The river flows babbling, and green trees along the bank provide shade.

## 逸
---
## ease; leisure; escape
### yì
---
- **definition:** ease; leisure; escape
- **pinyin:** yì
- **components:**
  - 辶
    - chuò
    - walk
  - 兔
    - tù
    - rabbit
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 辶 (chuò, "walk") + 兔 (tù, "rabbit") =
    - rabbit running ->
    - escape, ease
  - **interpretation:** The character 逸(yì) combines the 辶 (chuò, "walk"), which signifies movement, with 兔 (tù, "rabbit"), which adds the sound and also suggests the idea of a rabbit swiftly escaping. Together, they convey the notion of escaping or fleeing with ease, embodying the meanings of both leisure and escape.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )在( )安( )静(靜)的( )庭( )院( )中( )享( )受( )逸( )趣( )。
    - Tā zài ānjìng de tíngyuàn zhōng xiǎngshòu yìqù.
    - He enjoys leisure in the quiet courtyard.
  - 他( )喜( )欢(歡)逸( )居( )，远(遠)离(離)城( )市( )喧( )嚣(囂)。
    - Tā xǐhuān yìjū, yuǎnlí chéngshì xuānxiāo.
    - He likes to live in seclusion, away from the city's hustle and bustle.
  - 假( )日( )来(來)临(臨)时(時)，他( )计(計)划(劃)逸( )出( )尘(塵)嚣(囂)。
    - Jiàrì láilín shí, tā jìhuà yìchū chénxiāo.
    - When the holiday arrives, he plans to escape the hustle and bustle.
### 逸 → 辶(辵)
- **traditional:** 辵
- **definition:** walk
- **pinyin:** chuò
- **components:**
  - 辵
    - chuò
    - walk
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** combining form
  - **description:**
    - Simplified form of 辵 (chuò, "walk") ->
    - used as a radical in conjunction with other components
  - **interpretation:** 辶(辵) (chuò, "walk") is derived by simplifying 能(mp) (néng, "self-reliant") structure from the character 辵. This simplified form emphasizes the act of motion or walking and is widely used as a radical in various motion-related characters.
- **examples:**
  - 学(學)习(習)汉(漢)字( )时(時)，辶( )作( )为(為)部( )首( )出( )现(現)。
    - Xuéxí hànzì shí(學習漢字時)，chuò zuòwéi bùshǒu chūxiàn(辶作為部首出現)。
    - When studying Chinese characters, '辶' appears as a radical.
  - 许(許)多( )表( )示( )走( )路( )的( )字( )，都( )含( )有( )辶( )这(這)一( )部( )分( )。
    - Xǔduō biǎoshì zǒulù de zì(許多表示走路的字)，dōu hányǒu chuò zhè yī bùfen(都含有辶這一部分)。
    - Many characters that denote walking contain the radical '辶'.
  - 古( )人( )写(寫)字( )时(時)，常( )利( )用( )辶( )表( )达(達)行( )走( )之( )意( )。
    - Gǔrén xiězì shí(古人寫字時)，cháng lìyòng chuò biǎodá xíngzǒu zhī yì(常利用辶表達行走之意)。
    - When ancient people wrote characters, they often used '辶' to express the idea of walking.
### 逸 → 辶(辵) → 辵
- **definition:** walk
- **pinyin:** chuò
- **components:**
  - 彳
    - chì
    - walk
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - foot
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 彳 (chì, "walk") + 止 (zhǐ, "foot" or "to stop") ->
    - 𣥆 ->
    - stylized as 龰 ->
    - 辵
  - **interpretation:** The character 辵 combines 彳 (chì, "walk"), which represents movement, with 止 (zhǐ, "foot" or "to stop"), suggesting the concept of stepping or traveling forward. This character represents the idea of movement, particularly the action of walking or traveling, as originally depicted by the more conservative form 𣥆. Over time, the bottom component has been stylized as 龰, forming the current representation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )辵( )在( )乡(鄉)间(間)的( )小( )路( )上( )。
    - Tā chuò zài xiāngjiān de xiǎolù shàng.
    - He walks on the rural path.
  - 晨( )光( )洒(灑)满(滿)大( )地( )，他( )辵( )出( )门(門)开(開)始( )旅( )程( )。
    - Chén guāng sǎ mǎn dàdì, tā chuò chūmén kāishǐ lǚchéng.
    - As the morning light fills the earth, he sets out to begin his journey.
### 逸 → 辶(辵) → 辵 → 彳
- **definition:** walk
- **pinyin:** chì
- **components:**
  - 行
    - xíng
    - go
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts 街 intersection viewed from above, simplified from 行 (xíng, "go")
  - **interpretation:** The character 彳(chì, "walking") represents the image of a street intersection, which symbolically indicates movement or walking. It is used to denote the concept of going or moving along a path.
- **examples:**
  - 他彳亍而行。
    - Tā chìchù ér xíng.
    - He ambled along slowly.
  - 老人彳步，穿( )过(過)小( )巷( )。
    - Lǎorén chì bù, chuānguò xiǎoxiàng.
    - The old man walked along, crossing the narrow alley.
  - 孩( )子( )们(們)看( )到( )蝴( )蝶( )，便( )兴(興)奋(奮)地( )彳( )走( )前( )去( )。
    - Háizimen kàn dào húdié, biàn xīngfèn de chì zǒu qiánqù.
    - Upon seeing the butterflies, the children excitedly walked forward.
### 逸 → 辶(辵) → 辵 → 彳 → 行
- **definition:** go
- **pinyin:** xíng
- **components:**
  - 彳
    - chì
    - step
  - 亍
    - chù
    - step
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Originally a symmetric depiction of a street intersection; simplified asymmetrically ->
    - 彳 (chì, "step") + 亍 (chù, "step")
  - **interpretation:** The character 行(xíng) visually represented a street intersection, with two symmetrical parts symbolizing the act of going or walking along paths. Despite its current breakdown into separate elements, it was originally a unified pictogram rather than a compound.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )行( )往( )学(學)校( )。
    - Wǒ xíng wǎng xuéxiào.
    - I go to school.
  - 他( )行( )于(於)青( )山( )之( )间(間)，徒步旅行至古城。
    - Tā xíng yú qīngshān zhī jiān, túbù lǚxíng zhì gǔchéng.
    - He walks among the green mountains and hikes to the ancient city.
  - 他( )们(們)行( )在( )林( )间(間)小( )路( )上( )。
    - Tāmen xíng zài línjiān xiǎolù shàng.
    - They walk on the forest trail.
### 逸 → 辶(辵) → 辵 → 彳 → 行 → 亍
- **definition:** step
- **pinyin:** chù
- **components:**
  - 丿
    - piě
    - slash
  - 一
    - yī
    - one
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 丿 (piě, "slash") + 一 (yī, "one") =
    - forming a path ->
    - step
  - **interpretation:** The character 亍 represents a form of walking or stepping through its simplistic depiction of a path or trail. Consisting of an introductory stroke 丿 (piě, "slash") followed by 一 (yī, "one"), it suggests the concept of a basic step or movement.
- **examples:**
  - 每走一亍，都( )是( )前( )进(進)的( )一( )步( )。
    - Měi zǒu yī chù, dōu shì qiánjìn de yībù.
    - Every step is a step forward.
  - 他( )细(細)细(細)品( )味( )着(著)生( )活( )的( )每( )一( )亍( )，步步向前。
    - Tā xìxì pǐnwèi zhe shēnghuó de měi yī chù, bù bù xiàng qián.
    - He savors every step of life and steadily moves forward.
### 逸 → 辶(辵) → 辵 → 止
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zhǐ
- **components:**
  - 趾
    - zhǐ
    - foot
  - 龰
    - yù
    - stylized feet
  - 手
    - shǒu
    - hand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Original: Foot pointing up with 3 toes and a sole ->
    - Stylized as 龰 ->
    - Derivative 趾 refers to original 'foot' meaning
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicting a footprint with toes and a sole, 止 (zhǐ, "foot") represents the foot. The character's form resembles a foot with three toes, analogous to how the hand is represented with fingers in other characters.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)的( )止( )需( )要( )静(靜)养(養)。
    - Tā shòu shāng de zhǐ xū yào jìng yǎng.
    - His injured foot needs adequate rest.
  - 他( )走( )路( )时(時)小( )心( )谨(謹)慎( )，因( )为(爲)止( )若( )受( )伤(傷)便( )难(難)前( )行( )。
    - Tā zǒu lù shí xiǎo xīn jǐn shèn, yīn wèi zhǐ ruò shòu shāng biàn nán qián xíng.
    - He walks carefully because if his foot gets injured, it becomes difficult to move forward.
### 逸 → 辶(辵) → 辵 → 止 → 趾
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zhǐ
- **components:**
  - 足
    - zú
    - foot
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - stop
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 足 (zú, "foot") + 止 (zhǐ, "stop") =
    - semantic: foot + phonetic: stop ->
    - toe
  - **interpretation:** The character combines 足 (zú, "foot") as the semantic indicator and 止 (zhǐ, "stop") as the phonetic component to represent the idea of a 'toe,' which is a part of the foot.
- **examples:**
  - 他的趾因寒冷而僵硬了。
    - tā de zhǐ yīn hánlěng ér jiāngyìng le.
    - His toe became stiff due to the cold.
  - 我( )走( )路( )时(時)，不小心扭到了趾。
    - wǒ zǒulù shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ dào le zhǐ.
    - While I was walking, I accidentally twisted my toe.
  - 古( )代( )剑(劍)客( )战(戰)斗(鬥)时(時)，常( )因( )疏( )忽( )伤(傷)及( )趾( )。
    - gǔdài jiànkè zhàndòu shí, cháng yīn shūhū shāng jí zhǐ.
    - Ancient swordsmen often injured their toes due to carelessness during battles.
### 逸 → 辶(辵) → 辵 → 止 → 趾 → 足
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zú
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a leg ->
    - originally depicted as ⽦ ->
    - represents 'foot'
  - **interpretation:** The character 足(zú, 'foot') is a pictographic representation of a human leg, which directly portrays the concept of a 'foot'. The pictographic origin emphasizes its fundamental meaning in the language.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)后(後)，他( )的( )足( )不( )能( )动(動)。
    - tā shòushāng hòu, tā de zú bù néng dòng。
    - After he was injured, his foot couldn't move.
  - 小( )明( )打( )球( )时(時)，不( )小( )心( )扭( )伤(傷)了( )足( )。
    - xiǎo míng dǎ qiú shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ shāng le zú。
    - When Xiaoming was playing ball, he accidentally twisted his foot.
  - 我( )每( )天( )锻(鍛)炼(鍊)足( )部( )。
    - wǒ měitiān duànliàn zúbù。
    - I exercise my foot muscles every day.
### 逸 → 辶(辵) → 辵 → 止 → 龰
- **definition:** stylized feet
- **pinyin:** yù
- **components:**
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - foot
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a stylized foot originally with three toes, representing 'foot' ->
    - evolved to 龰 as a stylization
  - **interpretation:** 龰(yù, "stylized feet") captures the essential elements of a foot as represented in early script forms, with three toes similar to portrayals of human hands, marking it as a specialized form of 止 (zhǐ, "foot"), distinct from similar characters like 夊, 夂 or 丑.
### 逸 → 辶(辵) → 辵 → 止 → 手
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a hand with fingers; top stroke is a bent over middle finger, horizontal strokes represent fingers
  - **interpretation:** The character 手 (shǒu, "hand") is a direct pictorial representation of a human hand with all five fingers, which distinguishes it from similar characters representing claws or simplified fingers. This visually conveys its meaning as a hand clearly.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )手( )写(寫)字( )。
    - Tā yòng shǒu xiě zì.
    - He writes with his hand.
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)牵(牽)着(著)我( )的( )手( )走( )路( )。
    - Māmā qiān zhe wǒ de shǒu zǒu lù.
    - Mom holds my hand while walking.
  - 他( )的( )手( )很( )温(溫)暖( )，带(帶)给(給)我( )力( )量( )。
    - Tā de shǒu hěn wēn nuǎn, dài gěi wǒ lìliàng.
    - His hand is very warm, giving me strength.
### 逸 → 兔
- **definition:** rabbit
- **pinyin:** tù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a rabbit
  - **interpretation:** The character 兔(tù, "rabbit") visually represents the profile of a rabbit, with exaggerated features such as the ears and posture, to capture the essence of the animal.
- **examples:**
  - 野( )兔( )警( )觉(覺)地( )观(觀)察( )周( )围(圍)。
    - Yě tù jǐngjué de guānchá zhōuwéi.
    - The wild rabbit watches its surroundings alertly.
  - 夜( )晚( )降( )临(臨)时(時)，兔( )子( )开(開)始( )活( )跃(躍)。
    - Yèwǎn jiànglín shí, tùzi kāishǐ huóyuè.
    - At nightfall, the rabbit starts to become active.

## 逸
### yì

## 推拉
---
## push and pull
### tuīlā
---
- **definition:** push and pull
- **pinyin:** tuīlā
- **components:**
  - 推
    - tuī
    - push
  - 拉
    - lā
    - pull
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 推 (tuī, "push"), 拉 (lā, "pull") are combined to describe the physical actions of pushing and pulling, often used for doors or handles.
  - **interpretation:** The word 推拉 (tuīlā) combines the actions of 'push' and 'pull', which are commonly used instructions on doors indicating how to open them. By joining these two characters, the compound signifies both actions necessary for operating certain mechanisms or entrances.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )力( )推( )拉( )门(門)。
    - Tā yònglì tuīlā mén.
    - He pushes and pulls the door forcefully.
  - 如( )果( )门(門)无(無)法( )自( )动(動)关(關)闭(閉)，请(請)尝(嘗)试(試)推( )拉( )操( )作( )。
    - Rúguǒ mén wúfǎ zìdòng guānbì, qǐng chángshì tuīlā cāozuò.
    - If the door cannot close automatically, please try pushing and pulling it.
### 推拉 → 推
- **definition:** push
- **pinyin:** tuī
- **components:**
  - 扌
    - shǒu
    - hand
  - 隹
    - zhuī
    - short-tailed bird
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 扌 (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic 隹 (zhuī, "short-tailed bird") =
    - push with the hand ->
    - push
  - **interpretation:** The character 推(tuī) combines 扌 (shǒu, "hand"), indicating the action done by hand, with 隹 (zhuī, "short-tailed bird"), which provides the phonetic cue. This combination visually represents the concept of pushing something with one's hand.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )推( )门(門)而( )入( )。
    - Tā tuī mén ér rù.
    - He pushed the door open and entered.
  - 我推他一把，他( )就( )赶(趕)快( )走( )开(開)。
    - Wǒ tuī tā yī bǎ, tā jiù gǎnkuài zǒu kāi.
    - I gave him a push, and he quickly moved away.
  - 警( )察( )推( )开(開)窗( )户(戶)查( )看( )。
    - Jǐngchá tuī kāi chuānghù chá kàn.
    - The police pushed the window open to inspect.
### 推拉 → 推 → 扌
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a stylized hand. The top stroke is the bent middle finger, and the horizontal strokes each represent two fingers.
  - **interpretation:** 扌 is a simplified form of 手 (shǒu, 'hand'), retaining the key elements that visually represent a human hand. It is commonly used as a component in other characters related to hand actions.
  - **simplification:** 扌 is derived as a cursive or shorthand form of 手 (shǒu, 'hand'), making it easier to incorporate into complex characters as a radical or component.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )伸( )出( )扌(手)接( )住( )了( )飞(飛)来(來)的( )球( )。
    - Tā shēnchū shǒu jiēzhù le fēilái de qiú.
    - He stretched out his hand to catch the flying ball.
  - 她( )用( )扌(手)制(製)作( )美( )丽(麗)的( )陶( )艺(藝)，展( )现(現)她( )的( )匠( )心( )。
    - Tā yòng shǒu zhìzuò měilì de táoyì, zhǎnxiàn tā de jiàngxīn.
    - She used her hand to create beautiful pottery, showcasing her craftsmanship.
  - 艺(藝)术(術)家( )细(細)心( )雕( )刻( )扌(手)的( )每( )一( )细(細)节(節)。
    - Yìshùjiā xìxīn diāokè shǒu de měiyī xìjié.
    - The artist meticulously carved every detail of the hand.
### 推拉 → 推 → 隹
- **definition:** short-tailed bird
- **pinyin:** zhuī
- **components:**
  - 鷕
    - yǎo
    - cry of a bird
  - 唯
    - wéi
    - only
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a short‐tailed bird (e.g., a sparrow) ->
    - also original form of 唯 (wéi, "only") before evolution into 鷕 (yǎo, "cry of a bird")
  - **interpretation:** 隹 (zhuī, "short-tailed bird") is a pictogram representing a bird with a short tail. Its depiction served as the foundation for other characters related to birds, such as 鷕 (yǎo, "cry of a bird").
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)只( )短( )尾( )的( )隹( )在( )树(樹)林( )中( )飞(飛)翔( )。
    - Zhè zhī duǎnwěi de zhuī zài shùlín zhōng fēixiáng.
    - This short-tailed bird flies in the forest.
  - 博( )物( )馆(館)里(裡)展( )出( )了( )一( )件( )以( )隹( )为(為)题(題)材( )的( )古( )代( )绘(繪)画(畫)。
    - Bówùguǎn lǐ zhǎnchūle yī jiàn yǐ zhuī wèi tícái de gǔdài huìhuà.
    - The museum displays an ancient painting themed on the short-tailed bird.
  - 远(遠)古( )诗(詩)篇( )中( )常( )用( )隹( )来(來)象( )征(徵)自( )由( )，寓( )意( )生( )动(動)。
    - Yuǎngǔ shīpiān zhōng cháng yòng zhuī lái xiàngzhēng zìyóu, yùyì shēngdòng.
    - In ancient poems, the short-tailed bird is often used to symbolize freedom, conveying vivid meaning.
### 推拉 → 推 → 隹 → 鷕
- **definition:** cry of a bird
- **pinyin:** yǎo
- **components:**
  - 唯
    - wéi
    - only
  - 鳥
    - niǎo
    - bird
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Originally 隹 ->
    - 唯 (with 口 added) phonetic + 鳥 semantic =
    - bird cry
  - **interpretation:** The character 鷕 (yǎo, 'cry of a bird') uses 唯 (wéi, 'only') for its phonetic component and 鳥 (niǎo, 'bird') to give the semantic context, indicating the character's meaning related to the sound a bird makes.
### 推拉 → 推 → 隹 → 鷕 → 唯
- **definition:** only
- **pinyin:** wéi
- **components:**
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
  - 隹
    - zhuī
    - short-tailed bird
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 口 (kǒu, "mouth") + 隹 (zhuī, "short-tailed bird") =
    - phonetic element 隹 with semantic element 口 for voice ->
    - meaning of 'only'
  - **interpretation:** The character 唯(wéi) combines 口 (kǒu, "mouth") indicating speech or vocal expression, with 隹 (zhuī, "short-tailed bird") which serves as a phonetic component. The combination suggests making a singular statement, hence 'only'.
- **examples:**
  - 唯有努力，才能成功。
    - Wéi yǒu nǔlì, cái néng chénggōng.
    - Only through hard work can one succeed.
  - 他( )是( )唯( )一( )选(選)择(擇)。
    - Tā shì wéiyī xuǎnzé.
    - He is the only option.
  - 那( )台( )电(電)脑(腦)是( )全( )场(場)里(裡)唯( )一( )款( )性( )能( )最( )强(強)的( )。
    - Nà tái diànnǎo shì quánchǎng lǐ wéiyī kuǎn xìngnéng zuì qiáng de.
    - That computer is the only one with the strongest performance in the entire showroom.
### 推拉 → 推 → 隹 → 鷕 → 唯 → 口
- **definition:** mouth
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.
### 推拉 → 推 → 隹 → 鷕 → 鳥
- **definition:** bird
- **pinyin:** niǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a bird with a prominent beak and tail feathers
  - **interpretation:** The character 鳥 (niǎo, "bird") visually represents a bird, featuring distinct elements such as a beak and tail feathers, capturing its essence through its graphical form.
- **examples:**
  - 花( )园(園)里(裡)有( )许(許)多( )鸟(鳥)，在空中翱翔。
    - Huāyuán lǐ yǒu xǔduō niǎo, zài kōngzhōng áoxiáng.
    - Many birds soar in the garden.
  - 树(樹)林( )中( )有( )一( )只(隻)鸟(鳥)，在( )枝( )头(頭)跳( )跃(躍)。
    - Shùlín zhōng yǒu yī zhī niǎo, zài zhītóu tiàoyuè.
    - A bird hops on a branch in the forest.
  - 海( )边(邊)的( )天( )空( )中( )飞(飛)翔( )着(著)鸟(鳥)。
    - Hǎibiān de tiānkōng zhōng fēixiáng zhe niǎo.
    - A bird soars in the sky by the seaside.
### 推拉 → 拉
- **definition:** pull
- **pinyin:** lā
- **components:**
  - 手
    - shǒu
    - hand
  - 立
    - lì
    - stand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - hand 手 (shǒu, 'hand') + phonetic 立 (lì, 'stand') =
    - action performed with the hand, using the sound of 立 to signify 'pull'
  - **interpretation:** The character 拉 (lā, 'pull') combines 手 (shǒu, 'hand') to indicate an action involving manipulation with a hand, while 立 (lì, 'stand') provides the phonetic pronunciation. The character effectively conveys the idea of using one's hand to 'pull' or 'drag' something.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )力( )拉( )门(門)。
    - Tā yònglì lā mén.
    - He pulls the door with force.
  - 她( )拉( )开(開)窗( )帘(簾)，让(讓)阳(陽)光( )进(進)来(來)。
    - Tā lā kāi chuānglián, ràng yángguāng jìn lái.
    - She pulls open the curtains to let the sunlight in.
  - 那( )只( )狗( )拉( )着(著)绳(繩)子( )，追( )赶(趕)着(著)主( )人( )。
    - Nà zhī gǒu lāzhe shéngzi, zhuīgǎn zhe zhǔrén.
    - The dog pulls its leash while chasing its owner.
### 推拉 → 拉 → 手
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a hand with fingers; top stroke is a bent over middle finger, horizontal strokes represent fingers
  - **interpretation:** The character 手 (shǒu, "hand") is a direct pictorial representation of a human hand with all five fingers, which distinguishes it from similar characters representing claws or simplified fingers. This visually conveys its meaning as a hand clearly.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )手( )写(寫)字( )。
    - Tā yòng shǒu xiě zì.
    - He writes with his hand.
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)牵(牽)着(著)我( )的( )手( )走( )路( )。
    - Māmā qiān zhe wǒ de shǒu zǒu lù.
    - Mom holds my hand while walking.
  - 他( )的( )手( )很( )温(溫)暖( )，带(帶)给(給)我( )力( )量( )。
    - Tā de shǒu hěn wēn nuǎn, dài gěi wǒ lìliàng.
    - His hand is very warm, giving me strength.
### 推拉 → 拉 → 立
- **definition:** stand
- **pinyin:** lì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a person standing on the ground
  - **interpretation:** The character 立 (lì, 'stand') intuitively represents the concept of standing by depicting a person in an upright position, emphasizing the posture.
- **examples:**
  - 小王立在窗前，等( )待( )朋( )友( )到( )来(來)。
    - Xiǎo Wáng lì zài chuāng qián, děngdài péngyǒu dàolái.
    - Xiao Wang stands at the window, waiting for his friend to arrive.
  - 园(園)中( )雕( )像( )静(靜)立( )于(於)石( )台(臺)上( )。
    - Yuán zhōng diāoxiàng jìng lì yú shí tái shàng.
    - The statue in the garden stands quietly on the stone platform.
  - 士( )兵( )集( )合( )后(後)，整( )齐(齊)地( )立( )定( )阵(陣)型( )。
    - Shìbīng jíhé hòu, zhěngqí de lì dìng zhènxíng.
    - After the soldiers assemble, they stand in formation neatly.

## 阿

## prefix/particle used in names or address
### ā
---
- **definition:** prefix/particle used in names or address
- **pinyin:** ā
- **components:**
  - 阝(阜)
    - fù
    - mound
  - 可
    - kě
    - can
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 阝(阜) (fù, 'mound') + phonetic: 可 (kě) =
    - sound component → used as a prefix/particle in names or address
  - **interpretation:** The character 阿 (ā) uses the semantic component 阝(阜) (fù, 'mound') to represent locality and a structure, combined with the phonetic 可 (kě) to indicate its pronunciation. It's often used as a prefix to give a sense of familiarity in names.
- **examples:**
  - 阿( )明( )今( )天( )来(來)学(學)校( )。
    - Ā míng jīntiān lái xuéxiào.
    - Ah Ming comes to school today.
  - 阿( )兰(蘭)正( )在( )等( )你( )，阿( )珍( )已( )经(經)到( )达(達)车(車)站( )。
    - Ā lán zhèngzài děng nǐ, ā zhēn yǐjīng dàodá chēzhàn.
    - Ah Lan is waiting for you, and Ah Zhen has already arrived at the station.
### 阿 → 阝
- **definition:** mound
- **pinyin:** fù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a small mound or hillock.
  - **interpretation:** The character 阝 (fù, 'mound') visually represents a small elevation of land, resembling a hillside or embankment.
- **examples:**
  - 小( )山( )顶(頂)上( )耸(聳)立( )着(著)阝(阜)。
    - Xiǎo shāndǐng shàng sǒnglì zhe fù.
    - A small mound stands atop the hill.
  - 山( )坡( )上( )长(長)满(滿)了( )绿(綠)色( )的( )草( )丛(叢)，山( )丘( )边(邊)坐( )落( )着(著)一( )座( )古( )老( )的( )阝(阜)。
    - Shānpō shàng cháng mǎn le lǜsè de cǎocóng, shānqiū biān zuòluò zhe yī zuò gǔlǎo de fù.
    - The hillside is covered with green grass, and an ancient mound sits by the hill.
  - 远(遠)处(處)沙( )丘( )边(邊)隐(隱)约(約)可( )见(見)一( )堆( )阝(阜)，仿(彷)佛(彿)历(歷)史( )的( )见(見)证(證)。
    - Yuǎnchù shāqiū biān yǐnyuē kějiàn yī duī fù, fǎngfú lìshǐ de jiànzhèng.
    - In the distance, a pile of mounds is barely visible by the sand dunes, as if witnessing history.
### 阿 → 可
- **definition:** can
- **pinyin:** kě
- **components:**
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
  - 丂
    - kǎo
    - exhaled air
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 口 (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic: 丂 (kǎo) =
    - reference to exhaled air "to sing" ->
    - capability to perform actions
  - **interpretation:** This character combines the semantic element 口 (kǒu, "mouth") with the phonetic 丂 (kǎo), referencing exhaling or vocal actions like singing, which suggests expressing capability or permission.
- **examples:**
  - 你( )可( )尝(嘗)试(試)一( )下( )新( )方( )法( )。
    - Nǐ kě chángshì yīxià xīn fāngfǎ.
    - You may try a new method.
  - 此( )事( )虽(雖)难(難)，你( )可( )坚(堅)持( )到( )底( )。
    - Cǐ shì suī nán, nǐ kě jiānchí dàodǐ.
    - Although this matter is difficult, you may persist to the end.
### 阿 → 可 → 口
- **definition:** mouth
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.
### 阿 → 可 → 丂
- **definition:** exhaled air
- **pinyin:** kǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts exhaled air, related to concepts of breath or vapors
  - **interpretation:** The character 丂(kǎo, "exhaled air") is a pictogram that represents the visual concept of breath or exhaled air, connected to expressions involving air or vapors rising.

## 好( )久( )不( )见(見)
---
## long time no see
### hǎojiǔ bújiàn
---
- **traditional:** 好久不見
- **definition:** long time no see
- **pinyin:** hǎojiǔ bújiàn
- **components:**
  - 好
    - hǎo
    - very; good
  - 久
    - jiǔ
    - long time
  - 不
    - bù
    - not; no
  - 见(見)
    - jiàn
    - see; meet
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 好 (hǎo, "very") + 久 (jiǔ, "long time") + 不 (bù, "not") + 见(見) (jiàn, "see"). This phrase is used when meeting someone after not having seen them for a while.
  - **interpretation:** 好( )久( )不( )见(見) (hǎojiǔ bújiàn, "long time no see") is a greeting used when you meet someone again after a long period has passed. It conveys the idea of being pleased to see each other after a long absence, reflecting friendliness and warmth.
- **examples:**
  - 你( )今( )天( )来(來)了( )，好( )久( )不( )见(見)。
    - Nǐ jīntiān láile, hǎojiǔ bújiàn.
    - You have come today, long time no see.
  - 我( )们(們)好( )久( )不( )见(見)。
    - Wǒmen hǎojiǔ bújiàn.
    - Long time no see, we haven't met for ages.
  - 老朋友，好( )久( )不( )见(見)！
    - Lǎo péngyǒu, hǎojiǔ bújiàn!
    - Old friend, long time no see!
### 好( )久( )不( )见(見) → 好
- **definition:** very; good
- **pinyin:** hǎo
- **components:**
  - 女
    - nǚ
    - woman
  - 子
    - zǐ
    - child
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 女 (nǚ, "woman") + 子 (zǐ, "child") =
    - a woman with a child ->
    - mutual affection, extended meaning 'good'
  - **interpretation:** The character 好(hǎo) ("good") combines 女(nǚ) ("woman") and 子(zǐ) ("child"), depicting the traditional view that it is good for a woman to have a child, symbolizing affinity and care. This visual representation of nurturing and affection extended metaphorically to mean general goodness or well-being.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)本( )书(書)写(寫)得( )很( )好( )。
    - Zhè běn shū xiě dé hěn hǎo.
    - This book is written very well.
  - 他今天心情很好，所( )以( )笑( )得( )很( )灿(燦)烂(爛)。
    - Tā jīntiān xīnqíng hěn hǎo, suǒyǐ xiào dé hěn cànlàn.
    - He is in a good mood today, so he smiles brightly.
### 好( )久( )不( )见(見) → 好 → 女
- **definition:** woman
- **pinyin:** nǚ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a woman kneeling or standing ->
    - enclosed area as remnant of right breast
  - **interpretation:** The character 女(nǚ, "woman") is a pictogram originally depicting a woman with highlighted bodily features, such as the breasts, symbolizing femininity. Its orientation changed from the Oracle Bone script to face right, simplifying over time while still retaining the essence of its original depiction.
- **examples:**
  - 那( )位( )才( )华(華)横(橫)溢( )的( )女( )，赢(贏)得( )了( )全( )场(場)注( )目( )。
    - Nà wèi cáihuá héngyì de nǚ, yíng dé le quánchǎng zhùmù.
    - That talented woman won everyone's attention.
  - 古时候‘女’常被用来象征温柔(古時候‘女’常被用來象徵溫柔)，而( )如( )今( )它( )代( )表( )独(獨)立( )自( )信( )。
    - Gǔshíhòu ‘nǚ’ cháng bèi yòng lái xiàngzhēng wēnróu, ér rújīn tā dàibiǎo dúlì zìxìn.
    - In ancient times, the character '女' was often used to symbolize gentleness, whereas today it represents independence and confidence.
### 好( )久( )不( )见(見) → 好 → 子
- **definition:** child
- **pinyin:** zǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a baby with a large head and spread arms, with legs wrapped in a blanket
  - **interpretation:** The character 子(zǐ, "child") is a pictorial representation of a baby, emphasizing characteristics like a large head and outstretched arms typical of infants, which conveys the essence of a child. This visual element helps infer its meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)小( )子( )很( )调(調)皮( )。
    - Zhè xiǎo zǐ hěn tiáopí.
    - This kid is very naughty.
  - 他( )的( )小( )子( )爱(愛)打( )篮(籃)球( )，每( )天( )都( )锻(鍛)炼(煉)身( )体(體)。
    - Tā de xiǎo zǐ ài dǎ lánqiú, měitiān dōu duànliàn shēntǐ.
    - His child loves playing basketball, and practices every day.
  - 孤( )独(獨)的( )子( )需( )要( )关(關)爱(愛)。
    - Gūdú de zǐ xūyào guān'ài.
    - A lonely child needs care.
### 好( )久( )不( )见(見) → 久
- **definition:** long time
- **pinyin:** jiǔ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a person bending the lower back ->
    - extended time
  - **interpretation:** The character 久 (jiǔ, "long time") visually represents a person leaning forward, symbolizing the passage of a significant amount of time. The bending posture suggests protracted duration.
- **examples:**
  - 我等待了很久。
    - Wǒ děngdài le hěn jiǔ.
    - I waited for a long time.
  - 分( )别(別)了( )很( )久( )，朋友仍然相聚。
    - Fēnbié le hěn jiǔ, péngyǒu réngrán xiāngjù.
    - It has been a long time since we parted, yet friends still meet.
  - 远(遠)行( )归(歸)来(來)已( )久( )，家( )门(門)依( )旧(舊)温(溫)暖( )。
    - Yuǎnxíng guīlái yǐ jiǔ, jiāmén yījiù wēnnuǎn.
    - It has been a long time since returning from a journey, yet the home remains welcoming.
### 好( )久( )不( )见(見) → 不
- **definition:** not; no
- **pinyin:** bù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Originally depicted as a calyx or flower stem, indicating negation - symbolically suggesting something not fully formed or not happening.
  - **interpretation:** The character 不(bù, "not") started as a pictographic representation of a calyx or incomplete form, symbolizing the idea of negation or absence.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )不( )喜( )欢(歡)吃( )辣( )椒( )。
    - Wǒ bù xǐhuān chī làjiāo.
    - I do not like eating chili.
  - 他( )不( )是( )医(醫)生( )，而( )是( )老( )师(師)。
    - Tā bù shì yīshēng, ér shì lǎoshī.
    - He is not a doctor, but a teacher.
  - 天( )气(氣)不( )好( )，我( )们(們)不( )出( )门(門)。
    - Tiānqì bù hǎo, wǒmen bù chūmén.
    - The weather is bad, and we do not go out.
### 好( )久( )不( )见(見) → 见(見)
- **traditional:** 見
- **definition:** see; meet
- **pinyin:** jiàn
- **components:**
  - 目
    - mù
    - eye
  - 人
    - rén
    - person
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Depicts an eye 目 (mù, "eye") over a person 人 (rén, "person") ->
    - representation of seeing or perception
  - **interpretation:** The character originally depicted an eye positioned over a person, symbolizing the act of seeing or encountering someone. This combination effectively conveys the idea of sight or meeting, aligning with its core meanings.
  - **simplification:** 見 (jiàn, "see") was simplified by reducing the number of strokes, using a simplified structure to retain both meaning and phonetic aspects effectively.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )见(見)到( )老( )朋( )友( )时(時)，心情十分愉快。
    - Wǒ jiàn dào lǎo péngyǒu shí, xīnqíng shífēn yúkuài.
    - I felt very happy when I met my old friend.
  - 我( )们(們)见(見)面( )了( )。
    - Wǒmen jiàn miàn le.
    - We met.
  - 在( )公( )园(園)里(裡)见(見)到( )一( )只(隻)小( )猫(貓)，让(讓)我( )十( )分( )高( )兴(興)。
    - Zài gōngyuán lǐ jiàn dào yī zhī xiǎo māo, ràng wǒ shífēn gāoxìng.
    - Seeing a little cat in the park made me very happy.
### 好( )久( )不( )见(見) → 见(見) → 目
- **definition:** eye
- **pinyin:** mù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a human eye, now represented and rotated 90 degrees ->
    - eye
  - **interpretation:** The character 目(mù, 'eye') is a pictogram representing a human eye. Its original orientation has changed over time, resembling an eye seen from a sideways view.
- **examples:**
  - 他的目光炯炯有神。
    - Tā de mùguāng jiǒngjiǒng yǒu shén.
    - His gaze is bright and full of spirit.
  - 她( )目( )不( )转(轉)睛( )，注( )视(視)前( )方( )。
    - Tā mù bù zhuǎn jīng, zhùshì qiánfāng.
    - She stared intently, gazing ahead.
  - 雄( )鹰(鷹)展( )翅( )，双(雙)目( )炯( )炯( )看( )天( )际(際)。
    - Xióng yīng zhǎn chì, shuāng mù jiǒngjiǒng kàn tiānjì.
    - The eagle spreads its wings, its eyes piercingly gaze at the horizon.
### 好( )久( )不( )见(見) → 见(見) → 人
- **definition:** person
- **pinyin:** rén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts side view of a standing man, highlighting an arm and a leg ->
    - visual representation of a person
  - **interpretation:** The character 人 (rén, 'person') effectively uses simple lines to depict the essential form of a human, making it immediately recognizable and intuitive as meaning 'person.'
- **examples:**
  - 他( )是( )个(個)好( )人( )。
    - Tā shì gè hǎo rén.
    - He is a good person.
  - 每( )个(個)人( )都( )有( )责(責)任( )，为(為)社( )会(會)作( )出( )贡(貢)献(獻)。
    - Měi gè rén dōu yǒu zérèn, wèi shèhuì zuòchū gòngxiàn.
    - Every person has a responsibility to contribute to society.
  - 人在旅途中，心( )灵(靈)获(獲)得( )了( )成( )长(長)。
    - Rén zài lǚtú zhōng, xīnlíng huòdé le chéngzhǎng.
    - People gain growth along their journey.
