## bimbo (lit. big chest no brains)
### xiōng dà wú nǎo
---
- **traditional:** 胸大無腦
- **definition:** bimbo (lit. big chest no brains)
- **pinyin:** xiōng dà wú nǎo
- **components:**
  - 胸
    - xiōng
    - chest
  - 大
    - dà
    - big; large; great; major
  - 无(無)
    - wú
    - no; not; without
  - 脑(腦)
    - nǎo
    - brain; mind; head
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 胸 (xiōng, "chest"), 大 (dà, "big"), 无(無) (wú, "no"), 脑(腦) (nǎo, "brain")
  - **interpretation:** This expression combines 胸 (xiōng, "chest") and 大 (dà, "big") to refer to a prominent physical feature, with 无(無) (wú, "no") and 脑(腦) (nǎo, "brain") implying a lack of intelligence. It is often used disparagingly to describe someone perceived as physically attractive but lacking in intelligence.
- **examples:**
  - 她( )常( )常( )被( )人( )称(稱)为(為)胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦)。
    - Tā chángcháng bèi rén chēngwéi xiōng dà wú nǎo.
    - She is often labeled a bimbo.
  - 有( )的( )明( )星( )虽(雖)然( )外( )表( )光( )鲜(鮮)，但( )其( )实(實)她( )们(們)不( )过(過)是( )胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦)。
    - Yǒu de míngxīng suīrán wàibiǎo guāngxían, dàn qíshí tāmen bùguò shì xiōng dà wú nǎo.
    - Some celebrities may appear glamorous on the outside, but in reality, they are just bimbos.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 胸
- **definition:** chest
- **pinyin:** xiōng
- **components:**
  - 月
    - yuè
    - flesh
  - 匈
    - xiōng
    - chest
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 月 (yuè, "flesh") + phonetic: 匈 (xiōng) =
    - part of the body related to flesh ->
    - chest
  - **interpretation:** The character 胸(xiōng) combines 月 (yuè, "flesh"), which indicates the character relates to a part of the body, with 匈 (xiōng), which provides the phonetic element. This suggests a focus on a body part, specifically the chest.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )的( )胸( )中( )充( )满(滿)勇( )气(氣)。
    - Tā de xiōng zhōng chōngmǎn yǒngqì.
    - His chest is full of courage.
  - 他( )坚(堅)持( )锻(鍛)炼(鍊)，以( )增( )强(強)胸( )的( )力( )量( )。
    - Tā jiānchí duànliàn, yǐ zēngqiáng xiōng de lìliàng.
    - He insists on exercising to strengthen the power of his chest.
  - 医(醫)生( )检(檢)查( )了( )他( )的( )胸( )。
    - Yīshēng jiǎnchále tā de xiōng.
    - The doctor examined his chest.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 胸 → 月
- **definition:** flesh
- **pinyin:** yuè
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a crescent moon
  - **interpretation:** The character 月(yuè, "moon") is a pictographic representation of a crescent moon, straightforwardly capturing the curved shape often associated with the moon's appearance. Though interchangeable with 夕 (xī, "evening") in the past, it distinctly represents the moon in this usage.
- **examples:**
  - 夜( )空( )挂(掛)着(著)明( )月( )。
    - Yè kōng guà zhe míng yuè.
    - The bright moon hangs in the night sky.
  - 人( )们(們)赏(賞)月( )，寄( )托(託)相( )思( )。
    - Rén men shǎng yuè, jì tuō xiāng sī.
    - People enjoy the moon, expressing their longing.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 胸 → 匈
- **definition:** chest
- **pinyin:** xiōng
- **components:**
  - 勹
    - bāo
    - wrap
  - 凶
    - xiōng
    - fierce
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Semantic: 勹 (bāo, "wrap") + Phonetic: 凶 (xiōng, "fierce") =
    - chest as an enclosed space
  - **interpretation:** 匈(xiōng) initially depicted the idea of an enclosed or wrapped area, with the phonetic 凶(xiōng) indicating the sound. The character later evolved into 胸(xiōng, "chest") by adding 肉(ròu, "meat") to emphasize its anatomical meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 他挺起匈，准(準)备(備)迎( )接( )挑( )战(戰)。
    - Tā tǐng qǐ xiōng, zhǔnbèi yíng jiē tiǎozhàn.
    - He puffed out his chest, ready to face the challenge.
  - 保( )护(護)好( )匈( )，有( )助( )于(於)健( )康( )。
    - Bǎohù hǎo xiōng, yǒu zhù yú jiànkāng.
    - Protecting your chest helps maintain health.
  - 深( )呼( )吸( )能( )使( )匈( )放( )松(鬆)。
    - Shēn hūxī néng shǐ xiōng fàngsōng.
    - Deep breathing can relax the chest.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 胸 → 匈 → 勹
- **definition:** wrap
- **pinyin:** bāo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a person bending over
  - **interpretation:** The character 勹(bāo, "wrap") illustrates a person bending over, signifying an action of encircling or enclosing, which aligns well with meanings related to wrapping or containing.
- **examples:**
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)用( )柔( )软(軟)的( )布( )包( )住( )易( )碎( )品( )。
    - Māmā yòng róuruǎn de bù bāo zhù yì suìpǐn.
    - Mother wraps fragile items with soft cloth.
  - 他( )熟( )练(練)地( )把( )礼(禮)物( )包( )成( )漂( )亮( )的( )形( )状(狀)，让(讓)人( )感( )受( )到( )温(溫)情( )。
    - Tā shúliàn de bǎ lǐwù bāo chéng piàoliang de xíngzhuàng, ràng rén gǎnshòu dào wēnqíng.
    - He skillfully wraps the gift into a beautiful shape, letting people feel warmth.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 胸 → 匈 → 凶
- **definition:** fierce
- **pinyin:** xiōng
- **components:**
  - 凵
    - kǎn
    - hole
  - 㐅
    - yá
    - rock, mud, stone, or bamboo in the hole
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Pictogram and/or Ideogram
  - **description:**
    - 凵 (hole) + 㐅 (indicating hole/representing rock, mud, stone, or bamboo in the hole) =
    - hole in the ground or an object within a hole ->
    - conveys idea of something fearsome or fierce
  - **interpretation:** The character 凶(xiōng, 'fierce') depicts a hole or pit, suggesting danger or something fearsome, possibly illustrating a trap or something threatening emerging from it. The components convey the sense of danger associated with the unknown or concealed.
- **examples:**
  - 那( )只(隻)野( )狗( )看( )起( )来(來)很( )凶( )。
    - Nà zhī yěgǒu kàn qǐlái hěn xiōng.
    - That stray dog looks very fierce.
  - 他( )讲(講)话(話)时(時)非( )常( )凶( )，让(讓)人( )害( )怕( )。
    - Tā jiǎnghuà shí fēicháng xiōng, ràng rén hàipà.
    - When he speaks, he is extremely fierce, which scares people.
  - 这(這)条(條)路( )在( )夜( )晚( )显(顯)得( )凶( )险(險)。
    - Zhè tiáo lù zài yèwǎn xiǎnde xiōngxiǎn.
    - This road appears dangerous at night.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 胸 → 匈 → 凶 → 凵
- **definition:** hole
- **pinyin:** kǎn
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a cave
  - **interpretation:** The character 凵 (kǎn, "container") visually represents the shape of a cave, which is an enclosure or hollow space. This depiction is straightforward, illustrating the basic meaning through a simple, open-bottomed rectangular shape.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)凵( )足( )以( )盛( )放( )鲜(鮮)花( )。
    - Zhège kǎn zúyǐ shèngfàng xiānhuā.
    - This container is sufficient for holding fresh flowers.
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)买(買)了( )一( )个(個)大( )凵( )，用( )来(來)给(給)花( )浇(澆)水( )。
    - Māmā mǎile yīgè dà kǎn, yòng lái gěi huā jiāoshuǐ.
    - Mom bought a big container to water the flowers.
  - 老( )店( )里(裡)展( )示( )了( )古( )代( )的( )凵( )，象( )征(徵)着(著)智( )慧( )和( )传(傳)承( )。
    - Lǎo diàn lǐ zhǎnshì le gǔdài de kǎn, xiàngzhēngzhe zhìhuì hé chuánchéng.
    - The old shop displayed an ancient container, symbolizing wisdom and heritage.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 胸 → 匈 → 凶 → 㐅
- **definition:** rock, mud, stone, or bamboo in the hole
- **pinyin:** yá
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** 㐅 depicts an unclear entity, connected in evolution to 五 (wǔ, 'five')
  - **interpretation:** The character 㐅's original meaning is uncertain, but it forms the basis for characters like 五 (wǔ, 'five'), showing its significance in numerals and related contexts.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 大
- **definition:** big; large; great; major
- **pinyin:** dà
- **components:**
  - 夫
    - fū
    - man
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a person facing forward ->
    - Borrowed phonetically for 'big'; original form of 夫 (fū, 'man')
  - **interpretation:** The character 大(dà) depicts a person facing forward, representing 'big' by suggesting the idea of someone standing prominently. Although often thought of as a person with outstretched arms, early scripts do not support this interpretation. It was originally the form of 夫 (fū, 'man') and was later phonetically borrowed for the meaning 'big.'
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)房( )子( )很( )大( )。
    - Zhè ge fángzi hěn dà.
    - This house is very big.
  - 大( )海( )波( )涛(濤)汹(洶)涌(湧)，展( )现(現)出( )大( )自( )然( )的( )力( )量( )。
    - Dàhǎi bōtāo xiōngyǒng, zhǎnxiàn chū dàzìrán de lìliàng.
    - The vast ocean waves surge, showcasing the power of nature.
  - 大( )人( )物( )登( )场(場)，留( )下( )传(傳)奇( )。
    - Dà rénwù dēngchǎng, liúxià chuánqí.
    - Great figures make an appearance, leaving behind legends.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 大 → 夫
- **definition:** man
- **pinyin:** fū
- **components:**
  - 大
    - dà
    - big
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a variant form of 大 (dà, "big") representing an adult man with a hairpin ->
    - became associated with 夫 (fū, "man").
  - **interpretation:** 夫 (fū, "man") was visually similar to 大 (dà, "big") and was originally interchangeable. The addition of the horizontal stroke was interpreted as a hairpin, symbolizing adulthood for men.
- **examples:**
  - 此( )夫( )心( )怀(懷)大( )志( )。
    - Cǐ fū xīn huái dà zhì.
    - This man has lofty aspirations.
  - 他( )勤( )劳(勞)努( )力( )，堪( )称(稱)良( )夫( )。
    - Tā qín láo nǔ lì, kān chēng liáng fū.
    - He works diligently and is regarded as an exemplary man.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 无(無)
- **traditional:** 無
- **definition:** no; not; without
- **pinyin:** wú
- **components:**
  - 亡
    - wáng
    - to lose
  - 元
    - yuán
    - origin
  - 天
    - tiān
    - heaven
  - 堯
    - yáo
    - legendary ruler
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** unknown
  - **description:** The character 无 was one among several historical representations for expressing 'no' or 'not any,' used alongside 無 (wú, 'not have') and 亡 (wáng, 'to lose'), especially during the Warring States period. The origin of 无 is uncertain, and it might have evolved as a variant of other characters like 元 (yuán, 'origin'), 天 (tiān, 'heaven'), or 堯 (yáo, 'legendary ruler'). Adopted officially in simplified script in 1956 by the PRC.
  - **interpretation:** Without a clear etymology, the character 无 represents a reduction and simplification that was standardized in modern times to denote the concept of absence or non-existence.
  - **simplification:** 无 is a simplified form adopted in 1956 to unify and clarify usage from various historical forms representing negation.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )无(無)车(車)。
    - Wǒ wú chē.
    - I don't have a car.
  - 他( )无(無)经(經)验(驗)，却(卻)有( )决(決)心( )。
    - Tā wú jīngyàn, què yǒu juéxīn.
    - He lacks experience, but has determination.
  - 生( )活( )中( )无(無)烦(煩)恼(惱)，心( )情( )自( )然( )轻(輕)松(鬆)。
    - Shēnghuó zhōng wú fánnǎo, xīnqíng zìrán qīngsōng.
    - When life is without worries, the mood naturally becomes relaxed.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 无(無) → 亡
- **definition:** to lose
- **pinyin:** wáng
- **components:**
  - 刀
    - dāo
    - knife
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:**
    - Line on 刀 (dāo, "knife") indicates cutting edge ->
    - disappearance of knowledge ->
    - perish
  - **interpretation:** The character 亡(wáng) visually represents the sharp, cutting edge of a knife, which metaphorically implies the loss of life or things 'perishing'. Over time, it came to signify broader concepts of disappearance and extinction.
- **examples:**
  - 国(國)破( )，家亡。
    - Guó pò, jiā wáng.
    - The country is broken, the family perishes.
  - 经(經)过(過)激( )战(戰)时(時)，士兵亡。
    - Jīngguò jīzhàn shí, shìbīng wáng.
    - During the intense battle, the soldiers perished.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 无(無) → 亡 → 刀
- **definition:** knife
- **pinyin:** dāo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a knife
  - **interpretation:** 刀 (dāo, "knife") is a pictographic representation of a knife or blade, with a sharp edge and a handle, resembling the form of a basic cutting tool.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )锋(鋒)利( )的( )刀( )，切( )开(開)蔬( )菜( )。
    - tā yòng fēnglì de dāo, qiē kāi shūcài.
    - He uses a sharp knife to cut vegetables.
  - 这(這)把( )刀( )非( )常( )锋(鋒)利( )。
    - zhè bǎ dāo fēicháng fēnglì.
    - This knife is very sharp.
  - 厨(廚)师(師)小( )心( )地( )持( )刀( )，为(為)客( )人( )切( )肉( )。
    - chúshī xiǎoxīn de chí dāo, wèi kèrén qiē ròu.
    - The chef holds the knife carefully and slices meat for the guest.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 无(無) → 元
- **definition:** origin
- **pinyin:** yuán
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a human head above a horizontal line, symbolizing the origin or the head of something
  - **interpretation:** 元 (yuán, 'origin') represents the head, which is often considered the starting point or leader, aligning with the concept of 'origin'. This setup also connects metaphorically to beginnings or first principles.
- **examples:**
  - 万(萬)物( )之( )元( )来(來)自( )于(於)混( )沌( )。
    - Wànwù zhī yuán láizì yú húndùn.
    - The origin of all things comes from chaos.
  - 道( )家( )的( )理( )念( )追( )寻(尋)心( )灵(靈)的( )元( )。
    - Dàojiā de lǐniàn zhuīxún xīnlíng de yuán.
    - Taoist philosophy seeks the origin of the soul.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 无(無) → 天
- **definition:** heaven
- **pinyin:** tiān
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Horizontal line above 大(dà, "big") to depict sky ->
    - anthropomorphic man with large head symbolizing heaven
  - **interpretation:** The character 天(tiān) originally depicts the sky as a horizontal line above a person with outstretched arms, 大(dà, "big"), emphasizing the vastness or domain of heaven. Over time, the character evolved into an anthropomorphic representation of heaven, which later influenced its interpretation in classical texts.
- **examples:**
  - 夜( )幕( )降( )临(臨)，天( )边(邊)星( )光( )闪(閃)烁(爍)。
    - Yèmù jiànglín, tiānbiān xīnguāng shǎnshuò.
    - As night falls, starlight gleams on the horizon.
  - 他仰望高空，祈( )求( )天( )佑( )众(眾)生( )。
    - Tā yǎngwàng gāokōng, qíqiú tiānyòu zhòngshēng.
    - He gazes up to the sky and prays for heaven's protection over all living beings.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 无(無) → 堯
- **definition:** legendary ruler
- **pinyin:** yáo
- **components:**
  - 垚
    - yáo
    - tall (of earth)
  - 兀
    - wù
    - towering and bald
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Phono-semantic compound / Ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phono-semantic: 垚 (yáo, "tall (of earth)") as phonetic + 兀 (wù, "towering and bald") as semantic ->
    - representing height and eminence. Ideogrammic: 垚 (yáo, "tall (of earth)") + 兀 (wù, "towering and bald") =
    - symbolizing an elevated stature ->
    - legendary ruler
  - **interpretation:** The character 堯 (yáo) combines phonetic and semantic elements to convey the idea of being tall or eminent, fitting for a legendary ruler known for his height and stature in tradition.
- **examples:**
  - 堯( )是( )传(傳)说(說)中( )的( )圣(聖)君( )。
    - Yáo shì chuánshuō zhōng de shèng jūn.
    - Yao is a legendary virtuous ruler.
  - 古( )代( )文( )献(獻)赞(讚)誉(譽)堯( )，称(稱)他( )治( )理( )有( )方( )。
    - Gǔdài wénxiàn zànyù Yáo, chēng tā zhìlǐ yǒu fāng.
    - Ancient texts praise Yao, saying he governed with wisdom.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 无(無) → 堯 → 垚
- **definition:** tall (of earth)
- **pinyin:** yáo
- **components:**
  - 土
    - tǔ
    - earth; dirt
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 土 (tǔ, "earth; dirt") + 土 (tǔ, "earth; dirt") + 土 (tǔ, "earth; dirt") =
    - accumulation of earth ->
    - tall
  - **interpretation:** The character 垚 depicts the repetition of the 土 (tǔ, "earth; dirt") character three times to signify the idea of a large or tall mass of earth, emphasizing height or elevation.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 无(無) → 堯 → 垚 → 土
- **definition:** earth; dirt
- **pinyin:** tǔ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a mound of earth
  - **interpretation:** The character 土(tǔ, "earth") visually represents a mound or piece of land, with a horizontal line indicating the surface and a vertical line the height of the mound. It's a straightforward depiction of earth or soil.
- **examples:**
  - 农(農)民( )耕( )土( )，种(種)出( )丰(豐)收( )。
    - Nóngmín gēng tǔ, zhòng chū fēngshōu.
    - Farmers cultivate the earth and harvest abundantly.
  - 院( )子( )里(裡)的( )土( )，适(適)合( )种(種)花( )。
    - Yuànzi lǐ de tǔ, shìhé zhòng huā.
    - The soil in the yard is suitable for planting flowers.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 无(無) → 堯 → 兀
- **definition:** towering and bald
- **pinyin:** wù
- **components:**
  - 尢
    - wān
    - man with bent legs
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** variant form
  - **description:** 兀 is a variant form of 尢 (wān, "man with bent legs")
  - **interpretation:** 兀 originally represented a man with bent legs, indicative of jumping or collapsing. It connects to this meaning by representing something that stands out or is elevated, as in 'towering.' It reflects the semantic notion of standing out both physically and metaphorically.
- **examples:**
  - 那座山峰兀立，宛如巨人昂首。
    - Nà zuò shānfēng wùlì, wǎnrú jùrén ángshǒu.
    - That mountain peak towers, like a giant with its head held high.
  - 古( )树(樹)枝( )干(幹)兀( )然( )裸( )露( )，诉(訴)说(說)着(著)岁(歲)月( )沧(滄)桑( )。
    - Gǔ shù zhīgàn wùrán luǒlù, sùshuōzhe suìyuè cāngsāng.
    - The ancient tree's branches stand bald and exposed, telling tales of time’s vicissitudes.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 无(無) → 堯 → 兀 → 尢
- **definition:** man with bent legs
- **pinyin:** wān
- **components:**
  - 尤
    - yóu
    - especially
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** variant form of 尢
  - **description:**
    - Pictogram: Originally depicts a hand with a wart ->
    - Variant form of 尤: retaining connotations of deformity
  - **interpretation:** 尢 is identified as a variant of 尤 and represents the notion of a man with bent legs, closely associating with deformity or deviation in form. The character still conveys variations perceived as abnormalities, similar to the original meaning of a hand with a wart.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )行( )走( )时(時)显(顯)露( )出( )尢( )样(樣)步( )态(態)，引( )得( )众(眾)人( )侧(側)目( )。
    - tā xíngzǒu shí xiǎnlù chū wān yàng bùtài, yǐn dé zhòngrén cèmù.
    - When he walked, his bent-legged gait was evident, attracting sidelong glances from the crowd.
  - 古( )籍( )记(記)载(載)尢( )人( )的( )形( )态(態)。
    - gǔjí jìzǎi wān rén de xíngtài.
    - Ancient texts record the appearance of a man with bent legs.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 无(無) → 堯 → 兀 → 尢 → 尤
- **definition:** especially
- **pinyin:** yóu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a hand with a wart ->
    - Original form of 疣 (yóu, "wart") and 肬(躍) (yóu, "elevated")
  - **interpretation:** 尤 (yóu, "hand with a wart") is a pictogram that initially represented the image of a hand marked by a wart. It served as the original form for characters related to irregularities like 疣 (yóu, "wart").
- **examples:**
  - 医(醫)生( )检(檢)查( )时(時)发(發)现(現)他( )左( )手( )有( )一( )块(塊)尤( )，需( )及( )时(時)治( )疗(療)。
    - Yīshēng jiǎnchá shí fāxiàn tā zuǒshǒu yǒu yí kuài yóu, xū jíshí zhìliáo.
    - During the examination, the doctor discovered that his left hand had a wart and needed prompt treatment.
  - 古( )人( )检(檢)测(測)身( )相( )时(時)，会(會)观(觀)察( )是( )否( )有( )尤( )。
    - Gǔ rén jiǎncè shēnxiàng shí, huì guānchá shìfǒu yǒu yóu.
    - In ancient physiognomy, people would observe whether a hand with a wart was present.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 脑(腦)
- **traditional:** 腦
- **definition:** brain; mind; head
- **pinyin:** nǎo
- **components:**
  - 匕
    - bǐ
    - spoon
  - 巛(川)
    - chuān
    - river
  - 囟
    - xìn
    - fontanel
  - 肉
    - ròu
    - flesh
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 匕 (bǐ, 'spoon') + 巛(川) (chuān, 'river', used here for 'hair') + 囟 (xìn, 'fontanel') ->
    - ideogrammic for 'head with hair and a spoon-like shape inside' ->
    - 匘 with 匕 replaced by 肉 (ròu, 'flesh') =
    - 'flesh inside a head' ->
    - modern form for 'brain'
  - **interpretation:** The character 脑(nǎo) ('brain') evolved from the idea of a fleshy interior within the head, emphasizing its conceptual association with the head and thoughts.
  - **simplification:** Simplified from 腦 by replacing the complex 囟(bottom part) with the simpler character 月(yuè), retaining the meaning linked to 'flesh within the head'.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )脑(腦)，思( )考( )问(問)题(題)。
    - Tā yòng nǎo, sī kǎo wèn tí.
    - He uses his brain to think over problems.
  - 他( )的( )脑(腦)很( )聪(聰)明( )！
    - Tā de nǎo hěn cōngmíng!
    - His brain is very smart!
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 脑(腦) → 匕
- **definition:** spoon
- **pinyin:** bǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts the shape of a spoon or dagger, indicating an object with a handle.
  - **interpretation:** The character 匕 (bǐ, "cane") originally depicts a spoon or dagger, highlighting its application as a tool with a handle, which conceptually relates to a cane.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )匕( )支( )撑(撐)自( )己( )，慢( )慢( )走( )过(過)崎( )岖(嶇)的( )小( )路( )。
    - Tā yòng bǐ zhīchēng zìjǐ, màn màn zǒuguò qíqū de xiǎolù.
    - He supports himself with his cane, and slowly walks along the rugged path.
  - 老人依靠匕，缓(緩)缓(緩)行( )走( )在( )乡(鄉)间(間)小( )道( )。
    - Lǎorén yīkào bǐ, huǎn huǎn xíngzǒu zài xiāngjiān xiǎodào.
    - The old man relies on his cane, and gently walks on the country lane.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 脑(腦) → 巛
- **definition:** river
- **pinyin:** chuān
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts water flowing
  - **interpretation:** The character 巛 (chuān, 'river') is a pictogram representing the flowing nature of a river or stream, emphasizing the movement and fluidity of water. It is visually related to characters like 川 (chuān, 'river'), each representing water movement.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 脑(腦) → 囟
- **definition:** fontanel
- **pinyin:** xìn
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a fontanelle
  - **interpretation:** The character 囟(xìn, "fontanel") is a pictogram representing the soft spot on a baby's head, known as the fontanelle. This depiction is a direct representation without phonetic elements.
- **examples:**
  - 婴(嬰)儿(兒)的( )囟( )部( )发(發)育( )得( )很( )快( )。
    - Yīng'ér de xìnbù fāyù de hěn kuài.
    - The baby's fontanel develops quickly.
  - 护(護)士( )轻(輕)轻(輕)地( )检(檢)查( )了( )婴(嬰)儿(兒)的( )囟( )门(門)。
    - Hùshì qīngqīng de jiǎnchá le yīng'ér de xìnmén.
    - The nurse gently checked the baby's fontanel.
  - 医(醫)生( )说(說)囟( )门(門)会(會)在( )一( )岁(歲)左( )右( )闭(閉)合( )。
    - Yīshēng shuō xìnmén huì zài yī suì zuǒyòu bìhé.
    - The doctor said the fontanel will close around the age of one.
### 胸( )大( )无(無)脑(腦) → 脑(腦) → 肉
- **definition:** flesh
- **pinyin:** ròu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a piece of flesh or meat with stripes representing texture.
  - **interpretation:** The character 肉(ròu, "flesh") depicts a piece of meat, meant to evoke the concept of flesh through its visual resemblance to a striped or sectioned piece of meat.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )喜( )欢(歡)吃( )肉( )。
    - Wǒ xǐhuān chī ròu.
    - I like eating meat.
  - 这(這)块(塊)肉( )很( )鲜(鮮)美( )，营(營)养(養)丰(豐)富( )。
    - Zhè kuài ròu hěn xiānměi, yíngyǎng fēngfù.
    - This piece of meat is very fresh and delicious, and it is nutritious.

## 枕( )头(頭)
---
## pillow
### zhěntou
---
- **traditional:** 枕頭
- **definition:** pillow
- **pinyin:** zhěntou
- **components:**
  - 枕
    - zhěn
    - pillow
  - 头(頭)
    - tóu
    - head
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 枕 (zhěn, "pillow") originally referred to an object to rest your head, and 頭(头) (tóu, "head") specifies the purpose of the pillow, indicating it is for the head.
  - **interpretation:** 枕( )頭(头) (zhěntou, "pillow") combines the concept of a supporting object for the head, making it clear the item is meant to hold or rest the head during sleep or rest.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )喜( )欢(歡)用( )柔( )软(軟)的( )枕( )头(頭)。
    - Wǒ xǐhuān yòng róuruǎn de zhěntou.
    - I like to use a soft pillow.
  - 睡( )觉(覺)时(時)，我( )总(總)是( )需( )要( )一( )个(個)舒( )服( )的( )枕( )头(頭)。
    - Shuìjiào shí, wǒ zǒng shì xūyào yī gè shūfu de zhěntou.
    - When sleeping, I always need a comfortable pillow.
  - 他( )送( )给(給)我( )一( )个(個)新( )枕( )头(頭)。
    - Tā sòng gěi wǒ yī gè xīn zhěntou.
    - He gave me a new pillow.
### 枕( )头(頭) → 枕
- **definition:** pillow
- **pinyin:** zhěn
- **components:**
  - 木
    - mù
    - wood
  - 冘
    - yín
    - sink
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 木 (mù, "wood") + phonetic 冘 (yín, "sink") =
    - wooden object for support ->
    - pillow
  - **interpretation:** The character 枕 (zhěn, "pillow") combines 木 (mù, "wood") as the material often used in ancient times for making pillows, with 冘 (yín) to suggest stability and support, reflecting its function as a supportive object for resting the head during sleep.
- **examples:**
  - 躺( )下( )休( )息( )时(時)，他( )把( )头(頭)轻(輕)轻(輕)枕( )在( )柔( )软(軟)的( )枕( )上( )。
    - Tǎng xià xiūxí shí, tā bǎ tóu qīngqīng zhěn zài róuruǎn de zhěn shàng.
    - When lying down to rest, he gently rests his head on a soft pillow.
  - 午( )后( )小( )憩( )时(時)，她( )翻( )开(開)了( )枕( )边(邊)书(書)轻(輕)阅(閱)古( )文( )。
    - Wǔhòu xiǎoqì shí, tā fānkāile zhěnbiān shū qīng yuè gǔwén.
    - During her afternoon nap, she opened her bedside book and quietly read ancient texts.
### 枕( )头(頭) → 枕 → 木
- **definition:** wood
- **pinyin:** mù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a tree with branches and roots
  - **interpretation:** The character 木(mù) depicts a tree, showing the trunk in the center and two slanting lines representing branches or roots. It directly signifies the concept of wood or a tree.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)把( )椅( )子( )的( )主( )要( )材( )料( )是( )木( )。
    - Zhè bǎ yǐzi de zhǔyào cáiliào shì mù.
    - The main material of this chair is wood.
  - 工匠精心雕刻木，制成精美家具。
    - Gōngjiàng jīngxīn diāo kè mù, zhìchéng jīngměi jiājù.
    - The craftsman carefully carved the wood, crafting exquisite furniture.
### 枕( )头(頭) → 枕 → 冘
- **definition:** sink
- **pinyin:** yín
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a man carrying an object on their shoulder ->
    - original form of 儋 (dān, "to carry on the shoulder")
  - **interpretation:** The character 冘(yín, "sink") is a pictogram that originally represented a person carrying something on the shoulder, which was the original form used for the verb "to carry on the shoulder." Over time, its meaning has evolved.
### 枕( )头(頭) → 头(頭)
- **traditional:** 頭
- **definition:** head
- **pinyin:** tóu
- **components:**
  - 页(頁)
    - yè
    - page/head
  - 豆
    - dòu
    - bean
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 页(頁) (yè, "page/head") + phonetic: 豆 (dòu, "bean") =
    - physical head form ->
    - head
  - **interpretation:** The character combines 页(頁) (yè, "page/head"), which provides a semantic component related to the top or head, with 豆 (dòu, "bean") for phonetic support, leading to the meaning "head."
  - **simplification:** 頭→头(頭): 豆 retained, 页(頁) simplified by removing parts for a cursive shorthand resembling 大 (dà, "big") to maintain a visual association with 'head'.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )的( )头(頭)很( )大( )。
    - Tā de tóu hěn dà.
    - His head is big.
  - 他( )的( )头(頭)发(髮)很( )浓(濃)密( )，闪(閃)耀( )着(著)健( )康( )的( )光( )泽(澤)。
    - Tā de tóufǎ hěn nóngmì, shǎnyào zhe jiànkāng de guāngzé.
    - His hair is thick, radiating the glow of health.
  - 他( )跑( )到( )山( )头(頭)上( )，俯( )瞰( )整( )个(個)村( )庄(莊)。
    - Tā pǎo dào shāntóu shàng, fǔkàn zhěnggè cūnzhuāng.
    - He ran to the hilltop and looked down over the entire village.
### 枕( )头(頭) → 头(頭) → 页(頁)
- **traditional:** 頁
- **definition:** page/head
- **pinyin:** yè
- **components:**
  - 首
    - shǒu
    - head
  - 卩
    - jié
    - kneeling person
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 首 (shǒu, "head") + 卩 (jié, "kneeling person") =
    - head of a kneeling person ->
    - head ->
    - later adopted for "page"
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicting the head of a kneeling person, 頁 (yè, "head") transitioned from describing a physical head to denote a "page" or "sheet," connecting the idea of a main part of a document or book.
  - **simplification:** 页(頁): The character was simplified from 頁 to 页 by retaining essential strokes, emphasizing cursive efficiency.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )翻( )开(開)书(書)，读(讀)到( )第( )一( )页(頁)。
    - Wǒ fān kāi shū, dú dào dì yī yè.
    - I opened the book and read the first page.
  - 他( )在( )古( )籍( )中( )找( )到( )了( )很( )多( )页(頁)，记(記)录(錄)着(著)历(歷)史( )秘( )密( )。
    - Tā zài gǔjí zhōng zhǎo dào le hěn duō yè, jìlù zhe lìshǐ mìmì.
    - He found many pages in the ancient texts that record historical secrets.
### 枕( )头(頭) → 头(頭) → 页(頁) → 首
- **definition:** head
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a head with the stylized representation of eyes and hair
  - **interpretation:** The character 首 (shǒu, 'head') depicts a head, with the top portion representing hair and a central line suggesting a facial feature, effectively conveying the concept of a head.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )昂( )首( )挺( )胸( )走( )上( )领(領)奖(獎)台( )。
    - Tā áng shǒu tǐng xiōng zǒu shàng lǐng jiǎng tái.
    - He walked onto the podium with his head held high.
  - 受( )伤(傷)后(後)，他( )低( )下( )首( )表( )示( )谦(謙)逊(遜)。
    - Shòu shāng hòu, tā dī xià shǒu biǎo shì qiān xùn.
    - After being injured, he lowered his head to show humility.
  - 他( )被( )选(選)为(為)公( )司( )的( )首( )席( )执(執)行( )官( )。
    - Tā bèi xuǎn wéi gōng sī de shǒu xí zhí xíng guān.
    - He was elected as the company's chief executive officer.
### 枕( )头(頭) → 头(頭) → 页(頁) → 卩
- **definition:** kneeling person
- **pinyin:** jié
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a kneeling person from sideview
  - **interpretation:** The character 卩 (jié, "kneeling person") is a simple pictogram showing the silhouette of a person kneeling. It is unrelated to other kneeling or similar figures in different contexts such as 欠 (qiàn, "yawn") where the mouth is open, or 女 (nǚ, "woman") where the figure represents a female. Its form is solely based on the posture of kneeling.
- **examples:**
  - 那( )位( )卩( )正( )虔( )诚(誠)祈( )祷(禱)。
    - Nà wèi jié zhèng qiánchéng qídǎo.
    - That kneeling person is praying devoutly.
  - 礼(禮)仪(儀)师(師)指( )出( )，卩( )代( )表( )谦(謙)卑( )与(與)敬( )意( )。
    - Lǐyí shī zhǐchū, jié dàibiǎo qiānbēi yǔ jìngyì.
    - The ritual master pointed out that the kneeling person represents humility and respect.
  - 在( )庄(莊)严(嚴)仪(儀)式( )中( )，卩( )象( )征(徵)着(著)内(內)心( )的( )虔( )敬( )。
    - Zài zhuāngyán yíshì zhōng, jié xiàngzhēngzhe nèixīn de qiánjìng.
    - In the solemn ceremony, the kneeling figure symbolizes inner devotion.
### 枕( )头(頭) → 头(頭) → 豆
- **definition:** bean
- **pinyin:** dòu
- **components:**
  - 菽
    - shū
    - pulse
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a container ->
    - borrowed phonetically for the plant name
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicted a container, 豆 (dòu, 'drum') was phonetically borrowed to represent the plant name for beans, eventually displacing the term 菽 (shū, 'pulse'). It is unrelated to the 豊 (fēng, 'abundant') character.
- **examples:**
  - 祭( )司( )敲( )豆( )奏( )乐(樂)，引( )来(來)神( )灵(靈)祝( )福( )。
    - Jìsī qiāo dòu zòuyuè, yǐn lái shénlíng zhùfú.
    - The priest beats the drum, summoning divine blessings.
  - 古( )风(風)庆(慶)典( )上( )敲( )响(響)豆( )，唤(喚)起( )怀(懷)古( )情( )怀(懷)。
    - Gǔfēng qìngdiǎn shàng qiāoxiǎng dòu, huànqǐ huáigǔ qínghuái.
    - At the ancient-style celebration, the beating of the drum evokes nostalgic sentiments.
  - 豆( )声(聲)震( )天( )。
    - Dòu shēng zhèntiān.
    - The sound of the drum shakes the heavens.
### 枕( )头(頭) → 头(頭) → 豆 → 菽
- **definition:** pulse
- **pinyin:** shū
- **components:**
  - 艹
    - cǎo
    - grass
  - 叔
    - shū
    - uncle
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 艹 (cǎo, "grass") + 叔 (shū, "uncle") =
    - plant-related term with phonetic value from 叔
  - **interpretation:** The character 菽(shū, "pulse") combines the semantic 艹 (cǎo, "grass") to indicate a plant-related concept, with the phonetic element 叔 (shū) to suggest its pronunciation. This reflects the agricultural context of 'pulse' as a type of leguminous plant.
- **examples:**
  - 农(農)人( )耕( )种(種)菽( )田( )，迎接秋收。
    - Nóng rén gēngzhòng shū tián, yíngjiē qiūshōu.
    - The farmers cultivate pulse fields, welcoming the autumn harvest.
  - 他( )用( )菽( )研( )磨( )出( )浓(濃)香( )豆( )浆(漿)，享受健康生活。
    - Tā yòng shū yánmó chū nóngxiāng dòujiāng, xiǎngshòu jiànkāng shēnghuó.
    - He grinds pulses to make fragrant soy milk, enjoying a healthy life.
  - 春( )天( )里(裏)菽( )花( )绽(綻)放( )。
    - Chūntiān lǐ shū huā zhànfàng.
    - In spring, the pulse flowers bloom.
### 枕( )头(頭) → 头(頭) → 豆 → 菽 → 艹
- **definition:** grass
- **pinyin:** cǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts blades of grass
  - **interpretation:** 艹 is a pictogram representing grass or plants, resembling blades of grass. It is commonly used as a radical in other characters related to grass or vegetation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )写(寫)字( )时(時)用( )艹( )作( )草( )字( )的( )偏( )旁( )。
    - Tā xiězì shí yòng cǎo zuò cǎozì de piānpáng.
    - He uses '艹' as the radical for grass in his characters.
  - 春( )天( )到( )来(來)时(時)，艹( )生( )长(長)得( )很( )快( )。
    - Chūntiān dàolái shí, cǎo shēngzhǎng de hěn kuài.
    - When spring arrives, grass grows rapidly.
### 枕( )头(頭) → 头(頭) → 豆 → 菽 → 叔
- **definition:** uncle
- **pinyin:** shū
- **components:**
  - 尗
    - shū
    - wooden stake
  - 又
    - yòu
    - hand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Ideogrammic compound and phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phonetic 尗 (shū, "wooden stake") + semantic 又 (yòu, "hand") =
    - using a wooden stake to dig the ground ->
    - phonetically borrowed for "paternal uncle"
  - **interpretation:** The character 叔(shū, "uncle") originally depicted using a hand to plant a wooden stake, representing construction or foundation setting. This was later phonetically borrowed to refer to a "paternal uncle" due to similar pronunciation rather than meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )叔( )爱(愛)讲(講)笑( )话(話)。
    - Wǒ shū ài jiǎng xiàohuà.
    - My uncle loves to tell jokes.
  - 我( )向( )叔( )讨(討)教( )古( )典( )文( )学(學)，他( )认(認)真( )地( )分( )享( )经(經)验(驗)。
    - Wǒ xiàng shū tǎojiào gǔdiǎn wénxué, tā rènzhēn de fēnxiǎng jīngyàn.
    - I sought advice on classical literature from my uncle, and he seriously shared his experience.
### 枕( )头(頭) → 头(頭) → 豆 → 菽 → 叔 → 尗
- **definition:** wooden stake
- **pinyin:** shū
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a wooden stake digging into the dirt
  - **interpretation:** The character 尗(shū, "wooden stake") is a pictogram representing a wooden stake penetrating the earth, highlighting its use in farming or construction.
### 枕( )头(頭) → 头(頭) → 豆 → 菽 → 叔 → 又
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** yòu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a right hand
  - **interpretation:** The character 又 (yòu, "hand") is a pictogram representing a right hand, showing the fingers and palm, which highlights its original meaning related to manual actions.
- **examples:**
  - 「又」这个字最早来源于手(「又」這個字最早來源於手)。
    - 「Yòu」 zhè gè zì zuì zǎo láiyuán yú shǒu。
    - The character '又' originally comes from the hand.
  - 很( )多( )汉(漢)字( )含( )有( )又( )部( )首( )，表( )示( )与(與)手( )有( )关(關)的( )动(動)作( )。
    - Hěn duō hànzì hányǒu Yòu bùshǒu, biǎoshì yǔ shǒu yǒuguān de dòngzuò。
    - Many Chinese characters include the '又' radical, indicating actions related to the hand.
  - 古( )人( )用( )又( )描( )绘(繪)手( )的( )形( )态(態)，体(體)现(現)了( )古( )汉(漢)字( )的( )独(獨)特( )魅( )力( )。
    - Gǔrén yòng Yòu miáohuì shǒu de xíngtài, tǐxiàn le gǔ hànzì de dútè mèilì。
    - Ancient people used '又' to depict the shape of the hand, showcasing the unique charm of ancient Chinese characters.

## carve; polish (jade)
### zhuó
---
- **definition:** carve; polish (jade)
- **pinyin:** zhuó
- **components:**
  - 玉
    - yù
    - jade
  - 石
    - shí
    - stone
  - 豆
    - dòu
    - bean
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 玉 (yù, "jade") + 石 (shí, "stone") =
    - related to working with jade ->
    - phonetic: 豆 (dòu) suggests pronunciation
  - **interpretation:** The character 琢(zhuó, "carve; polish") suggests the process of shaping and refining jade, a task that involves skillful use of stone tools. The presence of 玉 (yù, "jade") highlights the context of working with valuable stones, while 豆 (dòu) provides a phonetic clue to the character's pronunciation.
- **examples:**
  - 工( )匠( )琢( )玉( )雕( )龙(龍)。
    - Gōngjiàng zhuó yù diāo lóng.
    - The craftsman carves jade into a dragon.
  - 他琢磨古玉，寻(尋)找( )其( )中( )的( )美( )。
    - Tā zhuó mó gǔ yù, xúnzhǎo qízhōng de měi.
    - He polishes ancient jade, seeking the beauty within.
  - 他( )不( )断(斷)琢( )玉( )，雕( )琢( )出( )美( )丽(麗)纹(紋)路( )。
    - Tā bùduàn zhuó yù, diāozhuó chū měilì wénlù.
    - He constantly polishes jade, carving out beautiful patterns.
### 琢 → 玉
- **definition:** jade
- **pinyin:** yù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a cong (琮, a ritual jade totem)
  - **interpretation:** 玉 (yù, "jade") visually represents the concept of jade, either as a cong or three pieces of jade strung together. It is distinct from 王 (wáng, "king") thanks to the added dot, ensuring clarity in their usage and meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )收( )藏( )了( )一( )块(塊)古( )玉( )。
    - Tā shōucáng le yī kuài gǔ yù.
    - He collected a piece of antique jade.
  - 晴( )空( )万(萬)里( )时(時)，玉( )石( )在( )阳(陽)光( )下( )闪(閃)烁(爍)。
    - Qíngkōng wànlǐ shí, yù shí zài yángguāng xià shǎnshuò.
    - Under a clear sky, the jade sparkled under the sunlight.
  - 在( )宫(宮)廷( )中( )，玉( )制(製)的( )饰(飾)物( )象( )征(徵)着(著)高( )贵(貴)。
    - Zài gōngtíng zhōng, yù zhì de shìwù xiàngzhēng zhe gāoguì.
    - In the palace, jade ornaments symbolize nobility.
### 琢 → 石
- **definition:** stone
- **pinyin:** shí
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a stone beneath a cliff, where the upper part originally looked like a cliff (厂) but was distorted into 丆 over time.
  - **interpretation:** The character 石(shí, 'stone') visually represents a stone in a natural setting, highlighting the element of a cliff or mountain. Its visual design aims to reflect the appearance of rocks lodged or embedded in a mountainous or cliff-like setting.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )在( )河( )边(邊)发(發)现(現)一( )块(塊)石( )。
    - Tā zài hé biān fāxiàn yī kuài shí.
    - He discovered a piece of stone by the riverside.
  - 孩( )子( )们(們)喜( )欢(歡)堆( )石( )，玩( )得( )开(開)心( )。
    - Háizimen xǐhuān duī shí, wán de kāixīn.
    - Children like to stack stones, playing happily.
### 琢 → 豆
- **definition:** bean
- **pinyin:** dòu
- **components:**
  - 菽
    - shū
    - pulse
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a container ->
    - borrowed phonetically for the plant name
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicted a container, 豆 (dòu, 'drum') was phonetically borrowed to represent the plant name for beans, eventually displacing the term 菽 (shū, 'pulse'). It is unrelated to the 豊 (fēng, 'abundant') character.
- **examples:**
  - 祭( )司( )敲( )豆( )奏( )乐(樂)，引( )来(來)神( )灵(靈)祝( )福( )。
    - Jìsī qiāo dòu zòuyuè, yǐn lái shénlíng zhùfú.
    - The priest beats the drum, summoning divine blessings.
  - 古( )风(風)庆(慶)典( )上( )敲( )响(響)豆( )，唤(喚)起( )怀(懷)古( )情( )怀(懷)。
    - Gǔfēng qìngdiǎn shàng qiāoxiǎng dòu, huànqǐ huáigǔ qínghuái.
    - At the ancient-style celebration, the beating of the drum evokes nostalgic sentiments.
  - 豆( )声(聲)震( )天( )。
    - Dòu shēng zhèntiān.
    - The sound of the drum shakes the heavens.
### 琢 → 豆 → 菽
- **definition:** pulse
- **pinyin:** shū
- **components:**
  - 艹
    - cǎo
    - grass
  - 叔
    - shū
    - uncle
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 艹 (cǎo, "grass") + 叔 (shū, "uncle") =
    - plant-related term with phonetic value from 叔
  - **interpretation:** The character 菽(shū, "pulse") combines the semantic 艹 (cǎo, "grass") to indicate a plant-related concept, with the phonetic element 叔 (shū) to suggest its pronunciation. This reflects the agricultural context of 'pulse' as a type of leguminous plant.
- **examples:**
  - 农(農)人( )耕( )种(種)菽( )田( )，迎接秋收。
    - Nóng rén gēngzhòng shū tián, yíngjiē qiūshōu.
    - The farmers cultivate pulse fields, welcoming the autumn harvest.
  - 他( )用( )菽( )研( )磨( )出( )浓(濃)香( )豆( )浆(漿)，享受健康生活。
    - Tā yòng shū yánmó chū nóngxiāng dòujiāng, xiǎngshòu jiànkāng shēnghuó.
    - He grinds pulses to make fragrant soy milk, enjoying a healthy life.
  - 春( )天( )里(裏)菽( )花( )绽(綻)放( )。
    - Chūntiān lǐ shū huā zhànfàng.
    - In spring, the pulse flowers bloom.

## 想( )象(像)
---
## imagination; imagine
### xiǎngxiàng
---
- **traditional:** 想像
- **definition:** imagination; imagine
- **pinyin:** xiǎngxiàng
- **components:**
  - 想
    - xiǎng
    - think; ponder; consider; want
  - 象(像)
    - xiàng
    - image; likeness; elephant; phenomenon
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 想 (xiǎng, "think") combined with 象(像) (xiàng, "image") to express the concept of forming mental images or envisioning something in the mind.
  - **interpretation:** The word 想( )象(像) (xiǎngxiàng, "imagination; imagine") brings together the concepts of thinking and forming images, conveying the idea of creating or conceiving mental pictures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )喜( )欢(歡)想( )象(像)未( )来(來)，描( )绘(繪)梦(夢)想( )蓝(藍)图(圖)。
    - Tā xǐhuān xiǎngxiàng wèilái, miáohuì mèngxiǎng lántú.
    - He enjoys imagining the future and sketching a blueprint of his dreams.
  - 孩( )子( )们(們)沉( )浸( )在( )故( )事( )里(裡)，尽(盡)情( )想( )象(像)奇( )妙( )世( )界( )。
    - Háizimen chénjìn zài gùshì lǐ, jìnqíng xiǎngxiàng qímiào shìjiè.
    - Children are immersed in stories and unabashedly imagine a magical world.
  - 夜( )深( )人( )静(靜)时(時)，他( )常( )在( )心( )中( )想( )象(像)童( )年( )时(時)光( )。
    - Yè shēn rénjìng shí, tā cháng zài xīnzhōng xiǎngxiàng tóngnián shíguāng.
    - When the night is deep and quiet, he often imagines the times of his childhood in his heart.
### 想( )象(像) → 想
- **definition:** think; ponder; consider; want
- **pinyin:** xiǎng
- **components:**
  - 相
    - xiāng
    - appearance
  - 心
    - xīn
    - heart, mind
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phonetic: 相 (xiāng, "appearance") + Semantic: 心 (xīn, "heart, mind") =
    - visualizing in the mind ->
    - to think, to consider
  - **interpretation:** 想 (xiǎng, "think") combines the phonetic component 相 (xiāng, "appearance") with the semantic component 心 (xīn, "heart, mind") to convey the idea of visualizing or pondering something with the heart or mind. This effectively captures the essence of internal contemplation or consideration.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )想( )去( )图(圖)书(書)馆(館)。
    - Wǒ xiǎng qù túshūguǎn.
    - I want to go to the library.
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)想( )做( )饭(飯)，我( )们(們)想( )吃( )中( )餐( )。
    - Māmā xiǎng zuò fàn, wǒmen xiǎng chī zhōngcān.
    - Mom wants to cook, and we want to eat Chinese food.
  - 他想一想，就( )决(決)定( )了( )。
    - Tā xiǎng yīxiǎng, jiù juédìng le.
    - He gave it some thought, then made a decision.
### 想( )象(像) → 想 → 相
- **definition:** appearance
- **pinyin:** xiāng
- **components:**
  - 木
    - mù
    - tree
  - 目
    - mù
    - eye
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Semantic: 木 (mù, "tree") + Phonetic: 目 (mù) =
    - eye on tree ->
    - to look at each other ->
    - mutual
  - **interpretation:** The character 相(xiāng, "mutual") combines 目 (mù, "eye") suggesting seeing or observing, with 木 (mù, "tree"), reflecting the idea of looking out over a distance or surveying. This is extended to mean looking at each other, thus becoming associated with mutual or reciprocal actions.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )们(們)相( )互( )扶( )持( )。
    - Tāmen xiānghù fúchí.
    - They support each other.
  - 朋( )友( )之( )间(間)要( )相( )互( )理( )解( )，共( )同( )进(進)步( )。
    - Péngyǒu zhījiān yào xiānghù lǐjiě, gòngtóng jìnbù.
    - Friends should understand each other and make progress together.
  - 夫( )妻( )之( )间(間)应(應)相( )互( )尊( )重( )。
    - Fūqī zhījiān yīng xiānghù zūnzhòng.
    - Husbands and wives should mutually respect each other.
### 想( )象(像) → 想 → 相 → 木
- **definition:** tree
- **pinyin:** mù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a tree with branches and roots
  - **interpretation:** The character 木(mù) depicts a tree, showing the trunk in the center and two slanting lines representing branches or roots. It directly signifies the concept of wood or a tree.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)把( )椅( )子( )的( )主( )要( )材( )料( )是( )木( )。
    - Zhè bǎ yǐzi de zhǔyào cáiliào shì mù.
    - The main material of this chair is wood.
  - 工匠精心雕刻木，制成精美家具。
    - Gōngjiàng jīngxīn diāo kè mù, zhìchéng jīngměi jiājù.
    - The craftsman carefully carved the wood, crafting exquisite furniture.
### 想( )象(像) → 想 → 相 → 目
- **definition:** eye
- **pinyin:** mù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a human eye, now represented and rotated 90 degrees ->
    - eye
  - **interpretation:** The character 目(mù, 'eye') is a pictogram representing a human eye. Its original orientation has changed over time, resembling an eye seen from a sideways view.
- **examples:**
  - 他的目光炯炯有神。
    - Tā de mùguāng jiǒngjiǒng yǒu shén.
    - His gaze is bright and full of spirit.
  - 她( )目( )不( )转(轉)睛( )，注( )视(視)前( )方( )。
    - Tā mù bù zhuǎn jīng, zhùshì qiánfāng.
    - She stared intently, gazing ahead.
  - 雄( )鹰(鷹)展( )翅( )，双(雙)目( )炯( )炯( )看( )天( )际(際)。
    - Xióng yīng zhǎn chì, shuāng mù jiǒngjiǒng kàn tiānjì.
    - The eagle spreads its wings, its eyes piercingly gaze at the horizon.
### 想( )象(像) → 想 → 心
- **definition:** heart, mind
- **pinyin:** xīn
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a stylized representation of a heart
  - **interpretation:** The character 心(xīn, 'heart') visually represents the shape of a heart, emphasizing its central and essential role in both a physical and metaphorical sense.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )的( )心( )温(溫)暖( )无(無)比( )。
    - Tā de xīn wēnnuǎn wúbǐ.
    - His heart is incredibly warm.
  - 她( )把( )心( )放( )在( )艺(藝)术(術)上( )，追求完美。
    - Tā bǎ xīn fàng zài yìshù shàng, zhuīqiú wánměi.
    - She dedicates her heart to art, pursuing perfection.
  - 音( )乐(樂)直( )击(擊)心( )灵(靈)。
    - Yīnyuè zhí jī xīnlíng.
    - Music strikes straight at the heart (soul).
### 想( )象(像) → 象
- **definition:** image; likeness; elephant; phenomenon
- **pinyin:** xiàng
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts an elephant: ⺈ (trunk) + 𫩏 (head) + 𧰨 (body) ->
    - elephant
  - **interpretation:** The character 象 (xiàng, 'elephant') is a pictogram that visually represents an elephant with distinct features: the trunk, head, and body. This straightforward depiction was used to convey the concept of an elephant.
- **examples:**
  - 大( )象( )步( )伐( )缓(緩)慢( )。
    - dà xiàng bùfá huǎnmàn
    - Elephants move slowly.
  - 小( )象( )在( )河( )边(邊)戏(戲)水( )，妈(媽)妈(媽)紧(緊)紧(緊)护(護)卫(衛)。
    - xiǎo xiàng zài hé biān xì shuǐ, māma jǐn jǐn hùwèi
    - The baby elephant splashes in the river, while its mother watches closely.
  - 象( )牙( )被( )视(視)为(為)珍( )贵(貴)，常( )被( )用( )于(於)雕( )刻( )。
    - xiàng yá bèi shì wéi zhēn guì, cháng bèi yòng yú diāo kè
    - Ivory is considered precious and is often used for carving.

## 想( )象(像)
### xiǎngxiàng

## 义(義)
---
## surname Yi; righteousness
### Yì
---
- **traditional:** 義
- **definition:** surname Yi; righteousness
- **pinyin:** Yì
- **components:**
  - 乂
    - yì
    - to govern/cut
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 乂 (yì, "to govern/cut") was originally used for its sound ->
    - 刈 (yì, "to cut") displaced 乂 ->
    - a stroke was added on top of 乂 to distinguish it for the meaning of righteousness ->
    - final form 义(義)
  - **interpretation:** Originally, 乂 was used for both its phonetic and semantic meanings but was mainly displaced by 刈. To distinguish the idea of "righteousness," a stroke was added, creating a differentiation that allowed the character to develop its own unique meaning related to justice and propriety.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )行( )侠(俠)仗( )义(義)，扶( )危( )济(濟)困( )。
    - Tā xíng xiá zhàng yì, fú wēi jì kùn.
    - He upholds chivalry and helps those in distress.
  - 正( )义(義)必( )胜(勝)。
    - Zhèng yì bì shèng.
    - Justice will surely prevail.
  - 他( )始( )终(終)坚(堅)守( )义(義)，维(維)护(護)公( )正( )。
    - Tā shǐ zhōng jiān shǒu yì, wèi hù gōng zhèng.
    - He always adheres to righteousness and upholds fairness.
### 义(義) → 乂
- **definition:** to govern/cut
- **pinyin:** yì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a tool (similar to a sickle or a pair of scissors) for mowing or weeding ->
    - used for governing ->
    - final form 乂
  - **interpretation:** The character 乂 originally depicted a tool used for cutting, and by extension, it was associated with governing due to the notion of cutting or managing weeds as a metaphor for ruling effectively. This sense of order is crucial in governance.
- **examples:**
  - 这些年，他致力于地方的治理(這些年，他致力於地方的治理)。
    - Zhèxiē nián, tā zhìlì yú dìfāng de zhìlǐ.
    - Over the years, he has dedicated himself to local governance.
  - 为民者，先自齐以治家(為民者，先自齊以治家)。
    - Wèi mín zhě, xiān zì qí yǐ zhì jiā.
    - Those who govern for the people, first manage their own households.
  - 长(長)者( )都( )希( )望( )后(後)代( )能( )好( )好( )治( )理( )天( )下( )。
    - Zhǎng zhě dōu xīwàng hòudài néng hǎohǎo zhìlǐ tiānxià.
    - The elders hope that future generations can govern the world well.

## 控
---
## control; accuse
### kòng
---
- **definition:** control; accuse
- **pinyin:** kòng
- **components:**
  - 扌
    - shǒu
    - hand
  - 空
    - kōng
    - empty
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 扌 (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic: 空 (kōng, "empty") =
    - action of controlling or accusing with hands
  - **interpretation:** The character 控(kòng, "control; accuse") combines 扌 (shǒu, "hand"), indicating an action involving hands, with 空 (kōng, "empty"), which provides the pronunciation. This suggests an action where hands are used to manage or impose authority, as in control or accusation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )试(試)图(圖)控( )制( )局( )面( )，使( )情( )况(況)稳(穩)定( )。
    - Tā shìtú kòngzhì júmiàn, shǐ qíngkuàng wěndìng.
    - He tried to control the situation, stabilizing the circumstances.
  - 检(檢)察( )官( )控( )告( )嫌( )疑( )人( )，要( )求( )他( )接( )受( )审(審)判( )。
    - Jiǎncháguān kònggào xiányírén, yāoqiú tā jiēshòu shěnpàn.
    - The prosecutor accused the suspect and demanded that he face trial.
  - 运(運)动(動)员(員)在( )比( )赛(賽)中( )全( )力( )控( )球( )，争(爭)取( )胜(勝)利( )。
    - Yùndòngyuán zài bǐsài zhōng quánlì kòngqiú, zhēngqǔ shènglì.
    - The athlete fully controlled the ball during the match, striving for victory.
### 控 → 扌
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a stylized hand. The top stroke is the bent middle finger, and the horizontal strokes each represent two fingers.
  - **interpretation:** 扌 is a simplified form of 手 (shǒu, 'hand'), retaining the key elements that visually represent a human hand. It is commonly used as a component in other characters related to hand actions.
  - **simplification:** 扌 is derived as a cursive or shorthand form of 手 (shǒu, 'hand'), making it easier to incorporate into complex characters as a radical or component.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )伸( )出( )扌(手)接( )住( )了( )飞(飛)来(來)的( )球( )。
    - Tā shēnchū shǒu jiēzhù le fēilái de qiú.
    - He stretched out his hand to catch the flying ball.
  - 她( )用( )扌(手)制(製)作( )美( )丽(麗)的( )陶( )艺(藝)，展( )现(現)她( )的( )匠( )心( )。
    - Tā yòng shǒu zhìzuò měilì de táoyì, zhǎnxiàn tā de jiàngxīn.
    - She used her hand to create beautiful pottery, showcasing her craftsmanship.
  - 艺(藝)术(術)家( )细(細)心( )雕( )刻( )扌(手)的( )每( )一( )细(細)节(節)。
    - Yìshùjiā xìxīn diāokè shǒu de měiyī xìjié.
    - The artist meticulously carved every detail of the hand.
### 控 → 空
- **definition:** empty
- **pinyin:** kōng
- **components:**
  - 穴
    - xué
    - cave, hole
  - 工
    - gōng
    - work
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Semantic: 穴 (xué, "cave, hole") + Phonetic: 工 (gōng) =
    - cave-like space with 工 phonetically suggesting 'kōng' ->
    - empty
  - **interpretation:** The character 空(kōng) combines 穴 (xué, "cave, hole") to represent an empty or hollow space, alongside 工 (gōng) to provide the pronunciation, resulting in the meaning of "empty." It intuitively depicts a space with nothing inside.
- **examples:**
  - 杯子是空的。
    - Bēizi shì kōng de.
    - The cup is empty.
  - 他的心空空如也，没(沒)有( )一( )丝(絲)温(溫)暖( )。
    - Tā de xīn kōng kōng rú yě, méiyǒu yī sī wēnnuǎn.
    - His heart is utterly empty, with not a trace of warmth.
### 控 → 空 → 穴
- **definition:** cave, hole
- **pinyin:** xué
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a cave or hole, resembling a small dwelling or opening
  - **interpretation:** The character 穴 (xué, "cave") visually represents a hole or cave with an open entry, suggesting a sheltered space similar to a primitive dwelling or burrow.
- **examples:**
  - 山( )里(裡)隐(隱)匿( )着(著)一( )个(個)深( )深( )的( )穴( )。
    - Shān lǐ yǐn nì zhe yí gè shēn shēn de xué.
    - A deep cave is hidden in the mountains.
  - 探( )险(險)家( )在( )悬(懸)崖( )下( )发(發)现(現)了( )一( )个(個)幽( )静(靜)的( )穴( )，据(據)说(說)里(裡)面( )住( )着(著)古( )老( )的( )精( )灵(靈)。
    - Tàn xiǎnjiā zài xuán yá xià fāxiàn le yí gè yōu jìng de xué, jù shuō lǐmiàn zhùzhe gǔlǎo de jīng líng.
    - The explorer discovered a quiet cave beneath the cliff, and it is said that ancient spirits dwell inside.
  - 河( )岸( )边(邊)的( )岩( )石( )上( )有( )一( )个(個)小( )穴( )。
    - Hé àn biān de yán shí shàng yǒu yí gè xiǎo xué.
    - There is a small cave on the rock by the riverbank.
### 控 → 空 → 工
- **definition:** work
- **pinyin:** gōng
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a bladed tool to plow the ground and to mark a border, or a carpenter's square
  - **interpretation:** 工 (gōng, "work") represents either a tool used in agriculture for marking or a carpenter's square, reflecting its connection to traditional manual labor and craftsmanship.
- **examples:**
  - 他每天下午去做工。
    - Tā měitiān xiàwǔ qù zuò gōng.
    - He goes to work every afternoon.
  - 工( )厂(廠)里(裡)的( )机(機)器( )不( )停( )地( )运(運)转(轉)。
    - Gōng chǎng lǐ de jīqì bù tíng de yùnzhuǎn.
    - The machines in the factory run nonstop.
  - 这(這)位( )工( )人( )技( )艺(藝)精( )湛( )。
    - Zhè wèi gōngrén jìyì jīngzhàn.
    - This worker is highly skilled.

## 屌( )丝(絲)
---
## loser (internet slang)
### diǎosī
---
- **traditional:** 屌絲
- **definition:** loser (internet slang)
- **pinyin:** diǎosī
- **components:**
  - 屌
    - diǎo
    - male genitalia; slang for cocky
  - 丝(絲)
    - sī
    - silk; thread
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 屌 (diǎo, "male genitalia/slang for cocky/), 丝(絲) (sī, "silk/thread")
  - **interpretation:** 屌( )丝(絲) (diǎosī) is a slang term originating from Chinese internet culture, humorously referring to someone seen as a 'loser' or lacking in success by the traditional standards of wealth, prestige, or relationships. It juxtaposes the raw and blunt connotation of 屌 (diǎo, "male genitalia/slang for cocky") with the soft, delicate imagery of 丝(絲) (sī, "silk/thread"), creating an ironic label for individuals who might not meet conventional success criteria but embrace their circumstances with humor and defiance.
  - **simplification:** 丝→絲
- **examples:**
  - 他( )觉(覺)得( )自( )己( )是( )个(個)屌( )丝(絲)。
    - Tā juéde zìjǐ shìgè diǎosī.
    - He thinks he is a loser.
  - 他( )的( )朋( )友( )总(總)嘲( )笑( )他( )，认(認)为(為)他( )是( )个(個)屌( )丝(絲)。
    - Tā de péngyǒu zǒng cháoxiào tā, rènwéi tā shìgè diǎosī.
    - His friends always laugh at him, thinking he is a loser.
### 屌( )丝(絲) → 屌
- **definition:** male genitalia; slang for cocky
- **pinyin:** diǎo
- **components:**
  - 尭
    - yáo
    - high
  - 尸
    - shī
    - corpse
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 尭 (yáo, "high") as phonetic + 尸 (shī, "corpse") as semantic =
    - slang term originally referring to male genitalia ->
    - later extended to mean 'cocky' or 'arrogant'
  - **interpretation:** The character 屌(diao) cleverly combines elements to convey its vulgar meaning, using 尸 to set a bodily context and 尭 to suggest a crude and boastful tone.
- **examples:**
  - 他很屌。
    - Tā hěn diǎo.
    - He is really cocky.
  - 医(醫)生( )检(檢)查( )他( )的( )屌( )，发(發)现(現)有( )异(異)常( )。
    - Yīshēng jiǎnchá tā de diǎo, fāxiàn yǒu yìcháng.
    - The doctor examined his genitalia and found something abnormal.
### 屌( )丝(絲) → 屌 → 尭(堯)
- **traditional:** 堯
- **definition:** high
- **pinyin:** yáo
- **components:**
  - 垚
    - yáo
    - tall of earth
  - 兀
    - wù
    - towering and bald
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 垚 (yáo, "tall of earth") + 兀 (wù, "towering and bald") =
    - phonetic tall with semantic towering ->
    - high
  - **interpretation:** 堯 (yáo, "high") combines 垚 (yáo) and 兀 (wù) to signify something tall and prominent, reflective of its meaning 'high'. The ancient depiction included elements representing a structure rising from the earth, emphasizing elevation.
  - **simplification:** In Japanese shinjitai, 垚 was replaced with 卉 to form the simplified character 尧.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )梦(夢)想( )登( )上( )尭( )峰( )，俯( )瞰( )万(萬)里( )江( )山( )。
    - Tā mèngxiǎng dēng shàng yáofēng, fǔkàn wànlǐ jiāngshān.
    - He dreams of climbing the lofty peak and overlooking the vast country.
  - 这(這)座( )山( )峰( )巍( )然( )耸(聳)立( )，顶(頂)峰( )尭( )峙( )。
    - Zhè zuò shānfēng wēirán sǒnglì, dǐngfēng yáozhì.
    - This mountain rises majestically, its peak stands high.
### 屌( )丝(絲) → 屌 → 尭(堯) → 垚
- **definition:** tall of earth
- **pinyin:** yáo
- **components:**
  - 土
    - tǔ
    - earth
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 土 (tǔ, 'earth') tripled to depict 'tall of earth' ->
    - 土 + 土 + 土 =
    - 垚
  - **interpretation:** The character 垚(yáo, 'tall of earth') is created by tripling the element 土 (tǔ, 'earth'), symbolizing a large pile of earth or elevated ground.
### 屌( )丝(絲) → 屌 → 尭(堯) → 垚 → 土
- **definition:** earth
- **pinyin:** tǔ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a mound of earth
  - **interpretation:** The character 土(tǔ, "earth") visually represents a mound or piece of land, with a horizontal line indicating the surface and a vertical line the height of the mound. It's a straightforward depiction of earth or soil.
- **examples:**
  - 农(農)民( )耕( )土( )，种(種)出( )丰(豐)收( )。
    - Nóngmín gēng tǔ, zhòng chū fēngshōu.
    - Farmers cultivate the earth and harvest abundantly.
  - 院( )子( )里(裡)的( )土( )，适(適)合( )种(種)花( )。
    - Yuànzi lǐ de tǔ, shìhé zhòng huā.
    - The soil in the yard is suitable for planting flowers.
### 屌( )丝(絲) → 屌 → 尭(堯) → 兀
- **definition:** towering and bald
- **pinyin:** wù
- **components:**
  - 尢
    - wān
    - man with bent legs
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** variant form
  - **description:** 兀 is a variant form of 尢 (wān, "man with bent legs")
  - **interpretation:** 兀 originally represented a man with bent legs, indicative of jumping or collapsing. It connects to this meaning by representing something that stands out or is elevated, as in 'towering.' It reflects the semantic notion of standing out both physically and metaphorically.
- **examples:**
  - 那座山峰兀立，宛如巨人昂首。
    - Nà zuò shānfēng wùlì, wǎnrú jùrén ángshǒu.
    - That mountain peak towers, like a giant with its head held high.
  - 古( )树(樹)枝( )干(幹)兀( )然( )裸( )露( )，诉(訴)说(說)着(著)岁(歲)月( )沧(滄)桑( )。
    - Gǔ shù zhīgàn wùrán luǒlù, sùshuōzhe suìyuè cāngsāng.
    - The ancient tree's branches stand bald and exposed, telling tales of time’s vicissitudes.
### 屌( )丝(絲) → 屌 → 尭(堯) → 兀 → 尢
- **definition:** man with bent legs
- **pinyin:** wān
- **components:**
  - 尤
    - yóu
    - especially
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** variant form of 尢
  - **description:**
    - Pictogram: Originally depicts a hand with a wart ->
    - Variant form of 尤: retaining connotations of deformity
  - **interpretation:** 尢 is identified as a variant of 尤 and represents the notion of a man with bent legs, closely associating with deformity or deviation in form. The character still conveys variations perceived as abnormalities, similar to the original meaning of a hand with a wart.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )行( )走( )时(時)显(顯)露( )出( )尢( )样(樣)步( )态(態)，引( )得( )众(眾)人( )侧(側)目( )。
    - tā xíngzǒu shí xiǎnlù chū wān yàng bùtài, yǐn dé zhòngrén cèmù.
    - When he walked, his bent-legged gait was evident, attracting sidelong glances from the crowd.
  - 古( )籍( )记(記)载(載)尢( )人( )的( )形( )态(態)。
    - gǔjí jìzǎi wān rén de xíngtài.
    - Ancient texts record the appearance of a man with bent legs.
### 屌( )丝(絲) → 屌 → 尭(堯) → 兀 → 尢 → 尤
- **definition:** especially
- **pinyin:** yóu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a hand with a wart ->
    - Original form of 疣 (yóu, "wart") and 肬(躍) (yóu, "elevated")
  - **interpretation:** 尤 (yóu, "hand with a wart") is a pictogram that initially represented the image of a hand marked by a wart. It served as the original form for characters related to irregularities like 疣 (yóu, "wart").
- **examples:**
  - 医(醫)生( )检(檢)查( )时(時)发(發)现(現)他( )左( )手( )有( )一( )块(塊)尤( )，需( )及( )时(時)治( )疗(療)。
    - Yīshēng jiǎnchá shí fāxiàn tā zuǒshǒu yǒu yí kuài yóu, xū jíshí zhìliáo.
    - During the examination, the doctor discovered that his left hand had a wart and needed prompt treatment.
  - 古( )人( )检(檢)测(測)身( )相( )时(時)，会(會)观(觀)察( )是( )否( )有( )尤( )。
    - Gǔ rén jiǎncè shēnxiàng shí, huì guānchá shìfǒu yǒu yóu.
    - In ancient physiognomy, people would observe whether a hand with a wart was present.
### 屌( )丝(絲) → 屌 → 尸
- **definition:** corpse
- **pinyin:** shī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a man with legs bending ->
    - indicating a corpse's posture ->
    - linked to lying or resting position in ancient scripts
  - **interpretation:** 尸 (shī, "corpse") is a pictogram depicting a person in a position indicating death, mirroring a sitting or lying posture. This form captures the essence of a non-living body.
- **examples:**
  - 他在山洞中发现了尸(屍)。
    - Tā zài shāndòng zhōng fāxiàn le shī.
    - He discovered the corpse in the cave.
  - 考古学家在墓穴中找到了尸(屍)，他立即通知了当(當)局( )。
    - Kǎogǔ xuéjiā zài mùxué zhōng zhǎodào le shī, tā lìjí tōngzhī le dāngjú.
    - The archaeologists found the corpse in the tomb, and they immediately informed the authorities.
  - 夜( )幕( )降( )临(臨)时(時)，古( )庙(廟)前( )那( )具( )尸(屍)散( )发(發)出( )阴(陰)森( )气(氣)息( )。
    - Yèmù jiànglín shí, gǔ miào qián nà jù shī sànfā chū yīnsēn qìxī.
    - As night fell, the corpse in front of the ancient temple emitted a eerie aura.
### 屌( )丝(絲) → 丝(絲)
- **traditional:** 絲
- **definition:** silk; thread
- **pinyin:** sī
- **components:**
  - 糸
    - sī
    - silk
  - 糸
    - sī
    - silk
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 糸 (sī, "silk") + 糸 (sī, "silk") =
    - intertwined threads ->
    - silk/thread
  - **interpretation:** The character 絲 (sī, "silk") symbolizes the concept of silk or thread by combining two 糸 (sī, "silk") components, representing weaving or threading.
  - **simplification:** 絲 was simplified to 丝 by replacing the stacked 糸 forms with a cursive and more streamlined 𢇁 representation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )选(選)用( )蚕(蠶)丝(絲)，制(製)作( )精( )美( )的( )围(圍)巾( )。
    - Tā xuǎn yòng cán sī, zhì zuò jīng měi de wéi jīn.
    - He selects silkworm silk to make a beautiful scarf.
  - 古( )代( )诗(詩)人( )常( )以( )丝(絲)，比喻柔情。
    - Gǔ dài shī rén cháng yǐ sī, bǐ yù róu qíng.
    - Ancient poets often used silk as a metaphor for tenderness.
  - 这(這)件( )披( )肩( )由( )丝(絲)织(織)成( )精( )细(細)的( )花( )纹(紋)。
    - Zhè jiàn pī jiān yóu sī zhī chéng jīng xì de huā wén.
    - This shawl is woven from silk into intricate patterns.
### 屌( )丝(絲) → 丝(絲) → 糸
- **definition:** silk
- **pinyin:** sī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a skein of silk
  - **interpretation:** The character 糸(mì, "silk; thread") represents a pictorial representation of a skein of silk, visually capturing the concept of thread or fiber.
- **examples:**
  - 她( )手( )工( )制(製)作( )了( )一( )条(條)漂( )亮( )的( )丝(絲)巾( )。

    - She handcrafted a beautiful silk scarf.
  - 这(這)个(個)设(設)计(計)图(圖)必( )须(須)用( )细(細)糸( )画(畫)出( )来(來)。
    - Zhège shèjìtú bìxū yòng xì mì huà chūlái.
    - This design drawing must be sketched with fine thread.
  - 我( )们(們)参(參)观(觀)了( )古( )代( )的( )织(織)布( )技( )术(術)，对(對)丝(絲)绸(綢)制(製)作( )产(產)生( )了( )兴(興)趣( )。
    - Wǒmen cānguānle gǔdài de zhībù jìshù, duì sīchóu zhìzuò chǎnshēngle xìngqù.
    - We visited ancient weaving techniques and developed an interest in silk production.
