## Starbucks
### Xīngbākè
---
- **definition:** Starbucks
- **pinyin:** Xīngbākè
- **components:**
  - 星
    - xīng
    - star
  - 巴
    - bā
    - area; wish
  - 克
    - kè
    - to overcome; gram
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 星 (xīng, "star") + 巴 (bā, "area; wish") + 克 (kè, "to overcome; gram")
  - **interpretation:** 星巴克 (Xīngbākè) is a phonetic transcription of the name Starbucks, the international coffee company. The components 星 (xīng, "star"), 巴 (bā, "area; wish"), and 克 (kè, "to overcome; gram") are selected to approximate the sound of 'Starbucks' rather than to convey a specific meaningful translation.
- **examples:**
  - 我今天去星巴克，尝(嘗)试(試)新( )品( )。
    - Wǒ jīntiān qù Xīngbākè, chángshì xīn pǐn.
    - Today I went to Starbucks and tried new products.
  - 星( )巴( )克( )是( )我( )最( )喜( )欢(歡)的( )聚( )会(會)场(場)所( )。
    - Xīngbākè shì wǒ zuì xǐhuān de jùhuì chǎngsuǒ.
    - Starbucks is my favorite venue for gatherings.
  - 晚上我在星巴克，坐( )下( )来(來)写(寫)作( )业(業)。
    - Wǎnshàng wǒ zài Xīngbākè, zuò xiàlái xiě zuòyè.
    - At night, I sat down at Starbucks to do my homework.
### 星巴克 → 星
- **definition:** star
- **pinyin:** xīng
- **components:**
  - 晶
    - jīng
    - twinkling stars
  - 生
    - shēng
    - grow
  - 日
    - rì
    - sun
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phonetic: 晶 (jīng, "twinkling stars") + Phonetic: 生 (shēng, "grow") ->
    - 晶 later simplified to 日 (rì, "sun") ->
    - 星(星, "star")
  - **interpretation:** The character 星 (xīng, "star") combines the idea of twinkling from 晶 (jīng, "twinkling stars") with the concept of growth or life from 生 (shēng, "grow"). This combination effectively captures the shimmer and presence of stars in the night sky, aided by the later simplification of 晶 into the 日 (rì, "sun") component, maintaining a celestial association.
- **examples:**
  - 那( )颗(顆)星( )在( )黑( )夜( )中( )熠( )熠( )生( )辉(輝)。
    - Nà kē xīng zài hēiyè zhōng yìyì shēnghuī.
    - That star shines brilliantly in the dark night.
  - 天( )空( )布(佈)满(滿)星( )光( )，夜( )色( )显(顯)得( )格( )外( )神( )秘( )。
    - Tiānkōng bù mǎn xīnguāng, yèsè xiǎnde géwài shénmì.
    - The sky is filled with starlight, and the night appears especially mysterious.
### 星巴克 → 星 → 晶
- **definition:** twinkling stars
- **pinyin:** jīng
- **components:**
  - 日
    - rì
    - sun
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 日 (rì, "sun") tripled to depict multiple suns or sparkles ->
    - brightness or sparkle
  - **interpretation:** The character 晶(jīng) represents brightness by repeating the character 日 (rì, "sun") three times, symbolizing multiple sources of light or sparkles. This construction effectively conveys the sense of brilliance or clarity associated with crystals and sparkling objects.
- **examples:**
  - 透明的水晶，闪(閃)耀( )着(著)迷( )人( )的( )光( )芒( )。
    - Tòumíng de shuǐjīng, shǎnyào zhe mírén de guāngmáng.
    - The transparent crystal sparkles with enchanting light.
  - 湖面上映出倒影，散( )发(發)着(著)晶( )的( )光( )彩( )。
    - Húmiàn shàng yìngchū dàoyǐng, sànfā zhe jīng de guāngcǎi.
    - Reflections appear on the lake surface, emitting a sparkling brilliance.
  - 洁(潔)白( )的( )玉( )石( )中( )隐(隱)含( )着(著)晶( )的( )韵(韻)味( )。
    - Jiébái de yùshí zhōng yǐnhán zhe jīng de yùnwèi.
    - The pure jade subtly reveals a sparkling charm.
### 星巴克 → 星 → 晶 → 日
- **definition:** sun
- **pinyin:** rì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts the sun; a dot or line added in the center to distinguish from similar characters e.g. 囗
  - **interpretation:** As a pictogram, 日(rì, 'sun') directly represents the sun. In ancient times, the added dot helped visually differentiate it from other characters. This is analogous to the astronomical symbol ☉ and Egyptian hieroglyph 𓇳, both depicting the sun.
- **examples:**
  - 晨( )曦( )来(來)临(臨)时(時)，日( )出( )东(東)方( )。
    - Chénxī láilín shí, rìchū dōngfāng.
    - At the break of dawn, the sun rises in the east.
  - 温(溫)暖( )的( )日( )光( )洒(灑)满(滿)大( )地( )。
    - Wēnnuǎn de rìguāng sǎ mǎn dàdì.
    - The warm sunlight spreads over the earth.
  - 中( )午( )时(時)分( )，日( )高( )挂(掛)天( )。
    - Zhōngwǔ shífēn, rì gāo guà tiān.
    - At noon, the sun hangs high in the sky.
### 星巴克 → 星 → 生
- **definition:** grow
- **pinyin:** shēng
- **components:**
  - 屮
    - chè
    - sprout
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 屮 (chè, "sprout") on top of a horizontal line ->
    - signifies a plant sprouting from the ground ->
    - symbolizes life
  - **interpretation:** The character 生 (shēng, "life") represents the concept of life force or vitality, symbolized by a sprout emerging from the earth, embodying growth and renewal.
- **examples:**
  - 生( )命( )是( )一( )场(場)美( )丽(麗)的( )旅( )程( )。
    - Shēngmìng shì yī chǎng měilì de lǚchéng.
    - Life is a beautiful journey.
  - 他( )在( )战(戰)场(場)上( )奋(奮)力( )生( )存( )。
    - Tā zài zhànchǎng shàng fènlì shēngcún.
    - He fights to live on the battlefield.
  - 这(這)家( )餐( )厅(廳)的( )生( )鱼(魚)片( )非( )常( )新( )鲜(鮮)。
    - Zhè jiā cāntīng de shēngyúpiàn fēicháng xīnxiān.
    - The raw fish slices at this restaurant are very fresh.
### 星巴克 → 星 → 生 → 屮
- **definition:** sprout
- **pinyin:** chè
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a sprout emerging from the ground
  - **interpretation:** 屮 (chè, 'sprout') is a pictogram that directly represents a young plant or sprout emerging from the ground. Its visual form captures the essence of new growth, and it historically served as an early representation of plant life or new vegetation.
- **examples:**
  - 春( )雨( )滋( )润(潤)后(後)，小( )屮( )冲(沖)破( )泥( )土( )而( )出( )。
    - chūn yǔ zī rùn hòu, xiǎo chè chōng pò ní tǔ ér chū.
    - After the spring rain nurtured the earth, the little sprout broke through the soil.
  - 草屮初生露尖端。
    - cǎo chè chū shēng lù jiān duān.
    - The grass sprout's tip has just emerged.
### 星巴克 → 巴
- **definition:** area; wish
- **pinyin:** bā
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a snake coiled around a branch
  - **interpretation:** The character 巴(bā, "to cling") originally depicted a snake wrapped around a branch or object, symbolizing the idea of clinging or sticking to something.
- **examples:**
  - 她( )总(總)是( )巴( )着(著)妈(媽)妈(媽)。
    - Tā zǒngshì bā zhe māmā.
    - She always clings to her mother.
  - 巴( )先( )生( )今( )天( )出( )席( )会(會)议(議)，分( )享( )成( )功( )经(經)验(驗)。
    - Bā xiānshēng jīntiān chūxí huìyì, fēnxiǎng chénggōng jīngyàn.
    - Mr. Ba attended the meeting today, sharing his success experience.
### 星巴克 → 克
- **definition:** to overcome; gram
- **pinyin:** kè
- **components:**
  - 由
    - yóu
    - battle helmet
  - 皮
    - pí
    - pelt
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 由 (yóu, "battle helmet") + 皮 (pí, "pelt") =
    - to defeat or vanquish ->
    - 克
  - **interpretation:** The character 克 (kè) illustrates the concept of overcoming an opponent in battle, symbolized by the combination of a helmet and a pelt. Alternatively, it can depict a person carrying a load, signifying the ability to bear or undertake tasks competently.
- **examples:**
  - 这瓶药含50克(這瓶藥含50克)。
    - Zhè píng yào hán 50 kè.
    - This bottle of medicine contains 50 grams.
  - 他( )克( )服( )困( )难(難)，迈(邁)向( )成( )功( )。
    - Tā kèfú kùnnán, mài xiàng chénggōng.
    - He overcame difficulties and stepped towards success.
  - 她( )决(決)心( )克( )敌(敵)制( )胜(勝)，面( )对(對)挑( )战(戰)。
    - Tā juéxīn kè dí zhì shèng, miànduì tiǎozhàn.
    - She is determined to overcome her enemies and triumph when facing challenges.
### 星巴克 → 克 → 由
- **definition:** battle helmet
- **pinyin:** yóu
- **components:**
  - 十
    - shí
    - ten
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phonetic: 十 (shí, 'ten') + Semantic: 口 (kǒu, 'mouth') ->
    - container with a needle; oracle bones reflect original version of 怞 (yóu, 'cause') ->
    - final form 由
  - **interpretation:** The character 由 (yóu, 'cause') was originally a phono-semantic compound, combining 十 (shí, 'ten') as the phonetic component with 口 (kǒu, 'mouth') as the semantic component. This represents a container, indicating an ancient pronunciation. The character also has historical associations with oracle bone script and was an original form of 怞 (yóu, 'cause'), indicating the character's evolution from a depiction of an item to its modern meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)次( )事( )故( )由( )人( )为(為)失( )误(誤)造( )成( )。
    - Zhè cì shìgù yóu rénwéi shīwù zàochéng.
    - This accident was caused by human error.
  - 他( )的( )成( )功( )由( )坚(堅)持( )与(與)努( )力( )铸(鑄)就( )。
    - Tā de chénggōng yóu jiānchí yǔ nǔlì zhùjiù.
    - His success was built on perseverance and hard work.
  - 问(問)题(題)往( )往( )由( )细(細)节(節)引( )起( )。
    - Wèntí wǎngwǎng yóu xìjié yǐnqǐ.
    - Problems are often caused by details.
### 星巴克 → 克 → 由 → 十
- **definition:** ten
- **pinyin:** shí
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Original vertical stroke ->
    - later horizontal stroke added ->
    - forms a cross ->
    - represents 'ten'
  - **interpretation:** The character 十(shí, 'ten') represents the number ten with a cross shape that could be seen as tally marks. It evolved from a simple vertical stroke to a cross by adding a horizontal stroke, symbolizing a complete set of ten.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )有( )十( )本( )书(書)。
    - Wǒ yǒu shí běn shū.
    - I have ten books.
  - 花( )园(園)里(裡)有( )十( )棵( )树(樹)，景( )色( )十( )分( )优(優)美( )。
    - Huāyuán lǐ yǒu shí kē shù, jǐngsè shífēn yōuměi.
    - There are ten trees in the garden, and the scenery is very beautiful.
  - 十( )年( )磨( )一( )剑(劍)。
    - Shí nián mó yī jiàn.
    - It takes ten years of practice to hone a sword.
### 星巴克 → 克 → 由 → 口
- **definition:** mouth
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.
### 星巴克 → 克 → 皮
- **definition:** pelt
- **pinyin:** pí
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a surface or the skin of an animal
  - **interpretation:** The character 皮 (pí, 'surface') visually represents an animal hide or skin stretched out, emphasizing the idea of an outer covering or surface. It captures the concept of a boundary between the interior and exterior, as seen in animal skins used as material.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)张(張)桌( )子( )的( )皮( )很( )光( )滑( )。
    - Zhè zhāng zhuōzi de pí hěn guānghuá.
    - The surface of this table is very smooth.
  - 新( )鞋( )的( )皮( )非( )常( )柔( )软(軟)，适(適)合( )长(長)时(時)间(間)行( )走( )。
    - Xīn xié de pí fēicháng róuruǎn, shìhé cháng shíjiān xíngzǒu.
    - The surface of the new shoes is very soft and suitable for long walks.

## 高考
---
## national college entrance exam
### gāokǎo
---
- **definition:** national college entrance exam
- **pinyin:** gāokǎo
- **components:**
  - 高
    - gāo
    - high; tall; above average; loud
  - 考
    - kǎo
    - exam; test; to verify; to check
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 高 (gāo, "high") + 考 (kǎo, "exam") combines to form 高考 (gāokǎo, "national college entrance exam").
  - **interpretation:** 高考 (gāokǎo, "national college entrance exam") is a significant assessment in China that determines university entry for students. 高 (gāo, "high") indicates the level or importance of the exam, while 考 (kǎo, "exam") explicitly states its nature as a test.
- **examples:**
  - 高( )考( )决(決)定( )命( )运(運)。
    - Gāokǎo juédìng mìngyùn.
    - The national college entrance exam determines one's destiny.
  - 学(學)生( )努( )力( )复(複)习(習)，希( )望( )高( )考( )取( )得( )好( )成( )绩(績)。
    - Xuéshēng nǔlì fùxí, xīwàng gāokǎo qǔdé hǎo chéngjì.
    - Students study hard, hoping to achieve good results in the national college entrance exam.
### 高考 → 高
- **definition:** high; tall; above average; loud
- **pinyin:** gāo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a tall building
  - **interpretation:** The character 高(gāo, "high; tall") visually represents a tall structure or building, symbolizing height or elevation. Unlike 京(jīng, "capital"), it is straightforwardly a representation of height.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)座( )山( )高( )。
    - Zhè zuò shān gāo.
    - This mountain is high.
  - 那( )棵( )树(樹)长(長)得( )高( )，比( )其( )他( )树(樹)高( )很( )多( )。
    - Nà kē shù zhǎng de gāo, bǐ qítā shù gāo hěn duō.
    - That tree grows tall, much taller than the other trees.
  - 飞(飛)机(機)在( )高( )空( )飞(飛)行( )。
    - Fēijī zài gāokōng fēixíng.
    - The airplane flies in high altitude.
### 高考 → 考
- **definition:** exam; test; to verify; to check
- **pinyin:** kǎo
- **components:**
  - 老
    - lǎo
    - old
  - 丂
    - kǎo
    - phonetic component
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 老 (lǎo, "old") + 丂 (kǎo, phonetic component) =
    - old person giving an exam ->
    - exam, test
  - **interpretation:** The character 考 (kǎo, "exam") combines the semantic element 老 (lǎo, "old"), hinting at wisdom or authority, with the phonetic component 丂 (kǎo), signifying the act of verifying or testing knowledge.
- **examples:**
  - 明天，我( )要( )考( )数(數)学(學)。
    - Míngtiān, wǒ yào kǎo shùxué.
    - Tomorrow, I will take a math exam.
  - 上( )课(課)时(時)，老( )师(師)考( )查( )学(學)生( )作( )业(業)。
    - Shàngkè shí, lǎoshī kǎo chá xuéshēng zuòyè.
    - During class, the teacher checks the students' homework.
  - 政( )府( )考( )核( )公( )务(務)员(員)。
    - Zhèngfǔ kǎo hé gōngwùyuán.
    - The government evaluates civil servants.
### 高考 → 考 → 老
- **definition:** old
- **pinyin:** lǎo
- **components:**
  - 人
    - rén
    - man
  - 毛
    - máo
    - hair
  - 匕
    - bǐ
    - cane
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 人 (rén, "man") + 毛 (máo, "hair") + 匕 (bǐ, "cane") =
    - a man with long hair leaning on a cane ->
    - old man
  - **interpretation:** The character 老(lǎo) visually depicts aspects associated with age: a man with long hair and a cane. This combination intuitively suggests the concept of being elderly.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )虽(雖)然( )年( )老( )，但( )经(經)验(驗)丰(豐)富( )。
    - tā suīrán niánlǎo, dàn jīngyàn fēngfù.
    - He may be old, but he is experienced.
  - 他( )是( )我( )们(們)班( )的( )老( )老( )师(師)。
    - tā shì wǒmen bān de lǎo lǎoshī.
    - He is our class's venerable teacher.
  - 老( )故( )事( )传(傳)递(遞)智( )慧( )。
    - lǎo gùshi chuándì zhìhuì.
    - Old stories convey wisdom.
### 高考 → 考 → 老 → 人
- **definition:** man
- **pinyin:** rén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts side view of a standing man, highlighting an arm and a leg ->
    - visual representation of a person
  - **interpretation:** The character 人 (rén, 'person') effectively uses simple lines to depict the essential form of a human, making it immediately recognizable and intuitive as meaning 'person.'
- **examples:**
  - 他( )是( )个(個)好( )人( )。

    - Měi gè rén dōu yǒu zérèn, wèi shèhuì zuòchū gòngxiàn.
    - Every person has a responsibility to contribute to society.
  - 人在旅途中，心( )灵(靈)获(獲)得( )了( )成( )长(長)。
    - Rén zài lǚtú zhōng, xīnlíng huòdé le chéngzhǎng.
    - People gain growth along their journey.
### 高考 → 考 → 老 → 毛
- **definition:** hair
- **pinyin:** máo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a feather or a strand of hair
  - **interpretation:** The character 毛(máo, 'hair') visually represents a feather or hair, conveying the concept of something that is thin and soft.
- **examples:**
  - 猫(貓)的( )毛( )很( )柔( )软(軟)。
    - Māo de máo hěn róuruǎn.

  - 他( )早( )上( )醒( )来(來)时(時)，发(發)现(現)额(額)前( )飘(飄)落( )一( )缕(縷)毛( )。
    - Tā zǎoshang xǐng lái shí, fāxiàn é qián piāoluò yī lǚ máo.
    - When he woke up in the morning, he found a strand of hair floating on his forehead.
  - 他( )家( )小( )狗( )的( )毛( )非( )常( )蓬( )松(鬆)。
    - Tā jiā xiǎogǒu de máo fēicháng péngsōng.
    - His puppy's hair is very fluffy.
### 高考 → 考 → 老 → 匕
- **definition:** cane
- **pinyin:** bǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts the shape of a spoon or dagger, indicating an object with a handle.
  - **interpretation:** The character 匕 (bǐ, "cane") originally depicts a spoon or dagger, highlighting its application as a tool with a handle, which conceptually relates to a cane.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )匕( )支( )撑(撐)自( )己( )，慢( )慢( )走( )过(過)崎( )岖(嶇)的( )小( )路( )。
    - Tā yòng bǐ zhīchēng zìjǐ, màn màn zǒuguò qíqū de xiǎolù.
    - He supports himself with his cane, and slowly walks along the rugged path.
  - 老人依靠匕，缓(緩)缓(緩)行( )走( )在( )乡(鄉)间(間)小( )道( )。
    - Lǎorén yīkào bǐ, huǎn huǎn xíngzǒu zài xiāngjiān xiǎodào.
    - The old man relies on his cane, and gently walks on the country lane.
### 高考 → 考 → 丂
- **definition:** phonetic component
- **pinyin:** kǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a bending stem ->
    - used as a derivative component in other characters
  - **interpretation:** The character 丂 originally depicts a simple bent stem or blade of grass and has been used as a component in various other characters for its phonetic or traditional associations.

## Ximending, Taipei
### Xīmén Dīng
---
- **traditional:** 西門町
- **definition:** Ximending, Taipei
- **pinyin:** Xīmén Dīng
- **components:**
  - 西
    - xī
    - west
  - 門
    - mén
    - gate
  - 町
    - dīng
    - pavilion; village; ward
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 西 (xī, "west"), 門 (mén, "gate"), 町 (dīng, "pavilion; village; ward") is a term used in Taiwan to designate wards or urban districts.
  - **interpretation:** 西( )門(门)町( ) (Xīmén Dīng) refers to Ximending, a well-known shopping and entertainment district in Taipei, Taiwan. The name combines 西 (xī, "west") and 門 (mén, "gate"), indicating its historic location near the western gate of old Taipei, with 町 (dīng, "pavilion; village; ward") as a naming convention for urban districts, reflecting its vibrant community atmosphere.
  - **simplification:** 门
- **examples:**
  - 我( )打( )算( )这(這)个(個)星( )期( )去( )西( )门(門)町( )，体(體)验(驗)台(臺)北( )独(獨)特( )的( )文( )化( )。
    - Wǒ dǎsuàn zhège xīngqī qù Xīmén Dīng(Xīmén Dīng)，tǐyàn Táiběi dú tè de wénhuà(Táiběi dú tè de wénhuà)。
    - I plan to go to Ximending this week and experience Taipei's unique culture.
  - 我( )来(來)到( )西( )门(門)町( )，寻(尋)找( )时(時)尚( )新( )品( )。
    - Wǒ láidào Xīmén Dīng(Xīmén Dīng)，xúnzhǎo shíshàng xīnpǐn(xúnzhǎo shíshàng xīnpǐn)。
    - I came to Ximending to look for fashionable new products.
### 西( )门(門)町( ) → 西
- **definition:** west
- **pinyin:** xī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a bag or basket ->
    - borrowed for phonetic value to mean 'west'
  - **interpretation:** The character 西(xī, 'west') originally depicted a bag or basket and was phonetically borrowed to represent 'west'. Although traditionally linked to the image of a bird in a nest symbolizing the setting sun, this interpretation is disputed. Instead, it reflects a simple phonetic borrowing.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )家( )住( )在( )西( )边(邊)，附近有一座大山。
    - Tā jiā zhù zài xībiān, fùjìn yǒu yī zuò dà shān.
    - His home is on the west side, and there is a big mountain nearby.
  - 风(風)筝(箏)向( )西( )飞(飛)去( )。
    - Fēngzhēng xiàng xī fēi qù.
    - The kite flies toward the west.
### 西( )门(門)町( ) → 門
- **definition:** gate
- **pinyin:** mén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a double-door gate
  - **interpretation:** The character depicts a pair of doors, visually representing the concept of a door or gate, which is fundamental in indicating entrance and exit points in structures.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )走( )到( )门(門)口( )。
    - Wǒ zǒu dào ménkǒu.
    - I walk to the door.
  - 门(門)轻(輕)轻(輕)打( )开(開)，房( )客( )进(進)来(來)了( )。
    - Mén qīngqīng dǎkāi, fángkè jìn láile.
    - The door opened gently, and the tenant came in.
### 西( )门(門)町( ) → 町
- **definition:** pavilion; village; ward
- **pinyin:** dīng
- **components:**
  - 田
    - tián
    - field
  - 丁
    - dīng
    - fourth celestial stem
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 田 (field) + 丁 (phonetic) =
    - ward or village
  - **interpretation:** The character 町 combines 田(tián, “field”), representing land or area, with 丁(dīng, “fourth celestial stem”) to convey meanings related to an administrative district, such as a ward or village.
- **examples:**
  - 乡间有一座町，供( )人( )休( )憩( )赏(賞)景( )。
    - Xiāngjiān yǒuyī zuò dīng, gōng rén xiūqì shǎngjǐng.
    - In the countryside, there is a pavilion for relaxation and sightseeing.
  - 古老的町，坐( )落( )在( )青( )山( )之( )间(間)。
    - Gǔlǎo de dīng, zuòluò zài qīng shān zhī jiān.
    - The ancient pavilion is located among the green mountains.
  - 在( )町( )内(內)，居( )民( )们(們)保( )留( )着(著)传(傳)统(統)文( )化( )。
    - Zài dīng nèi, jūmín men bǎoliú zhe chuántǒng wénhuà.
    - Within the village, the residents preserve traditional culture.
### 西( )门(門)町( ) → 町 → 田
- **definition:** field
- **pinyin:** tián
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a field divided into four sections
  - **interpretation:** The character 田(tián, 'field') visually represents a field with sections, indicating its agricultural use and importance in ancient times.
- **examples:**
  - 农(農)民( )在( )田( )里(裡)耕( )作( )。
    - Nóngmín zài tián lǐ gēngzuò.
    - The farmer works in the field.
  - 那( )边(邊)的( )田( )长(長)满(滿)了( )庄(莊)稼( )，农(農)民( )收( )获(穫)忙( )碌( )。
    - Nàbiān de tián cháng mǎn le zhuāngjià, nóngmín shōuhuò mánglù.
    - The field over there is full of crops, and the farmer is busy harvesting.
  - 秋( )收( )时(時)节(節)，人( )们(們)忙( )于(於)收( )割( )田( )里(裡)的( )稻( )谷( )。
    - Qiūshōu shíjié, rénmen máng yú shōugē tián lǐ de dàogǔ.
    - During the harvest season, people are busy reaping the rice in the fields.
### 西( )门(門)町( ) → 町 → 丁
- **definition:** fourth celestial stem
- **pinyin:** dīng
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts the head of a nail ->
    - 丁
  - **interpretation:** The character 丁 originally depicted the head of a nail, emphasizing simplicity and directness. While it has developed other interpretations over time, such as a head or city wall, these relate to similar shapes and functions. Today, 釘 (dīng, 'nail') reflects this meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 工( )人( )熟( )练(練)地( )把( )丁( )敲( )进(進)了( )墙(牆)壁( )。
    - Gōngrén shúliàn de bǎ dīng qiāng jìnle qiángbì.
    - The worker skillfully hammered the nail into the wall.
  - 他( )精( )心( )挑( )选(選)了( )铁(鐵)质(質)丁( )，确(確)保( )家(傢)具(俱)稳(穩)固( )。
    - Tā jīngxīn tiāoxuǎnle tiě zhì dīng, quèbǎo jiājù wěngù.
    - He carefully selected the iron nail to ensure the furniture was stable.
  - 请(請)把( )散( )落( )的( )丁( )收( )好( )。
    - Qǐng bǎ sànluò de dīng shōuhǎo.
    - Please gather the scattered nails.

## rock climbing
### pānyán
---
- **definition:** rock climbing
- **pinyin:** pānyán
- **components:**
  - 攀
    - pān
    - climb
  - 岩
    - yán
    - rock; cliff
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 攀 (pān, "climb"), 岩 (yán, "rock; cliff")
  - **interpretation:** The word 攀岩 (pānyán) translates to "rock climbing," referring specifically to the activity of climbing on natural rock formations or artificial rock walls. 攀 (pān, "climb") represents the act of ascending, while 岩 (yán, "rock; cliff") specifies the natural surface being climbed. This combination effectively conveys the idea of physically climbing rock surfaces, either as a sport or recreational activity.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )喜( )欢(歡)攀( )岩( )。
    - Tā xǐhuān pānyán.
    - He likes rock climbing.
  - 周(週)末( )他( )去( )海( )边(邊)攀( )岩( )，体(體)验(驗)自( )然( )乐(樂)趣( )。
    - Zhōumò tā qù hǎibiān pānyán, tǐyàn zìrán lèqù.
    - On the weekend, he goes rock climbing by the seaside, experiencing the joy of nature.
  - 小( )明( )通( )过(過)攀( )岩( )，不( )断(斷)挑( )战(戰)自( )己( )。
    - Xiǎomíng tōngguò pānyán, bùduàn tiǎozhàn zìjǐ.
    - Xiaoming continuously challenges himself through rock climbing.
### 攀岩 → 攀
- **definition:** climb
- **pinyin:** pān
- **components:**
  - 樊
    - fán
  - 手
    - shǒu
    - hand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phonetic: 樊 (fán) + Semantic: 手 (shǒu, "hand") =
    - climbing using hands ->
    - climb
  - **interpretation:** The character 攀(pān, "climb") vividly conveys the action of climbing by combining the phonetic component 樊 (fán) with a depiction of a hand, emphasizing the physical effort involved.
- **examples:**
  - 他攀藤而上。
    - Tā pān téng ér shàng.
    - He climbs up the vines.
  - 探( )险(險)家( )攀( )登( )险(險)峰( )，追( )寻(尋)梦(夢)想( )之( )顶(頂)。
    - Tànxiǎnjiā pāndēng xiǎnfēng, zhuīxún mèngxiǎng zhī dǐng.
    - The adventurer climbs treacherous peaks, seeking the pinnacle of his dreams.
### 攀岩 → 攀 → 樊
- **pinyin:** fán
- **components:**
  - 𠬜
    - unknown
    - unknown
  - 棥
    - unknown
    - unknown
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound and ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 𠬜 (ungkown, "unknown") + phonetic: 棥 (unknown, "unknown") =
    - phono-semantic composition ->
    - 樊 (fán, "enclosure")
  - **interpretation:** The character 樊(fán) combines an unknown semantic component and the phonetic component 棥. Traditionally, it represents phonetic and semantic functionality together, symbolizing an enclosure or fence, although further clarification of 𠬜 is needed.
- **examples:**
  - 他姓樊。
    - Tā xìng fán.
    - His surname is Fan.
  - 樊( )先( )生( )今( )天( )来(來)访(訪)，带(帶)来(來)了( )珍( )贵(貴)的( )礼(禮)物( )。
    - Fán xiānshēng jīntiān lái fǎng, dài lái le zhēnguì de lǐwù.
    - Mr. Fan visited today, and he brought precious gifts.
### 攀岩 → 攀 → 樊 → 𠬜
- **definition:** unknown
- **pinyin:** unknown
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an ancient form of a kneeling or resting figure
  - **interpretation:** The character 𠬜 is an ancient pictogram that appears to depict a kneeling or resting figure, potentially used historically to represent states of rest or submission. However, specific details about its historical usage or associated sounds may be lost or unknown today.
### 攀岩 → 攀 → 樊 → 棥
- **definition:** unknown
- **pinyin:** unknown
- **components:**
  - 樊
    - fán
    - fence
  - 𠬜
    - unknown
    - unknown
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phonetic 棥 with semantic component (𠬜) ->
    - variant of 樊 (fán, "fence")
  - **interpretation:** 棥 is a variant form of 樊 (fán, "fence"), using the same phonetic element but differing in its semantic component. It combines the phonetic element 棥, possibly indicating a similar sound, with semantic hints from 𠬜, whose exact meaning in this context is not clearly defined.
### 攀岩 → 攀 → 手
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a hand with fingers; top stroke is a bent over middle finger, horizontal strokes represent fingers
  - **interpretation:** The character 手 (shǒu, "hand") is a direct pictorial representation of a human hand with all five fingers, which distinguishes it from similar characters representing claws or simplified fingers. This visually conveys its meaning as a hand clearly.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )手( )写(寫)字( )。
    - Tā yòng shǒu xiě zì.
    - He writes with his hand.
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)牵(牽)着(著)我( )的( )手( )走( )路( )。
    - Māmā qiān zhe wǒ de shǒu zǒu lù.
    - Mom holds my hand while walking.
  - 他( )的( )手( )很( )温(溫)暖( )，带(帶)给(給)我( )力( )量( )。
    - Tā de shǒu hěn wēn nuǎn, dài gěi wǒ lìliàng.
    - His hand is very warm, giving me strength.
### 攀岩 → 岩(巖)
- **traditional:** 巖
- **definition:** rock; cliff
- **pinyin:** yán
- **components:**
  - 山
    - shān
    - hill
  - 石
    - shí
    - rock
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 山 (shān, "hill") + 石 (shí, "rock") =
    - combined elements representing a rocky hill or cliff ->
    - 岩(巖) (yán, "rock; cliff")
  - **interpretation:** The character 岩(巖) (yán, "rock; cliff") combines 山 (shān, "hill") and 石 (shí, "rock") to convey the image of a rocky hill or a cliff. The original form 巖 emphasized the phonetic 嚴 (yán), but later 岩 became the more common form.
  - **simplification:** The character 岩(巖) was originally 巖 in traditional script, simplified by dropping the 嚴 component and directly combining 山 and 石 to retain both the sound and meaning efficiently.
- **examples:**
  - 那( )块(塊)岩( )静(靜)静(靜)矗( )立( )在( )河( )边(邊)。
    - Nà kuài yán jìng jìng chùlì zài hé biān.
    - That rock stands quietly by the river bank.
  - 登( )上( )岩( )顶(頂)，看( )尽(盡)山( )川( )风(風)光( )。
    - Dēng shàng yán dǐng, kàn jìn shān chuān fēng guāng.
    - Climb to the top of the rock and behold the scenic mountains and rivers.
  - 海( )浪( )拍( )打( )着(著)悬(懸)岩( )，发(發)出( )低( )沉( )的( )轰(轟)鸣(鳴)。
    - Hǎi làng pāidǎ zhe xuán yán, fāchū dīchén de hōngmíng.
    - The waves crash against the cliff, producing a deep, rumbling sound.
### 攀岩 → 岩(巖) → 山
- **definition:** hill
- **pinyin:** shān
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts three mountain peaks
  - **interpretation:** The character 山(shān, "mountain") visually represents the concept of a mountain using three vertical lines that suggest peaks. This depiction is consistent with early forms found in Oracle Bone Script, emphasizing the notion of natural elevation associated with mountains.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )们(們)去( )爬( )山( )。
    - Wǒmen qù pá shān.
    - We go climb the mountain.
  - 那( )座( )大( )山( )让(讓)人( )惊(驚)叹(嘆)。
    - Nà zuò dà shān ràng rén jīngtàn.
    - That mountain astonishes people.
  - 远(遠)处(處)的( )山( )隐(隱)约(約)在( )云(雲)中( )，令( )人( )心( )旷(曠)神( )怡( )。
    - Yuǎnchù de shān yǐnyuē zài yún zhōng, lìng rén xīnkuàng shényí.
    - The distant mountain fades into the clouds, leaving one in a state of peace.
### 攀岩 → 岩(巖) → 石
- **definition:** rock
- **pinyin:** shí
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a stone beneath a cliff, where the upper part originally looked like a cliff (厂) but was distorted into 丆 over time.
  - **interpretation:** The character 石(shí, 'stone') visually represents a stone in a natural setting, highlighting the element of a cliff or mountain. Its visual design aims to reflect the appearance of rocks lodged or embedded in a mountainous or cliff-like setting.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )在( )河( )边(邊)发(發)现(現)一( )块(塊)石( )。
    - Tā zài hé biān fāxiàn yī kuài shí.
    - He discovered a piece of stone by the riverside.
  - 孩( )子( )们(們)喜( )欢(歡)堆( )石( )，玩( )得( )开(開)心( )。
    - Háizimen xǐhuān duī shí, wán de kāixīn.
    - Children like to stack stones, playing happily.

## 叫
---
## call; shout
### jiào
---
- **definition:** call; shout
- **pinyin:** jiào
- **components:**
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
  - 丩
    - jiū
    - twist
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 口 (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 丩 (jiū, "twist") =
    - sound produced by mouth ->
    - call; shout
  - **interpretation:** The character 叫 (jiào, "call; shout") combines 口, representing a mouth making sounds, with 丩, a phonetic element. This reflects the act of shouting or calling, which involves using the mouth.
- **examples:**
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)叫( )我( )吃( )饭(飯)。
    - Māmā jiào wǒ chīfàn。
    - Mom calls me to eat.
  - 他( )叫( )朋( )友( )快( )来(來)，以( )免( )错(錯)过(過)盛( )会(會)。
    - Tā jiào péngyǒu kuài lái, yǐmiǎn cuòguò shènghuì。
    - He calls his friend to come quickly, so as not to miss the grand event.
  - 校( )长(長)叫( )学(學)生( )认(認)真( )听(聽)讲(講)。
    - Xiàozhǎng jiào xuéshēng rènzhēn tīngjiǎng。
    - The principal calls the students to listen attentively.
### 叫 → 口
- **definition:** mouth
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.
### 叫 → 丩
- **definition:** twist
- **pinyin:** jiū
- **components:**
  - 糾
    - jiū
    - entangle
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts two entangled ropes ->
    - original form of 糾 (jiū, 'entangle')
  - **interpretation:** 丩 (jiū, 'twist') visually represents two ropes intertwined, maintaining the concept of twisting or entangling. Its shape has influenced its derivative form 糾 (jiū, 'entangle'), which reflects the same idea in modern usage.
### 叫 → 丩 → 糾
- **definition:** entangle
- **pinyin:** jiū
- **components:**
  - 糸
    - sī
    - silk
  - 丩
    - jiū
    - to entangle
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 糸 (sī, "silk") + phonetic 丩 (jiū, "to entangle") ->
    - 糾 (jiū, "correct")
  - **interpretation:** The character 糾 (jiū, "correct") combines 糸 (sī, "silk") to suggest the idea of threads or strings involved in entangling or correcting, with 丩 (jiū, "to entangle") providing the phonetic element. Originally, 丩 referred to the concept of entanglement directly, and 糹 (the silk radical) was later added to emphasize the textile-related meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 他糾我( )的( )口( )误(誤)，并(並)讲(講)解( )正( )确(確)发(發)音( )。
    - Tā jiū wǒ de kǒuwù, bìng jiǎngjiě zhèngquè fāyīn.
    - He corrected my slip of the tongue and explained the correct pronunciation.
  - 经理要求员工糾报(報)告( )中( )的( )遗(遺)漏( )，以( )确(確)保( )准(準)确(確)性( )。
    - Jīnglǐ yāoqiú yuángōng jiū bàogào zhōng de yílòu, yǐ quèbǎo zhǔnquèxìng.
    - The manager required the employees to correct the omissions in the report to ensure accuracy.
  - 专家在讨论中糾理( )论(論)漏( )洞( )。
    - Zhuānjiā zài tǎolùn zhōng jiū lǐlùn lòudòng.
    - The expert corrected the theoretical loopholes during the discussion.
### 叫 → 丩 → 糾 → 糸
- **definition:** silk
- **pinyin:** sī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a skein of silk
  - **interpretation:** The character 糸(mì, "silk; thread") represents a pictorial representation of a skein of silk, visually capturing the concept of thread or fiber.
- **examples:**
  - 她( )手( )工( )制(製)作( )了( )一( )条(條)漂( )亮( )的( )丝(絲)巾( )。
    - Tā shǒugōng zhìzuòle yī tiáo piàoliang de sī jīn.
    - She handcrafted a beautiful silk scarf.
  - 这(這)个(個)设(設)计(計)图(圖)必( )须(須)用( )细(細)糸( )画(畫)出( )来(來)。
    - Zhège shèjìtú bìxū yòng xì mì huà chūlái.
    - This design drawing must be sketched with fine thread.
  - 我( )们(們)参(參)观(觀)了( )古( )代( )的( )织(織)布( )技( )术(術)，对(對)丝(絲)绸(綢)制(製)作( )产(產)生( )了( )兴(興)趣( )。
    - Wǒmen cānguānle gǔdài de zhībù jìshù, duì sīchóu zhìzuò chǎnshēngle xìngqù.
    - We visited ancient weaving techniques and developed an interest in silk production.

## 晶晶
---
## sparkling; gleaming
### jīngjīng
---
- **definition:** sparkling; gleaming
- **pinyin:** jīngjīng
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 晶 (jīng, "sparkle") repeated for emphasis, indicating something that is sparkling or gleaming.
  - **interpretation:** The repetition of 晶 (jīng, "sparkle") emphasizes the brightness or gleaming quality of an object or light. In Chinese, repeating a character can intensify the meaning, suggesting something that is shining or reflecting light vibrantly.
- **examples:**
  - 夜( )空( )布(佈)满(滿)星( )辰( )，晶( )晶( )光( )芒( )点(點)缀(綴)其( )间(間)。
    - Yè kōng bù mǎn xīngchén, jīngjīng guāngmáng diǎn zhuì qí jiān.
    - The night sky was filled with stars, and sparkling light decorated it.
  - 湖( )水( )泛( )起( )晶( )晶( )波( )纹(紋)，给(給)寂( )静(靜)的( )夜( )添( )了( )生( )机(機)。
    - Hú shuǐ fàn qǐ jīngjīng bōwén, gěi jìjìng de yè tiān le shēngjī.
    - The lake water rippled with sparkling waves, adding life to the quiet night.
  - 那( )颗(顆)钻(鑽)石( )晶( )晶( )夺(奪)目( )。
    - Nà kē zuànshí jīngjīng duó mù.
    - That diamond dazzled with a sparkling brilliance.
### 晶晶 → 晶
- **definition:** sparkle; sparkle; crystal; bright
- **pinyin:** jīng
- **components:**
  - 日
    - rì
    - sun
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 日 (rì, "sun") tripled to depict multiple suns or sparkles ->
    - brightness or sparkle
  - **interpretation:** The character 晶(jīng) represents brightness by repeating the character 日 (rì, "sun") three times, symbolizing multiple sources of light or sparkles. This construction effectively conveys the sense of brilliance or clarity associated with crystals and sparkling objects.
- **examples:**
  - 透明的水晶，闪(閃)耀( )着(著)迷( )人( )的( )光( )芒( )。
    - Tòumíng de shuǐjīng, shǎnyào zhe mírén de guāngmáng.
    - The transparent crystal sparkles with enchanting light.
  - 湖面上映出倒影，散( )发(發)着(著)晶( )的( )光( )彩( )。
    - Húmiàn shàng yìngchū dàoyǐng, sànfā zhe jīng de guāngcǎi.
    - Reflections appear on the lake surface, emitting a sparkling brilliance.
  - 洁(潔)白( )的( )玉( )石( )中( )隐(隱)含( )着(著)晶( )的( )韵(韻)味( )。
    - Jiébái de yùshí zhōng yǐnhán zhe jīng de yùnwèi.
    - The pure jade subtly reveals a sparkling charm.
### 晶晶 → 晶 → 日
- **definition:** sun
- **pinyin:** rì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts the sun; a dot or line added in the center to distinguish from similar characters e.g. 囗
  - **interpretation:** As a pictogram, 日(rì, 'sun') directly represents the sun. In ancient times, the added dot helped visually differentiate it from other characters. This is analogous to the astronomical symbol ☉ and Egyptian hieroglyph 𓇳, both depicting the sun.
- **examples:**
  - 晨( )曦( )来(來)临(臨)时(時)，日( )出( )东(東)方( )。
    - Chénxī láilín shí, rìchū dōngfāng.
    - At the break of dawn, the sun rises in the east.
  - 温(溫)暖( )的( )日( )光( )洒(灑)满(滿)大( )地( )。
    - Wēnnuǎn de rìguāng sǎ mǎn dàdì.
    - The warm sunlight spreads over the earth.
  - 中( )午( )时(時)分( )，日( )高( )挂(掛)天( )。
    - Zhōngwǔ shífēn, rì gāo guà tiān.
    - At noon, the sun hangs high in the sky.

## 汤(湯)
---
## soup
### tāng
---
- **traditional:** 湯
- **definition:** soup
- **pinyin:** tāng
- **components:**
  - 氵
    - shuǐ
    - water
  - 昜
    - yáng
    - bright
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 氵 (shuǐ, "water") + 昜 (yáng, "bright") =
    - liquid plus phonetic component ->
    - soup
  - **interpretation:** The character 湯 (tāng, "soup") combines the water radical 氵 (shuǐ, "water") to indicate liquid with the phonetic component 昜 (yáng, "bright"). This suggests an association with hot or cooked liquids, appropriate for soup.
  - **simplification:** 湯 simplified to 汤(湯) by altering 昜 to 𠃓 (a simpler component) for ease of writing while maintaining the phonetic similarity.
- **examples:**
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)煮( )了( )一( )锅(鍋)汤(湯)。
    - māmā zhǔ le yì guō tāng.
    - Mom cooked a pot of soup.
  - 汤(湯)里(裡)有( )蔬( )菜( )，味( )道( )鲜(鮮)美( )。
    - tāng lǐ yǒu shūcài, wèidào xiānměi.
    - The soup has vegetables, and it tastes delicious.
  - 我( )们(們)去( )饭(飯)馆(館)点(點)汤(湯)。
    - wǒmen qù fànguǎn diǎn tāng.
    - We went to the restaurant to order soup.
### 汤(湯) → 氵
- **definition:** water
- **pinyin:** shuǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 氵is the radical form of 水 (shuǐ, "water") ->
    - depicts flowing water, compressed and stylized next to characters.
  - **interpretation:** The character 氵is a simplified variant used as a radical to represent water-related meanings in other characters. It retains the flowing water depiction of 水 (shuǐ, "water") but is stylized to fit alongside other components.
- **examples:**
  - 水是生命之源。
    - Shuǐ shì shēngmìng zhī yuán.
    - Water is the source of life.
  - 汉(漢)字( )中( )偏( )旁( )氵( )，代表水。
    - Hànzì zhōng piān páng shuǐ, dàibiǎo shuǐ.
    - In Chinese characters, the radical '氵' signifies water.
  - 河水清澈透明，鱼(魚)儿(兒)欢(歡)快( )戏(戲)水( )。
    - Hé shuǐ qīngchè tòumíng, yú er huānkuaì xì shuǐ.
    - The river water is clear and transparent, and the fish happily play in it.
### 汤(湯) → 昜
- **definition:** bright
- **pinyin:** yáng
- **components:**
  - 日
    - rì
    - sun
  - 丂
    - kǎo
    - breath
  - 彡
    - xiǎn
    - rays of light
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 日 (rì, "sun") + 丂 (kǎo, "breath") + 彡 (xiǎn, "rays of light") =
    - rays of the sun shining over a tree branch
  - **interpretation:** The character 昜(yáng) portrays the concept of the sun's rays shining down, symbolized by 日 (rì, "sun") with 丂 representing a tree branch and 彡 depicting the sun's rays. This compound visually encapsulates the light given off by the sun.
- **examples:**
  - 日( )光( )照( )耀( )下( )时(時)，旗( )帜(幟)高( )昜( )飘(飄)扬(揚)。
    - Rìguāng zhàoyào xià shí, qízhì gāo yáng piāoyáng.
    - Under the shining sunlight, the flag is raised high.
  - 风(風)儿(兒)吹( )过(過)柳( )梢( )时(時)，心( )中( )的( )梦(夢)想( )也( )随(隨)昜( )起( )。
    - Fēng ér chuīguò liǔ shāo shí, xīn zhōng de mèngxiǎng yě suí yáng qǐ.
    - As the wind passes the tips of the willows, the dreams in the heart rise as well.
### 汤(湯) → 昜 → 日
- **definition:** sun
- **pinyin:** rì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts the sun; a dot or line added in the center to distinguish from similar characters e.g. 囗
  - **interpretation:** As a pictogram, 日(rì, 'sun') directly represents the sun. In ancient times, the added dot helped visually differentiate it from other characters. This is analogous to the astronomical symbol ☉ and Egyptian hieroglyph 𓇳, both depicting the sun.
- **examples:**
  - 晨( )曦( )来(來)临(臨)时(時)，日( )出( )东(東)方( )。
    - Chénxī láilín shí, rìchū dōngfāng.
    - At the break of dawn, the sun rises in the east.
  - 温(溫)暖( )的( )日( )光( )洒(灑)满(滿)大( )地( )。
    - Wēnnuǎn de rìguāng sǎ mǎn dàdì.
    - The warm sunlight spreads over the earth.
  - 中( )午( )时(時)分( )，日( )高( )挂(掛)天( )。
    - Zhōngwǔ shífēn, rì gāo guà tiān.
    - At noon, the sun hangs high in the sky.
### 汤(湯) → 昜 → 丂
- **definition:** breath
- **pinyin:** kǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts an upward flow of breath or vapor ->
    - used to denote breath
  - **interpretation:** The character 丂 (kǎo, "breath") is a pictogram that represents an upward flow of breath or vapor, suggesting the process of exhaling. Its form captures the movement associated with breathing.
### 汤(湯) → 昜 → 彡
- **definition:** rays of light
- **pinyin:** xiǎn
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts three strands of hair ->
    - used to indicate three light rays in certain contexts
  - **interpretation:** 彡(shān, "rays") originally depicted strands of hair but also came to represent light rays, reflecting a change in symbolic meaning over time. Its use as a representation of 'ornament' and in characters like 昜 and 參 highlights its versatility in indicating rays or decorative elements.
- **examples:**
  - 天( )空( )洒(灑)下( )金( )色( )的( )彡( )，大( )地( )迎( )来(來)温(溫)暖( )。
    - Tiānkōng sǎ xià jīnsè de shān, dàdì yíng lái wēnnuǎn.
    - The sky showers golden rays, and the earth welcomes warmth.
  - 晨( )曦( )中( )的( )彡( )闪(閃)耀( )着(著)希( )望( )。
    - Chénxī zhōng de shān shǎnyào zhe xīwàng.
    - The rays in the morning light shine with hope.

## 超
---
## exceed; surpass
### chāo
---
- **definition:** exceed; surpass
- **pinyin:** chāo
- **components:**
  - 走
    - zǒu
    - run
  - 召
    - zhào
    - summon
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 走 (zǒu, "run") + phonetic: 召 (zhào, "summon") =
    - running to summon or surpass ->
    - exceed
  - **interpretation:** The character 超 (chāo) combines the concept of running 走 (zǒu, "run") with a sound from 召 (zhào, "summon"), implying going beyond or surpassing norms or limits, just like running might surpass a walking pace.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )的( )成( )绩(績)超( )出( )班( )级(級)平( )均( )水( )平( )。
    - tā de chéngjì chāochū bānjí píngjūn shuǐpíng
    - His performance exceeds the class average.
  - 他( )在( )比( )赛(賽)中( )超( )越( )对(對)手( )，赢(贏)得( )冠( )军(軍)。
    - tā zài bǐsài zhōng chāoyuè duìshǒu, yíngdé guànjūn
    - He surpassed his opponents in the competition and won the championship.
  - 他( )的( )热(熱)情( )超( )乎( )想( )象( )，鼓( )舞( )了( )全( )体(體)队(隊)员(員)。
    - tā de rèqíng chāohū xiǎngxiàng, gǔwǔ le quántǐ duìyuán
    - His enthusiasm exceeded all expectations and inspired the whole team.
### 超 → 走
- **definition:** run
- **pinyin:** zǒu
- **components:**
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - foot
  - 夭
    - yāo
    - young
  - 土
    - tǔ
    - earth
  - 龰
    - yuè
    - walking
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Running man (original depiction) + 止 (zhǐ, "foot") added later ->
    - top 夭 (yāo, "young") simplified to 土 (tǔ, "earth"), bottom stylized as 龰 ->
    - modern 走
  - **interpretation:** The character originally depicted a man running, with an added foot to emphasize movement. Over time, elements like the top 夭 were simplified to 土, and the bottom was stylized to 龰, although they are unrelated to the original meaning.
- **examples:**
  - 看( )到( )前( )方( )火( )光( )时(時)，他( )急( )忙( )走( )向( )安( )全( )地( )带(帶)。
    - Kàndào qiánfāng huǒguāng shí, tā jímáng zǒu xiàng ānquán dìdài.
    - When he saw the firelight ahead, he hurriedly ran toward safety.
  - 比( )赛(賽)开(開)始( )后(後)，选(選)手( )们(們)奋(奮)力( )走( )向( )终(終)点(點)。
    - Bǐsài kāishǐ hòu, xuǎnshǒu men fènlì zǒu xiàng zhōngdiǎn.
    - After the race began, the contestants ran towards the finish line.
### 超 → 走 → 止
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zhǐ
- **components:**
  - 趾
    - zhǐ
    - foot
  - 龰
    - yù
    - stylized feet
  - 手
    - shǒu
    - hand
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Original: Foot pointing up with 3 toes and a sole ->
    - Stylized as 龰 ->
    - Derivative 趾 refers to original 'foot' meaning
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicting a footprint with toes and a sole, 止 (zhǐ, "foot") represents the foot. The character's form resembles a foot with three toes, analogous to how the hand is represented with fingers in other characters.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)的( )止( )需( )要( )静(靜)养(養)。
    - Tā shòu shāng de zhǐ xū yào jìng yǎng.
    - His injured foot needs adequate rest.
  - 他( )走( )路( )时(時)小( )心( )谨(謹)慎( )，因( )为(爲)止( )若( )受( )伤(傷)便( )难(難)前( )行( )。
    - Tā zǒu lù shí xiǎo xīn jǐn shèn, yīn wèi zhǐ ruò shòu shāng biàn nán qián xíng.
    - He walks carefully because if his foot gets injured, it becomes difficult to move forward.
### 超 → 走 → 止 → 趾
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zhǐ
- **components:**
  - 足
    - zú
    - foot
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - stop
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 足 (zú, "foot") + 止 (zhǐ, "stop") =
    - semantic: foot + phonetic: stop ->
    - toe
  - **interpretation:** The character combines 足 (zú, "foot") as the semantic indicator and 止 (zhǐ, "stop") as the phonetic component to represent the idea of a 'toe,' which is a part of the foot.
- **examples:**
  - 他的趾因寒冷而僵硬了。
    - tā de zhǐ yīn hánlěng ér jiāngyìng le.
    - His toe became stiff due to the cold.
  - 我( )走( )路( )时(時)，不小心扭到了趾。
    - wǒ zǒulù shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ dào le zhǐ.
    - While I was walking, I accidentally twisted my toe.
  - 古( )代( )剑(劍)客( )战(戰)斗(鬥)时(時)，常( )因( )疏( )忽( )伤(傷)及( )趾( )。
    - gǔdài jiànkè zhàndòu shí, cháng yīn shūhū shāng jí zhǐ.
    - Ancient swordsmen often injured their toes due to carelessness during battles.
### 超 → 走 → 止 → 趾 → 足
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zú
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a leg ->
    - originally depicted as ⽦ ->
    - represents 'foot'
  - **interpretation:** The character 足(zú, 'foot') is a pictographic representation of a human leg, which directly portrays the concept of a 'foot'. The pictographic origin emphasizes its fundamental meaning in the language.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)后(後)，他( )的( )足( )不( )能( )动(動)。
    - tā shòushāng hòu, tā de zú bù néng dòng。
    - After he was injured, his foot couldn't move.
  - 小( )明( )打( )球( )时(時)，不( )小( )心( )扭( )伤(傷)了( )足( )。
    - xiǎo míng dǎ qiú shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ shāng le zú。
    - When Xiaoming was playing ball, he accidentally twisted his foot.
  - 我( )每( )天( )锻(鍛)炼(鍊)足( )部( )。
    - wǒ měitiān duànliàn zúbù。
    - I exercise my foot muscles every day.
### 超 → 走 → 止 → 龰
- **definition:** stylized feet
- **pinyin:** yù
- **components:**
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - foot; stop
  - 爪
    - zhǎo
    - claw; hand
  - 寸
    - cùn
    - inch
  - 九
    - jiǔ
    - nine
  - 又
    - yòu
    - again
  - 彐
    - jì
    - snout
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a stylized foot with three toes pointing up, evolved from the original footprint depiction 止 (zhǐ, "foot; stop")
  - **interpretation:** 龰(yù, "stylized feet") is a pictogram representing a foot. The character has evolved to be a stylized depiction of human feet, originally indicating footprints with three toes in oracle script, similar to the depiction of hands in characters like 爪, 寸, 九, 又, and 彐.
### 超 → 走 → 止 → 龰 → 爪
- **definition:** claw; hand
- **pinyin:** zhǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a hand grabbing from above, resembles a claw ->
    - stylized to represent claws
  - **interpretation:** The character 爪 (zhǎo, "claw") originally depicted a hand grabbing from above, stylized to resemble the form of claws.
- **examples:**
  - 猫(貓)的( )爪( )非( )常( )敏( )捷( )。
    - Māo de zhǎo fēicháng mǐnjié.
    - The cat's claws are very agile.
  - 狗( )的( )爪( )稳(穩)固( )，使( )它( )轻(輕)松(鬆)爬( )山( )。
    - Gǒu de zhǎo wěngù, shǐ tā qīngsōng páshān.
    - The dog's claws are sturdy, allowing it to climb mountains easily.
  - 鹰(鷹)的( )爪( )锋(鋒)利( )，能( )牢( )牢( )抓( )住( )猎(獵)物( )。
    - Yīng de zhǎo fēnglì, néng láoláo zhuā zhù lièwù.
    - The eagle's claws are sharp, enabling it to firmly grasp its prey.
### 超 → 走 → 止 → 龰 → 寸
- **definition:** inch
- **pinyin:** cùn
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:**
    - Line on the hand (ideographic) emphasizing the elbow ->
    - represents measurement unit between wrist and specific forearm position ->
    - meaning 'inch'
  - **interpretation:** 寸 (cùn, "inch") originated as a depiction of a line on the hand, indicating the area near the elbow. This specific placement was considered significant for pulse palpation, originally suggesting a forearm position, which then came to denote the measurement 'inch'.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)块(塊)布( )每( )一( )寸( )都( )是( )精( )心( )制(製)作( )。
    - Zhè kuài bù měi yī cùn dōu shì jīngxīn zhìzuò.
    - Every inch of this cloth is crafted with care.
  - 请(請)用( )卷( )尺( )量( )一( )寸( )，每( )一( )寸( )数(數)据(據)都( )准(準)确(確)。
    - Qǐng yòng juǎnchǐ liàng yī cùn, měi yī cùn shùjù dōu zhǔnquè.
    - Please use a tape measure to measure an inch, every inch's data is accurate.
### 超 → 走 → 止 → 龰 → 九
- **definition:** nine
- **pinyin:** jiǔ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a stylized hand with bent elbow ->
    - original meaning 'elbow' lost ->
    - adopted as 'nine' for metaphorical bumping against ten
  - **interpretation:** 九 (jiǔ, 'nine') originated as a depiction of a hand with a bent elbow, similar to 手 (shǒu, 'hand'). Over time, its original meaning 'elbow' was lost, and it came to represent 'nine' symbolizing the bump of a fist against ten.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )有( )九( )本( )书(書)。
    - Wǒ yǒu jiǔ běn shū.
    - I have nine books.
  - 九( )个(個)学(學)生( )努( )力( )学(學)习(習)，追求卓越。
    - Jiǔ gè xuéshēng nǔlì xuéxí, zhuīqiú zhuóyuè.
    - Nine students study diligently and pursue excellence.
  - 房( )间(間)里(裡)有( )九( )把( )椅( )子( )。
    - Fángjiān lǐ yǒu jiǔ bǎ yǐzi.
    - There are nine chairs in the room.
### 超 → 走 → 止 → 龰 → 又
- **definition:** again
- **pinyin:** yòu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a right hand
  - **interpretation:** The character 又 (yòu, "hand") is a pictogram representing a right hand, showing the fingers and palm, which highlights its original meaning related to manual actions.
- **examples:**
  - 「又」这个字最早来源于手(「又」這個字最早來源於手)。
    - 「Yòu」 zhè gè zì zuì zǎo láiyuán yú shǒu。
    - The character '又' originally comes from the hand.
  - 很( )多( )汉(漢)字( )含( )有( )又( )部( )首( )，表( )示( )与(與)手( )有( )关(關)的( )动(動)作( )。
    - Hěn duō hànzì hányǒu Yòu bùshǒu, biǎoshì yǔ shǒu yǒuguān de dòngzuò。
    - Many Chinese characters include the '又' radical, indicating actions related to the hand.
  - 古( )人( )用( )又( )描( )绘(繪)手( )的( )形( )态(態)，体(體)现(現)了( )古( )汉(漢)字( )的( )独(獨)特( )魅( )力( )。
    - Gǔrén yòng Yòu miáohuì shǒu de xíngtài, tǐxiàn le gǔ hànzì de dútè mèilì。
    - Ancient people used '又' to depict the shape of the hand, showcasing the unique charm of ancient Chinese characters.
### 超 → 走 → 止 → 龰 → 彐
- **definition:** snout
- **pinyin:** jì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a hand with fingers stylized as three lines, holding something
  - **interpretation:** The character 彐(jì, 'snout') is a pictogram that resembles a hand grasping an object, with its fingers represented by three lines. While originally a variant of 彑, depicting a pig's snout, in many characters, it’s simplified to symbolize a hand, often portrayed with three fingers. This visual stylization captures the essence of the hand in a simplified form, commonly seen in various characters.
- **examples:**
  - 大( )猪(豬)的( )彐( )显(顯)得( )特( )别(別)精( )致(緻)。
    - Dà zhū de jì xiǎn de tèbié jīngzhì.
    - The pig's snout appears particularly exquisite.
  - 我( )在( )书(書)中( )见(見)到( )彐( )，了( )解( )到( )它( )代( )表( )了( )动(動)物( )的( )鼻( )部( )。
    - Wǒ zài shū zhōng jiàn dào jì, liǎo jiě dào tā dài biǎo le dòngwù de bí bù.
    - I encountered the snout in the book and learned that it represents an animal's nose.
  - 古( )代( )铭(銘)文( )中( )彐( )的( )形( )象( )生( )动(動)。
    - Gǔdài míngwén zhōng jì de xíngxiàng shēngdòng.
    - In ancient inscriptions, the depiction of the snout is vivid.
### 超 → 走 → 止 → 手
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a hand with fingers; top stroke is a bent over middle finger, horizontal strokes represent fingers
  - **interpretation:** The character 手 (shǒu, "hand") is a direct pictorial representation of a human hand with all five fingers, which distinguishes it from similar characters representing claws or simplified fingers. This visually conveys its meaning as a hand clearly.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )手( )写(寫)字( )。
    - Tā yòng shǒu xiě zì.
    - He writes with his hand.
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)牵(牽)着(著)我( )的( )手( )走( )路( )。
    - Māmā qiān zhe wǒ de shǒu zǒu lù.
    - Mom holds my hand while walking.
  - 他( )的( )手( )很( )温(溫)暖( )，带(帶)给(給)我( )力( )量( )。
    - Tā de shǒu hěn wēn nuǎn, dài gěi wǒ lìliàng.
    - His hand is very warm, giving me strength.
### 超 → 走 → 夭
- **definition:** young
- **pinyin:** yāo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a figure leaning forward, presumably running ->
    - energetic, young
  - **interpretation:** The character 夭(yāo) ("young") visually represents someone leaning forward in a motion that suggests energy and vitality associated with youthfulness. This imagery aligns with the concept of being lively or young.
- **examples:**
  - 他还夭，意( )气(氣)风(風)发(發)。
    - Tā hái yāo, yìqì fēngfā。
    - He is still young, full of youthful vigor.
  - 小女孩还夭，充( )满(滿)无(無)限( )活( )力( )。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái hái yāo, chōngmǎn wúxiàn huólì。
    - The little girl is still young, filled with boundless energy.
### 超 → 走 → 土
- **definition:** earth
- **pinyin:** tǔ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a mound of earth
  - **interpretation:** The character 土(tǔ, "earth") visually represents a mound or piece of land, with a horizontal line indicating the surface and a vertical line the height of the mound. It's a straightforward depiction of earth or soil.
- **examples:**
  - 农(農)民( )耕( )土( )，种(種)出( )丰(豐)收( )。
    - Nóngmín gēng tǔ, zhòng chū fēngshōu.
    - Farmers cultivate the earth and harvest abundantly.
  - 院( )子( )里(裡)的( )土( )，适(適)合( )种(種)花( )。
    - Yuànzi lǐ de tǔ, shìhé zhòng huā.
    - The soil in the yard is suitable for planting flowers.
### 超 → 召
- **definition:** summon
- **pinyin:** zhào
- **components:**
  - 刀
    - dāo
    - knife
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 刀 (dāo, "knife") + 口 (kǒu, "mouth") =
    - call out with one's mouth ->
    - summon
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicted hands over a wine vessel, symbolizing hospitality or serving wine, illustrating summoning guests. Later evolved to include 口 to emphasize calling out or summoning orally, while 刀 serves as a phonetic marker.
- **examples:**
  - 国(國)王( )召( )集( )忠( )诚(誠)的( )骑(騎)士( )，前( )往( )讨(討)论(論)国(國)事( )。
    - guówáng zhàojí zhōngchéng de qíshì, qiánwǎng tǎolùn guóshì.
    - The king summons loyal knights, heading off to discuss state affairs.
  - 校( )长(長)召( )见(見)家( )长(長)，讨(討)论(論)学(學)生( )表( )现(現)。
    - xiàozhǎng zhàojiàn jiāzhǎng, tǎolùn xuéshēng biǎoxiàn.
    - The principal summons the parents to discuss the students' performance.
### 超 → 召 → 刀
- **definition:** knife
- **pinyin:** dāo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a knife
  - **interpretation:** 刀 (dāo, "knife") is a pictographic representation of a knife or blade, with a sharp edge and a handle, resembling the form of a basic cutting tool.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )锋(鋒)利( )的( )刀( )，切( )开(開)蔬( )菜( )。
    - tā yòng fēnglì de dāo, qiē kāi shūcài.
    - He uses a sharp knife to cut vegetables.
  - 这(這)把( )刀( )非( )常( )锋(鋒)利( )。
    - zhè bǎ dāo fēicháng fēnglì.
    - This knife is very sharp.
  - 厨(廚)师(師)小( )心( )地( )持( )刀( )，为(為)客( )人( )切( )肉( )。
    - chúshī xiǎoxīn de chí dāo, wèi kèrén qiē ròu.
    - The chef holds the knife carefully and slices meat for the guest.
### 超 → 召 → 口
- **definition:** mouth
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.

## 高潮
---
## high tide; climax
### gāocháo
---
- **definition:** high tide; climax
- **pinyin:** gāocháo
- **components:**
  - 高
    - gāo
    - high; tall; above; elevated
  - 潮
    - cháo
    - tide; wave; current; moist
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 高 (gāo, "high") + 潮 (cháo, "tide") =
    - high tide or climax
  - **interpretation:** The word combines the idea of a tide reaching its highest point with the metaphorical use of reaching a peak experience, such as a climax in a narrative or emotional build-up. This dual meaning reflects the physical phenomenon of a tide and the figurative peak in various situations.
- **examples:**
  - 海( )浪( )达(達)到( )高( )潮( )时(時)，船( )只(隻)紧(緊)急( )返( )航( )。
    - Hǎilàng dádào gāocháo shí, chuánzhī jǐnjí fǎnháng.
    - When the waves reach high tide, the ships return urgently.
  - 电(電)影( )情( )节(節)达(達)到( )高( )潮( )时(時)，观(觀)众(眾)情( )绪(緒)高( )涨(漲)。
    - Diànyǐng qíngjié dádào gāocháo shí, guānzhòng qíngxù gāozhǎng.
    - When the film's plot reaches its climax, the audience's emotions surge.
### 高潮 → 高
- **definition:** high; tall; above; elevated
- **pinyin:** gāo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a tall building
  - **interpretation:** The character 高(gāo, "high; tall") visually represents a tall structure or building, symbolizing height or elevation. Unlike 京(jīng, "capital"), it is straightforwardly a representation of height.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)座( )山( )高( )。
    - Zhè zuò shān gāo.
    - This mountain is high.
  - 那( )棵( )树(樹)长(長)得( )高( )，比( )其( )他( )树(樹)高( )很( )多( )。
    - Nà kē shù zhǎng de gāo, bǐ qítā shù gāo hěn duō.
    - That tree grows tall, much taller than the other trees.
  - 飞(飛)机(機)在( )高( )空( )飞(飛)行( )。
    - Fēijī zài gāokōng fēixíng.
    - The airplane flies in high altitude.
### 高潮 → 潮
- **definition:** tide; wave; current; moist
- **pinyin:** cháo
- **components:**
  - 氵
    - shuǐ
    - water
  - 朝
    - zhāo
    - morning
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 氵 (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic: 朝 (zhāo, "morning") =
    - water movements ->
    - tide
  - **interpretation:** The character 潮 (cháo, "tide") combines the water radical 氵 (shuǐ, "water") with the phonetic component 朝 (zhāo, "morning"), indicating movements or changes associated with water, such as tides.
- **examples:**
  - 海( )潮( )退( )去( )时(時)，沙( )滩(灘)变(變)得( )湿(濕)润(潤)。
    - Hǎi cháo tuìqù shí, shātān biàn dé shīrùn.
    - When the tide recedes, the beach becomes moist.
  - 他( )家( )衣( )服( )总(總)是( )半( )潮( )半( )湿(濕)。
    - Tā jiā yīfú zǒng shì bàn cháo bàn shī.
    - The clothes at his home are always somewhat damp.
  - 晚( )风(風)吹( )来(來)，海( )边(邊)的( )潮( )声(聲)渐(漸)起( )。
    - Wǎn fēng chuī lái, hǎibiān de cháo shēng jiàn qǐ.
    - The evening breeze blows, and the sound of the tide by the sea gradually rises.
### 高潮 → 潮 → 氵
- **definition:** water
- **pinyin:** shuǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 氵is the radical form of 水 (shuǐ, "water") ->
    - depicts flowing water, compressed and stylized next to characters.
  - **interpretation:** The character 氵is a simplified variant used as a radical to represent water-related meanings in other characters. It retains the flowing water depiction of 水 (shuǐ, "water") but is stylized to fit alongside other components.
- **examples:**
  - 水是生命之源。
    - Shuǐ shì shēngmìng zhī yuán.
    - Water is the source of life.
  - 汉(漢)字( )中( )偏( )旁( )氵( )，代表水。
    - Hànzì zhōng piān páng shuǐ, dàibiǎo shuǐ.
    - In Chinese characters, the radical '氵' signifies water.
  - 河水清澈透明，鱼(魚)儿(兒)欢(歡)快( )戏(戲)水( )。
    - Hé shuǐ qīngchè tòumíng, yú er huānkuaì xì shuǐ.
    - The river water is clear and transparent, and the fish happily play in it.
### 高潮 → 潮 → 朝
- **definition:** morning
- **pinyin:** zhāo
- **components:**
  - 月
    - yuè
    - moon
  - 𦣞
    - unknown
    - phonetic component
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Semantic: 月 (yuè, 'moon') + Phonetic: 𦣞 (unknown) =
    - aspects of the day ->
    - morning
  - **interpretation:** The character 朝 (zhāo, 'morning') combines the semantic component 月 (yuè, 'moon') suggesting a time associated with the moon, while the phonetic component 𦣞 lends its sound. Together, these elements capture the transition from night to day, or morning.
- **examples:**
  - 朝風拂面。
    - Zhāo fēng fú miàn.
    - The morning breeze caresses one's face.
  - 朝露滋潤花朵。
    - Zhāo lù zī rùn huā duǒ.
    - The morning dew nourishes the flowers.
  - 朝霜悄降山野，預兆豐收季節。
    - Zhāo shuāng qiāo jiàng shān yě, yù zhào fēng shōu jì jié.
    - The morning frost silently falls on the mountains and fields, signaling a season of abundant harvest.
### 高潮 → 潮 → 朝 → 月
- **definition:** moon
- **pinyin:** yuè
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a crescent moon
  - **interpretation:** The character 月(yuè, "moon") is a pictographic representation of a crescent moon, straightforwardly capturing the curved shape often associated with the moon's appearance. Though interchangeable with 夕 (xī, "evening") in the past, it distinctly represents the moon in this usage.
- **examples:**
  - 夜( )空( )挂(掛)着(著)明( )月( )。

### 阴(陰)道( ) → 阴(陰)
- **traditional:** 陰
- **definition:** concealed; shade; moon; female
- **pinyin:** yīn
- **components:**
  - 阜
    - fù
    - mound
  - 侌
    - yīn
    - phonetic
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Semantic: 阜 (fù, "mound") + Phonetic: 侌 (yīn) =
    - concealed places or shade ->
    - moon and female associations
  - **interpretation:** The character 阴(陰) (yīn, "concealed") uses 阜 (fù, "mound") to suggest a connection to physical features like hills or mounds that create shade, while 侌 (yīn) contributes the sound. This relates to concepts of concealment, shadow, and indirectly the moon and femininity in traditional Chinese thought.
  - **simplification:** The character 阴 replaces the phonetic 侌 (yīn) with 月 (yuè, "moon"), making it visually more simple and retaining the association with moon-related meanings.
- **examples:**
  - 北( )方( )的( )阴(陰)雨( )绵(綿)绵(綿)，让(讓)人( )感( )到( )忧(憂)郁(鬱)。
    - Běifāng de yīnyǔ miánmián, ràng rén gǎndào yōuyù.
    - The continuous gloomy drizzle in the north makes people feel depressed.
  - 她( )展( )现(現)出( )阴(陰)柔( )之( )美( )，令( )人( )着(著)迷( )。
    - Tā zhǎnxiàn chū yīnróu zhī měi, lìng rén zháomí.
    - She displays a unique feminine grace that captivates people.
  - 夜( )幕( )降( )临(臨)时(時)，月( )色( )显(顯)得( )格( )外( )阴(陰)柔( )。
    - Yèmù jiànglín shí, yuèsè xiǎndé géwài yīnróu.
    - As night falls, the moonlight appears especially soft and feminine.

### 阴(陰)道( ) → 道
- **definition:** path; way; method; teaching
- **pinyin:** dào
- **components:**

  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - foot

  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Semantic 行 (háng, "street") + phonetic 𩠐 + semantic 止 (zhǐ, "foot") ->
    - 亍 disappears, components combine into 辵 ->
    - final form 道
  - **interpretation:** The character 道(dào) combines elements associated with a street and foot to convey the meaning of a path or way. The evolution and merging of components reflect the concept of movement along a path, ultimately forming the character we recognize today.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )们(們)走( )上( )正( )道( )。
    - Wǒmen zǒu shàng zhèng dào.

    - Gǔrén tōngguò xiūshēn dá dào, tànqiú zìrán de fǎzé.
    - Ancient people attained the Tao through self-cultivation and sought the laws of nature.
  - 武( )术(術)不( )仅(僅)是( )一( )种(種)技( )艺(藝)，更( )是( )一( )种(種)修( )道( )之( )路( )。

    - 彳 (chì, "step") + 亍 (chù, "step")
  - **interpretation:** The character 行(xíng) visually represented a street intersection, with two symmetrical parts symbolizing the act of going or walking along paths. Despite its current breakdown into separate elements, it was originally a unified pictogram rather than a compound.

### 阴(陰)道( ) → 道 → 止
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zhǐ
- **components:**
  - 趾
    - zhǐ
    - foot
  - 龰
    - yù
    - stylized feet
  - 手
    - shǒu
    - hand

  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Original: Foot pointing up with 3 toes and a sole ->
    - Stylized as 龰 ->
    - Derivative 趾 refers to original 'foot' meaning
  - **interpretation:** Originally depicting a footprint with toes and a sole, 止 (zhǐ, "foot") represents the foot. The character's form resembles a foot with three toes, analogous to how the hand is represented with fingers in other characters.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)的( )止( )需( )要( )静(靜)养(養)。
    - Tā shòu shāng de zhǐ xū yào jìng yǎng.
    - His injured foot needs adequate rest.
  - 他( )走( )路( )时(時)小( )心( )谨(謹)慎( )，因( )为(爲)止( )若( )受( )伤(傷)便( )难(難)前( )行( )。
    - Tā zǒu lù shí xiǎo xīn jǐn shèn, yīn wèi zhǐ ruò shòu shāng biàn nán qián xíng.

### 阴(陰)道( ) → 道 → 止 → 趾
- **definition:** foot

- **components:**
  - 足
    - zú
    - foot
  - 止
    - zhǐ
    - stop

  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 足 (zú, "foot") + 止 (zhǐ, "stop") =
    - semantic: foot + phonetic: stop ->
    - toe
  - **interpretation:** The character combines 足 (zú, "foot") as the semantic indicator and 止 (zhǐ, "stop") as the phonetic component to represent the idea of a 'toe,' which is a part of the foot.
- **examples:**
  - 他的趾因寒冷而僵硬了。

    - His toe became stiff due to the cold.
  - 我( )走( )路( )时(時)，不小心扭到了趾。

    - While I was walking, I accidentally twisted my toe.
  - 古( )代( )剑(劍)客( )战(戰)斗(鬥)时(時)，常( )因( )疏( )忽( )伤(傷)及( )趾( )。
    - gǔdài jiànkè zhàndòu shí, cháng yīn shūhū shāng jí zhǐ.
    - Ancient swordsmen often injured their toes due to carelessness during battles.
### 阴(陰)道( ) → 道 → 止 → 趾 → 足
- **definition:** foot
- **pinyin:** zú
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a leg ->
    - originally depicted as ⽦ ->
    - represents 'foot'
  - **interpretation:** The character 足(zú, 'foot') is a pictographic representation of a human leg, which directly portrays the concept of a 'foot'. The pictographic origin emphasizes its fundamental meaning in the language.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )受( )伤(傷)后(後)，他( )的( )足( )不( )能( )动(動)。
    - tā shòushāng hòu, tā de zú bù néng dòng。
    - After he was injured, his foot couldn't move.
  - 小( )明( )打( )球( )时(時)，不( )小( )心( )扭( )伤(傷)了( )足( )。
    - xiǎo míng dǎ qiú shí, bù xiǎoxīn niǔ shāng le zú。
    - When Xiaoming was playing ball, he accidentally twisted his foot.
  - 我( )每( )天( )锻(鍛)炼(鍊)足( )部( )。
    - wǒ měitiān duànliàn zúbù。
    - I exercise my foot muscles every day.
### 阴(陰)道( ) → 道 → 止 → 龰
- **definition:** stylized feet
- **pinyin:** yù
- **components:**
  - 趾
    - zhǐ
    - toe
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a footprint pointing up as 龰 ->
    - stylized as 趾 (zhǐ, "toe") ->
    - derivative usage as stylized feet
  - **interpretation:** The character 龰 (yù, "stylized feet") is a stylization of a footprint pointing upwards, originally depicting a foot. This character evolved into 趾 (zhǐ, "toe") which represents the original meaning more directly. In ancient scripts, it was shown with three toes and a sole, signifying its depiction of a human foot.
### 阴(陰)道( ) → 道 → 止 → 手
- **definition:** hand
- **pinyin:** shǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a hand with fingers; top stroke is a bent over middle finger, horizontal strokes represent fingers
  - **interpretation:** The character 手 (shǒu, "hand") is a direct pictorial representation of a human hand with all five fingers, which distinguishes it from similar characters representing claws or simplified fingers. This visually conveys its meaning as a hand clearly.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )用( )手( )写(寫)字( )。
    - Tā yòng shǒu xiě zì.
    - He writes with his hand.
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)牵(牽)着(著)我( )的( )手( )走( )路( )。
    - Māmā qiān zhe wǒ de shǒu zǒu lù.

    - Tā de shǒu hěn wēn nuǎn, dài gěi wǒ lìliàng.
    - His hand is very warm, giving me strength.

  - 具
    - jù
    - tool; implement
