## Da’an District, Taipei
### Dà’ān Qū
---
- **traditional:** 大安區
- **definition:** Da’an District, Taipei
- **pinyin:** Dà’ān Qū
- **components:**
  - 大安
    - Dà’ān
    - Da’an; great peace
  - 区(區)
    - qū
    - district; area; region
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 大 (dà, "big") + 安 (ān, "peace") form the name 大安 (Dà’ān, "Da’an") to refer to 'Da’an District,' a significant district in Taipei, Taiwan. It is named with the aspiration for 'great peace,' and these character meanings are common in place names throughout Chinese culture. 區(区) (qū, "district") indicates an administrative division.
  - **interpretation:** The combination 大安 (Dà’ān, "Da’an") implies a place of 'great peace' or tranquility. The name reflects aspirations or historical ideals for harmony within the region. Located in Taipei, 大( )安( )區(区) (Dà’ān Qū, "Da’an District") is known for its dynamic neighborhoods and vibrant culture.
  - **simplification:** 區
- **examples:**
  - 我住在大( )安( )区(區)。
    - Wǒ zhù zài Dà’ān Qū.
    - I live in Da’an District.
  - 大( )安( )区(區)融( )合( )传(傳)统(統)与(與)现(現)代( )，交( )通( )便( )利( )且( )设(設)施( )完( )善( )。
    - Dà’ān Qū rónghé chuántǒng yǔ xiàndài, jiāotōng biànlì qiě shèshī wánshàn.
    - Da’an District integrates tradition with modernity, with convenient transportation and complete facilities.
  - 大( )安( )区(區)是( )台( )北( )的( )文( )化( )中( )心( )，吸( )引( )了( )众(眾)多( )游(遊)客( )。
    - Dà’ān Qū shì Táiběi de wénhuà zhōngxīn, xīyǐn le zhòngduō yóukè.
    - Da’an District is the cultural center of Taipei, attracting many tourists.
### 大( )安( )区(區) → 大安
- **definition:** Da’an; great peace
- **pinyin:** Dà’ān
- **components:**
  - 大
    - dà
    - great; big; large; vast
  - 安
    - ān
    - peace; safe; quiet; calm
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 大 (dà, "great") + 安 (ān, "peace")
  - **interpretation:** The word 大安 (dà'ān) combines 大 (dà, "great") and 安 (ān, "peace") to convey the concept of 'great peace.' This term can symbolize a state or aspiration of profound tranquility and stability. It might also refer to place names, as 'Da’an' (大安) is a common toponym in Chinese-speaking regions.
- **examples:**
  - 大( )安( )象( )征(徵)了( )永( )恒(恆)的( )和( )平( )。
    - Dà’ān xiàngzhēng le yǒnghéng de hépíng.
    - Da'an symbolizes eternal peace.
  - 古( )时(時)人( )们(們)追( )求( )大( )安( )，以( )获(獲)得( )心( )灵(靈)的( )安( )宁(寧)。
    - Gǔshí rénmen zhuīqiú Dà’ān, yǐ huòdé xīnlíng de ānníng.
    - In ancient times, people pursued Da'an to attain inner tranquility.
  - 走( )进(進)大( )安( )社( )区(區)，体(體)验(驗)宁(寧)静(靜)生( )活( )。
    - Zǒu jìn Dà’ān shèqū, tǐyàn níngjìng shēnghuó.
    - Step into the Da'an community and experience a peaceful life.
### 大( )安( )区(區) → 大安 → 大
- **definition:** great; big; large; vast
- **pinyin:** dà
- **components:**
  - 夫
    - fū
    - man
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a person facing forward ->
    - Borrowed phonetically for 'big'; original form of 夫 (fū, 'man')
  - **interpretation:** The character 大(dà) depicts a person facing forward, representing 'big' by suggesting the idea of someone standing prominently. Although often thought of as a person with outstretched arms, early scripts do not support this interpretation. It was originally the form of 夫 (fū, 'man') and was later phonetically borrowed for the meaning 'big.'
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)个(個)房( )子( )很( )大( )。
    - Zhè ge fángzi hěn dà.
    - This house is very big.
  - 大( )海( )波( )涛(濤)汹(洶)涌(湧)，展( )现(現)出( )大( )自( )然( )的( )力( )量( )。
    - Dàhǎi bōtāo xiōngyǒng, zhǎnxiàn chū dàzìrán de lìliàng.
    - The vast ocean waves surge, showcasing the power of nature.
  - 大( )人( )物( )登( )场(場)，留( )下( )传(傳)奇( )。
    - Dà rénwù dēngchǎng, liúxià chuánqí.
    - Great figures make an appearance, leaving behind legends.
### 大( )安( )区(區) → 大安 → 大 → 夫
- **definition:** man
- **pinyin:** fū
- **components:**
  - 大
    - dà
    - big
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a variant form of 大 (dà, "big") representing an adult man with a hairpin ->
    - became associated with 夫 (fū, "man").
  - **interpretation:** 夫 (fū, "man") was visually similar to 大 (dà, "big") and was originally interchangeable. The addition of the horizontal stroke was interpreted as a hairpin, symbolizing adulthood for men.
- **examples:**
  - 此( )夫( )心( )怀(懷)大( )志( )。
    - Cǐ fū xīn huái dà zhì.
    - This man has lofty aspirations.
  - 他( )勤( )劳(勞)努( )力( )，堪( )称(稱)良( )夫( )。
    - Tā qín láo nǔ lì, kān chēng liáng fū.
    - He works diligently and is regarded as an exemplary man.
### 大( )安( )区(區) → 大安 → 安
- **definition:** peace; safe; quiet; calm
- **pinyin:** ān
- **components:**
  - 宀
    - mián
    - house
  - 女
    - nǚ
    - a kneeling woman
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 宀 (mián, "house") + 女 (nǚ, "a kneeling woman") =
    - a woman at ease in the house ->
    - peace, safety, calm
  - **interpretation:** The combination of a house and a kneeling woman conveys the sense of tranquility and safety; it symbolizes a harmonious and secure environment.
- **examples:**
  - 小( )村( )庄(莊)一( )直( )很( )安( )。
    - Xiǎo cūnzhuāng yīzhí hěn ān.
    - The small village has always been peaceful.
  - 经(經)过(過)风(風)雨( )洗( )礼(禮)，生( )活( )变(變)得( )安( )。
    - Jīngguò fēngyǔ xǐlǐ, shēnghuó biàn de ān.
    - After enduring storms and rains, life becomes calm.
  - 远(遠)离(離)喧( )嚣(囂)后(後)，他( )找( )到( )了( )内(內)心( )的( )安( )。
    - Yuǎnlí xuānxiāo hòu, tā zhǎodàole nèixīn de ān.
    - After escaping the hustle and bustle, he found inner peace.
### 大( )安( )区(區) → 大安 → 安 → 宀
- **definition:** house
- **pinyin:** mián
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a house with four walls ->
    - roof
  - **interpretation:** 宀(mián, "roof") is a pictogram representing a house with four walls. It emphasizes an enclosed space, distinguishing it from similar structures like 广(guǎng, "broad") which depicts a building with three walls. This makes 宀 a logical choice for representing a roof in various characters.
- **examples:**
  - 含( )有( )宀( )的( )汉(漢)字( )代( )表( )家( )的( )温(溫)暖( )。
    - Hán yǒu mián de hànzì dàibiǎo jiā de wēnnuǎn.
    - Chinese characters that include the radical '宀' represent the warmth of home.
  - 屋( )顶(頂)遮( )风(風)挡(擋)雨( )，家( )园(園)散( )发(發)温(溫)馨( )。
    - Wūdǐng zhē fēng dǎng yǔ, jiāyuán sànfā wēnxīn.
    - The roof shields from wind and rain, and the home radiates warmth.
### 大( )安( )区(區) → 大安 → 安 → 女
- **definition:** a kneeling woman
- **pinyin:** nǚ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a woman kneeling or standing ->
    - enclosed area as remnant of right breast
  - **interpretation:** The character 女(nǚ, "woman") is a pictogram originally depicting a woman with highlighted bodily features, such as the breasts, symbolizing femininity. Its orientation changed from the Oracle Bone script to face right, simplifying over time while still retaining the essence of its original depiction.
- **examples:**
  - 那( )位( )才( )华(華)横(橫)溢( )的( )女( )，赢(贏)得( )了( )全( )场(場)注( )目( )。
    - Nà wèi cáihuá héngyì de nǚ, yíng dé le quánchǎng zhùmù.
    - That talented woman won everyone's attention.
  - 古时候‘女’常被用来象征温柔(古時候‘女’常被用來象徵溫柔)，而( )如( )今( )它( )代( )表( )独(獨)立( )自( )信( )。
    - Gǔshíhòu ‘nǚ’ cháng bèi yòng lái xiàngzhēng wēnróu, ér rújīn tā dàibiǎo dúlì zìxìn.
    - In ancient times, the character '女' was often used to symbolize gentleness, whereas today it represents independence and confidence.
### 大( )安( )区(區) → 区(區)
- **traditional:** 區
- **definition:** district; area; region
- **pinyin:** qū
- **components:**
  - 匸
    - xì
    - enclosure
  - 品
    - pǐn
    - goods/people
  - 乂
    - yì
    - to manage
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 匸 (enclosed space) + 品 (many people) =
    - many people within an enclosure ->
    - area, district
  - **interpretation:** The character 區 (qū, 'district') visually represents an area where many people reside, symbolized by 品 (pǐn, 'goods/people') enclosed within 匸 (xì, 'enclosure'). This layout effectively conveys the idea of a defined space containing a community or district.
  - **simplification:** 區 was simplified to 区(區) by reducing 品 to 乂 (yì, 'to manage'), keeping the enclosure meaning while reducing strokes.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )住( )在( )繁( )华(華)的( )区(區)。
    - Wǒ zhù zài fán huá de qū.
    - I live in a bustling district.
  - 古( )代( )文( )人( )常( )在( )这(這)区(區)，寻(尋)觅(覓)灵(靈)感( )。
    - Gǔ dài wén rén cháng zài zhè qū, xún mì líng gǎn.
    - Ancient literati often sought inspiration in this region.
  - 他( )参(參)观(觀)了( )多( )个(個)区(區)，体(體)验(驗)了( )不( )同( )的( )文( )化( )。
    - Tā cān guān le duō gè qū, tǐ yàn le bù tóng de wén huà.
    - He visited several districts and experienced diverse cultures.
### 大( )安( )区(區) → 区(區) → 匸
- **definition:** enclosure
- **pinyin:** xì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 𠃊 (symbolic graphic) ->
    - depicts an enclosure hiding something or someone ->
    - evolved to represent a concealed space or item
  - **interpretation:** The character 匸(xì, 'box') is a pictogram representing a concealed or enclosed space. Its graphical form suggests something contained or hidden, which aligns with its meaning as a 'box' or an enclosure. Unlike other related pictograms that depict physical containers, 匸 emphasizes the aspect of concealment or secrecy.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )把( )匸( )搬( )到( )了( )房( )间(間)角( )落( )。
    - Tā bǎ xì bān dào le fángjiān jiǎoluò.
    - He moved the box to the corner of the room.
  - 我( )们(們)在( )仓(倉)库(庫)找( )到( )了( )匸( )，里(裡)面( )装(裝)有( )秘( )密( )文( )件( )。
    - Wǒmen zài cāngkù zhǎodào le xì, lǐmiàn zhuāng yǒu mìmì wénjiàn.
    - We found the box in the warehouse, and it contained secret documents.
  - 工( )厂(廠)工( )人( )把( )旧(舊)匸( )清( )理( )了( )，然( )后(後)再( )利( )用( )。
    - Gōngchǎng gōngrén bǎ jiù xì qīnglǐ le, ránhòu zài lìyòng.
    - Factory workers cleaned the old box and then reused it.
### 大( )安( )区(區) → 区(區) → 品
- **definition:** goods/people
- **pinyin:** pǐn
- **components:**
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - Three 口 (kǒu, 'mouth') arranged together =
    - multiple mouths speaking or consuming ->
    - representing many or various items
  - **interpretation:** The use of three mouths suggests plurality or variety. It's similar in structure to 晶 (jīng, 'sparkle'), which uses three 日 (rì, 'sun') to suggest brightness or vibrancy with multiple light sources.
- **examples:**
  - 比( )赛(賽)吸( )引( )了( )数(數)以( )千( )计(計)的( )品( )，个(個)个(個)英( )姿( )飒(颯)爽( )。
    - Bǐsài xīyǐnle shù yǐ qiān jì de pǐn, gège yīngzī sàshuǎng.
    - The competition attracted thousands of people, and everyone looked heroic.
  - 节(節)日( )庆(慶)典( )上( )品( )众(眾)多( )，场(場)面( )热(熱)烈( )非( )凡( )。
    - Jiérì qìngdiǎn shàng pǐn zhòngduō, chǎngmiàn rèliè fēifán.
    - The festive celebration saw a large crowd, and the scene was extraordinarily lively.
  - 广(廣)场(場)上( )聚( )集( )了( )众(眾)多( )品( )，大( )家( )热(熱)情( )互( )动(動)。
    - Guǎngchǎng shàng jùjíle zhòngduō pǐn, dàjiā rèqíng hùdòng.
    - The square gathered a multitude of people, and everyone interacted enthusiastically.
### 大( )安( )区(區) → 区(區) → 品 → 口
- **definition:** mouth
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.
### 大( )安( )区(區) → 区(區) → 乂
- **definition:** to manage
- **pinyin:** yì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a tool (similar to a sickle or a pair of scissors) for mowing or weeding ->
    - used for governing ->
    - final form 乂
  - **interpretation:** The character 乂 originally depicted a tool used for cutting, and by extension, it was associated with governing due to the notion of cutting or managing weeds as a metaphor for ruling effectively. This sense of order is crucial in governance.
- **examples:**
  - 这些年，他致力于地方的治理(這些年，他致力於地方的治理)。
    - Zhèxiē nián, tā zhìlì yú dìfāng de zhìlǐ.
    - Over the years, he has dedicated himself to local governance.
  - 为民者，先自齐以治家(為民者，先自齊以治家)。
    - Wèi mín zhě, xiān zì qí yǐ zhì jiā.
    - Those who govern for the people, first manage their own households.
  - 长(長)者( )都( )希( )望( )后(後)代( )能( )好( )好( )治( )理( )天( )下( )。
    - Zhǎng zhě dōu xīwàng hòudài néng hǎohǎo zhìlǐ tiānxià.
    - The elders hope that future generations can govern the world well.

### Dà’ān Qū

## 酱(醬)
---
## sauce
### jiàng
---
- **traditional:** 醬
- **definition:** sauce
- **pinyin:** jiàng
- **components:**
  - 酉
    - yǒu
    - drink
  - 將
    - jiāng
    - lead
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 酉 (yǒu, "drink") + 將 (jiāng, "lead") =
    - liquid for cooking or drinking ->
    - sauce
  - **interpretation:** The character combines the idea of fermented liquids, like those used in drink, with a phonetic hint from 將 (jiāng, "lead"), suggesting a mixture or preparation, which fits the concept of a sauce.
  - **simplification:** 醬 simplified to 酱 by replacing 將 (jiāng, "lead") with its cursive form 丬 (pán, "half tree trunk") and 夕 (xī, "evening") for phonetic similarity.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)瓶( )酱(醬)很( )好( )吃( )。
    - Zhè píng jiàng hěn hǎo chī.
    - This bottle of sauce is delicious.
  - 妈(媽)妈(媽)做( )菜( )时(時)，总(總)喜( )欢(歡)用( )自( )制(製)的( )酱(醬)。
    - Māmā zuò cài shí, zǒng xǐhuān yòng zì zhì de jiàng.
    - When cooking, mom always likes using homemade sauce.
  - 厨(廚)房( )里(裡)的( )酱(醬)香( )浓(濃)郁( )。
    - Chúfáng lǐ de jiàng xiāng nóng yù.
    - The aroma of the sauce in the kitchen is rich.
### 酱(醬) → 酉
- **definition:** drink
- **pinyin:** yǒu
- **components:**
  - 酒
    - jiǔ
    - alcohol
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a wine vessel with a pointed bottom ->
    - original character for 酒 (jiǔ, "alcohol")
  - **interpretation:** 酉 (yǒu, "complete cycle") originally depicted a wine vessel, indicating the integral role of alcohol in traditional cycles, such as moon phases or ceremonial events. It is the original character for 酒 (jiǔ, "alcohol"), highlighting its use in fermentation processes.
- **examples:**
  - 每( )一( )次( )回( )望( )旧(舊)地( )，都( )仿(彷)佛(彿)见(見)证(證)了( )一( )个(個)酉( )的( )完( )结(結)。
    - Měi yīcì huíwàng jiùdì, dōu fǎngfú jiànzhèngle yīgè yǒu de wánjié.
    - Every time I look back, it seems to witness a complete cycle coming to an end.
  - 日( )落( )黄(黃)昏( )后(後)，大( )地( )经(經)历(歷)了( )一( )个(個)酉( )的( )循( )环(環)。
    - Rìluò huánghūn hòu, dàdì jīnglìle yīgè yǒu de xúnhuán.
    - After sunset, the land has undergone a complete cycle.
### 酱(醬) → 酉 → 酒
- **definition:** alcohol
- **pinyin:** jiǔ
- **components:**
  - 氵
    - shuǐ
    - water
  - 酉
    - yǒu
    - complete cycle
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 氵 (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic: 酉 (yǒu, "complete cycle") =
    - a liquid associated with 酉 (fermentation/vessels) ->
    - alcohol
  - **interpretation:** The character 酒 (jiǔ, "alcohol") uses 氵 (shuǐ, "water") to indicate a liquid, and 酉 (yǒu, "complete cycle") to suggest fermentation or storage vessels, representing alcoholic beverages.
- **examples:**
  - 他喝了太多酒。
    - Tā hēle tài duō jiǔ.
    - He drank too much alcohol.
  - 夜( )深( )人( )静(靜)时(時)，他( )独(獨)自( )品( )酒( )。
    - Yè shēn rén jìng shí, tā dúzì pǐn jiǔ.
    - In the quiet of the night, he savored his drink alone.
  - 那杯香醇的酒，令( )他( )回( )味( )无(無)穷(窮)。
    - Nà bēi xiāngchún de jiǔ, lìng tā huíwèi wúqióng.
    - That glass of aromatic alcohol left him with an unforgettable aftertaste.
### 酱(醬) → 酉 → 酒 → 氵
- **definition:** water
- **pinyin:** shuǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 氵is the radical form of 水 (shuǐ, "water") ->
    - depicts flowing water, compressed and stylized next to characters.
  - **interpretation:** The character 氵is a simplified variant used as a radical to represent water-related meanings in other characters. It retains the flowing water depiction of 水 (shuǐ, "water") but is stylized to fit alongside other components.
- **examples:**
  - 水是生命之源。
    - Shuǐ shì shēngmìng zhī yuán.
    - Water is the source of life.
  - 汉(漢)字( )中( )偏( )旁( )氵( )，代表水。
    - Hànzì zhōng piān páng shuǐ, dàibiǎo shuǐ.
    - In Chinese characters, the radical '氵' signifies water.
  - 河水清澈透明，鱼(魚)儿(兒)欢(歡)快( )戏(戲)水( )。
    - Hé shuǐ qīngchè tòumíng, yú er huānkuaì xì shuǐ.
    - The river water is clear and transparent, and the fish happily play in it.
### 酱(醬) → 將
- **definition:** lead
- **pinyin:** jiāng
- **components:**
  - 爿
    - pán
    - half of a tree trunk
  - 寸
    - cùn
    - inch
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 爿 (pán, "half of a tree trunk") + phonetic: 寸 (cùn) =
    - something measured in leadership ->
    - lead
  - **interpretation:** The character 將 (jiāng, "lead") combines 爿 (pán, "half of a tree trunk"), suggesting a foundation or base, with 寸 (cùn), indicating measurement or precision. This implies the careful guidance or leadership required to manage and direct.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )將( )带(帶)领(領)队(隊)伍( )，迈(邁)向( )胜(勝)利( )。
    - Tā jiāng dài lǐng duì wǔ, mài xiàng shèng lì.
    - He will lead the team towards victory.
  - 他( )將( )引( )导(導)学(學)生( )。
    - Tā jiāng yǐn dǎo xué shēng.
    - He will lead the students.
### 酱(醬) → 將 → 爿
- **definition:** half of a tree trunk
- **pinyin:** pán
- **components:**
  - 牀
    - chuáng
    - bed
  - 臧
    - zāng
    - favored
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a bed ->
    - Original form of 牀 (chuáng, "bed") ->
    - Contracted form in 臧 (zāng, "favored")
  - **interpretation:** The character 爿(pán, "bed") originally depicted the structure of a bed. It later became the foundational form for the character 牀 (chuáng, "bed") and appears in a contracted form within the character 臧 (zāng, "favored").
- **examples:**
  - 他( )躺( )在( )软(軟)软(軟)的( )爿( )上( )。
    - Tā tǎng zài ruǎn ruǎn de pán shàng.
    - He lies on the soft bed.
  - 夜( )深( )人( )静(靜)时(時)，爿( )边(邊)飘(飄)散( )着( )淡( )淡( )的( )香( )气(氣)。
    - Yè shēn rén jìng shí, pán biān piāo sàn zhe dàn dàn de xiāng qì.
    - When the night is quiet, a faint fragrance drifts by the bed.
  - 她( )精( )心( )整( )理( )过(過)的( )爿( )让(讓)人( )看( )了( )舒( )心( )。
    - Tā jīngxīn zhěnglǐ guò de pán ràng rén kàn le shūxīn.
    - The bed she meticulously arranged looks very comforting.
### 酱(醬) → 將 → 爿 → 牀
- **definition:** bed
- **pinyin:** chuáng
- **components:**
  - 爿(牀)
    - qiáng
    - half of a tree trunk
  - 木
    - mù
    - tree
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 牀 (chuáng, "bed") composed of 爿(牀) (qiáng, "half of a tree trunk") + 木 (mù, "tree") =
    - tree trunk used for support ->
    - bed or wooden bed frame
  - **interpretation:** 牀 (chuáng, "bed") is a phono-semantic compound where 爿(牀) (qiáng, "half of a tree trunk") provides the phonetic element, and 木 (mù, "tree") gives the semantic component, representing its construction from wood.
  - **simplification:** 床 incorporates 广 (guǎng, "house") indicating its placement inside, making 日 (rì, "sun") unnecessary.
- **examples:**
  - 这张牀很( )舒( )适(適)。
    - Zhè zhāng chuáng hěn shūshì.
    - This bed is very comfortable.
  - 晚( )上( )睡( )觉(覺)时(時)，我躺在牀上休息。
    - Wǎnshàng shuìjiào shí, wǒ tǎng zài chuáng shàng xiūxí.
    - When it is time to sleep at night, I lie on the bed to rest.
### 酱(醬) → 將 → 爿 → 牀 → 木
- **definition:** tree
- **pinyin:** mù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a tree with branches and roots
  - **interpretation:** The character 木(mù) depicts a tree, showing the trunk in the center and two slanting lines representing branches or roots. It directly signifies the concept of wood or a tree.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)把( )椅( )子( )的( )主( )要( )材( )料( )是( )木( )。
    - Zhè bǎ yǐzi de zhǔyào cáiliào shì mù.
    - The main material of this chair is wood.
  - 工匠精心雕刻木，制成精美家具。
    - Gōngjiàng jīngxīn diāo kè mù, zhìchéng jīngměi jiājù.
    - The craftsman carefully carved the wood, crafting exquisite furniture.
### 酱(醬) → 將 → 爿 → 臧
- **definition:** favored
- **pinyin:** zāng
- **components:**
  - 臣
    - chén
    - eye
  - 戈
    - gē
    - spear/weapon
  - 爿
    - pán
    - silk panel
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 臣 (chén, "eye") + 戈 (gē, "spear/weapon") =
    - prisoner of war with blinded eyes ->
    - later 爿 (pán) added for phonetic value ->
    - final form 臧
  - **interpretation:** Initially, 臧 depicted a slave or prisoner of war, with the components representing visually inhibited individuals (臣) controlled by force (戈). The character evolved to include a phonetic component 爿, suggesting an additional layer of meaning or a phonetic shift over time.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )将(將)粮(糧)食( )臧( )在( )仓(倉)库(庫)中( )。
    - Tā jiāng liángshí zāng zài cāngkù zhōng.
    - He stores the grain in the warehouse.
  - 工( )人( )先( )清( )点(點)物( )资(資)，后( )将(將)工( )具( )臧( )于(於)仓(倉)房( )。
    - Gōngrén xiān qīngdiǎn wùzī, hòu jiāng gōngjù zāng yú cāngfáng.
    - The workers first count the supplies, then store the tools in the warehouse.
### 酱(醬) → 將 → 爿 → 臧 → 臣
- **definition:** eye
- **pinyin:** chén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a vertical eye of a man looking downwards
  - **interpretation:** The character 臣(chén) originally depicted an eye oriented vertically, symbolizing a person looking downwards, conveying subordination or observance. Over time, it evolved to represent roles such as 'slave,' 'servant,' and 'minister,' emphasizing relationships defined by subservience.
- **examples:**
  - 臣( )愿(願)为(為)国(國)家( )尽(盡)忠( )。
    - Chén yuàn wèi guójiā jìnzhōng。
    - I, as a loyal subject, am willing to serve the nation faithfully.
  - 陛( )下( )召( )见(見)臣( )，臣必不辱使命。
    - Bìxià zhàojiàn chén, chén bì bù rǔ shǐmìng。
    - Your Majesty summons me, and I will not fail in my duty.
  - 昔( )日( )臣( )拜( )见(見)明( )君( )，如( )今( )仍( )怀(懷)赤( )诚(誠)之( )心( )。
    - Xīrì chén bàijiàn míngjūn, rújīn réng huái chìchéng zhī xīn。
    - In the past, I paid my respects to a wise ruler, and even now I remain sincerely devoted.
### 酱(醬) → 將 → 爿 → 臧 → 戈
- **definition:** spear/weapon
- **pinyin:** gē
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a spear or halberd, a type of weapon
  - **interpretation:** The character 戈(gē, 'spear') visually represents a spear or halberd, highlighting its function and importance as a weapon in ancient Chinese warfare.
- **examples:**
  - 古( )代( )战(戰)士( )持( )戈( )，勇( )闯(闖)敌(敵)营(營)。
    - Gǔdài zhànshì chí gē, yǒng chuǎng dí yíng.
    - Ancient warriors wielded the spear, bravely attacking the enemy camp.
  - 博( )物( )馆(館)珍( )藏( )古( )兵( )器( )，展( )厅(廳)陈(陳)列( )戈( )。
    - Bówùguǎn zhēncáng gǔ bīngqì, zhǎntīng chénliè gē.
    - The museum preserves ancient weapons, and the exhibition hall displays a spear.
### 酱(醬) → 將 → 寸
- **definition:** inch
- **pinyin:** cùn
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogram
  - **description:**
    - Line on the hand (ideographic) emphasizing the elbow ->
    - represents measurement unit between wrist and specific forearm position ->
    - meaning 'inch'
  - **interpretation:** 寸 (cùn, "inch") originated as a depiction of a line on the hand, indicating the area near the elbow. This specific placement was considered significant for pulse palpation, originally suggesting a forearm position, which then came to denote the measurement 'inch'.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)块(塊)布( )每( )一( )寸( )都( )是( )精( )心( )制(製)作( )。
    - Zhè kuài bù měi yī cùn dōu shì jīngxīn zhìzuò.
    - Every inch of this cloth is crafted with care.
  - 请(請)用( )卷( )尺( )量( )一( )寸( )，每( )一( )寸( )数(數)据(據)都( )准(準)确(確)。
    - Qǐng yòng juǎnchǐ liàng yī cùn, měi yī cùn shùjù dōu zhǔnquè.
    - Please use a tape measure to measure an inch, every inch's data is accurate.

## 商( )场(場)
---
## shopping mall
### shāngchǎng
---
- **traditional:** 商場
- **definition:** shopping mall
- **pinyin:** shāngchǎng
- **components:**
  - 商
    - shāng
    - commerce; business
  - 场(場)
    - chǎng
    - open space; place; field; stage
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 商 (shāng, "commerce") + 场(場) (chǎng, "open space" or "place") =
    - a place for commerce
  - **interpretation:** A 商( )场(場) (shāngchǎng, "shopping mall") is a large open space dedicated to commerce where various shops and businesses are situated. The combination of 商 (shāng, "commerce") with 场(場) (chǎng, "open space" or "place") conveys the idea of a designated area for trade and services, aligning with modern commercial practices.
  - **simplification:** Customary simplification by converting 場 to 场
- **examples:**
  - 她( )喜( )欢(歡)在( )商( )场(場)，在( )那( )里(裏)逛( )街( )。
    - Tā xǐhuān zài shāngchǎng, zài nàlǐ guàngjiē.
    - She likes shopping at the mall, strolling around there.
  - 我( )打( )算( )去( )商( )场(場)，看( )看( )新( )款( )服( )装(裝)。
    - Wǒ dǎsuàn qù shāngchǎng, kànkan xīnkuǎn fúzhuāng.
    - I plan to go to the mall to check out the new fashion clothes.
  - 周( )末( )时(時)，我( )们(們)在( )商( )场(場)放( )松(鬆)休( )闲(閒)。
    - Zhōumò shí, wǒmen zài shāngchǎng fàngsōng xiūxián.
    - On weekends, we relax at the shopping mall.
### 商( )场(場) → 商
- **definition:** commerce; business
- **pinyin:** shāng
- **components:**
  - 㕯
    - yīn
    - to speak slowly
  - 章
    - zhāng
    - chapter
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 㕯 ("to speak slowly") + phonetic 章 ("zhāng") ->
    - originally meant estimating from the outside ->
    - developed to mean trading/commerce
  - **interpretation:** The character 商(shāng) originally described the concept of estimating, which is linked to trading where one evaluates items before purchasing or exchanging them. Over time, the meaning shifted to specifically denote commerce or trade. The semantic component 㕯 relates to the idea of communication or deliberation, which is fundamental in trading contexts.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )对(對)商( )机(機)充( )满(滿)兴(興)趣( )。
    - Wǒ duì shāngjī chōngmǎn xìngqù.
    - I am very interested in business opportunities.
  - 他( )进(進)入( )商( )界( )谋(謀)求( )发(發)展( )。
    - Tā jìnrù shāngjiè móuqiú fāzhǎn.
    - He entered the business world to seek development.
  - 商( )人( )讲(講)求( )信( )誉(譽)，消( )费(費)者( )追( )求( )质(質)量( )。
    - Shāngrén jiǎngqiú xìn yù, xiāofèizhě zhuīqiú zhìliàng.
    - Businesspeople value reputation, and consumers pursue quality.
### 商( )场(場) → 商 → 㕯
- **definition:** to speak slowly
- **pinyin:** yīn
- **components:**
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
  - 壬
    - rén
    - ninth heavenly stem
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 口 (kǒu, "mouth") + 壬 (rén, "ninth heavenly stem") =
    - mouth speaking slowly
  - **interpretation:** The character 㕯 combines the element 口 (kǒu, "mouth") with 壬 (rén, "ninth heavenly stem"), suggesting speech that is considered slow or deliberate. The use of these components signifies the control and modulation of speech.
### 商( )场(場) → 商 → 㕯 → 口
- **definition:** mouth
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.
### 商( )场(場) → 商 → 㕯 → 壬
- **definition:** ninth heavenly stem
- **pinyin:** rén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a weaving tool (e.g., spool or bobbin), a carrying pole for 任 (rèn, "assign"), or a double-bladed axe ->
    - original meaning uncertain ->
    - used as ninth heavenly stem
  - **interpretation:** 壬 originally depicted either a weaving tool, a carrying pole, or a double-bladed axe. Its specific pictorial representation remains unclear, but it was later used in the context of the Chinese calendrical system as the ninth heavenly stem.
- **examples:**
  - 在( )纪(紀)年( )系( )统(統)中( )，壬( )为(為)第( )九( )个(個)天( )干( )。
    - zài jì nián xì tǒng zhōng，rén wéi dì jiǔ gè tiān gān。
    - In the chronological system, Ren is the ninth heavenly stem.
  - 古( )人( )以( )壬( )配( )合( )地( )支( )论(論)命( )。
    - gǔ rén yǐ rén pèi hé dì zhī lùn mìng。
    - Ancient scholars used Ren in conjunction with the earthly branches to determine fate.
  - 夜( )深( )人( )静(靜)时(時)，古( )籍( )中( )常( )见(見)壬( )的( )记(記)载(載)。
    - yè shēn rén jìng shí，gǔ jí zhōng cháng jiàn rén de jì zǎi。
    - In the quiet depths of night, ancient texts often record the character Ren.
### 商( )场(場) → 商 → 章
- **definition:** chapter
- **pinyin:** zhāng
- **components:**
  - 辛
    - xīn
    - chisel
  - 日
    - rì
    - sun
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 辛 (xīn, "chisel") + 日 (rì, "sun") =
    - chisel piercing a piece of jade ->
    - originally meant "to treat jade" ->
    - later evolved to "chapter"
  - **interpretation:** The character 章 initially depicted a chisel (辛) piercing a piece of jade, represented by the 日 symbol. This depiction symbolized the process of treating jade. Over time, it likely evolved to be used for the concept of "chapter," as jade carvings and inscriptions often represented chapters or sections in a work.
- **examples:**
  - 第( )一( )章( )让(讓)人( )印( )象( )深( )刻( )。
    - Dì yī zhāng ràng rén yìnxiàng shēnkè.
    - The first chapter left a deep impression.
  - 他( )们(們)讨(討)论(論)了( )第( )二( )章( )的( )内(內)容( )。
    - Tāmen tǎolùn le dì èr zhāng de nèiróng.
    - They discussed the content of the second chapter.
  - 第( )一( )章( )启(啟)发(發)了( )读(讀)者( )，第( )二( )章( )展( )开(開)了( )故( )事( )。
    - Dì yī zhāng qǐfā le dúzhě, dì èr zhāng zhǎnkāi le gùshì.
    - The first chapter inspired the readers, and the second chapter unfolded the story.
### 商( )场(場) → 商 → 章 → 辛
- **definition:** chisel
- **pinyin:** xīn
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a tool—chisel, awl, spike—used to mark slaves and criminals ->
    - 辛
  - **interpretation:** The character 辛(xīn, 'spice'), while meaning 'spice' in contemporary usage, originally depicted a tool used for branding slaves and criminals, indicating its historical connection to marking and intensity.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )在( )厨(廚)房( )里(裡)加( )了( )些( )辛( )，使( )得( )菜( )的( )味( )道( )更( )加( )丰(豐)富( )。
    - Wǒ zài chúfáng lǐ jiā le xiē xīn, shǐdé cài de wèidào gèngjiā fēngfù.
    - I added some spice in the kitchen, making the dish more flavorful.
  - 这(這)家( )餐( )馆(館)以( )独(獨)特( )的( )辛( )著( )称(稱)，吸引了很多食客。
    - Zhè jiā cānguǎn yǐ dútè de xīn zhùchēng, xīyǐn le hěn duō shíkè.
    - This restaurant is renowned for its unique spice, attracting many diners.
### 商( )场(場) → 商 → 章 → 日
- **definition:** sun
- **pinyin:** rì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts the sun; a dot or line added in the center to distinguish from similar characters e.g. 囗
  - **interpretation:** As a pictogram, 日(rì, 'sun') directly represents the sun. In ancient times, the added dot helped visually differentiate it from other characters. This is analogous to the astronomical symbol ☉ and Egyptian hieroglyph 𓇳, both depicting the sun.
- **examples:**
  - 晨( )曦( )来(來)临(臨)时(時)，日( )出( )东(東)方( )。
    - Chénxī láilín shí, rìchū dōngfāng.
    - At the break of dawn, the sun rises in the east.
  - 温(溫)暖( )的( )日( )光( )洒(灑)满(滿)大( )地( )。
    - Wēnnuǎn de rìguāng sǎ mǎn dàdì.
    - The warm sunlight spreads over the earth.
  - 中( )午( )时(時)分( )，日( )高( )挂(掛)天( )。
    - Zhōngwǔ shífēn, rì gāo guà tiān.
    - At noon, the sun hangs high in the sky.
### 商( )场(場) → 场(場)
- **traditional:** 場
- **definition:** open space; place; field; stage
- **pinyin:** chǎng
- **components:**
  - 土
    - tǔ
    - earth
  - 昜
    - yáng
    - sun
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 土 (tǔ, "earth") + phonetic: 昜 (yáng, "sun") =
    - place associated with land "open space" ->
    - field or stage
  - **interpretation:** 场(場) (chǎng, "open space") combines the semantics of 土 (tǔ, "earth") with the phonetic indication of 昜 (yáng, "sun") to denote an open area or place, such as a field or stage.
  - **simplification:** 场(場): Simplified by adjusting the phonetic component 昜 visually, preserving the core semantic and phonetic elements.
- **examples:**
  - 体(體)育( )场(場)上，运(運)动(動)员(員)比( )赛(賽)。
    - Tǐyùchǎng shàng, yùndòngyuán bǐsài.
    - At the stadium, athletes compete.
  - 露( )天( )剧(劇)场(場)中，演( )员(員)演( )出( )。
    - Lùtiān jùchǎng zhōng, yǎnyuán yǎnchū.
    - In the open-air theater, actors perform.
  - 演( )唱( )会(會)场(場)内，歌( )迷( )热(熱)情( )洋( )溢( )。
    - Yǎnchànghuì chǎng nèi, gēmí rèqíng yángyì.
    - Inside the concert venue, fans exude enthusiasm.
### 商( )场(場) → 场(場) → 土
- **definition:** earth
- **pinyin:** tǔ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a mound of earth
  - **interpretation:** The character 土(tǔ, "earth") visually represents a mound or piece of land, with a horizontal line indicating the surface and a vertical line the height of the mound. It's a straightforward depiction of earth or soil.
- **examples:**
  - 农(農)民( )耕( )土( )，种(種)出( )丰(豐)收( )。
    - Nóngmín gēng tǔ, zhòng chū fēngshōu.
    - Farmers cultivate the earth and harvest abundantly.
  - 院( )子( )里(裡)的( )土( )，适(適)合( )种(種)花( )。
    - Yuànzi lǐ de tǔ, shìhé zhòng huā.
    - The soil in the yard is suitable for planting flowers.
### 商( )场(場) → 场(場) → 昜
- **definition:** sun
- **pinyin:** yáng
- **components:**
  - 日
    - rì
    - sun
  - 丂
    - kǎo
    - breath
  - 彡
    - xiǎn
    - rays of light
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 日 (rì, "sun") + 丂 (kǎo, "breath") + 彡 (xiǎn, "rays of light") =
    - rays of the sun shining over a tree branch
  - **interpretation:** The character 昜(yáng) portrays the concept of the sun's rays shining down, symbolized by 日 (rì, "sun") with 丂 representing a tree branch and 彡 depicting the sun's rays. This compound visually encapsulates the light given off by the sun.
- **examples:**
  - 日( )光( )照( )耀( )下( )时(時)，旗( )帜(幟)高( )昜( )飘(飄)扬(揚)。
    - Rìguāng zhàoyào xià shí, qízhì gāo yáng piāoyáng.
    - Under the shining sunlight, the flag is raised high.
  - 风(風)儿(兒)吹( )过(過)柳( )梢( )时(時)，心( )中( )的( )梦(夢)想( )也( )随(隨)昜( )起( )。
    - Fēng ér chuīguò liǔ shāo shí, xīn zhōng de mèngxiǎng yě suí yáng qǐ.
    - As the wind passes the tips of the willows, the dreams in the heart rise as well.
### 商( )场(場) → 场(場) → 昜 → 日
- **definition:** sun
- **pinyin:** rì
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts the sun; a dot or line added in the center to distinguish from similar characters e.g. 囗
  - **interpretation:** As a pictogram, 日(rì, 'sun') directly represents the sun. In ancient times, the added dot helped visually differentiate it from other characters. This is analogous to the astronomical symbol ☉ and Egyptian hieroglyph 𓇳, both depicting the sun.
- **examples:**
  - 晨( )曦( )来(來)临(臨)时(時)，日( )出( )东(東)方( )。
    - Chénxī láilín shí, rìchū dōngfāng.
    - At the break of dawn, the sun rises in the east.
  - 温(溫)暖( )的( )日( )光( )洒(灑)满(滿)大( )地( )。
    - Wēnnuǎn de rìguāng sǎ mǎn dàdì.
    - The warm sunlight spreads over the earth.
  - 中( )午( )时(時)分( )，日( )高( )挂(掛)天( )。
    - Zhōngwǔ shífēn, rì gāo guà tiān.
    - At noon, the sun hangs high in the sky.
### 商( )场(場) → 场(場) → 昜 → 丂
- **definition:** breath
- **pinyin:** kǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** conflation
  - **description:**
    - Combination of several unrelated characters over time ->
    - final meaning 'breath'
  - **interpretation:** The character 丂 originally represented or was influenced by a number of unrelated characters and concepts that were later combined or confused into this simple form, acquiring the meaning of 'breath' in its modern usage.
### 商( )场(場) → 场(場) → 昜 → 彡
- **definition:** rays of light
- **pinyin:** xiǎn
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts three strands of hair ->
    - used to indicate three light rays in certain contexts
  - **interpretation:** 彡(shān, "rays") originally depicted strands of hair but also came to represent light rays, reflecting a change in symbolic meaning over time. Its use as a representation of 'ornament' and in characters like 昜 and 參 highlights its versatility in indicating rays or decorative elements.
- **examples:**
  - 天( )空( )洒(灑)下( )金( )色( )的( )彡( )，大( )地( )迎( )来(來)温(溫)暖( )。
    - Tiānkōng sǎ xià jīnsè de shān, dàdì yíng lái wēnnuǎn.
    - The sky showers golden rays, and the earth welcomes warmth.
  - 晨( )曦( )中( )的( )彡( )闪(閃)耀( )着(著)希( )望( )。
    - Chénxī zhōng de shān shǎnyào zhe xīwàng.
    - The rays in the morning light shine with hope.

## 商( )场(場)

## 洲
---
## continent; river island
### zhōu
---
- **definition:** continent; river island
- **pinyin:** zhōu
- **components:**
  - 水
    - shuǐ
    - water
  - 州
    - zhōu
    - administrative division
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 水 (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic: 州 (zhōu, "administrative division") =
    - area surrounded by water ->
    - continent; river island
  - **interpretation:** The character combines 水 (shuǐ, "water") to signify areas associated with water bodies and 州 (zhōu, "administrative division") which provides the sound element. This construction effectively depicts landmasses surrounded by water like continents or river islands.
- **examples:**
  - 亚(亞)洲( )是( )一( )个(個)巨( )大( )的( )洲( )。
    - Yàzhōu shì yīgè jùdà de zhōu.
    - Asia is a vast continent.
  - 非( )洲( )气(氣)候( )炎( )热(熱)，拥(擁)有( )丰(豐)富( )物( )产(產)的( )洲( )。
    - Fēizhōu qìhòu yánrè, yǒngyǒu fēngfù wùchǎn de zhōu.
    - Africa has a hot climate, a continent with abundant resources.
  - 河( )中( )心( )的( )小( )洲( )为(為)水( )鸟(鳥)搭( )建( )栖(棲)息( )地( )。
    - Hé zhōngxīn de xiǎo zhōu wéi shuǐniǎo dājiàn qīxīdì.
    - The small river island at the center of the river provides a habitat for waterfowl.
### 洲 → 水
- **definition:** water
- **pinyin:** shuǐ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts flowing water
  - **interpretation:** 水 (shuǐ, 'water') is a pictogram that directly represents the appearance of flowing water, making it intuitive and easy to recognize. Its simplicity and direct visual reference to its meaning have allowed it to remain unchanged over millennia.
- **examples:**
  - 水( )是( )一( )种(種)生( )命( )之( )源( )。
    - Shuǐ shì yī zhǒng shēngmìng zhī yuán.
    - Water is the source of life.
  - 夏( )天( )很( )热(熱)，喝水能解暑。
    - Xiàtiān hěn rè, hē shuǐ néng jiě shǔ.
    - It is very hot in summer, drinking water can relieve the heat.
  - 河水潺潺，沿( )岸( )绿(綠)树(樹)成( )荫(蔭)。
    - Hé shuǐ chánchán, yán'àn lǜshù chéng yīn.
    - The river flows babbling, and green trees along the bank provide shade.
### 洲 → 州
- **definition:** administrative division
- **pinyin:** zhōu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an islet in a river
  - **interpretation:** 州 (zhōu, "administrative division") was originally a pictogram representing a small piece of land or islet amidst water, illustrating the ancient practice of using distinct land areas as administrative divisions.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )计(計)划(劃)参(參)观(觀)几(幾)个(個)州( )。
    - Wǒ jìhuà cānguān jǐ gè zhōu.
    - I plan to visit several states.
  - 美( )国(國)的( )各( )州( )都( )有( )独(獨)特( )的( )风(風)景( )。
    - Měiguó de gè zhōu dōu yǒu dútè de fēngjǐng.
    - Every state in the United States has unique scenery.
  - 州( )政( )府( )负(負)责(責)管( )理( )地( )方( )事( )务(務)，政( )策( )由( )州( )长(長)制( )定( )。
    - Zhōu zhèngfǔ fùzé guǎnlǐ dìfāng shìwù, zhèngcè yóu zhōu zhǎng zhìdìng.
    - The state government is responsible for managing local affairs, and the policies are set by the governor.

## 声(聲)
---
## sound; voice
### shēng
---
- **traditional:** 聲
- **definition:** sound; voice
- **pinyin:** shēng
- **components:**
  - 殸
    - qīng
    - sound of metal or stone
  - 耳
    - ěr
    - ear
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - phonetic: 殸 (qīng, 'sound of metal or stone') + semantic: 耳 (ěr, 'ear') =
    - sound perceived by the ear ->
    - sound; voice
  - **interpretation:** The character 聲 combines the sound-related context provided by 殸 and the perception aspect represented by 耳, underlining that sound is something heard by the ear.
  - **simplification:** 声(聲): The component 殸 (qīng, 'sound of metal or stone') was omitted, simplifying the character while retaining the primary semantic aspect 口 (kǒu, 'mouth') from the singing component.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )的( )声(聲)音( )非( )常( )好( )听(聽)。
    - Tā de shēngyīn fēicháng hǎotīng。
    - His voice is very pleasant.
  - 音( )乐(樂)产(產)生( )美( )妙( )的( )声(聲)音( )，唤(喚)醒( )了( )沉( )睡( )的( )心( )灵(靈)。
    - Yīnyuè chǎnshēng měimiào de shēngyīn, huànxǐng le chénshuì de xīnlíng。
    - Music produces beautiful sounds, awakening the sleeping soul.
  - 悠( )扬(揚)的( )琴( )声(聲)在( )房( )间(間)里(裏)回( )荡(蕩)，伴( )着(著)月( )光( )显(顯)得( )宁(寧)静(靜)。
    - Yōuyáng de qínshēng zài fángjiān lǐ huídàng, bànzhe yuèguāng xiǎnde níngjìng。
    - The melodious sound of the lute echoes in the room, and with the moonlight it appears tranquil.
### 声(聲) → 殸
- **definition:** sound of metal or stone
- **pinyin:** qīng
- **components:**
  - 聲(声)
    - shēng
    - stone chime
  - 殳
    - shū
    - hand holding a hammer or a mallet
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 聲(声) (shēng, 'stone chime') + 殳 (shū, 'hand holding a hammer or a mallet') =
    - a stone chime being struck by a mallet
  - **interpretation:** The character 殸(qīng, 'sound of metal or stone') represents the sound produced by striking a stone chime with a mallet. It visually conveys the action of producing sound and is therefore an ideogrammic compound.
### 声(聲) → 殸 → 聲
- **definition:** stone chime
- **pinyin:** shēng
- **components:**
  - 殸
    - kēng
    - stone chime
  - 耳
    - ěr
    - ear
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - phonetic 殸 (kēng, "stone chime") + semantic 耳 (ěr, "ear") =
    - sound heard by the ear ->
    - stone chime
  - **interpretation:** The character 聲(shēng, "stone chime") combines the phonetic element 殸 (kēng, "stone chime") with 耳 (ěr, "ear") to represent sounds, specifically the sound of a stone chime as heard by the ear.
  - **simplification:** 声 is a simplified and variant form of 聲, retaining the primary phonetic and semantic elements.
- **examples:**
  - 古( )寺( )中( )传(傳)来(來)石( )制(製)聲( )的( )回( )响(響)。
    - Gǔ sì zhōng chuán lái shí zhì shēng de huí xiǎng.
    - The echo of the stone chime came from the ancient temple.
  - 夜( )深( )时(時)，山( )谷( )中( )传(傳)出( )石( )制(製)聲( )的( )旋( )律( )。
    - Yè shēn shí, shān gǔ zhōng chuán chū shí zhì shēng de xuán lǜ.
    - At night, a melody from the stone chime echoed through the mountain valley.
### 声(聲) → 殸 → 聲 → 耳
- **definition:** ear
- **pinyin:** ěr
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an ear
  - **interpretation:** The character 耳 (ěr, "ear") is a pictogram that visually represents an ear with its distinctive shape. This straightforward representation made it intuitive for ancient speakers to associate the character directly with the concept of an ear.
- **examples:**
  - 音( )乐(樂)直( )入( )耳( )。
    - Yīn yuè zhí rù ěr.
    - The music goes straight to the ear.
  - 他( )亲(親)耳( )听(聽)到( )谣(謠)言( )，心( )中( )充( )满(滿)疑( )虑(慮)。
    - Tā qīn ěr tīng dào yáo yán, xīn zhōng chōng mǎn yí lǜ.
    - He heard the rumor with his own ears, and his heart was filled with doubt.
### 声(聲) → 殸 → 殳
- **definition:** hand holding a hammer or a mallet
- **pinyin:** shū
- **components:**
  - 又
    - yòu
    - tool
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 𠘧 (hand) + 又 (tool) =
    - depicts hand holding a tool ->
    - borrowed meaning of bamboo halberd
  - **interpretation:** The character originally depicted a hand holding a tool, suggesting the action of beating. It was later borrowed to refer to a specific type of weapon, a bamboo halberd.
- **examples:**
  - 古( )代( )战(戰)场(場)上( )，殳( )曾( )被( )视(視)为(為)关(關)键(鍵)武( )器( )。
    - Gǔdài zhànchǎng shàng, shū céng bèi shì wéi guānjiàn wǔqì.
    - On ancient battlefields, the shū was once considered a crucial weapon.
  - 兵( )器( )展( )览(覽)中( )，殳( )闪(閃)耀( )着(著)历(歷)史( )的( )光( )芒( )。
    - Bīngqì zhǎnlǎn zhōng, shū shǎnyào zhe lìshǐ de guāngmáng.
    - In the arms exhibition, the shū shone with the brilliance of history.
### 声(聲) → 殸 → 殳 → 又
- **definition:** tool
- **pinyin:** yòu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a right hand
  - **interpretation:** The character 又 (yòu, "hand") is a pictogram representing a right hand, showing the fingers and palm, which highlights its original meaning related to manual actions.
- **examples:**
  - 「又」这个字最早来源于手(「又」這個字最早來源於手)。
    - 「Yòu」 zhè gè zì zuì zǎo láiyuán yú shǒu。
    - The character '又' originally comes from the hand.
  - 很( )多( )汉(漢)字( )含( )有( )又( )部( )首( )，表( )示( )与(與)手( )有( )关(關)的( )动(動)作( )。
    - Hěn duō hànzì hányǒu Yòu bùshǒu, biǎoshì yǔ shǒu yǒuguān de dòngzuò。
    - Many Chinese characters include the '又' radical, indicating actions related to the hand.
  - 古( )人( )用( )又( )描( )绘(繪)手( )的( )形( )态(態)，体(體)现(現)了( )古( )汉(漢)字( )的( )独(獨)特( )魅( )力( )。
    - Gǔrén yòng Yòu miáohuì shǒu de xíngtài, tǐxiàn le gǔ hànzì de dútè mèilì。
    - Ancient people used '又' to depict the shape of the hand, showcasing the unique charm of ancient Chinese characters.

## 户(戶)
---
## household; door
### hù
---
- **traditional:** 戶
- **definition:** household; door
- **pinyin:** hù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** Pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts one-half of 門 (mén, "gate")
  - **interpretation:** The character 戶 (hù, "household; door") resembles the side view of a gate, representing its function as an entrance or access point similar to a door or household entry.
- **examples:**
  - 每( )户(戶)人( )家( )都( )有( )自( )己( )的( )故( )事( )。
    - Měi hù rénjiā dōu yǒu zìjǐ de gùshì.
    - Every household has its own story.
  - 这(這)个(個)小( )区(區)住( )着(著)数(數)百( )户(戶)，居( )民( )和( )睦( )相( )处(處)。
    - Zhège xiǎoqū zhùzhe shù bǎi hù, jūmín hémù xiāngchǔ.
    - Hundreds of households live in this community, and the residents live in harmony.

## 凯(凱)里( )
---
## Kaili City
### Kǎilǐ
---
- **traditional:** 凱里
- **definition:** Kaili City
- **pinyin:** Kǎilǐ
- **components:**
  - 凯(凱)
    - kǎi
    - triumphant; victorious
  - 里
    - lǐ
    - village; neighborhood; inner; inside
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:**
    - 凯(凱) (kǎi, "triumphant") + 里 (lǐ, "village; neighborhood") =
    - the name of Kaili City, suggesting a place of triumph.
  - **interpretation:** The name 凯(凱)里( ) (Kǎilǐ) combines the notion of triumph with the concept of community or village. It suggests a place of success or victory, possibly reflecting aspirations or historical significance attributed to Kaili City.
- **examples:**
  - 我( )计(計)划(劃)明( )天( )去( )凯(凱)里( )。
    - Wǒ jìhuà míngtiān qù Kǎilǐ.
    - I plan to go to Kaili tomorrow.
  - 凯(凱)里( )风(風)光( )迷( )人( )，吸( )引( )了( )众(眾)多( )游(遊)客( )。
    - Kǎilǐ fēngguāng mírén, xīyǐnle zhòngduō yóukè.
    - The scenery of Kaili is charming, attracting many tourists.
  - 在( )贵(貴)州( )的( )腹( )地( )有( )凯(凱)里( )。
    - Zài Guìzhōu de fùdì yǒu Kǎilǐ.
    - Kaili is located in the heartland of Guizhou.
### 凯(凱)里( ) → 凯(凱)
- **traditional:** 凱
- **definition:** triumphant; victorious
- **pinyin:** kǎi
- **components:**
  - 豈
    - qǐ
    - drum
  - 几
    - jǐ
    - phonetic
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - Phono-semantic: 豈 (qǐ, "drum") + 几 (jǐ, phonetic) =
    - music of triumph ->
    - victorious
  - **interpretation:** '凱' combines 豈 (qǐ, "drum") representing the triumphant music and 几 (jǐ) as the phonetic component. Originally, this composition reflected music played during victorious celebrations.
  - **simplification:** Simplified as 凯 by replacing 豈 (qǐ, "drum") with 己 (jǐ, "self") for phonetic resemblance, reducing complexity.
- **examples:**
  - 凯(凱)带(帶)领(領)球( )队(隊)夺(奪)得( )冠( )军(軍)。
    - Kǎi dàilǐng qiúduì duódé guànjūn.
    - Kai led the team to win the championship.
  - 比( )赛(賽)结(結)束( )后(後)，他( )以( )凯(凱)的( )姿( )态(態)走( )向( )领(領)奖(獎)台( )。
    - Bǐsài jiéshù hòu, tā yǐ kǎi de zītài zǒuxiàng lǐngjiǎngtái.
    - After the game, he walked to the podium with a victorious demeanor.
  - 在( )艰(艱)难(難)比( )赛(賽)中( )，他( )展( )现(現)出( )凯(凱)的( )风(風)采( )。
    - Zài jiānnán bǐsài zhōng, tā zhǎnxiàn chū kǎi de fēngcǎi.
    - In the tough competition, he demonstrated a triumphant air.
### 凯(凱)里( ) → 凯(凱) → 豈
- **definition:** drum
- **pinyin:** qǐ
- **components:**
  - 壴
    - zhù
    - drum
  - 凱
    - kǎi
    - music of triumph
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** variation
  - **description:**
    - Possibly a variation of 壴 (zhù, "drum") with the top part modified ->
    - related to 凱 (kǎi, "music of triumph")
  - **interpretation:** The character 豈(qǐ) originally may have depicted or been associated with a drum, symbolizing its connection to the idea of musical triumph. The modified top part differentiates it from similar characters like 壴 (zhù, "drum"), leading to its unique modern meaning of "how" in expressions.
- **examples:**
  - 若不努力，豈能成功？
    - ruò bù nǔ lì, qǐ néng chéng gōng?
    - If you do not work hard, how can you succeed?
  - 只要心中有夢，豈會輕言放棄？
    - zhǐ yào xīn zhōng yǒu mèng, qǐ huì qīng yán fàng qì?
    - As long as you have a dream in your heart, how could you easily give up?
  - 豈可輕忽天命！
    - qǐ kě qīng hū tiān mìng!
    - How can one disregard destiny!
### 凯(凱)里( ) → 凯(凱) → 豈 → 壴
- **definition:** drum
- **pinyin:** zhù
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts a drum 壴, with a base and feathers 土 on top ->
    - stylized in the Kangxi radical 鼓
  - **interpretation:** The character 壴 (zhù, "drum") visually represents a drum with two ends, with elements contributing to its structure, such as a base and feathers. It was later incorporated into characters like 鼓 (gǔ, "drum") by adding elements that illustrate additional actions like hands holding a drumstick.
- **examples:**
  - 这(這)面( )壴( )声(聲)震( )天( )动(動)地( )。
    - Zhè miàn zhù shēng zhèn tiān dòng dì.
    - The sound of this drum shakes the heavens and the earth.
  - 战(戰)士( )敲( )响(響)壴( )，示( )警( )敌(敵)人( )。
    - Zhànshì qiāo xiǎng zhù, shì jǐng dírén.
    - The soldier beats the drum to warn the enemy.
  - 庄(莊)严(嚴)仪(儀)式( )上( )，祭( )司( )击(擊)打( )壴( )，宣( )告( )传(傳)统(統)。
    - Zhuāngyán yíshì shàng, jìsī jī dǎ zhù, xuānguò chuántǒng.
    - At the solemn ceremony, the priest beats the drum to proclaim tradition.
### 凯(凱)里( ) → 凯(凱) → 豈 → 凱
- **definition:** music of triumph
- **pinyin:** kǎi
- **components:**
  - 豈
    - qǐ
    - drum
  - 几
    - jī
    - small table
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 豈 (qǐ, "drum") + 几 (jī, "small table") =
    - phonetic element from 豈, semantic element from 豈 or 几 ->
    - originally referred to triumph music
  - **interpretation:** The character 凱(kǎi) uses 豈 (qǐ, "drum") as the phonetic component, reflecting its original associative meaning with drum music used in celebrations of victory, and 几 (jī, "small table") as part of its semantic construction.
  - **simplification:** The character was simplified to 凯 by using a different component structure that still reflects the phonetic and semantic elements.
- **examples:**
  - 战(戰)场(場)上( )胜(勝)利( )的( )号(號)角( )吹( )响(響)了( )凱( )。
    - Zhànchǎng shàng shènglì de hàojiǎo chuīxiǎng le kǎi.
    - On the battlefield, the victorious bugle sounded the music of triumph.
  - 庆(慶)功( )宴( )上( )，乐(樂)师(師)奏( )响(響)凱( )。
    - Qìnggōngyàn shàng, yuèshī zòu xiǎng kǎi.
    - At the victory banquet, the musicians played the music of triumph.
  - 胜(勝)利( )的( )旋( )律( )激( )励(勵)士( )兵( )，凱( )悠( )扬(揚)在( )耳( )畔( )。
    - Shènglì de xuánlǜ jīlì shìbīng, kǎi yōuyáng zài ěrpàn.
    - The victorious melody inspired the soldiers, while the music of triumph resonated softly by their ears.
### 凯(凱)里( ) → 凯(凱) → 豈 → 凱 → 几(幾)
- **traditional:** 幾
- **definition:** small table
- **pinyin:** jī
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a small table or stand
  - **interpretation:** The character 几(幾) (jī, "small table") is a pictogram that visually represents a small table or a similar piece of furniture. Its form suggests the minimalist shape of such an object, emphasizing its practicality and simplicity.
  - **simplification:** The simplified 几(幾) retains the basic pictorial representation while eliminating extra strokes found in the traditional form.
- **examples:**
  - 请(請)把( )书(書)放( )在( )几( )上( )。
    - Qǐng bǎ shū fàng zài jī shàng.
    - Please put the book on the small table.
  - 那( )张(張)几( )很( )漂( )亮( )，吸( )引( )了( )许(許)多( )目( )光( )。
    - Nà zhāng jī hěn piàoliang, xīyǐnle xǔduō mùguāng.
    - That small table is very beautiful, attracting many gazes.
  - 客( )厅(廳)里(裡)的( )几( )透( )露( )出( )古( )典( )韵(韻)味( )，让(讓)人( )感( )觉(覺)舒( )适(適)。
    - Kètīng lǐ de jī tòulù chū gǔdiǎn yùnwèi, ràng rén gǎnjué shūshì.
    - The small table in the living room exudes a classical charm, making people feel comfortable.
### 凯(凱)里( ) → 里
- **definition:** village; neighborhood; inner; inside
- **pinyin:** lǐ
- **components:**
  - 田
    - tián
    - field
  - 土
    - tǔ
    - soil; earth
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 田 (tián, "field") + 土 (tǔ, "soil; earth") =
    - a measure of land and fields ->
    - village
  - **interpretation:** The combination of a field with soil symbolizes an area or measure of land, which is foundational to the concept of a village or community space, often defined by its fields.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )从(從)城( )市( )来(來)，进(進)入( )了( )里( )。
    - Tā cóng chéngshì lái, jìnrùle lǐ.
    - He came from the city and entered the village.
  - 经(經)过(過)长(長)途( )跋( )涉( )，他( )终(終)于(於)到( )达(達)了( )里( )。
    - Jīngguò chángtú báshè, tā zhōngyú dàodále lǐ.
    - After a long journey, he finally reached the village.
  - 故( )里( )永( )存( )于(於)他( )心( )中( )。
    - Gùlǐ yǒngcún yú tā xīnzhōng.
    - His native village forever lives in his heart.
### 凯(凱)里( ) → 里 → 田
- **definition:** field
- **pinyin:** tián
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a field divided into four sections
  - **interpretation:** The character 田(tián, 'field') visually represents a field with sections, indicating its agricultural use and importance in ancient times.
- **examples:**
  - 农(農)民( )在( )田( )里(裡)耕( )作( )。
    - Nóngmín zài tián lǐ gēngzuò.
    - The farmer works in the field.
  - 那( )边(邊)的( )田( )长(長)满(滿)了( )庄(莊)稼( )，农(農)民( )收( )获(穫)忙( )碌( )。
    - Nàbiān de tián cháng mǎn le zhuāngjià, nóngmín shōuhuò mánglù.
    - The field over there is full of crops, and the farmer is busy harvesting.
  - 秋( )收( )时(時)节(節)，人( )们(們)忙( )于(於)收( )割( )田( )里(裡)的( )稻( )谷( )。
    - Qiūshōu shíjié, rénmen máng yú shōugē tián lǐ de dàogǔ.
    - During the harvest season, people are busy reaping the rice in the fields.
### 凯(凱)里( ) → 里 → 土
- **definition:** soil; earth
- **pinyin:** tǔ
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a mound of earth
  - **interpretation:** The character 土(tǔ, "earth") visually represents a mound or piece of land, with a horizontal line indicating the surface and a vertical line the height of the mound. It's a straightforward depiction of earth or soil.
- **examples:**
  - 农(農)民( )耕( )土( )，种(種)出( )丰(豐)收( )。
    - Nóngmín gēng tǔ, zhòng chū fēngshōu.
    - Farmers cultivate the earth and harvest abundantly.
  - 院( )子( )里(裡)的( )土( )，适(適)合( )种(種)花( )。
    - Yuànzi lǐ de tǔ, shìhé zhòng huā.
    - The soil in the yard is suitable for planting flowers.

## 荷花
---
## lotus flower
### héhuā
---
- **definition:** lotus flower
- **pinyin:** héhuā
- **components:**
  - 荷
    - hé
    - lotus; burden
  - 花
    - huā
    - flower
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** compound word
  - **description:** 荷 (hé, "lotus") + 花 (huā, "flower") creates the word 荷花 (héhuā, "lotus flower"). 荷 (hé, "lotus") refers to the lotus plant, a significant symbol in various Asian cultures, often representing purity and spiritual awakening, while 花 (huā, "flower") denotes the flowering part of the plant. Together, they specify the blooming part of the lotus plant.
  - **interpretation:** The combination of 荷 (hé, "lotus") and 花 (huā, "flower") specifies the flower part of the lotus plant, highlighting its importance in many Eastern cultural, religious, and philosophical contexts.
- **examples:**
  - 夏( )日( )降( )临(臨)时(時)，荷( )花( )绽(綻)放( )光( )彩( )。
    - xiàrì jiànglín shí, héhuā zhànfàng guāngcǎi.
    - When summer arrives, the lotus flower blooms brilliantly.
  - 池( )边(邊)有( )荷( )花( )，迎( )风(風)摇(搖)曳( )动(動)人( )。
    - chí biān yǒu héhuā, yíng fēng yáoyè dòngrén.
    - By the pond, there are lotus flowers, swaying gracefully in the breeze.
### 荷花 → 荷
- **definition:** lotus; burden
- **pinyin:** hé
- **components:**
  - 艸
    - cǎo
    - grass; plant
  - 何
    - hé
    - phonetic component
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - 艸 (cǎo, "grass; plant") + 何 (hé) =
    - plant-related meaning with 何 as phonetic ->
    - meaning of lotus or burden
  - **interpretation:** The character 荷 combines the radical 艸 (cǎo, "grass; plant") to indicate its association with plants, specifically the lotus, while 何 (hé) provides the phonetic clue. The lotus is considered both a plant and symbolically associated with roles and burdens.
- **examples:**
  - 夏( )日( )的( )池( )塘( )边(邊)，荷( )花( )优(優)雅( )盛( )开(開)。
    - Xiàrì de chítáng biān, héhuā yōuyǎ shèngkāi。
    - By the pond on a summer day, lotus flowers bloom gracefully.
  - 他( )肩( )负(負)重( )担(擔)，难(難)以( )释(釋)怀(懷)那( )沉( )沉( )的( )荷( )。
    - Tā jiānfù zhòngdān, nán yǐ shìhuái nà chénchén de hé。
    - He bears a heavy load, unable to let go of that persistent burden.
### 荷花 → 荷 → 艸
- **definition:** grass; plant
- **pinyin:** cǎo
- **components:**
  - 屮
    - chè
    - grass
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts grass blades ->
    - duplication of 屮 (chè, 'grass') ->
    - grass imagery
  - **interpretation:** The character 艸(cǎo, 'grass') is a pictorial representation of grass blades. It is essentially a duplication of the character 屮 (chè, 'grass'), visually suggesting the appearance of grass tufts through repetition, emphasizing the concept of grass or vegetation.
- **examples:**
  - 公( )园(園)里(裡)绿(綠)艸( )茵( )茵( )。
    - Gōngyuán lǐ lǜ cǎo yīn yīn.
    - The park is filled with lush grass.
  - 晨( )露( )滋( )润(潤)艸( )丛(叢)，微( )风(風)使( )它( )们(們)起( )舞( )。
    - Chénlù zīrùn cǎo cóng, wēifēng shǐ tāmen qǐwǔ.
    - The morning dew nourishes the cluster of grass, and the breeze makes them dance.
  - 秋( )天( )时(時)艸( )变(變)黄(黃)，冬天它又枯萎。
    - Qiūtiān shí cǎo biàn huáng, dōngtiān tā yòu kūwěi.
    - In autumn, the grass turns yellow, and in winter, it withers.
### 荷花 → 荷 → 艸 → 屮
- **definition:** grass
- **pinyin:** chè
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts a sprout emerging from the ground
  - **interpretation:** 屮 (chè, 'sprout') is a pictogram that directly represents a young plant or sprout emerging from the ground. Its visual form captures the essence of new growth, and it historically served as an early representation of plant life or new vegetation.
- **examples:**
  - 春( )雨( )滋( )润(潤)后(後)，小( )屮( )冲(沖)破( )泥( )土( )而( )出( )。
    - chūn yǔ zī rùn hòu, xiǎo chè chōng pò ní tǔ ér chū.
    - After the spring rain nurtured the earth, the little sprout broke through the soil.
  - 草屮初生露尖端。
    - cǎo chè chū shēng lù jiān duān.
    - The grass sprout's tip has just emerged.
### 荷花 → 荷 → 何
- **definition:** phonetic component
- **pinyin:** hé
- **components:**
  - 亻
    - rén
    - person
  - 可
    - kě
    - can, may
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 亻 (rén, "person") + phonetic: 可 (kě, "can, may") =
    - person's ability to do something ->
    - interrogative 'what, why'
  - **interpretation:** The character 何 (hé, "what") combines the person radical 亻 (rén, "person") with 可 (kě, "can, may") as the phonetic component, indicating actions or attributes related to people, hence its use in asking questions starting with 'what' or 'why'.
- **examples:**
  - 何( )是( )汉(漢)字( )中( )常( )见(見)的( )声(聲)旁( )。
    - Hé shì hànzì zhōng chángjiàn de shēngpáng.
    - ‘Hé’ is a common phonetic component in Chinese characters.
  - 研( )究( )者( )探( )讨(討)了( )何( )在( )不( )同( )字( )中( )的( )作( )用( )，并(並)强(強)调(調)它( )的( )重( )要( )性( )。
    - Yánjiū zhě tàntǎo le hé zài bùtóng zì zhōng de zuòyòng, bìng qiángdiào tā de zhòngyào xìng.
    - Researchers discussed the role of 'hé' in various characters and emphasized its importance.
  - 学(學)者( )研( )究( )何( )的( )音( )韵(韻)功( )能( )。
    - Xuézhě yánjiū hé de yīnyùn gōngnéng.
    - Scholars study the phonological function of 'hé'.
### 荷花 → 荷 → 何 → 亻
- **definition:** person
- **pinyin:** rén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 亻 (rén, "person") is a stylization of 人 (rén, "person") ->
    - simplified for use as a component in other characters
  - **interpretation:** 亻 (rén, "person") is used as a component form of 人 (rén, "person") in compound characters, representing the human or person-related aspect.
- **examples:**
  - 亻( )是( )构(構)成( )许(許)多( )汉(漢)字( )的( )关(關)键(鍵)部( )首( )。
    - Rén shì gòuchéng xǔduō hànzì de guānjiàn bùshǒu.
    - The radical 亻 is a key component in many Chinese characters.
  - 学(學)习(習)汉(漢)字( )时(時)，了( )解( )亻( )的( )意( )义(義)很( )重( )要( )。
    - Xuéxí hànzì shí, liǎojiě rén de yìyì hěn zhòngyào.
    - When learning Chinese characters, understanding the meaning of 亻 is important.
  - 古( )人( )造( )字( )时(時)常( )取( )亻( )以( )表( )达(達)人( )物( )情( )感( )，因( )此( )亻( )贯(貫)穿( )于(於)文( )明( )史( )中( )。
    - Gǔrén zào zì shí cháng qǔ rén yǐ biǎodá rénwù qínggǎn, yīncǐ rén guànchuān yú wénmíng shǐ zhōng.
    - Ancient people often used the radical 亻 to express human emotions in character formation, and thus 亻 runs through the history of civilization.
### 荷花 → 荷 → 何 → 可
- **definition:** can, may
- **pinyin:** kě
- **components:**
  - 口
    - kǒu
    - mouth
  - 丂
    - kǎo
    - exhaled air
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic: 口 (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic: 丂 (kǎo) =
    - reference to exhaled air "to sing" ->
    - capability to perform actions
  - **interpretation:** This character combines the semantic element 口 (kǒu, "mouth") with the phonetic 丂 (kǎo), referencing exhaling or vocal actions like singing, which suggests expressing capability or permission.
- **examples:**
  - 你( )可( )尝(嘗)试(試)一( )下( )新( )方( )法( )。
    - Nǐ kě chángshì yīxià xīn fāngfǎ.
    - You may try a new method.
  - 此( )事( )虽(雖)难(難)，你( )可( )坚(堅)持( )到( )底( )。
    - Cǐ shì suī nán, nǐ kě jiānchí dàodǐ.
    - Although this matter is difficult, you may persist to the end.
### 荷花 → 荷 → 何 → 可 → 口
- **definition:** mouth
- **pinyin:** kǒu
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts an open mouth.
  - **interpretation:** The character 口(kǒu, 'mouth') is a straightforward pictogram that visually represents an open mouth. It is a fundamental character in the Chinese language, often used both as an independent word and as a component in more complex characters, lending a semantic association to ideas relating to speech, openings, or enclosures.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )张(張)开(開)口( )，大( )声(聲)喊( )叫( )。
    - Tā zhāngkāi kǒu, dàshēng hǎnjiào.
    - He opened his mouth and shouted loudly.
  - 这(這)口( )饭(飯)入( )口( )即( )化( )。
    - Zhè kǒu fàn rùkǒu jíhuà.
    - This mouthful of food melts in your mouth.
  - 小( )女( )孩( )咬( )了( )一( )口( )苹(蘋)果( )，露出甜美的笑容。
    - Xiǎo nǚhái yǎole yī kǒu píngguǒ, lùchū tiánměi de xiàoróng.
    - The little girl took a bite of the apple and smiled sweetly.
### 荷花 → 荷 → 何 → 可 → 丂
- **definition:** exhaled air
- **pinyin:** kǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - Depicts exhaled air or perhaps a stylized representation of breath ->
    - associated with exhalation
  - **interpretation:** 丂 is a pictogram depicting the movement or sensation of exhaled air. It captures the concept of breath or air moving outwards, hence its association with exhalation.
### 荷花 → 花
- **definition:** flower
- **pinyin:** huā
- **components:**
  - 艹
    - cǎo
    - grass; plant
  - 化
    - huà
    - transform
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** phono-semantic compound
  - **description:**
    - semantic 艹 (cǎo, "grass; plant") + phonetic 化 (huà, "transform") =
    - plant + phonetic indicator ->
    - flower
  - **interpretation:** 花 (huā, "flower") combines 艹 (cǎo, "grass; plant") indicating its association with vegetation, and 化 (huà, "transform") for its phonetic value. This illustrates both the natural aspect and the phonetic pronunciation.
- **examples:**
  - 花香四溢。
    - huā xiāng sì yì。
    - The fragrance of flowers fills the air.
  - 阳(陽)光( )明( )媚( )，花( )儿(兒)争(爭)艳(艷)。
    - yáng guāng míng mèi，huā ér zhēng yàn。
    - The bright sunshine shines as the flowers compete in beauty.
  - 她( )用( )花( )装(裝)点(點)房( )间(間)。
    - tā yòng huā zhuāng diǎn fáng jiān。
    - She decorates the room with flowers.
### 荷花 → 花 → 艹
- **definition:** grass; plant
- **pinyin:** cǎo
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:** Depicts blades of grass
  - **interpretation:** 艹 is a pictogram representing grass or plants, resembling blades of grass. It is commonly used as a radical in other characters related to grass or vegetation.
- **examples:**
  - 他( )写(寫)字( )时(時)用( )艹( )作( )草( )字( )的( )偏( )旁( )。
    - Tā xiězì shí yòng cǎo zuò cǎozì de piānpáng.
    - He uses '艹' as the radical for grass in his characters.
  - 春( )天( )到( )来(來)时(時)，艹( )生( )长(長)得( )很( )快( )。
    - Chūntiān dàolái shí, cǎo shēngzhǎng de hěn kuài.
    - When spring arrives, grass grows rapidly.
### 荷花 → 花 → 化
- **definition:** transform
- **pinyin:** huà
- **components:**
  - 亻
    - rén
    - person
  - 𠤎
    - rén
    - person
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - 亻 (rén, "person") + 𠤎 (rén, "person") upside down =
    - two people, one upright, one reversed ->
    - transformation or change
  - **interpretation:** The character 化 (huà, "transform") visually represents change or transformation, signified by two 人 (rén, "person") characters in opposite orientations, suggesting reversal or alteration.
- **examples:**
  - 科( )学(學)进(進)步( )，推( )动(動)社( )会(會)化( )转(轉)型( )。
    - Kēxué jìnbù, tuīdòng shèhuì huà zhuǎnxíng.
    - Scientific progress transforms social structures.
  - 时(時)间(間)能( )让(讓)伤(傷)痛( )化( )作( )记(記)忆(憶)。
    - Shíjiān néng ràng shāngtòng huà zuò jìyì.
    - Time transforms pain into memories.
  - 困( )境( )中( )他( )不( )断(斷)努( )力( )，生( )命( )终(終)将(將)化( )为(為)辉(輝)煌( )。
    - Kùnjìng zhōng tā bùduàn nǔlì, shēngmìng zhōng jiāng huà wéi huīhuáng.
    - Amid difficulties he perseveres, and his life will eventually transform into brilliance.
### 荷花 → 花 → 化 → 亻
- **definition:** person
- **pinyin:** rén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** pictogram
  - **description:**
    - 亻 (rén, "person") is a stylization of 人 (rén, "person") ->
    - simplified for use as a component in other characters
  - **interpretation:** 亻 (rén, "person") is used as a component form of 人 (rén, "person") in compound characters, representing the human or person-related aspect.
- **examples:**
  - 亻( )是( )构(構)成( )许(許)多( )汉(漢)字( )的( )关(關)键(鍵)部( )首( )。
    - Rén shì gòuchéng xǔduō hànzì de guānjiàn bùshǒu.
    - The radical 亻 is a key component in many Chinese characters.
  - 学(學)习(習)汉(漢)字( )时(時)，了( )解( )亻( )的( )意( )义(義)很( )重( )要( )。
    - Xuéxí hànzì shí, liǎojiě rén de yìyì hěn zhòngyào.
    - When learning Chinese characters, understanding the meaning of 亻 is important.
  - 古( )人( )造( )字( )时(時)常( )取( )亻( )以( )表( )达(達)人( )物( )情( )感( )，因( )此( )亻( )贯(貫)穿( )于(於)文( )明( )史( )中( )。
    - Gǔrén zào zì shí cháng qǔ rén yǐ biǎodá rénwù qínggǎn, yīncǐ rén guànchuān yú wénmíng shǐ zhōng.
    - Ancient people often used the radical 亻 to express human emotions in character formation, and thus 亻 runs through the history of civilization.
### 荷花 → 花 → 化 → 𠤎
- **definition:** person
- **pinyin:** rén
- **etymology:**
  - **type:** ideogrammic compound
  - **description:**
    - Two 人 (rén, "person"), one upright (亻) and one upside down (𠤎) ->
    - reversal or change
  - **interpretation:** 924
- **examples:**
  - 他是勇敢的𠤎。
    - Tā shì yǒnggǎn de rén.
    - He is a brave person.
  - 这(這)个(個)𠤎( )勤( )奋(奮)好( )学(學)，为(為)家( )人( )争(爭)光( )。
    - Zhège rén qínfèn hàoxué, wèi jiārén zhēngguāng.
    - This person is diligent and studious, bringing honor to his family.
  - 老( )𠤎( )智( )慧( )丰(豐)富( )。
    - Lǎo rén zhìhuì fēngfù.
    - The elderly person is wise and knowledgeable.
